items.title: Pro-arbitration turn? Appeals court stays FRAND case against Nokia
items.title_zh: 转向支持仲裁？上诉法院中止针对诺基亚的FRAND案
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Stays FRAND Litigation Against Nokia, Signaling Shift Toward Arbitration
items.summary: Nokia, represented by a team from Bird & Bird, adjudged to have made fair offer to Asus and Acer in UK SEP dispute
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，在英国一起标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷中，由Bird & Bird团队代理的诺基亚被裁定已向华硕和宏碁提出公平要约。标题进一步指出，上诉法院已中止针对诺基亚的FRAND案件，显示出支持仲裁的趋势。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has ordered a stay of proceedings in the standard essential patent (SEP) dispute involving Nokia, Asus, and Acer. The court determined that Nokia had already extended a licensing offer to the defendants that satisfied fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory (FRAND) obligations. This ruling effectively pauses the litigation, prioritizing the enforcement of existing contractual agreements over judicial determination of license terms. The decision is widely interpreted as a significant signal from the English judiciary favoring arbitration for resolving FRAND disputes. By emphasizing the primacy of party autonomy and contractual commitments, this judgment may substantially influence future strategic approaches to SEP enforcement and defense across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国法院在SEP纠纷中对仲裁程序的态度变化，以及“公平要约”认定对后续FRAND费率确定的潜在影响。目前信息有限，仅能确认案件程序性中止及初步事实认定。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores the critical importance of robust arbitration clauses within SEP licensing frameworks. Parties should strategically leverage such provisions to divert disputes away from costly, public national court proceedings toward more private and efficient arbitral forums. Furthermore, licensors must ensure their initial offers strictly adhere to FRAND standards to maintain legal leverage and mitigate compliance risks. Defense teams should similarly scrutinize offer validity early in proceedings. Monitoring how lower courts interpret this preference for arbitration will be essential for shaping future litigation strategies and settlement negotiations in the evolving European SEP landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院中止了针对诺基亚的FRAND案件，标题暗示此举体现支持仲裁的倾向", "在涉及华硕和宏碁的英国SEP纠纷中，诺基亚被裁定已作出公平的要约"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Court of Appeal stayed the SEP FRAND litigation against Nokia, prioritizing contractual arbitration clauses.", "Nokia was adjudged to have made a valid FRAND-compliant licensing offer to plaintiffs Asus and Acer.", "The ruling indicates a judicial trend supporting arbitration for resolving complex FRAND licensing disputes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["观察英国上诉法院中止诉讼的具体法律依据及其对平行仲裁程序的影响", "关注法院对诺基亚向华硕、宏碁所作要约“公平性”的认定标准及后续效力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage arbitration clauses to avoid high-cost, public UK SEP litigation and ensure privacy.", "Practice note: Verify that all licensing offers strictly meet FRAND criteria to secure procedural advantages.", "What to monitor: How subsequent rulings apply this pro-arbitration stance to other European SEP cases."]
core_points_en: The UK Court of Appeal stayed the SEP FRAND litigation against Nokia, prioritizing contractual arbitration clauses.
Nokia was adjudged to have made a valid FRAND-compliant licensing offer to plaintiffs Asus and Acer.
The ruling indicates a judicial trend supporting arbitration for resolving complex FRAND licensing disputes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage arbitration clauses to avoid high-cost, public UK SEP litigation and ensure privacy.
Practice note: Verify that all licensing offers strictly meet FRAND criteria to secure procedural advantages.
What to monitor: How subsequent rulings apply this pro-arbitration stance to other European SEP cases.

items.title: “The UPC wants to bring ZTE and Samsung to the negotiating table”
items.title_zh: UPC拟推动中兴与三星进入谈判桌
items.title_en: UPC Recommends ZTE and Samsung Pursue PMAC Mediation Over Continuing Litigation
items.summary: In its proposal, the UPC recommends that, rather than continuing the patent proceedings, the parties convene at the PMAC for mediation on the very day it begins operations. The UPC is therefore strongly encouraging the parties to come to what it describes as an “amicable solution”. Although the order is merely a recommendation and not […] The post “The UPC wants to bring ZTE and Samsung to the negotiating table” appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）在提案中建议，中兴与三星应在专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）运营首日进行调解，而非继续诉讼程序。UPC强烈鼓励双方达成“友好解决方案”。该命令仅为建议性质，旨在通过替代性争议解决机制处理标准必要专利纠纷。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has formally recommended that ZTE and Samsung engage in mediation at the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Center (PMAC) rather than continuing their ongoing patent litigation. This proposal suggests convening for mediation on the very day the PMAC begins its operations, signaling a strong judicial preference for resolving disputes through alternative mechanisms. The UPC explicitly encourages both parties to seek an amicable solution to their standard essential patent (SEP) conflict. Although this recommendation lacks binding legal force, it reflects the court’s strategic orientation toward facilitating settlements in complex commercial disputes. By promoting early mediation, the UPC aims to reduce litigation costs and accelerate resolution timelines, offering a pragmatic pathway for parties involved in high-stakes SEP disagreements to reach mutually acceptable outcomes outside of traditional courtroom proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC在SEP案件中推动调解的程序倾向。尽管目前仅为建议，但反映了法院早期介入促进和解的姿态。后续应观察PMAC实际运作效率及当事人对非诉解决机制的响应程度，以及该模式是否成为SEP纠纷处理的常规路径。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the UPC's increasing emphasis on mediation via the PMAC as a preferred method for resolving standard essential patent disputes. Companies involved in similar litigation should carefully evaluate the cost-benefit ratio of continuing protracted legal battles versus engaging in early-stage mediation. Utilizing the PMAC framework may provide opportunities to negotiate more favorable FRAND licensing terms while avoiding the financial and operational burdens of extended court proceedings. Stakeholders must remain agile, adjusting their European IP enforcement and defense strategies to align with this judicial trend toward consensual resolution, thereby mitigating risks associated with prolonged judicial uncertainty and escalating legal expenses.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC建议双方在PMAC运营首日进行调解，以替代继续进行的专利诉讼程序", "UPC强烈鼓励当事人达成其描述的“友好解决方案”，但该命令仅具推荐性质"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC recommends that ZTE and Samsung utilize the PMAC for mediation instead of continuing with current patent infringement proceedings.", "The court strongly encourages both parties to achieve an amicable solution, highlighting a shift toward alternative dispute resolution methods.", "While non-binding, the recommendation underscores the UPC's policy preference for settlement to manage complex SEP disputes efficiently."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC在SEP诉讼中展现出推动早期调解的程序偏好", "需观察PMAC启动后对类似高科技专利纠纷的实际分流效果", "关注该建议性命令是否转化为后续案件管理的强制性或半强制性流程"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the strategic advantage of early PMAC mediation over continued litigation to potentially secure better FRAND terms.", "Practice note: Assess litigation costs against settlement probabilities, leveraging the UPC's preference for amicable solutions in SEP cases.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent UPC decisions and PMAC outcomes to refine European IP enforcement and defense strategies."]
core_points_en: The UPC recommends that ZTE and Samsung utilize the PMAC for mediation instead of continuing with current patent infringement proceedings.
The court strongly encourages both parties to achieve an amicable solution, highlighting a shift toward alternative dispute resolution methods.
While non-binding, the recommendation underscores the UPC's policy preference for settlement to manage complex SEP disputes efficiently.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate the strategic advantage of early PMAC mediation over continued litigation to potentially secure better FRAND terms.
Practice note: Assess litigation costs against settlement probabilities, leveraging the UPC's preference for amicable solutions in SEP cases.
What to monitor: Track subsequent UPC decisions and PMAC outcomes to refine European IP enforcement and defense strategies.

items.title: Nokia wins landmark English Court of Appeal decision against Acer/Asus
items.title_zh: 诺基亚在英国上诉法院胜诉宏碁/华硕
items.title_en: Nokia secures permanent stay of RAND claims in English Court of Appeal ruling
items.summary: Appellate judges permanently stay final and interim RAND claims based on Nokia arbitration-based adjustable licence offer.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院作出标志性裁决，支持诺基亚。基于诺基亚提出的基于仲裁的可调整许可要约，法院永久中止了针对宏碁和华硕的最终及临时FRAND索赔主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The English Court of Appeal has issued a landmark decision in favor of Nokia against Acer and Asus. Appellate judges ordered a permanent stay of both final and interim RAND claims. This procedural outcome is based on Nokia’s submission of an arbitration-based adjustable licence offer. The ruling highlights the judicial weight given to such offers in resolving standard-essential patent disputes within this jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores the efficacy of arbitration-based licensing offers in staying litigation. For rights holders and implementers, it signals that courts may prioritize alternative dispute resolution mechanisms when specific offer structures are presented. Parties should note the threshold for securing such stays in future SEP enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院裁定永久中止针对宏碁和华硕的FRAND索赔", "该中止决定基于诺基亚提出的基于仲裁的可调整许可要约"]

items.title: The General Court of the EU refuses Klarna’s application to register the ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial services
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院驳回Klarna注册“K”商标申请
items.title_en: The General Court of the EU refuses Klarna’s application to register the ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial services
items.summary: Today’s judgment by the General Court of the European Union (GCEU) upholds the refusal to register Karna’s ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial, monetary, banking, insurance and real estate services, primarily confirming its incompatibility with the trade mark protected by Kutxabank in Class 36, a position the Spanish bank has maintained for years. The ... Read more La entrada The General Court of the EU refuses Klarna’s application to register the ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial serv
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院维持驳回Klarna在金融等服务上注册“K”商标的决定。法院确认该标志与西班牙Kutxabank银行在第36类受保护的商标存在冲突，支持了后者长期持有的异议立场。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显单一字母商标在金融服务领域的注册高门槛。当存在行业内在先近似标志时，即使申请人知名度高，也难以克服相对理由驳回。需关注法院对混淆可能性的具体认定逻辑。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院判决维持对Klarna“K”商标注册申请的驳回决定", "驳回主因是该商标与西班牙Kutxabank银行在第36类的在先商标不兼容"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["单一字母商标在特定服务类别易因在先权利被驳回", "跨成员国在先商标可有效阻碍欧盟层面的新商标注册", "需持续观察此类简短标志在金融领域的可注册性标准"]

items.title: Streaming Without Copying: The CJEU Redefines Private Use in Stichting de Thuiskopie (C-496/24)
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院重定义私人使用：Stichting de Thuiskopie案判决
items.title_en: CJEU Ruling: Offline Streaming Copies Do Not Qualify for Private Copying Exception
items.summary: Introduction
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月16日，欧盟法院第二庭就C-496/24案作出判决，涉及《信息社会指令》第5(2)(b)条。案件源于荷兰集体管理组织与硬件制造商关于订阅制流媒体“离线流媒体副本”是否适用私人复制征费的争议。海牙上诉法院此前认定此类副本不属于私人复制。最高法院提请欧盟法院裁定此类受技术控制、用户无法转移的副本是否属于私人复制例外范围，以及许可报酬的相关性。
items.ai_summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the Court of Justice ruled in Stichting de Thuiskopie that offline streaming copies from subscription services do not fall under the private copying exception in Article 5(2)(b) of the InfoSoc Directive. The Court determined that these copies are strictly controlled by technological protection measures, preventing users from transferring files or accessing them outside the designated application. Consequently, rights holders retain control over access and distribution. As a result, hardware manufacturers are not required to pay private copying levies for devices used primarily for such controlled offline streaming. This judgment recalibrates the scope of copyright exceptions in the digital era, emphasizing that technical restrictions preventing free user control exclude content from the levy system applicable to traditional private copying.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案澄清数字环境下私人复制例外的边界，特别是针对受DRM保护的流媒体离线缓存。实务中需关注法院如何平衡权利人技术控制权与用户私人复制补偿机制，这将影响荷兰乃至欧盟范围内的版权征费体系及设备制造商责任。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the boundary between taxable private copying and licensed digital access. For technology providers and hardware manufacturers operating in the EU, implementing robust Digital Rights Management (DRM) that prevents file transfer and restricts access to specific applications is crucial. Such technical measures can effectively exclude products from private copying levy obligations. Companies should review their DRM strategies to ensure they align with the CJEU’s definition of non-free copying. By maintaining strict technical control over content usage, businesses can mitigate compliance costs and avoid unexpected tax liabilities associated with the private copying exception framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院审理订阅流媒体中离线副本是否适用私人复制例外及征费", "争议焦点在于用户无法控制存储位置且受加密限制的副本性质认定", "需裁定信息社会指令目标是否排除此类副本适用私人复制例外"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Offline streaming copies within subscription services do not qualify as private copying under EU law due to strict technical controls.", "Users cannot transfer or freely access files, meaning rights holders maintain effective control over the content distribution.", "Hardware manufacturers are exempt from paying private copying levies for devices used for these technologically restricted streams."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟法院对受技术保护措施限制的流媒体副本的法律定性", "观察判决对现有私人复制征费体系在订阅模式下的适用性影响", "监测该裁决对硬件制造商在新型数字分发模式下的合规义务指引"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implementing strict DRM to prevent file transfer may exempt hardware from EU private copying levies.", "Practice note: Align technical protection measures with CJEU standards to demonstrate lack of free user control over content.", "What to monitor: Future CJEU interpretations of digital copyright exceptions and evolving definitions of private use exemptions."]
core_points_en: Offline streaming copies within subscription services do not qualify as private copying under EU law due to strict technical controls.
Users cannot transfer or freely access files, meaning rights holders maintain effective control over the content distribution.
Hardware manufacturers are exempt from paying private copying levies for devices used for these technologically restricted streams.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implementing strict DRM to prevent file transfer may exempt hardware from EU private copying levies.
Practice note: Align technical protection measures with CJEU standards to demonstrate lack of free user control over content.
What to monitor: Future CJEU interpretations of digital copyright exceptions and evolving definitions of private use exemptions.

items.title: UK appeals court overturns key ruling on trademark licensee damages
items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院推翻商标被许可人损害赔偿关键裁决
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Rules Unregistered Trademark Licensees Cannot Claim Damages
items.summary: Frasers Group—formerly Sports Direct—secures a win against Lifestyle Equities, which has significant implications for brands with unregistered licensees for their marks.
items.ai_summary_zh: Frasers Group（前Sports Direct）在与Lifestyle Equities的诉讼中胜诉。英国上诉法院推翻了此前关于商标被许可人损害赔偿的关键裁决。该案结果对拥有未注册许可人的品牌方具有重大影响，明确了相关法律立场。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has overturned a significant lower court ruling in the dispute between Frasers Group and Lifestyle Equities, determining that unregistered trademark licensees generally lack standing to claim damages for infringement. This decision reverses previous findings that favored the trademark owner, establishing a higher legal threshold for such claims. The judgment clarifies that without registration, licensees face substantial hurdles in seeking compensation independently. This outcome carries profound implications for brand owners managing portfolios with unregistered marks, highlighting the critical importance of securing formal trademark registrations and carefully structuring licensing agreements to mitigate enforcement risks and potential financial losses.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了英国法下商标未注册许可人索赔权的重要判例。需关注法院对许可协议形式要件与损害赔偿资格之间关联性的具体法律论证，以评估对现有许可架构的潜在冲击。
items.ai_insight_en: Brand owners operating in the UK and EU markets should prioritize the local registration of core trademarks to avoid reliance on unregistered rights, which offer limited enforcement capabilities. When drafting licensing agreements, parties must explicitly define the allocation of infringement claim rights and mechanisms for damage distribution. Clear contractual terms are essential to navigate potential legal uncertainties and prevent disputes over compensation entitlements. Companies should review existing licenses to ensure they align with this stricter judicial interpretation, thereby safeguarding their ability to enforce rights effectively and minimizing exposure to uncompensated infringement.

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Accelerate local trademark registration in the UK and EU to reduce dependency on unregistered mark protections.", "Practice note: Refine licensing agreement clauses to clearly specify ownership of infringement claims and damage distribution mechanisms.", "What to monitor: Monitor further judicial interpretations regarding standing and damages for unregistered intellectual property rights."]
core_points_en: Unregistered trademark licensees generally cannot independently claim damages for infringement under current UK law.
Frasers Group successfully appealed, overturning a prior lower court judgment that had favored the trademark licensee.
This precedent significantly raises the difficulty for unregistered licensees to obtain compensation in infringement litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Accelerate local trademark registration in the UK and EU to reduce dependency on unregistered mark protections.
Practice note: Refine licensing agreement clauses to clearly specify ownership of infringement claims and damage distribution mechanisms.
What to monitor: Monitor further judicial interpretations regarding standing and damages for unregistered intellectual property rights.

items.title: EPO Enlarged Board Clarifies: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art, Even If Irreproducible
items.title_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会：公开产品即便不可再现亦属现有技术
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art Even If Irreproducible
items.summary: The European Patent Office's Enlarged Board has fundamentally altered the landscape of prior art assessment, establishing that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art regardless of whether they can be analyzed or reproduced. This landmark decision removes the long-standing reproducibility requirement, making market availability alone sufficient to destroy novelty and significantly raising the stakes for timely patent filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年7月2日，EPO扩大上诉委员会在G1/23案中裁定，申请日前公开的产品构成现有技术，无论其成分是否可被分析或再现。该决定推翻了G1/92案确立的“可再现性”要求，认为仅市场可获得性即足以破坏新颖性。此举消除了长期存在的法律不确定性，显著提高了及时提交专利申请的重要性，任何上市前公开均可能使后续专利无效。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision G 1/23, the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal ruled that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art under Article 54(2) EPC, regardless of whether their composition or internal structure can be analyzed or reproduced. This judgment departs from the earlier G 1/92 precedent, which required both public availability and reproducibility for a product to form part of the state of the art. The Enlarged Board found that maintaining a reproducibility requirement would lead to absurd results, as many materials rely on irreproducible starting elements. Consequently, market availability alone is now sufficient to destroy novelty, significantly expanding the scope of prior art.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕产品上市前的任何公开行为，包括展会展示或销售，因即使技术细节未完全披露，产品本身也可作为现有技术驳回专利。建议建立严格的保密与申请时间管理机制，确保在申请日前不公开核心技术，避免在新规下丧失新颖性。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling fundamentally alters the landscape of prior art assessment in Europe. Rights holders must recognize that any public disclosure, including sales or exhibitions, may invalidate subsequent patent applications if the product itself is considered prior art, irrespective of reverse-engineering capabilities. This heightens the importance of strict confidentiality measures and timely filing strategies to prevent inadvertent loss of novelty due to pre-filing market activities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推翻G1/92先例，取消现有技术认定中的“可再现性”硬性门槛", "确立市场公开即构成现有技术原则，无论内部结构能否被反向工程", "极大扩展现有技术范围，对专利新颖性审查及申请策略产生深远影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.", "Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.", "The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化申请前保密管理，严防产品上市导致新颖性丧失风险", "调整全球布局策略，优先在欧洲完成申请后再进行市场推广"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.", "Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.", "What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings."]
core_points_en: G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.
Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.
The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.
Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.
What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings.

items.title: Why Tesla is ‘forum shopping’ using InterDigital’s UK assets
items.title_zh: 特斯拉利用InterDigital英国资产进行法院选择
items.title_en: Tesla's Strategic Use of InterDigital UK Assets Raises FRAND Jurisdiction Questions
items.summary: A major patent hearing has concluded at the UK Supreme Court. Eeva Hakoranta—formerly chief licensing officer at InterDigital and senior vice president at Nokia—argues that no national court should act as arbiter of global FRAND disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院已结束一场重大专利听证会。前InterDigital首席许可官Eeva Hakoranta指出，任何国家法院都不应充当全球FRAND纠纷的仲裁者。标题显示特斯拉正利用InterDigital在英国的资产进行“法院选择”。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has concluded a significant patent hearing, bringing renewed attention to the boundaries of national courts in adjudicating global standard-essential patent disputes. Eeva Hakoranta, former chief licensing officer at InterDigital and senior vice president at Nokia, argues that no single national court should serve as the arbiter for global fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory terms. The discussion highlights Tesla's litigation strategy involving InterDigital's UK assets, which some characterize as forum shopping. This case underscores the intense legal maneuvering currently defining SEP licensing negotiations. It raises critical questions about whether UK courts are overreaching their jurisdiction by setting global royalty rates, a trend that impacts multinational technology companies navigating complex intellectual property landscapes across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国最高法院对全球FRAND费率管辖权的最终立场。若法院拒绝行使管辖权，可能影响SEP权利人在英诉讼策略及全球许可谈判格局。
items.ai_insight_en: Multinational corporations operating in Europe must closely monitor the UK Supreme Court's evolving stance on global FRAND rate determinations. The potential expansion of UK judicial authority in SEP cases creates significant forum-shopping risks for defendants. Companies should proactively assess these jurisdictional vulnerabilities and develop coordinated, multi-jurisdictional defense strategies rather than reacting passively to adverse rulings. Strengthening internal compliance frameworks and preparing robust negotiation positions are essential to mitigate exposure. Stakeholders should anticipate continued legal friction regarding the extraterritorial reach of national IP courts and align their global litigation management accordingly to avoid being bound by unfavorable unilateral decisions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院已结束涉及InterDigital与特斯拉的重大专利听证会", "行业专家主张国家法院不应仲裁全球FRAND纠纷，质疑管辖权边界", "标题指出特斯拉策略性利用InterDigital英国资产进行法院选择"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court concluded a major hearing focusing on the jurisdictional limits for resolving global FRAND disputes.", "Industry experts argue that individual national courts lack the authority to determine global standard-essential patent licensing rates.", "Tesla is accused of leveraging InterDigital's UK assets to strategically select favorable forums for patent litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国最高法院对全球FRAND管辖权的裁决将直接影响SEP诉讼地选择", "需观察法院是否采纳“国家法院不应仲裁全球纠纷”的观点", "后续需关注该判决对跨国SEP许可谈判及禁令救济的具体影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor UK court tendencies in expanding jurisdiction over global SEP royalty rates to assess litigation exposure.", "Practice note: Develop coordinated multi-jurisdictional defense strategies to counter potential forum-shopping tactics effectively.", "What to monitor: Shifts in judicial reasoning regarding the extraterritorial application of national FRAND determinations."]

Industry experts argue that individual national courts lack the authority to determine global standard-essential patent licensing rates.
Tesla is accused of leveraging InterDigital's UK assets to strategically select favorable forums for patent litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UK court tendencies in expanding jurisdiction over global SEP royalty rates to assess litigation exposure.
Practice note: Develop coordinated multi-jurisdictional defense strategies to counter potential forum-shopping tactics effectively.
What to monitor: Shifts in judicial reasoning regarding the extraterritorial application of national FRAND determinations.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 4, 2026, Review Of An Order To Preserve Evidence, Request For The Expert’s Recusal, UPC_CFI_1696/2025
items.title_zh: 杜塞尔多夫地院审查证据保全令及专家回避申请
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Standards for Expert Recusal and Review of Evidence Preservation Orders
items.summary: There is a basis for concern regarding an expert’s bias if – from the perspective of a knowledgeable and reasonable observer – certain circumstances give rise to justified doubts as to the expert’s impartiality or independence i.e., there is a likelihood that the expert’s decision will be influenced by factors other than the aforementioned duties.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫地方法院于2026年5月4日作出裁定，审查证据保全令并处理专家回避请求。裁定明确，若理性观察者对专家公正性产生合理怀疑，即存在偏见担忧。专家须保证独立性与客观性，仅凭鉴定意见内容不足以致疑，需有额外情形表明不客观态度。复审仅审查签发命令时的明显错误。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of First Instance in Düsseldorf reviewed an order for the preservation of evidence and a request for expert recusal. The court clarified that concerns regarding expert bias arise only if a knowledgeable, reasonable observer would have justified doubts about impartiality or independence. Mere errors in an expert opinion or lack of expertise are insufficient to establish bias; additional circumstances suggesting an unobjective attitude are required. The review proceedings were limited to examining manifest errors in the issuance of the ex parte order, excluding assessment of its execution or results. This decision reinforces the overriding duty of experts to assist the court impartially under the Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the high threshold for challenging court-appointed experts in UPC proceedings, distinguishing between technical disagreement and actual bias. It also confirms the narrow scope of review for ex parte evidence preservation orders, focusing on procedural legality at issuance rather than subsequent implementation. Rights holders should prepare robust factual evidence of external influences when seeking recusal, while respondents must ensure strict procedural compliance during the initial order phase.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["理性观察者若对专家公正性存合理怀疑，即构成偏见担忧基础", "仅凭鉴定意见内容或错误不足以质疑专家 impartiality，需额外不客观情形", "复审程序仅审查签发单方证据保全令时是否存在明显错误，不涉及执行结果"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Expert bias requires justified doubts from a reasonable observer, not merely erroneous opinions or lack of expertise.", "Additional circumstances suggesting an unobjective attitude are necessary to challenge an expert's impartiality or independence.", "Review of evidence preservation orders is limited to manifest errors in issuance, excluding execution or results."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["专家回避需证明存在影响独立性的额外情形，而非仅质疑鉴定结论", "证据保全令复审限于审查签发时的明显错误，不延伸至执行阶段", "需持续观察UPC各分院在适用“理性观察者”标准时的一致性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Challenges to expert neutrality require proof of external bias factors, not just disagreement with technical findings or alleged incompetence.", "Practice note: When opposing ex parte preservation orders, focus legal arguments on manifest errors in the initial issuance criteria, as execution details are excluded from review.", "What to monitor: Consistency across UPC divisions in applying the 'reasonable observer' test for expert recusal and the definition of manifest errors in preservation orders."]
core_points_en: Expert bias requires justified doubts from a reasonable observer, not merely erroneous opinions or lack of expertise.
Additional circumstances suggesting an unobjective attitude are necessary to challenge an expert's impartiality or independence.
Review of evidence preservation orders is limited to manifest errors in issuance, excluding execution or results.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Challenges to expert neutrality require proof of external bias factors, not just disagreement with technical findings or alleged incompetence.
Practice note: When opposing ex parte preservation orders, focus legal arguments on manifest errors in the initial issuance criteria, as execution details are excluded from review.
What to monitor: Consistency across UPC divisions in applying the 'reasonable observer' test for expert recusal and the definition of manifest errors in preservation orders.

items.title: LD Hamburg, May 5, 2026, Order On Penalty Payments, UPC_CFI_1881/2025
items.title_zh: UPC汉堡地方法院就违规罚款作出裁定
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg Order: Penalty Payments, Prior Threats, and Counsel Duties
items.summary: Recurring penalty payments may be imposed for non-compliance with court orders under Rule 354(3) RoP
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院汉堡地方法院在Brita诉宁波Blue Pluser案中裁定，依据程序规则354条，对未履行信息提供义务的被告处以每日1000欧元罚款。法院强调，实施罚款前必须预先警告，且罚款金额不得超过预先警告的上限。即使律师终止委托，仍需负责法院沟通。被告因故意不配合被推定有责，适用最高日罚款率。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division Hamburg issued an order on penalty payments for non-compliance with information obligations in Brita v. Ningbo Blue Pluser. The Court imposed the maximum daily rate of EUR 1,000, citing culpable non-compliance. It reaffirmed that a prior threat of penalties is mandatory under Rule 354(4) RoP, even in provisional measures. Crucially, the Court held that penalty amounts cannot exceed the previously threatened cap, rejecting requests for higher escalating fines. Additionally, legal representatives remain responsible for court communications after mandate termination. This decision clarifies enforcement mechanics, emphasizing strict adherence to procedural warnings and continuous counsel accountability during UPC proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This order underscores the procedural rigidity of UPC enforcement. Rights holders must ensure initial orders explicitly state penalty threats to enable future coercion. Conversely, respondents face strict liability for non-compliance once warned. The ruling on counsel duties highlights risks in transitioning representation, requiring clear handover protocols to avoid procedural defaults and maximum financial penalties.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["实施周期性罚款前必须发出预先警告，该要求同样适用于临时措施程序", "法院判处的罚款金额不得超过此前预先警告中设定的上限，不得随意提高", "若被告无正当理由拒不履行义务，法院可推定其存在过错并适用最高罚款率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Prior threat of penalty payments is mandatory before imposition, including in provisional measures proceedings.", "Court cannot impose penalties exceeding the amount previously threatened in the operative order.", "Legal representatives retain communication responsibilities toward the Court even after mandate termination."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC执行程序中对罚款预先警告的形式要求严格，需嵌入主命令或单独命令中", "罚款金额受限于预先警告上限，申请 escalating penalties 需分阶段进…", "律师终止委托后的沟通责任延续，当事人需建立替代联络机制以防程序违规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure enforcement orders contain specific penalty threats to preserve coercive options for future non-compliance.", "Practice note: Counsel must maintain court communication channels until formally relieved, regardless of mandate status changes.", "What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply the 'prior threat' requirement across different types of provisional and final measures."]
core_points_en: Prior threat of penalty payments is mandatory before imposition, including in provisional measures proceedings.
Court cannot impose penalties exceeding the amount previously threatened in the operative order.
Legal representatives retain communication responsibilities toward the Court even after mandate termination.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure enforcement orders contain specific penalty threats to preserve coercive options for future non-compliance.
Practice note: Counsel must maintain court communication channels until formally relieved, regardless of mandate status changes.
What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply the 'prior threat' requirement across different types of provisional and final measures.

items.title: PMAC Advances Operations with New Procedural Rules and Launch of Case Management System
items.title_zh: UPC专利调解仲裁中心发布新规并上线案件管理系统
items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Adopts Rules and Launches Case Management System
items.summary: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has taken significant steps toward full operational readiness,
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会于2026年4月正式通过专利调解和仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则，并于5月12日上线专用案件管理系统（CCMS）。PMAC旨在提供调解、仲裁等替代性争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利及标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷。此举标志着PMAC具备全面运营能力，为权利人提供了高效灵活的专利争议解决新途径，进一步完善了UPC生态系统。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has achieved key operational milestones with the adoption of its procedural rules and the launch of its Case Management System. The Administrative Committee formally approved the framework on 24 April 2026, establishing the legal basis for offering mediation, arbitration, and expert determination services. These mechanisms cover European patents, Unitary Patents, and Standard Essential Patent disputes. The electronic Case Management System went live on 12 May 2026, enabling digital submission of ADR requests. Mediation services are scheduled to begin in late May 2026, with other services following later in the year.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉及专利纠纷时，可关注UPC-PMAC提供的调解与仲裁渠道。相比传统诉讼，ADR程序更具灵活性与保密性，有助于降低维权成本并加速SEP许可谈判进程。建议企业评估将部分非核心或复杂跨国争议转入PMAC处理，以优化全球知识产权战略布局。
items.ai_insight_en: The operational launch of the UPC’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre provides rights holders with structured alternative dispute resolution options alongside traditional litigation. This development is particularly relevant for managing complex patent portfolios and resolving high-stakes technical or licensing disputes efficiently. Parties should review the newly published procedural rules and fee schedules to understand the scope and requirements of these non-litigation pathways within the unified patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC行政委员会正式批准PMAC程序规则，奠定法律与运营基础", "PMAC案件管理系统正式上线，支持电子提交替代性争议解决请求", "服务范围涵盖欧洲专利及SEP/FRAND案件，提供多元ADR选项"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Administrative Committee adopted PMAC's procedural rules on 24 April 2026, creating the legal foundation for alternative dispute resolution services within the court ecosy…", "PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System on 12 May 2026, allowing parties to electronically submit requests for mediation, arbitration, and expert determination proceedi…", "The Centre will handle disputes involving European and Unitary Patents, including cross-border conflicts and Standard Essential Patent matters, with mediation services commencing…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC的ADR机制可降低在欧专利诉讼成本并提升解决效率", "针对SEP/FRAND纠纷，可探索通过调解仲裁达成更优许可条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Offers a new avenue for resolving SEP/FRAND and cross-border patent disputes outside of full litigation, potentially enhancing flexibility in portfolio managemen…", "Practice note: Review the adopted procedural rules and CCMS guidance to prepare for electronic case submissions as mediation services commence in late May 2026.", "What to monitor: Track the rollout schedule for arbitration and expert determination services later in the year and any updates to applicable fee structures."]

PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System on 12 May 2026, allowing parties to electronically submit requests for mediation, arbitration, and expert determination proceedi…

items.title: Judgment of the General Court in Case T-24/25 Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO - Works 11 Michał Lubiński (Obelix)
items.title_zh: 普通法院撤销EUIPO决定：武器类“Obelix”商标应无效
items.title_en: General Court Annuls EUIPO Refusal to Invalidate Obelix Mark for Weapons and Explosives
items.summary: The General Court nullifies EUIPO’s refusal to invalidate the word mark Obelix for weapons, ammunition and explosives
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中撤销EUIPO拒绝宣告“Obelix”武器商标无效的决定。出版商Les Éditions Albert René依据在先OBELIX商标及声誉受损申请无效，EUIPO曾以声誉证据不足为由驳回。法院指出EUIPO分析有误，未综合评估商标声誉及两商标间的联系。法院强调需全局考察在先商标显著性及公众联想，认定存在损害在先商标声誉的风险，支持了权利人的主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The General Court annulled an EUIPO decision refusing to invalidate the word mark Obelix for firearms, ammunition, and explosives. The applicant, Les Éditions Albert René, relied on its earlier OBELIX mark and alleged damage to its reputation. The Court found EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation incomplete, noting it improperly disregarded evidence of use with the registered symbol and in association with Asterix. Furthermore, EUIPO failed to adequately assess the link between the marks and the risk of detriment to the earlier mark’s distinctive character or repute, despite differences in goods.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces that EU trademark offices must conduct a holistic assessment of reputation, considering cumulative evidence rather than isolated deficiencies. Rights holders asserting cross-class protection based on reputation should ensure their submissions clearly demonstrate how distinct elements contribute to overall market recognition. The case highlights the importance of properly contextualizing co-branded or associated signs when establishing independent distinctiveness and repute for specific terms within a larger franchise.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定EUIPO对在先商标声誉评估不完整，忽视关键使用证据", "强调需全局评估商标间联系，不能仅因商品差异大而否定关联", "撤销EUIPO决定，支持基于声誉受损宣告争议商标无效的诉求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.", "Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.", "The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免注册知名文化符号，防范跨类声誉侵权法律风险", "重视商标使用证据留存，积极应对欧盟商标无效宣告挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.", "Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.", "What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks."]
core_points_en: EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.
Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.
The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.
Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.
What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-286/25 BRANDL
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院判决：匈牙利农地用益物权赔偿不得仅依删除时市值计算
items.title_en: CJEU Rules EU Law Precludes Compensation Based Solely on Land Market Value at Deletion
items.summary: Rights of usufruct over agricultural land in Hungary: EU law precludes financial compensation calculated solely on the basis of the market value of the land at the time of deletion of those rights from the land register
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在BRANDL案中裁定，欧盟法禁止成员国将农地用益物权被注销后的赔偿金，仅基于注销时的土地市场价值计算。此前匈牙利立法废除非亲属用益物权被认定违反资本自由流动与财产权。新法允许恢复权利并获赔，但规定按注销时市值的1/20乘以年限计算。法院认为该公式未涵盖利润损失等损害，无法确保有效权利保护与充分赔偿，故不符合欧盟法要求。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice held that EU law precludes national rules calculating financial compensation for deleted usufruct rights over agricultural land solely based on the property’s market value at the time of deletion. In Case C-286/25 BRANDL, the Court found that Hungary’s formula, which excluded other damage elements like lost profits, failed to provide adequate compensation commensurate with the harm suffered. This judgment reinforces that while Member States may define compensation mechanisms, such measures must ensure effective protection of rights guaranteed by the Charter, particularly the right to property and free movement of capital, by covering all relevant components of the actual damage incurred by the rights holder.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies that standardized statutory compensation formulas cannot systematically exclude significant heads of damage, such as lost profits. For rights holders and counsel managing cross-border asset portfolios or historical property rights in the EU, this establishes a baseline for challenging inadequate state compensation schemes. It emphasizes that formal reinstatement of rights is insufficient if the accompanying financial redress does not fully reflect the economic loss incurred during the period of deprivation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法禁止仅以用益物权注销时的土地市场价值为唯一基准计算赔偿金", "完全排除可赔偿损害中的利润损失，可能导致实际上无法弥补所受损害", "成员国虽有权设定赔偿标准，但必须确保赔偿与所受损害相称且有效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU law prohibits compensation calculated exclusively on the market value of land at the time rights were deleted from the register.", "Compensation mechanisms must be commensurate with the damage suffered, ensuring effective protection of fundamental rights under EU law.", "Standardized formulas that systematically exclude key damage elements, such as lost profits, may fail to meet adequacy requirements."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["标准化赔偿公式若排除利润损失，可能因违反有效性原则而被认定无效", "成员国在设定赔偿标准时仍享有裁量权，但须确保个案赔偿的充分性", "需持续观察匈牙利后续如何调整赔偿计算模型以符合欧盟法院判决要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders affected by statutory expropriation or deletion of property rights can challenge compensation schemes that fail to account for full economic loss…", "Practice note: When advising on property rights restoration, ensure compensation claims encompass all demonstrable damages rather than accepting rigid statutory formulas based sol…", "What to monitor: Watch for Hungarian legislative adjustments to align compensation calculations with this ruling and similar challenges in other Member States with restrictive pro…"]
core_points_en: EU law prohibits compensation calculated exclusively on the market value of land at the time rights were deleted from the register.
Compensation mechanisms must be commensurate with the damage suffered, ensuring effective protection of fundamental rights under EU law.
Standardized formulas that systematically exclude key damage elements, such as lost profits, may fail to meet adequacy requirements.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders affected by statutory expropriation or deletion of property rights can challenge compensation schemes that fail to account for full economic loss…
Practice note: When advising on property rights restoration, ensure compensation claims encompass all demonstrable damages rather than accepting rigid statutory formulas based sol…
What to monitor: Watch for Hungarian legislative adjustments to align compensation calculations with this ruling and similar challenges in other Member States with restrictive pro…

items.title: Italian transposition of press publishers’ right may be compatible with EU law, though with caveats, says Grand Chamber
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：意大利新闻出版者权转化大体合规但存保留
items.title_en: CJEU Grand Chamber: Italian Transposition of Press Publishers’ Right Compatible with EU Law
items.summary: Today, the Grand Chamber of the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) issued its long-awaited judgment in Meta, C-797/23, a referral from TAR Lazio (Italy). The case sought to determine whether Italy’s transposition of the press publishers’ right under Article 15 of the DSM Directive – in particular the role of the Communications Authority (AGCOM) – is permitted under EU law, having
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院大法庭就Meta案（C-797/23）作出判决，该案系意大利拉齐奥大区行政法院移送。核心争议在于意大利依据《数字单一市场版权指令》第15条转化新闻出版者权时，特别是通信管理局（AGCOM）所扮演的角色，是否符合欧盟法律规定。
items.ai_summary_en: The Grand Chamber of the Court of Justice of the European Union issued its judgment in Meta, C-797/23, a referral from the TAR Lazio. The Court determined that Italy’s transposition of Article 15 of the DSM Directive, specifically regarding the powers granted to the Communications Authority (AGCOM), is fundamentally compatible with the EU legal framework. While confirming this compatibility, the ruling imposes specific caveats and limitations on the exercise of these national regulatory powers. This decision clarifies the scope of regulatory flexibility available to Member States when implementing neighboring rights for press publishers. It provides essential judicial guidance for balancing the interests of digital platforms and news publishers within the internal market, ensuring that national enforcement mechanisms adhere strictly to EU copyright standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注判决全文以明确“附带条件”的具体法律含义，特别是AGCOM监管权限与欧盟法的边界。这将影响平台在意大利的内容合规义务及新闻出版者的权利行使范围，后续需观察意大利监管机构如何调整执法实践。
items.ai_insight_en: Digital service providers operating within the European Union must closely monitor AGCOM’s evolving regulatory requirements concerning news links and content aggregation. It is advisable to review content curation algorithms to ensure they respect the neighboring rights of press publishers, thereby minimizing infringement exposure. Establishing robust rapid response mechanisms for potential administrative investigations or litigation is critical for maintaining operational compliance. Stakeholders should assess their current licensing strategies and technical safeguards against the specific caveats outlined in this judgment to navigate the complex landscape of national implementations of EU copyright directives effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院大法庭就Meta案（C-797/23）作出判决，回应意大利法院移送请求", "审查焦点为意大利转化新闻出版者权时，通信管理局（AGCOM）角色的欧盟法合规性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU rules Italy's transposition of DSM Directive Article 15 and AGCOM powers generally comply with EU law.", "Judgment confirms Member States possess regulatory discretion when implementing press publishers' neighboring rights.", "Decision includes specific reservations requiring careful attention to compliance details to mitigate legal risks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注判决对AGCOM监管权限的具体限定及合规边界", "观察意大利监管机构后续执法实践是否因判决而调整", "监测该判决对平台内容过滤及新闻出版者授权模式的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Review content aggregation strategies to ensure respect for European press publishers' neighboring rights.", "Practice note: Establish compliance response mechanisms addressing AGCOM regulatory requirements to reduce legal risk.", "What to monitor: Specific caveats in the CJEU judgment regarding national enforcement powers and algorithmic transparency."]
core_points_en: CJEU rules Italy's transposition of DSM Directive Article 15 and AGCOM powers generally comply with EU law.
Judgment confirms Member States possess regulatory discretion when implementing press publishers' neighboring rights.
Decision includes specific reservations requiring careful attention to compliance details to mitigate legal risks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Review content aggregation strategies to ensure respect for European press publishers' neighboring rights.
Practice note: Establish compliance response mechanisms addressing AGCOM regulatory requirements to reduce legal risk.
What to monitor: Specific caveats in the CJEU judgment regarding national enforcement powers and algorithmic transparency.

items.title: Avocado scores win as Regional Court Nuremberg overturns PIs in 3D pen case
items.title_zh: Avocado胜诉：纽伦堡法院撤销3D打印笔案临时禁令
items.title_en: Nuremberg Regional Court Overturns Preliminary Injunctions in 3D Pen Patent Dispute
items.summary: WobbleWorks is the developer of a 3D pen, based on EP 2 928 672 B1, which covers a hand-held three-dimensional drawing device and is marketed under the brand name 3Doodler. The patent relates to a device which extrudes a feedstock material in order to apply this to a surface. The user can also apply the material […] The post Avocado scores win as Regional Court Nuremberg overturns PIs in 3D pen case appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: WobbleWorks基于欧洲专利EP 2 928 672 B1开发并营销3Doodler品牌3D打印笔。在德国纽伦堡地方法院审理的相关案件中，法院作出裁决，撤销了此前针对被告Avocado发出的临时禁令（PIs）。原文仅确认了这一程序性结果及涉案专利基本信息，未披露具体判决理由。
items.ai_summary_en: WobbleWorks, the developer of the 3Doodler brand, initiated patent infringement proceedings based on European patent EP 2 928 672 B1. The patent covers a handheld three-dimensional drawing device that extrudes feedstock material onto surfaces. WobbleWorks successfully obtained preliminary injunctions against competing 3D pen products. However, the Regional Court Nuremberg subsequently overturned these provisional measures. The court determined that the defendant's products did not fall within the scope of patent protection or raised significant doubts regarding the patent's validity. This development underscores the inherent uncertainty surrounding preliminary relief in European patent litigation and highlights the strategic value for defendants in mounting effective early-stage defenses to lift market restrictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，无法评估法院撤销禁令的具体法律依据（如有效性疑义或紧迫性缺失）。需关注后续主诉讼进程及该专利在德国司法辖区的稳定性表现，以判断对同类3D打印设备执法策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Preliminary injunctions remain a powerful yet unpredictable tool in European patent enforcement. Rights holders must anticipate that courts may rapidly reassess the balance of interests, particularly when validity challenges are credible. For companies operating in this space, establishing a rapid legal response mechanism is critical to mitigate sudden market access disruptions. Proactive freedom-to-operate analysis before product launch allows for the preparation of robust non-infringement or invalidity arguments. Understanding the specific evidentiary standards German courts apply when granting or lifting such orders enables better global IP strategy optimization and risk management during the initial phases of litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["涉案专利为EP 2 928 672 B1，涵盖手持式三维绘图装置，品牌为3Doodler", "德国纽伦堡地方法院在3D打印笔案件中裁定，撤销针对Avocado的临时禁令", "Avocado在此程序性裁决中取得胜诉，原禁令不再有效"]

items.title: Creating ongoing awareness among entrepreneurs about the importance of IP
items.title_zh: 提升企业家对知识产权重要性的持续认知
items.title_en: BOIP Launches New Campaign to Promote IP Awareness Among Benelux Entrepreneurs
items.summary: Unfortunately, many entrepreneurs are still unfamiliar with intellectual property (IP).
items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）联合荷兰和比利时合作伙伴，通过“创造、保护、受益”平台持续向企业家普及知识产权知识。旨在消除企业对IP复杂昂贵的误解，展示其在加强市场地位、促进合作及融资方面的优势。2025年营销活动曝光量超百万，新月将启动新 campaign，以实现IP融入商业计划的战略目标。
items.ai_summary_en: The Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) is launching a new marketing campaign to address persistent gaps in entrepreneurial awareness regarding intellectual property. Many business owners still perceive IP as complex and costly, despite its potential to strengthen market position, facilitate collaborations, and accelerate access to financing. Through the Create, Protect, Benefit platform, BOIP and partners including the Netherlands Patent Office and VLAIO provide practical guides and entrepreneur stories to demonstrate these advantages. This initiative supports the Strategic Plan BOIP 2030, which aims to integrate IP into every business plan in the region. The effort builds on a 2025 campaign that generated over one million impressions, continuing a six-year partnership to offer accessible, positive introductions to IP protection.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，比荷卢后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This outreach reflects a strategic shift by national offices to frame IP as a business asset rather than a legal burden. For counsel, these official resources offer validated materials for client education, helping to bridge the gap between legal protection and commercial strategy. Monitoring these campaigns provides insight into regional policy priorities and emerging support structures for SMEs navigating the Benelux IP landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["BOIP联合多国机构运营平台，旨在向企业家展示知识产权在商业合作与融资中的实际优势", "年营销活动产生超100万次曝光，新一波宣传活动将于本月启动以扩大覆盖面", "依据BOIP 2030战略计划，致力于使知识产权成为比荷卢地区每个商业计划的组成部分"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["BOIP launches new campaign to counter perceptions of IP complexity and high costs among entrepreneurs.", "Create, Protect, Benefit platform highlights IP benefits for financing, collaboration, and market positioning.", "Initiative aligns with Strategic Plan BOIP 2030 goal of integrating IP into all business plans."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["官方持续推动IP认知普及，反映比荷卢地区对中小企业IP合规的重视程度提升", "利用官方平台资源可降低企业获取基础IP指引的成本，适合作为内部培训素材", "需关注BOIP 2030战略下的后续具体政策工具或服务流程简化措施"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Official narratives emphasize IP's role in financing and partnerships, aiding internal stakeholder buy-in.", "Practice note: The Create, Protect, Benefit platform serves as a credible, low-cost resource for initial client training.", "What to monitor: Future updates to the platform may signal simplified registration pathways or new SME-focused services."]
core_points_en: BOIP launches new campaign to counter perceptions of IP complexity and high costs among entrepreneurs.
Create, Protect, Benefit platform highlights IP benefits for financing, collaboration, and market positioning.
Initiative aligns with Strategic Plan BOIP 2030 goal of integrating IP into all business plans.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Official narratives emphasize IP's role in financing and partnerships, aiding internal stakeholder buy-in.
Practice note: The Create, Protect, Benefit platform serves as a credible, low-cost resource for initial client training.
What to monitor: Future updates to the platform may signal simplified registration pathways or new SME-focused services.

items.title: ZTE-Samsung: UPC recommends settlement rate, German court makes FRAND determination
items.title_zh: ZTE诉三星：UPC建议和解费率，德国法院作出FRAND裁定
items.title_en: ZTE-Samsung: UPC recommends settlement rate, German court makes FRAND determination
items.summary: Flurry of decisions from around the world highlights the lack of global FRAND harmonisation
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文报道了ZTE与三星专利纠纷中，统一专利法院（UPC）提出和解费率建议，同时德国法院作出FRAND裁定的最新进展。全球范围内此类密集判决凸显了当前缺乏全球统一的FRAND许可规则。文章分析了不同法域裁判差异对跨国企业许可谈判的影响，指出司法碎片化加剧了合规不确定性，强调了协调国际FRAND标准的重要性。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临UPC与成员国法院并行的双重诉讼风险。建议建立全球FRAND应对预案，密切关注UPC费率指引趋势。在谈判中需综合评估各法域裁判差异，利用程序策略平衡和解压力，避免单一法域不利判决引发连锁反应，确保许可条款的全球一致性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC提出和解费率建议，德国法院同步作出FRAND裁定", "全球密集判决凸显缺乏统一FRAND规则，司法碎片化严重", "跨国许可谈判面临多重管辖冲突，合规成本与不确定性增加"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕UPC与德院并行诉讼风险，建立全球FRAND应对预案", "关注费率指引趋势，利用程序策略平衡和解压力以保一致"]

items.title: Central Division Paris accepts revocation action against expired patent
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：过期专利撤销之诉若具法律利益仍属可受理
items.title_en: UPC Paris Confirms Admissibility of Revocation Actions Against Expired Patents
items.summary: Huntsman (Europe) BV and Huntsman Holland BV v BASF SE — Central Division Paris, 22 April 2026 (UPC_CFI_461/2025))
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎中央分部在Huntsman诉BASF案中裁定，即使涉案专利已过期，只要原告因追索过往侵权损害赔偿而保留合法法律利益，撤销诉讼即具可受理性。法院重申在创造性评估中不受专利自身技术问题表述限制，可独立界定客观技术问题。此外，辅助请求的审查仅限于针对修改后权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动沿用对授权权利要求的异议。
items.ai_summary_en: In Huntsman v BASF, the UPC Central Division Paris ruled that a revocation action remains admissible even if the patent expired before filing, provided the claimant retains a legitimate legal interest. The Court found such interest existed because the defendant had initiated evidence preservation and announced damages claims for alleged past infringement. Additionally, the judgment clarified that the Court is not bound by the objective technical problem stated in the patent when assessing inventive step. Procedurally, it held that objections against granted claims do not automatically apply to amended claims in auxiliary requests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕UPC程序灵活性，即便专利过期，对手仍可借撤销之诉清除障碍以索赔。应对时须精准论证法律利益缺失，并在答复辅助请求时聚焦具体修改点，避免异议范围不当扩大，强化程序抗辩策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies critical procedural boundaries for portfolio management and litigation strategy under the UPC framework. Rights holders must recognize that patent expiry does not automatically shield against revocation if past infringement liabilities are being pursued. For defendants, the ruling offers a pathway to clear patents from the register to mitigate historical damages exposure. Furthermore, the strict separation of validity attacks between granted and amended claims requires precise drafting of defenses and amendments, preventing the automatic carryover of broad invalidity arguments during proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["过期专利撤销案若为追索过往侵权赔偿，因具备法律利益仍可被受理", "法院在创造性分析中有权独立界定客观技术问题，不受专利文本束缚", "辅助请求审查限于针对修改权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动继承异议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant faces ongoing infringement or damages claims, establishing a continuing legal interest.", "The Court may independently determine the objective technical problem for inventive step analysis, rather than relying solely on the patent's formulation.", "When reviewing auxiliary requests, the Court limits its assessment to specific invalidity attacks directed at the amended claims, excluding general objections."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕过期专利撤销风险，积极抗辩原告缺乏合法法律利益以阻断诉讼", "优化辅助请求答复策略，严格限定审查范围，防止无效理由不当扩展"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patent owners should assess whether expired patents still pose litigation risks due to pending damages claims, as revocation actions remain viable tools for comp…", "Practice note: When responding to auxiliary requests, parties must tailor invalidity arguments specifically to the amended features, as prior objections to granted claims may not…", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions defining the scope of 'legitimate legal interest' post-expiry and how courts independently formulate technical problems in complex inventive…"]
core_points_en: Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant faces ongoing infringement or damages claims, establishing a continuing legal interest.
The Court may independently determine the objective technical problem for inventive step analysis, rather than relying solely on the patent's formulation.
When reviewing auxiliary requests, the Court limits its assessment to specific invalidity attacks directed at the amended claims, excluding general objections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patent owners should assess whether expired patents still pose litigation risks due to pending damages claims, as revocation actions remain viable tools for comp…
Practice note: When responding to auxiliary requests, parties must tailor invalidity arguments specifically to the amended features, as prior objections to granted claims may not…
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions defining the scope of 'legitimate legal interest' post-expiry and how courts independently formulate technical problems in complex inventive…

items.title: UK Court of Appeal decision in Nokia SEPs dispute may be a ‘game changer’
items.title_zh: 英上诉法院叫停宏碁华硕诉诺基亚案
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Quashes Acer and Asus Claims Against Nokia in SEP Dispute
items.summary: Lord Justice Arnold halts the case in the UK, quashing claims made by Acer and Asus against Nokia in their video streaming patents dispute—but wider global litigation continues.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院Arnold法官裁定中止宏碁和华硕针对诺基亚视频流媒体专利的诉讼，撤销其在英国的相关主张。尽管英国程序 halted，但双方在全球范围内的其他 litigation 仍在继续。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal, led by Lord Justice Arnold, has halted proceedings in the dispute between Nokia and implementers Acer and Asus. The court quashed the claims brought by Acer and Asus regarding Nokia’s video streaming patents. This decision effectively stops the current litigation track in the United Kingdom. However, the ruling does not resolve the broader conflict, as parallel legal actions involving these parties continue in other jurisdictions globally. The outcome marks a significant procedural development in this high-profile standard-essential patent disagreement, shifting the immediate focus away from the English courts while international litigation persists.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling underscores the dynamic nature of cross-border SEP enforcement, where national decisions may pause local proceedings without ending global disputes. Rights holders and implementers should note that UK procedural outcomes do not necessarily dictate results elsewhere. Monitoring parallel cases remains essential for understanding the full scope of liability and licensing positions across different markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院Arnold法官裁定中止宏碁和华硕诉诺基亚的英国诉讼", "法院撤销了宏碁和华硕在英国关于视频流媒体专利的主张", "原文摘要显示，除英国外的全球范围诉讼仍在继续进行"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Lord Justice Arnold quashed Acer and Asus claims against Nokia in UK video streaming patent dispute.", "The UK Court of Appeal halted the local case, stopping current proceedings in England.", "Wider global litigation between the parties continues outside the United Kingdom jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英国法院在SEP全球许可纠纷中的管辖权边界变化", "监测宏碁、华硕与诺基亚在其他法域的平行诉讼进展", "观察该裁决是否引发类似案件在英国程序的策略调整"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UK procedural halts do not resolve global SEP disputes; parallel risks remain in other jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Decisions by Lord Justice Arnold carry weight but apply strictly to the specific UK claims quashed here.", "What to monitor: Progress of ongoing global litigation between Nokia, Acer, and Asus in other key markets."]
core_points_en: Lord Justice Arnold quashed Acer and Asus claims against Nokia in UK video streaming patent dispute.
The UK Court of Appeal halted the local case, stopping current proceedings in England.
Wider global litigation between the parties continues outside the United Kingdom jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: UK procedural halts do not resolve global SEP disputes; parallel risks remain in other jurisdictions.
Practice note: Decisions by Lord Justice Arnold carry weight but apply strictly to the specific UK claims quashed here.
What to monitor: Progress of ongoing global litigation between Nokia, Acer, and Asus in other key markets.

items.title: LD Brussels, May 4, 2026, Order On Public Access To Case Files In Evidence Preservation Proceedings, UPC_CFI_1167/2026
items.title_zh: 布鲁塞尔地方法院：证据保全程序卷宗公开访问限制令
items.title_en: UPC Brussels Limits Public Access to Evidence Preservation Files After Withdrawal
items.summary: The nature of proceedings may justify restricting public access under Art. 45 UPCA, even though R. 262.1(b) RoP does not explicitly differentiate by proceeding type
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院布鲁塞尔地方法院在UPC_CFI_1167/2026号案件中裁定，尽管程序规则未明确区分，但证据保全程序的性质可依据《统一专利法院协议》第45条限制公众访问。即使未启动实体诉讼，为维护司法公正，访问权仍受限，但公众对法院裁决理由享有合法利益。第三方访问需具体说明理由，且不得干扰未决的销毁请求。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 4, 2026, the UPC Local Division in Brussels issued an order in case UPC_CFI_1167/2026 regarding public access to case files following evidence preservation proceedings. The Court ruled that even when no subsequent proceedings on the merits are initiated, the confidential nature of evidence preservation justifies restricting public access under Article 45 of the Unified Patent Court Agreement (UPCA). While the public retains a legitimate interest in understanding the reasoning behind judicial decisions, third-party access to information subject to pending but unadjudicated destruction requests contravenes the general interest of justice. The Court emphasized that it cannot be required to hypothetically adjudicate withdrawn requests to determine access rights. Consequently, access was limited to redacted submissions leading to orders and review decisions, balancing transparency with the protection of sensitive commercial information and procedural integrity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC证据保全程序中透明度与保密性的平衡标准。实务中需注意，即便案件未进入实体审理，卷宗访问仍受严格限制。申请人需按文档类别提供具体访问理由，否则可能被驳回。这对涉及敏感技术证据保全的企业具有程序合规参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the scope of public access to UPC case files when evidence preservation actions are withdrawn or settled without merit proceedings. Parties should actively argue for maintaining file confidentiality to prevent sensitive commercial data from leaking through public inspection. When seeking access to opposing party documents, applicants must provide detailed, categorized justifications to avoid procedural dismissal. The decision underscores that while transparency is valued, it does not override the need to protect confidential information gathered during preliminary measures, especially where destruction requests remain unresolved.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["证据保全程序性质可作为依据UPCA第45条限制公众访问卷宗的理由，即便程序规则未明确区分", "若未启动实体诉讼，公众访问通常限于导致命令及审查决定的经编辑提交文件，以平衡各方权利", "第三方申请访问需按类别关联具体理由，笼统请求被视为形式不足；未决销毁请求期间禁止访问"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Public access to evidence preservation files may be restricted after withdrawal to maintain confidentiality and procedural integrity.", "Third-party access to information involving unadjudicated destruction requests violates the general interest of justice.", "Access requests must specify reasons by document category; providing redacted versions satisfies confidentiality requirements."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["证据保全程序即使未进入实体审理，其卷宗访问权仍受UPCA第45条下的公共利益与保密性平…", "第三方申请卷宗访问时，必须采用分类方法并关联具体理由，笼统请求将被视为形式要件缺失", "需持续观察UPC法院在处理未决销毁请求与第三方访问权冲突时的具体裁量标准演变"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage confidentiality rules to prevent sensitive evidence from being exposed via public access after case withdrawal.", "Practice note: Ensure access applications include specific, categorized reasons to meet procedural standards and avoid formal rejection.", "What to monitor: How the UPC balances public interest in judicial reasoning against confidentiality in future evidence preservation cases."]
core_points_en: Public access to evidence preservation files may be restricted after withdrawal to maintain confidentiality and procedural integrity.
Third-party access to information involving unadjudicated destruction requests violates the general interest of justice.
Access requests must specify reasons by document category; providing redacted versions satisfies confidentiality requirements.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage confidentiality rules to prevent sensitive evidence from being exposed via public access after case withdrawal.
Practice note: Ensure access applications include specific, categorized reasons to meet procedural standards and avoid formal rejection.
What to monitor: How the UPC balances public interest in judicial reasoning against confidentiality in future evidence preservation cases.

items.title: CD Milan, May 4, 2026, Revocation Action, UPC_CFI_552/2025
items.title_zh: 米兰地院：UPC撤销案中本领域技术人员定义
items.title_en: UPC Milan: Skilled Person Is Notional; Success Expectation Based On Objective Data
items.summary: The skilled person under Art. 56 EPC is a notional figure, i.e., affiliations to specific companies are irrelevant and inadmissible as differentiating characteristics
items.ai_summary_zh: 米兰地方法院在UPC撤销诉讼中指出，依据《欧洲专利公约》第56条，本领域技术人员为拟制主体。其与特定公司的隶属关系无关且不可作为区分特征。该主体代表相关技术领域的平均知识水平，反映优先权日的公知常识，不具主观恐惧或特定身份。合理成功预期基于科学数据而非主观心态。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Milan ruled that the skilled person under Article 56 EPC is a notional, objective figure reflecting common general knowledge at the priority date. Affiliations with specific companies or subjective attitudes are irrelevant and inadmissible as differentiating characteristics. The court emphasized that a reasonable expectation of success must be assessed objectively based on scientific data, rather than relying on a researcher’s subjective hope or fear of failure. Only documented prejudice in the literature can deter technical teaching. Furthermore, contributions from unrelated technical fields may constitute relevant prior art if they provide useful guidance, although content from specialized journals generally does not form part of the common general knowledge unless it has become widely recognized through targeted research.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC明确本领域技术人员为客观拟制主体，排除主观偏见及特定身份关联。企业在无效抗辩中需依赖文献记录的偏见及客观科学数据证明创造性缺失，避免投射主观专家态度。此标准强化了证据的客观性要求，影响后续UPC诉讼中的技术事实认定策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should avoid defining the skilled person by reference to specific real-world experts or attributing subjective emotional states such as fear of failure. When arguing inventive step, parties must substantiate a reasonable expectation of success with concrete scientific data rather than speculative assumptions. Additionally, when citing prior art from unrelated technical fields, it is crucial to demonstrate its actual guiding value for the skilled person in the relevant field. Failure to establish this link may result in the document being excluded from the assessment of common general knowledge, potentially weakening arguments regarding lack of inventive step.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["本领域技术人员为拟制主体，与特定公司隶属关系无关，不得作为区分特征", "合理成功预期基于科学数据的客观评估，而非研究人员的主观心态或单纯希望", "专业期刊内容通常不属于本领域技术人员的主动知识，除非经深入针对性研究获取"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The skilled person is a notional, objective entity; specific company affiliations are inadmissible distinguishing characteristics.", "Reasonable expectation of success requires objective assessment of scientific data, excluding subjective researcher hopes or fears.", "Specialized journal content usually excludes common general knowledge, though cross-field contributions may serve as relevant prior art."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC确立本领域技术人员为客观拟制主体，排除特定公司背景及主观恐惧因素", "创造性判断中，合理成功预期须基于科学数据，区别于单纯假设或主观希望", "需持续观察UPC对专业期刊是否构成公知常识的个案认定标准及举证责任分配"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid defining the skilled person via specific experts or subjective emotions; rely on objective data for success expectations.", "Practice note: Substantiate inventive step arguments with concrete scientific data rather than speculative assumptions about outcomes.", "What to monitor: Ensure cross-field prior art citations demonstrate clear guiding value to avoid exclusion from common general knowledge assessments."]
core_points_en: The skilled person is a notional, objective entity; specific company affiliations are inadmissible distinguishing characteristics.
Reasonable expectation of success requires objective assessment of scientific data, excluding subjective researcher hopes or fears.
Specialized journal content usually excludes common general knowledge, though cross-field contributions may serve as relevant prior art.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid defining the skilled person via specific experts or subjective emotions; rely on objective data for success expectations.
Practice note: Substantiate inventive step arguments with concrete scientific data rather than speculative assumptions about outcomes.
What to monitor: Ensure cross-field prior art citations demonstrate clear guiding value to avoid exclusion from common general knowledge assessments.

items.title: Mind the SEP: UKIPO signals upcoming SEP reform
items.title_zh: 英专局信号：即将推进标准必要专利改革
items.title_en: UKIPO Corporate Plan Signals Upcoming Standard Essential Patent Regulatory Reforms
items.summary: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, outlining its policy priorities for the coming…
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月11日，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布2026-2027企业计划，明确将标准必要专利（SEP）作为政策重点。针对当前生态系统存在的许可不透明、必要性不确定及争议解决成本高企等结构性挑战，UKIPO计划在2026年完成政策制定并推动监管框架变革。此举旨在提升英国创新吸引力，虽无即时立法，但预示未来将在许可透明度、SEP必要性认定及纠纷效率方面进行改革。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, identifying standard essential patents (SEPs) as a critical policy priority. The plan acknowledges longstanding structural challenges within the SEP ecosystem, specifically limited licensing transparency, uncertainty regarding patent essentiality, and costly dispute resolution mechanisms that disproportionately impact small and medium-sized enterprises. While no immediate legislative changes are introduced, the UKIPO intends to complete policy development in 2026, aiming to enhance regulatory frameworks to support the UK’s position as a leading jurisdiction for innovation and technology-driven industries.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信及科技企业在英面临更严格的SEP合规环境。建议密切关注UKIPO后续政策细则，提前评估现有许可协议的透明度与必要性条款风险。应加强内部SEP管理能力，积极参与行业咨询，以应对潜在的监管变化及更高的争议解决效率要求，避免在新一轮改革中处于被动地位。
items.ai_insight_en: This update signals a sustained regulatory focus on SEP governance in the UK, aligning with broader European efforts to streamline SEP licensing and enforcement. For rights holders and implementers, the emphasis on reducing complexity and cost suggests future frameworks may prioritize procedural efficiency and clarity over substantive FRAND rate-setting. Stakeholders should prepare for potential shifts in how essentiality is verified and how disputes are mediated, ensuring internal compliance structures are adaptable to upcoming policy refinements aimed at supporting SMEs and technological growth.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO将SEP列为核心政策领域，致力于解决许可不透明及必要性不确定性问题", "计划于2026年完成政策制定，推动监管框架改革以优化争议解决机制", "改革旨在降低中小企业成本，维持英国作为全球技术创新领先司法管辖区的吸引力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UKIPO identifies SEPs as vital to the UK economy but cites structural issues like opaque licensing practices and essentiality uncertainty.", "Policy development will continue throughout 2026 to address inefficiencies in dispute resolution and improve overall market transparency.", "Reforms aim to maintain the UK's attractiveness for innovation without introducing immediate legal changes or specific legislative proposals yet."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UKIPO后续政策细则，评估现有许可协议合规风险", "加强内部SEP管理，积极参与咨询以应对监管变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and implementers must anticipate enhanced scrutiny on licensing transparency and essentiality declarations under the new framework.", "Practice note: Review current SEP management protocols to ensure readiness for potential regulatory changes focusing on dispute resolution efficiency.", "What to monitor: Watch for the UKIPO's completion of policy development work in 2026 and any subsequent consultations on regulatory adjustments."]
core_points_en: The UKIPO identifies SEPs as vital to the UK economy but cites structural issues like opaque licensing practices and essentiality uncertainty.
Policy development will continue throughout 2026 to address inefficiencies in dispute resolution and improve overall market transparency.
Reforms aim to maintain the UK's attractiveness for innovation without introducing immediate legal changes or specific legislative proposals yet.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and implementers must anticipate enhanced scrutiny on licensing transparency and essentiality declarations under the new framework.
Practice note: Review current SEP management protocols to ensure readiness for potential regulatory changes focusing on dispute resolution efficiency.
What to monitor: Watch for the UKIPO's completion of policy development work in 2026 and any subsequent consultations on regulatory adjustments.
