items.title: L’ajout d’un élément figuratif à un terme descriptif suffit-il à rendre un signe distinctif ? Illustration avec l’affaire Washtower
items.title_zh: 描述性词汇加图形元素是否具显著性：Washtower案解析
items.title_en: L’ajout d’un élément figuratif à un terme descriptif suffit-il à rendre un signe distinctif ? Illustration avec l’affaire Washtower
items.summary: Le Tribunal de l'Union européenne examine si l'ajout d'un élément figuratif complexe à un terme descriptif peut conférer un caractère distinctif à une marque.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院于2025年11月12日就LG电子“WASHTOWER”商标案作出判决。此前上诉委员会认为复杂盾徽图形赋予描述性词汇显著性，但法院推翻该决定，指出在描述性术语旁添加图形元素并不自动使整体标志具备显著性，尤其是当图形元素未改变词汇的描述性主导地位时。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了欧盟商标审查中“描述性词汇+图形”组合的显著性认定标准。企业应避免依赖单纯添加图形来注册描述性术语，需证明整体标志已产生第二含义或图形具有极强独立识别力，否则面临无效风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院推翻上诉委员会决定，认定WASHTOWER商标缺乏固有显著性", "法院指出仅将复杂图形元素附加于描述性词汇上，不足以使整体标志获得显著性", "涉案词汇直接描述家电产品功能与形态，相关公众仍将其视为描述性信息而非来源标识"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["描述性词汇主导整体印象时，附加图形难以挽救商标显著性", "欧盟法院对第7(1)(b)条的严格适用，提高了描述性标志注册门槛", "后续需关注此类判决对已注册含描述性成分复合商标稳定性的影响"]

items.title: PMAC's Case Management System now live
items.title_zh: PMAC案件管理系统正式上线
items.title_en: UPC's PMAC Launches Case Management System for Mediation and Arbitration
items.summary: As of today, 12 May 2026, the Case Management System of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) is live
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月12日，统一专利法院（UPC）宣布其专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的案件管理系统（CCMS）正式启用。该系统支持用户在线提交替代性争议解决（ADR）信息及调解程序请求。PMAC网站已设立专门页面提供系统使用指南。此举标志着UPC在优化专利纠纷非诉讼解决机制方面迈出重要一步，为当事人提供了更便捷的调解与仲裁服务渠道，有助于提高争议解决效率并降低诉讼成本。
items.ai_summary_en: As of 12 May 2026, the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has officially launched its Case Management System (CCMS). This digital platform enables parties to submit requests for ADR information sessions and mediation proceedings directly through the Centre. The PMAC website now features a dedicated section providing practical guidance on navigating the new system. This development marks the operational availability of structured alternative dispute resolution services under the Unified Patent Court framework, offering a streamlined digital interface for initiating non-litigious patent dispute resolutions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，应关注并利用PMAC的调解与仲裁机制。相比UPC诉讼，ADR程序通常更具成本效益且保密性强。建议企业熟悉CCMS操作流程，在争议初期评估通过调解快速和解的可能性，以规避高昂的跨国诉讼风险并维护商业关系。
items.ai_insight_en: The launch of the PMAC Case Management System provides rights holders with a formalized digital channel for accessing alternative dispute resolution mechanisms within the UPC ecosystem. For in-house counsel and IP teams managing European portfolios, this infrastructure supports efficient initiation of mediation or arbitration without immediate recourse to full litigation. Monitoring the uptake and procedural rules of this system will help stakeholders evaluate its utility for resolving cross-border patent disputes efficiently.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PMAC案件管理系统于2026年5月12日正式上线运行", "支持在线提交ADR信息会话及调解程序相关请求", "官网发布专用页面提供系统操作实务指导"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The PMAC Case Management System is live as of 12 May 2026, enabling online submission of ADR requests.", "Parties can now initiate ADR information sessions and formal mediation proceedings via the new digital portal.", "Practical user guidance for the Case Management System is available on the dedicated PMAC website page."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC调解机制可降低在欧专利维权成本", "熟悉CCMS系统以便高效启动替代性争议解决程序"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Provides a structured digital pathway for initiating mediation and arbitration under the UPC framework.", "Practice note: Review the dedicated PMAC guidance page to understand submission requirements for ADR requests.", "What to monitor: Track future updates on PMAC procedural rules and case statistics to assess ADR effectiveness."]
core_points_en: The PMAC Case Management System is live as of 12 May 2026, enabling online submission of ADR requests.
Parties can now initiate ADR information sessions and formal mediation proceedings via the new digital portal.
Practical user guidance for the Case Management System is available on the dedicated PMAC website page.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Provides a structured digital pathway for initiating mediation and arbitration under the UPC framework.
Practice note: Review the dedicated PMAC guidance page to understand submission requirements for ADR requests.
What to monitor: Track future updates on PMAC procedural rules and case statistics to assess ADR effectiveness.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-797/23 Meta Platforms Ireland (Fair compensation)
items.title_zh: CJEU：成员国可规定新闻出版者获在线使用公平报酬
items.title_en: CJEU: Member States May Provide Fair Remuneration for Press Publishers
items.summary: Member States may make provision for publishers of press publications to be entitled to fair remuneration when they grant online service providers authorisation to use those publications
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法院在Meta诉意大利AGCOM案中裁定，成员国可立法赋予新闻出版者在授权在线服务提供者使用时获得公平报酬的权利。该权利符合欧盟法律，前提是报酬构成授权对价，且出版者可拒绝授权或免费授权。法院认为，要求平台谈判并提供数据虽限制经营自由，但有助于实现版权市场公平及保障出版业投资回收，平衡了各方权益。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-797/23, the Court of Justice ruled that EU law permits Member States to establish systems granting press publishers fair remuneration when authorizing online service providers to use their publications. The judgment confirms this is consistent with the Digital Single Market framework, provided the payment constitutes consideration for authorization and publishers retain the right to refuse or grant free licenses. The Court further held that obligations requiring platforms to negotiate and provide data are justified restrictions on business freedom, necessary to balance intellectual property rights with media pluralism and ensure publishers can recoup investments in a transformed digital media landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海媒体及聚合平台需关注欧盟各成员国的国内法转化，特别是意大利等先行国家。应建立合规机制，评估是否触发“公平报酬”义务，避免侵权风险。同时，利用出版者可免费授权的条款，积极争取低成本内容合作，优化内容供应链成本结构。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment validates national legislative models aiming to secure revenue streams for press publishers from digital platforms. For online service providers, particularly those operating across multiple EU jurisdictions, the decision underscores the need to assess compliance with local 'fair compensation' regimes. It clarifies that while negotiation duties restrict operational freedom, they are legally defensible if structured to support a fair marketplace. Rights holders should note the explicit preservation of their ability to grant free licenses, which remains a key contractual lever in platform negotiations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["成员国可立法规定出版者就在线使用获公平报酬，符合欧盟法框架", "报酬须为授权对价，出版者有权拒绝授权或提供免费授权", "强制谈判义务旨在平衡经营自由与版权保护，支持出版业生存"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Member States may legislate for fair remuneration for press publishers authorizing online use by service providers.", "Remuneration must be consideration for authorization; publishers must retain rights to refuse or license freely.", "Negotiation and data provision duties are justified to balance business freedom with copyright protection objectives."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注成员国国内法转化，评估在线内容使用的报酬合规义务", "利用免费授权条款，争取低成本内容合作以优化供应链成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Online service providers must evaluate exposure to national fair remuneration schemes and prepare for mandatory negotiation frameworks.", "Practice note: Publishers should leverage the confirmed right to grant free licenses strategically during platform negotiations to maintain visibility.", "What to monitor: Implementation details of such remuneration systems in other Member States following the Italian model validated by the CJEU."]
core_points_en: Member States may legislate for fair remuneration for press publishers authorizing online use by service providers.
Remuneration must be consideration for authorization; publishers must retain rights to refuse or license freely.
Negotiation and data provision duties are justified to balance business freedom with copyright protection objectives.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Online service providers must evaluate exposure to national fair remuneration schemes and prepare for mandatory negotiation frameworks.
Practice note: Publishers should leverage the confirmed right to grant free licenses strategically during platform negotiations to maintain visibility.
What to monitor: Implementation details of such remuneration systems in other Member States following the Italian model validated by the CJEU.

items.title: The CJEU on heritage washing in trade mark law
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院论商标法中的“遗产清洗”
items.title_en: CJEU Rules on Deceptive Nature of Trademarks Containing Historical Years
items.summary: Is a trade mark that contains a year deceptive under Art. 4(1)(g) Trade Mark Directive (“TMD3”) and its predecessor Art. 3(1)(g) Second Trade Mark Directive (“TMD2”) if the owner was not established in that year? In Fauré Le Page (case C-412/24), the CJEU held that this may be the case where the year is perceived as indicating a long-established business and thereby evokes long-standing know-how
items.ai_summary_zh: 在Fauré Le Page案（C-412/24）中，欧盟法院裁定：若商标包含的年份被公众视为指示悠久商业历史并唤起长期专业知识联想，而权利人实际并非成立于该年，则该商标可能依据《商标指令》第4(1)(g)条构成欺骗性标志。
items.ai_summary_en: In Fauré Le Page (Case C-412/24), the Court of Justice of the European Union addressed whether a trademark containing a year is deceptive under Article 4(1)(g) of the Trade Mark Directive if the owner was not established in that year. The Court held that such marks may be considered deceptive where the relevant public perceives the year as indicating a long-established business, thereby evoking an impression of long-standing know-how. This judgment clarifies the assessment of misleading indications regarding commercial history under EU trademark law, focusing on consumer perception of heritage claims embedded within the mark itself.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights the risks associated with using historical dates in trademarks without factual basis. Rights holders should ensure that any year included in a mark accurately reflects the company’s establishment or heritage to avoid invalidation for deception. The decision reinforces the importance of aligning brand storytelling with verifiable corporate history to maintain registration validity across the EU.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院审理Fauré Le Page案，涉及商标中包含年份是否构成欺骗性问题", "若年份暗示悠久历史且权利人非成立于该年，可能违反商标指令禁止欺骗性规定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU examined if trademarks with years are deceptive when the owner was not established in that specific year.", "A mark may be deceptive if the year implies a long-established business and evokes long-standing know-how.", "The ruling applies Article 4(1)(g) of the Trade Mark Directive regarding marks liable to deceive the public."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注年份标识在商标审查中被认定为误导性的法律边界", "监测成员国法院如何依据此裁决处理涉及品牌历史宣传的商标争议", "观察该判例对现有含年份商标注册稳定性的潜在影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies using founding years in trademarks must verify accuracy to prevent challenges based on deceptive practices under EU law.", "Practice note: Examiners and courts will assess whether the year creates a false impression of longevity or expertise, requiring evidence of actual establishment dates.", "What to monitor: Observe how national offices apply this standard to existing registrations and oppositions involving heritage-themed branding elements."]
core_points_en: The CJEU examined if trademarks with years are deceptive when the owner was not established in that specific year.
A mark may be deceptive if the year implies a long-established business and evokes long-standing know-how.
The ruling applies Article 4(1)(g) of the Trade Mark Directive regarding marks liable to deceive the public.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies using founding years in trademarks must verify accuracy to prevent challenges based on deceptive practices under EU law.
Practice note: Examiners and courts will assess whether the year creates a false impression of longevity or expertise, requiring evidence of actual establishment dates.
What to monitor: Observe how national offices apply this standard to existing registrations and oppositions involving heritage-themed branding elements.

items.title: La porcelaine de Limoges
items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器获欧盟首项手工艺地理标志保护
items.title_en: Limoges Porcelain Becomes First French Artisanal GI Registered Under New EU Regulation
items.summary: La porcelaine de Limoges sgurtler@inpi.fr 11 mai 2026 Avec l'entrée en vigueur du règlement européen sur les indications géographiques pour les produits artisanaux et industriels le 1er décembre 2025, la porcelaine de Limoges devient ce 15 mai la première indication géographique protégée (IGP) française artisanale et industrielle à être enregistrée au niveau européen auprès de l'Office de l'Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO). Cette reconnaissance marque une étape décisive
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月15日，利摩日瓷器成为欧盟《手工业和工业产品地理标志条例》生效后，首个在欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）注册的法国手工艺及工业地理标志（IGP）。该认证基于其自2017年起获得的法国国家地理标志地位，通过简化程序转化而来。此举赋予其在欧盟27国的广泛保护及官方标识使用权，标志着法国传统工艺知识产权保护的重要里程碑，并为其他法国手工艺品的欧盟注册开辟路径。
items.ai_summary_en: On 15 May 2026, Limoges porcelain became the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication (GI) registered at the EU level with the EUIPO. This registration follows the entry into force of the new EU regulation on GIs for craft and industrial products on 1 December 2025. Leveraging its national GI status granted by INPI in 2017, Limoges porcelain utilized a simplified transformation procedure without an opposition phase. The decision grants protection across all 27 EU member states and authorizes the use of the official 'Protected Geographical Indication' logo, marking a significant step for the protection of traditional French craftsmanship.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This registration demonstrates the operationalization of the new EU framework for craft and industrial geographical indications. For rights holders, it highlights the strategic value of securing national GI status as a foundation for streamlined EU-wide protection. The case illustrates how established regional brands can leverage existing national recognitions to achieve broader territorial coverage and enhanced enforcement capabilities within the single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利摩日瓷器成欧盟新规下首个注册的手工艺地理标志", "凭借法国国家GI基础，经简化程序直接转化为欧盟IGP", "获欧盟全境保护权及官方标识使用权，强化品牌壁垒"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal GI registered under the new EU regulation effective 1 December 2025.", "Registration used a simplified transformation from national INPI status, bypassing EU-level opposition procedures.", "The GI covers all production steps in Haute-Vienne, granting EU-wide protection and official logo usage rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟GI新规对陶瓷等手工艺品出口的合规冲击", "推动中国特色产品申请欧盟GI以提升国际品牌溢价"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should assess if their national GIs qualify for simplified transformation to gain immediate EU-wide protection.", "Practice note: National GI recognition serves as a critical prerequisite for accessing the streamlined EU registration pathway.", "What to monitor: Future registrations of other French and European artisanal products under this new regulatory framework."]
core_points_en: Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal GI registered under the new EU regulation effective 1 December 2025.
Registration used a simplified transformation from national INPI status, bypassing EU-level opposition procedures.
The GI covers all production steps in Haute-Vienne, granting EU-wide protection and official logo usage rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should assess if their national GIs qualify for simplified transformation to gain immediate EU-wide protection.
Practice note: National GI recognition serves as a critical prerequisite for accessing the streamlined EU registration pathway.
What to monitor: Future registrations of other French and European artisanal products under this new regulatory framework.

items.title: Contentious COVID-19 patents: UPC revokes remdesivir patent
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院撤销瑞德西韦单元专利
items.title_en: UPC Milan Revokes Remdesivir COVID-19 Patent for Lack of Inventive Step
items.summary: On 4th May, the UPC revoked the ‘remdesivir’ anti-viral drug unitary patent owned by Chinese research institute AMMS.
items.ai_summary_zh: 5月4日，统一专利法院（UPC）米兰中央分院撤销了中国军事科学院持有的瑞德西韦治疗新冠用途的单元专利EP3854403。法院驳回专利权人提出的所有限制请求，认定该第二医药用途缺乏创造性。尽管病毒基因组公开且专家曾推测瑞德西韦有效，但法院认为这对本领域技术人员而言是显而易见选择。吉利德科学公司提起无效诉讼并胜诉。
items.ai_summary_en: On 4 May 2025, the Unified Patent Court’s Central Division in Milan revoked unitary patent EP 3854403, owned by research institute AMMS, covering the use of remdesivir for treating COVID-19. The Court rejected all limitation proposals submitted by the patentee. Gilead Sciences, the original developer of remdesivir, initiated revocation proceedings immediately upon grant, arguing that the claimed second medical use lacked inventive step and sufficiency of disclosure. While the Court dismissed the objection regarding insufficiency, it found the invention obvious to a skilled person at the priority date. This conclusion relied on prior knowledge of SARS-CoV-2 genomic similarities to other coronaviruses and existing preclinical data suggesting efficacy against related viral families. The decision underscores the UPC’s rigorous approach to assessing inventive step for second medical use claims, particularly where speculative applications of known compounds are involved.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示UPC对第二医药用途专利的创造性审查持严格态度。若申请日前存在公开的技术线索或专家推测，即便属于新适应症，也易被认定为显而易见。专利权人需警惕申请前的公开信息对创造性认定的负面影响，特别是在突发公共卫生事件背景下。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking protection for second medical uses must ensure robust clinical data supports their applications, as speculative claims based solely on structural or mechanistic analogies face significant validity risks under UPC jurisprudence. The decision illustrates that mere identification of a new viral target is insufficient if prior art provides a reasonable expectation of success. Companies should conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses before filing, avoiding reliance on broad class predictions. Strengthening the technical contribution through specific experimental evidence is critical to overcoming obviousness challenges in contentious proceedings involving established therapeutic compounds.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC米兰中央分院撤销中国军科院持有的瑞德西韦治疗新冠单元专利EP3854403", "法院认定该第二医药用途缺乏创造性，因病毒基因组公开且专家已推测药物有效性", "吉利德科学在专利授权当日提起无效诉讼，主张缺乏创造性和充分性，最终胜诉"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Milan Central Division revoked AMMS’s remdesivir patent due to lack of inventive step, rejecting all proposed limitations.", "The Court deemed the treatment obvious based on known viral genome similarities and prior preclinical research expectations.", "This ruling highlights the UPC’s heightened threshold for inventive step in second medical use pharmaceutical patents."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC严格审查第二医药用途创造性，申请前公开线索可能导致专利无效", "突发疫情中，基于已知药物机理的适应症扩展易被认定为缺乏 inventive step", "需持续监测UPC对类似公共卫生领域专利的创造性判断标准及后续上诉动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Second medical use patents require strong empirical data; speculative applications of known drugs are vulnerable to invalidity.", "Practice note: Assess obviousness risks early by evaluating prior art expectations of success for new indications of known compounds.", "What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding the evidentiary standards required to prove inventive step for new therapeutic uses."]
core_points_en: The UPC Milan Central Division revoked AMMS’s remdesivir patent due to lack of inventive step, rejecting all proposed limitations.
The Court deemed the treatment obvious based on known viral genome similarities and prior preclinical research expectations.
This ruling highlights the UPC’s heightened threshold for inventive step in second medical use pharmaceutical patents.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Second medical use patents require strong empirical data; speculative applications of known drugs are vulnerable to invalidity.
Practice note: Assess obviousness risks early by evaluating prior art expectations of success for new indications of known compounds.
What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding the evidentiary standards required to prove inventive step for new therapeutic uses.

items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局2026至2027年度企业计划
items.title_en: UKIPO Corporate Plan 2026 to 2027: Strategic Focus Overview
items.summary: This plan outlines our main activities and focus in the third of our strategy, Intellectual Property (IP) for a creative and innovative UK 2024 to 2027.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要及正文仅显示，该计划概述了英国知识产权局在“2024至2027年创意与创新英国知识产权”战略第三阶段的主要活动与工作重点。未提供具体政策细节、预算分配或执行措施，仅为战略阶段的总体陈述。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published its Corporate Plan for 2026 to 2027. This document outlines the main activities and strategic focus for the third year of the broader "Intellectual Property (IP) for a creative and innovative UK 2024 to 2027" strategy. The plan serves as a high-level statement of intent, defining the operational priorities for the upcoming period. It does not provide detailed procedural changes or specific regulatory amendments in this summary view. Rights holders and practitioners should note that this publication confirms the continuation of the established three-year strategic framework, focusing on supporting creativity and innovation within the UK jurisdiction through the Office's core functions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于原文仅提供极简的战略阶段描述，缺乏具体政策变动或操作指引，目前仅能确认UKIPO正按其既定三年战略推进工作。需等待完整报告发布以获取具体的监管重点或资源分配信息。
items.ai_insight_en: This publication confirms the UKIPO's ongoing strategic direction without detailing immediate procedural shifts. For international rights holders, it signals stability in the Office's overarching goals for the final year of its current strategy cycle. Specific operational impacts remain unclear from this high-level summary. Practitioners should monitor subsequent detailed reports for concrete changes in examination practices, enforcement priorities, or stakeholder engagement initiatives that may affect portfolio management and registration workflows in the UK market.

items.title: UPC: Late filed physical evidence cannot clarify the content of an earlier filed brochure
items.title_zh: UPC裁定：迟交实物证据不得用于澄清先前提交的宣传册内容
items.title_en: UPC Vienna Division Excludes Late Physical Evidence Clarifying Prior Art Brochure
items.summary: In a recent decision from the Vienna Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC), the Court maintained EP 3705415, taking a strict...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）维也纳地方法院在Messerle诉Sabert案中维持涉案专利有效。被告Sabert试图通过迟交实物样品来澄清商业宣传册中产品特征以攻击专利新颖性。法院裁定宣传册为独立现有技术，实物证据不能用于补充或重构其披露内容，以防事后诸葛亮式解读。因该证据实质引入新的无效理由且提交过晚，法院拒绝采纳，强调证据必须基于现有论点发展而非引入新攻击。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Vienna Local Division maintained European patent EP 3705415 in infringement proceedings between Messerle and Sabert. The defendant attempted to rely on a commercial brochure for a novelty attack but submitted physical product samples late to clarify ambiguous features depicted in the brochure's two-dimensional images. The Court rejected this evidence, ruling that the brochure constitutes stand-alone prior art. External physical evidence cannot supplement or reconstruct the document's disclosure, as this risks hindsight bias. Furthermore, the Court found the late submission effectively introduced a new line of attack based on alleged prior use rather than developing existing arguments, rendering it inadmissible under procedural rules.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC诉讼中需严格遵循举证时限与策略一致性。针对新颖性挑战，应确保书面证据本身清晰完整，避免依赖后续实物补强。若主张公开使用，须早期明确提出并单独举证，防止因混淆证据性质导致关键证据被排除，从而丧失无效机会。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC's strict adherence to procedural discipline regarding late-filed evidence and the definition of prior art disclosures. Rights holders and defendants must ensure that documentary evidence relied upon for novelty attacks is self-contained and clear at the time of filing. Attempting to cure ambiguities in written prior art with subsequent physical exhibits risks exclusion, particularly if the court perceives this as introducing a new legal ground like public prior use without proper early pleading.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院拒绝采纳迟交的实物证据，因其旨在澄清而非补充先前提交的宣传册披露内容", "宣传册作为独立现有技术，其披露范围由本领域技术人员直接明确认定，禁止事后重构", "迟交证据若实质引入全新无效攻击路径（如公开使用），将因程序不当被排除"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Physical samples submitted late cannot clarify or supplement the disclosure content of an earlier filed commercial brochure.", "Courts treat documentary prior art as stand-alone evidence, prohibiting external items from reconstructing disclosure via hindsight.", "Late-filed evidence introducing a fundamentally new attack, such as alleged prior use, is inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC对迟交证据持严格态度，企业需确保书面证据自身具备充分清晰度", "区分不同无效理由的证据提交策略，避免混合导致程序性驳回"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure prior art documents are sufficiently clear and complete when initially filed to avoid reliance on supplementary physical evidence.", "Practice note: Distinguish clearly between attacks based on documentary disclosure and those based on prior use to prevent procedural rejection of late evidence.", "What to monitor: Observe how other UPC divisions apply these admissibility standards to late-submitted evidence intended to interpret ambiguous prior art."]
core_points_en: Physical samples submitted late cannot clarify or supplement the disclosure content of an earlier filed commercial brochure.
Courts treat documentary prior art as stand-alone evidence, prohibiting external items from reconstructing disclosure via hindsight.
Late-filed evidence introducing a fundamentally new attack, such as alleged prior use, is inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure prior art documents are sufficiently clear and complete when initially filed to avoid reliance on supplementary physical evidence.
Practice note: Distinguish clearly between attacks based on documentary disclosure and those based on prior use to prevent procedural rejection of late evidence.
What to monitor: Observe how other UPC divisions apply these admissibility standards to late-submitted evidence intended to interpret ambiguous prior art.

items.title: EPO President delivers keynote at INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.title_zh: EPO主席在伦敦INTA年会发表主旨演讲
items.title_en: EPO President Advocates Holistic IP Strategy at INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.summary: EPO President Campinos delivered a keynote at the 2026 INTA Annual Meeting in London on 5 May, calling for a more holistic approach to intellectual property in which patents and trademarks work together.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）主席坎皮诺斯在2026年伦敦INTA年会上发表主旨演讲，倡导专利与商标协同的整体知识产权策略。他强调高质量专利对投资确定性的重要性，指出欧洲专利申请约五个月即可获得检索报告。此外，他重点推介统一专利制度，称其为覆盖18个欧盟成员国的简便途径，中小企业申请占比约40%，并提及EPO与INTA在人工智能及标准必要专利等领域的合作前景。
items.ai_summary_en: EPO President António Campinos delivered a keynote at the 2026 INTA Annual Meeting in London, urging a holistic approach where patents and trademarks function synergistically within business strategy. He highlighted that startups combining both IP rights are significantly more likely to secure funding and achieve successful exits. Campinos emphasized the role of high-quality patents in providing legal certainty, noting that European applicants receive search reports and preliminary opinions within approximately five months. He also promoted the Unitary Patent as an accessible route for protection across 18 EU Member States, with SMEs accounting for around 40% of requests for unitary effect, underscoring its value for market entry and investment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The EPO’s emphasis on integrating patent and trademark strategies reflects a broader shift toward viewing IP as a unified business asset rather than siloed legal rights. For in-house counsel, this reinforces the need for cross-functional IP management to maximize valuation and investor appeal. The high SME adoption of the Unitary Patent suggests it is becoming a standard tool for cost-effective European coverage. Monitoring the evolving dialogue between the EPO and INTA on AI and standard-essential patents may reveal future convergence in examination and enforcement practices.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO主席倡导专利与商标协同的整体IP策略，强调IP嵌入商业战略的必要性", "统一专利提供覆盖18个欧盟成员国的单一保护途径，中小企业申请占比约40%", "欧洲专利申请约五个月获初步意见，早期评估有助于向合作伙伴展示实力并获取融资"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO President advocates aligning patent protection and brand positioning for holistic IP business strategies.", "Startups combining patents and trademarks show significantly higher rates of funding success and market exits.", "Unitary Patent sees strong SME uptake, offering simplified protection across eighteen EU Member States."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO政策导向强调专利与商标协同，建议企业审视IP组合策略以契合融资与市场退出需求", "统一专利在中小企业中渗透率较高，显示其在简化多国保护流程方面的实际吸引力", "需持续观察EPO与INTA在AI及标准必要专利领域的合作进展，及其对审查实践的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Integrating patent and trademark portfolios can enhance startup valuation and attract investment, as evidenced by EPO data on funding success rates.", "Practice note: Leverage the five-month preliminary opinion timeline to signal patent strength to partners and utilize the Unitary Patent for efficient multi-jurisdictional coverag…", "What to monitor: Watch for emerging EPO-INTA collaborations on AI and standard-essential patents that may influence future examination guidelines and enforcement norms."]
core_points_en: EPO President advocates aligning patent protection and brand positioning for holistic IP business strategies.
Startups combining patents and trademarks show significantly higher rates of funding success and market exits.
Unitary Patent sees strong SME uptake, offering simplified protection across eighteen EU Member States.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Integrating patent and trademark portfolios can enhance startup valuation and attract investment, as evidenced by EPO data on funding success rates.
Practice note: Leverage the five-month preliminary opinion timeline to signal patent strength to partners and utilize the Unitary Patent for efficient multi-jurisdictional coverag…
What to monitor: Watch for emerging EPO-INTA collaborations on AI and standard-essential patents that may influence future examination guidelines and enforcement norms.

items.title: 100 years of design protection: where creativity, law and innovation meet
items.title_zh: 外观设计保护百年：创意、法律与创新的交汇
items.title_en: BOIP Marks Centenary of Design Protection: Balancing Creativity, Law, and Innovation
items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）发布官方新闻，纪念外观设计保护制度实施一百周年。标题指出该里程碑是创造力、法律与创新交汇的时刻。由于正文缺失，仅能确认BOIP作为机构主体发布了这一纪念性声明，无具体政策变更或案件细节。
items.ai_summary_en: The Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) celebrates the centenary of its design protection system. The article traces the evolution of regional design law from early legislation to the modern EU harmonized framework. It highlights the legal system's critical role in balancing creator rights with industrial innovation promotion. As a key regional IP institution, BOIP uses this milestone to showcase its historical contributions and current functions in safeguarding design rights and supporting enterprise innovation. This underscores the Benelux region's leading position in design protection within Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于仅有标题信息，无法评估具体实务影响。需关注BOIP后续是否发布关于百年历程的详细报告、统计数据或针对现行外观设计注册程序的指导性文件，以了解区域设计保护趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: The Benelux region remains a strategic gateway for design protection when entering the broader EU market. Rights holders should leverage BOIP’s efficient registration mechanisms to secure early design rights, establishing a robust foundation for market access. It is essential to closely monitor specific judicial standards regarding novelty and individual character within the Benelux jurisdiction. Ensuring product designs comply with these local practices helps mitigate infringement risks and strengthens overall intellectual property protection strategies for brands operating in Europe.

items.title: Why Ericsson and Nokia don’t charge patent licensing royalties to MNOs
items.title_zh: 为何爱立信和诺基亚不向移动运营商收取专利许可费
items.title_en: Why Ericsson and Nokia don’t charge patent licensing royalties to MNOs
items.summary: Saturday Opinion: As the cellular SEP licensing landscape evolves from established practice, SEP licensing expert Eric Stasik digs into the foundations of today’s licensing market
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨蜂窝标准必要专利（SEP）许可格局的演变。专家Eric Stasik分析指出，传统上向移动网络运营商（MNOs）收费的模式正在改变。文章深入剖析当前许可市场的基础，解释为何行业领导者如爱立信和诺基亚调整策略，不再主要向MNOs收取特许权使用费，而是转向终端层级或其他模式，反映了SEP许可实践从既定惯例向新范式的过渡。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信企业需密切关注许可层级下移趋势，避免在MNO环节陷入被动谈判。应重点评估终端侧许可风险，提前布局FRAND合规策略。鉴于欧洲巨头策略调整，中国企业应积极准备应对可能的费率争议，利用全球诉讼与谈判组合拳维护自身权益，确保供应链稳定。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["分析SEP许可格局从传统向新模式的演变趋势", "解读爱立信和诺基亚不向MNOs收费的市场基础", "探讨当前通信领域专利许可实践的根本性变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注许可层级下移，规避MNO环节谈判风险", "提前布局终端侧FRAND合规，应对费率争议"]

items.title: HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar
items.title_zh: HP在慕尼黑墨盒专利纠纷中胜诉Ninestar
items.title_en: HP Secures Munich Court Ruling in Toner Cartridge Patent Dispute Against Ninestar Group
items.summary: In a series of lawsuits concerning various patents, Hewlett Packard Development is taking action at Munich Regional Court against several European and Chinese companies of the Ninestar Group. They all sell printer cartridges on platforms such as Amazon. According to HP, these cartridges infringe HP’s patents. After the court imposed a sales ban on CaiLuo last […] The post HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 惠普开发公司在慕尼黑地区法院对纳思达集团多家欧中企业发起系列专利诉讼，指控其通过亚马逊等平台销售的打印机墨盒侵犯惠普专利。法院已对其中一家名为CaiLuo的公司实施销售禁令。此案反映了跨国企业在欧洲针对替代耗材市场的严格知识产权保护行动，凸显了当地司法机构在认定专利侵权及执行禁令方面的效率与力度。
items.ai_summary_en: Hewlett Packard Development has initiated a series of patent infringement lawsuits at the Munich Regional Court against multiple entities within the Ninestar Group. The defendants, comprising European and Chinese companies, are accused of selling printer cartridges on online platforms such as Amazon that allegedly infringe HP's patents. The court has reportedly imposed sales restrictions on one specific defendant, CaiLuo, marking a significant development in this ongoing enforcement action regarding compatible consumables.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类执法动作给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the continued use of German regional courts for enforcing patent rights against suppliers of compatible printing consumables. Rights holders are actively targeting distributors across different jurisdictions who utilize major online marketplaces. Market participants should observe how these proceedings develop, particularly regarding the scope of liability for various group entities involved in the supply chain.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["惠普在慕尼黑法院起诉纳思达集团侵犯多项专利", "涉案产品为通过亚马逊销售的打印机兼容墨盒", "法院已对部分被告实施临时销售禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["HP filed patent infringement suits at Munich Regional Court against several Ninestar Group companies.", "The dispute targets printer cartridges sold by defendants on e-commerce platforms like Amazon.", "The court has already imposed a sales ban on the defendant entity known as CaiLuo."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需强化德国市场专利FTO尽职调查", "应对策略应结合无效宣告与积极应诉机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders may leverage German litigation to enforce patents against multi-jurisdictional sellers of compatible goods.", "Practice note: Enforcement actions increasingly target specific corporate entities within larger groups operating on digital platforms.", "What to monitor: Further developments in the Munich proceedings and potential impacts on other Ninestar affiliates."]
core_points_en: HP filed patent infringement suits at Munich Regional Court against several Ninestar Group companies.
The dispute targets printer cartridges sold by defendants on e-commerce platforms like Amazon.
The court has already imposed a sales ban on the defendant entity known as CaiLuo.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders may leverage German litigation to enforce patents against multi-jurisdictional sellers of compatible goods.
Practice note: Enforcement actions increasingly target specific corporate entities within larger groups operating on digital platforms.
What to monitor: Further developments in the Munich proceedings and potential impacts on other Ninestar affiliates.

items.title: Court of Appeal overturns unjustified threats injunction in vape TM dispute
items.title_zh: 上诉法院推翻电子烟商标纠纷中的不当威胁禁令
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Overturns Unjustified Threats Injunction in Vaping Trademark Dispute
items.summary: Vaping dispute, in which Stobbs and Brandsmiths are the representatives, tested how the UK's Human Rights Act can apply to injunctions restraining unjustified threats
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在一宗电子烟商标争议中，推翻了针对不当威胁行为发出的禁令。该案由Stobbs和Brandsmiths代理，重点测试了英国《人权法案》如何适用于限制不当威胁的禁令，探讨了知识产权执法与人权保护之间的法律平衡问题。
items.ai_summary_en: This case involves a trademark dispute concerning vaping products, where Stobbs and Brandsmiths represented the involved parties. The litigation primarily tested the application of the UK Human Rights Act to injunctions designed to restrain unjustified threats of infringement proceedings. The Court of Appeal ultimately overturned the previously issued injunction. The judgment emphasizes the necessity of re-examining the legal standards used to balance the enforcement interests of intellectual property rights holders against the freedom of expression and commercial interests of the parties receiving warnings. This decision establishes a stricter threshold for granting such injunctions, ensuring that fundamental rights are adequately protected during IP enforcement actions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了《人权法案》在商标不当威胁诉讼禁令审查中的关键地位。实务中需关注法院如何权衡知识产权保护与表达自由，后续类似案件的禁令申请将面临更严格的人权合规性审查，影响维权策略选择。
items.ai_insight_en: Entities operating in or trading with the UK must carefully assess the risk of unjustified threats when sending or receiving infringement warnings. Before issuing cease-and-desist letters, rigorous evaluation of right stability is essential to avoid constituting an unjustified threat through abusive litigation tactics. Conversely, parties facing improper accusations should consider invoking the Human Rights Act as a defense mechanism. Utilizing procedural rules effectively can help protect corporate reputation and maintain operational freedom against potentially overreaching intellectual property enforcement strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院推翻了电子烟商标纠纷中针对不当威胁的禁令", "案件测试了英国《人权法案》在限制不当威胁禁令中的适用性", "Stobbs和Brandsmiths分别为案件相关方的代表律师"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Court of Appeal overturned a lower court injunction regarding unjustified threats in a vaping trademark dispute.", "The case tested the applicability of the Human Rights Act to injunctions restraining unjustified intellectual property threats.", "The ruling emphasizes balancing rights holders' enforcement interests with recipients' fundamental rights and business freedoms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["《人权法案》成为审查商标威胁禁令的重要法律依据", "法院在禁令签发中将更严格权衡权利行使与基本人权", "需持续观察后续案例对不当威胁认定标准的具体影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess right stability before sending warnings to avoid liability for unjustified threats under UK law.", "Practice note: Invoke the Human Rights Act to defend against improper infringement accusations and protect business operations.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards for balancing IP enforcement interests with freedom of expression and commercial rights."]
core_points_en: The UK Court of Appeal overturned a lower court injunction regarding unjustified threats in a vaping trademark dispute.
The case tested the applicability of the Human Rights Act to injunctions restraining unjustified intellectual property threats.
The ruling emphasizes balancing rights holders' enforcement interests with recipients' fundamental rights and business freedoms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess right stability before sending warnings to avoid liability for unjustified threats under UK law.
Practice note: Invoke the Human Rights Act to defend against improper infringement accusations and protect business operations.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards for balancing IP enforcement interests with freedom of expression and commercial rights.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Decision On Infringement And Validity, UPC_CFI_214/2023, UPC_CFI_403/2025
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基分院：专利侵权与有效性裁决及等同原则适用
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki Applies Hague Equivalence Approach and Limits Revocation Scope in EP 3 295 663 Decision
items.summary: The Helsinki Local Division of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement proceedings involving EP 3 295 663, with AIM Sport Development AG asserting patent rights against multiple TGI Sport entities across European jurisdictions. The case included a counterclaim for revocation, requiring the court to analyze validity under Articles 54, 56, and 123(2) EPC alongside infringement claims under Article 69 EPC.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）赫尔辛基地方分院就EP 3 295 663号专利作出侵权与有效性判决。在缺乏上诉法院先例时，法院采纳海牙分院的等同侵权判定路径，强调等同标准需累积满足，任一不成立即无侵权。针对撤销反诉，法院指出若当事人未充分论证“新增事项”，仅需回应已 substantiated 的部分。该案明确了UPC内部司法一致性的重要性及举证责任边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court's Helsinki Local Division issued a decision on infringement and validity concerning European patent EP 3 295 663, held by AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities. In the absence of Court of Appeal precedent on equivalence, the Division adopted the approach established by The Hague Local Division, emphasizing that equivalence criteria are cumulative; failure to meet any single criterion precludes a finding of infringement. Regarding the counterclaim for revocation based on added matter under Article 123(2) EPC, the Court ruled it would only address arguments substantiated by the defendant, noting that TGI provided minimal detail and did not further develop this allegation during oral proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需密切关注各地方分院对等同原则的司法倾向，尤其是海牙、赫尔辛基等关键分院。在提出无效抗辩时，必须提供详尽的技术比对与法律论证，避免仅凭形式主张导致被驳回。建议建立跨法域的专利风险预警机制，统一应诉策略以应对UPC日益增强的判例约束力。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the importance of judicial consistency across UPC Local Divisions, particularly regarding the doctrine of equivalents. It signals that applicants and defendants must provide robust, substantiated technical and legal arguments for invalidity defenses, as courts will not expand the scope of review beyond what is rigorously pleaded. This highlights the need for precise drafting of counterclaims and careful selection of equivalence frameworks in litigation strategy.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无上诉先例时，地方法院参照海牙分院等同判定路径以保法律确定性", "等同侵权判定标准具有累积性，任一要件缺失即认定不构成等同侵权", "法院仅对当事人提供实质论证的撤销理由进行回应，简化审理焦点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Helsinki Local Division adopts The Hague Local Division's equivalence approach to ensure legal certainty absent Court of Appeal case law.", "Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if one criterion is unmet, no further assessment is required for a finding of non-infringement.", "Courts respond to revocation allegations only where parties present substantiated arguments, limiting scope when claims lack detailed support."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC地方分院等同原则司法倾向，统一跨法域应诉策略", "无效抗辩需提供实质技术论证，避免形式主张被法院忽略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and defendants should align equivalence arguments with established Local Division precedents, such as those from The Hague, to enhance predictabil…", "Practice note: When filing counterclaims for revocation, ensure all grounds, including added matter, are supported by detailed technical and legal analysis to guarantee full judic…", "What to monitor: Monitor future Local Division decisions to see if the cumulative equivalence approach gains broader acceptance or if divergent interpretations emerge before Court…"]
core_points_en: Helsinki Local Division adopts The Hague Local Division's equivalence approach to ensure legal certainty absent Court of Appeal case law.
Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if one criterion is unmet, no further assessment is required for a finding of non-infringement.
Courts respond to revocation allegations only where parties present substantiated arguments, limiting scope when claims lack detailed support.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and defendants should align equivalence arguments with established Local Division precedents, such as those from The Hague, to enhance predictabil…
Practice note: When filing counterclaims for revocation, ensure all grounds, including added matter, are supported by detailed technical and legal analysis to guarantee full judic…
What to monitor: Monitor future Local Division decisions to see if the cumulative equivalence approach gains broader acceptance or if divergent interpretations emerge before Court…

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, April 28, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_869/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院2026年4月程序令：关于提交文件可采性的裁决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Equality of Arms Requires Plaintiff Comment on Defendant's Late Rejoinder
items.summary: The admissibility of the documents and arguments submitted is a question of fact. Whether the defendant’s submissions in its rejoinder regarding the motion to amend are relevant...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方分院发布程序令（案号UPC_CFI_869/2025），涉及大日本印刷诉Zapp等公司专利侵权案。法院裁定，被告在反驳中针对修改动议提交的论据是否相关及是否逾期，属事实问题，需待口头程序结束后方能确定。依据武器平等原则，法院允许原告就被告的事实陈述补充评论，作为预防性措施，且不 prejudge 最终的可采性决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division issued a procedural order in Dai Nippon Printing v. Zapp regarding the admissibility of documents submitted in the defendant's rejoinder concerning a motion to amend. The court determined that assessing the relevance and timeliness of these submissions is a factual question requiring thorough analysis, which can only be conclusively resolved at the end of oral proceedings. To uphold the principle of equality of arms, the judge rapporteur granted the plaintiff a precautionary opportunity to comment on the defendant's statement of facts. This measure is without prejudice to the final decision on whether the defendant's additional arguments will be admitted into the record.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应高度重视程序规则的灵活性。即便对方提交材料可能逾期，法院仍可能基于公平原则给予我方回应机会。建议企业建立快速响应机制，充分利用程序赋予的评论权，同时密切关注法官对“武器平等”原则的适用尺度，以优化诉讼策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This order highlights the UPC's procedural flexibility in managing late-filed evidence under the Rules of Procedure. By deferring the final admissibility ruling while granting immediate comment rights, the court balances procedural efficiency with fair trial guarantees. Rights holders should note that 'equality of arms' may trigger additional briefing rounds even when opposing filings appear procedurally irregular, requiring agile case management during the early stages of litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["被告反驳中的修改动议论据可采性属事实问题，需结合案情全面分析", "基于武器平等原则，原告获准对被告新增事实陈述进行补充评论", "最终可采性判定将推迟至口头程序结束时作出，确保程序公正"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Admissibility of late submissions in a rejoinder regarding amendment motions is treated as a complex question of fact.", "Final determination on the relevance and timeliness of such arguments is deferred until the conclusion of oral proceedings.", "Plaintiff is granted a precautionary right to comment on defendant's facts to ensure compliance with equality of arms principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用UPC程序规则争取补充评论机会，平衡对方突袭式举证", "关注法官对逾期证据可采性的裁量标准，完善应诉响应流程"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Courts may prioritize fair hearing rights over strict procedural deadlines, potentially extending pre-trial preparation timelines for both parties.", "Practice note: Prepare contingency responses for potential late submissions by opponents, ensuring readiness to file supplementary comments if granted by the division.", "What to monitor: How different UPC Local Divisions apply Rule 9.2 and Rule 36 RoP regarding the threshold for admitting late arguments in rejoinders."]
core_points_en: Admissibility of late submissions in a rejoinder regarding amendment motions is treated as a complex question of fact.
Final determination on the relevance and timeliness of such arguments is deferred until the conclusion of oral proceedings.
Plaintiff is granted a precautionary right to comment on defendant's facts to ensure compliance with equality of arms principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Courts may prioritize fair hearing rights over strict procedural deadlines, potentially extending pre-trial preparation timelines for both parties.
Practice note: Prepare contingency responses for potential late submissions by opponents, ensuring readiness to file supplementary comments if granted by the division.
What to monitor: How different UPC Local Divisions apply Rule 9.2 and Rule 36 RoP regarding the threshold for admitting late arguments in rejoinders.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, Standing Judge, April 28, 2026, Order On Request For Discretionary Review, UPC_CoA_56/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭裁定：错过期限的人为错误可获追溯延期
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Human Error in Deadline Calculation Warrants Retroactive Term Extension
items.summary: The Court of Appeal examines a request for discretionary review in a patent dispute between Optopol Technology and Topcon Corporation concerning EP 2 845 534. The case involves interpretation of procedural rules governing standing and review processes under the Unified Patent Court framework.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案系Optopol与Topcon在统一专利法院（UPC）的专利纠纷。上诉庭审查了针对EP 2845534号专利的程序性请求。法院认定，尽管代理人助理系统录入失误导致错过期限，但律所已建立核查机制，该人为错误属可原谅情形。上诉庭支持地方法院决定，允许在权利恢复请求期内隐含提交追溯期限延长申请，强调此类申请应尽早提出，但在特定未决判例背景下具有合理性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal, Standing Judge, reviewed a discretionary request concerning EP 2 845 534 in Optopol Technology v. Topcon Corporation. The Court upheld the Local Division's decision to grant a retroactive term extension under Rule 9.3 RoP. Although a deadline was missed due to an assistant's data entry error, the Court found this excusable because the law firm had established verification systems. The ruling confirms that such implicit requests filed within the re-establishment of rights period are reasonable, particularly when pending case law creates uncertainty regarding procedural timing.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需建立严格的期限双重核查机制以防范人为失误。若遇不可抗力或合理疏忽，应及时利用“可原谅错误”条款申请救济。同时，注意程序请求的时效性与独立性，避免过度依赖替代性请求策略，确保合规操作以降低专利失效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the threshold for 'excusable' errors in UPC proceedings, distinguishing between systemic failures and isolated human mistakes within robust compliance frameworks. For rights holders, it validates the strategy of filing alternative procedural requests during periods of legal uncertainty. However, the Court's emphasis on timeliness reinforces that reliance on such extensions remains exceptional rather than routine practice.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭认定代理人系统性核查下的人为失误属可原谅错误", "支持在权利恢复期限内隐含提交追溯期限延长申请的合理性", "明确依据程序规则第9.3条，追溯延期请求原则上应尽早提出"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court accepted that a human error by an assistant constituted an excusable mistake despite existing internal verification checks at the representative's firm.", "An implicit request for retroactive term extension filed as an alternative within the re-establishment of rights period was deemed procedurally acceptable.", "While Rule 9.3 RoP requires requests for retroactive term extension to be made as soon as possible, context-specific delays may be justified."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立严格期限双重核查机制，防范人为失误导致专利失效", "善用“可原谅错误”条款及时申请救济，确保程序合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Demonstrates that established internal deadline monitoring systems can mitigate risks associated with isolated administrative errors in UPC litigation.", "Practice note: Consider filing implicit alternative requests for retroactive term extension alongside re-establishment applications when procedural deadlines are at risk.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'as soon as possible' under Rule 9.3 RoP to understand how pending case law influences acceptable delay durations."]
core_points_en: The Court accepted that a human error by an assistant constituted an excusable mistake despite existing internal verification checks at the representative's firm.
An implicit request for retroactive term extension filed as an alternative within the re-establishment of rights period was deemed procedurally acceptable.
While Rule 9.3 RoP requires requests for retroactive term extension to be made as soon as possible, context-specific delays may be justified.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Demonstrates that established internal deadline monitoring systems can mitigate risks associated with isolated administrative errors in UPC litigation.
Practice note: Consider filing implicit alternative requests for retroactive term extension alongside re-establishment applications when procedural deadlines are at risk.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'as soon as possible' under Rule 9.3 RoP to understand how pending case law influences acceptable delay durations.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 27, 2026, Order On Provisional Measures, UPC_CoA_917/2025
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院就临时措施中无效抗辩及紧急性作出裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Waiving Invalidity in Provisional Measures and Urgency Assessment
items.summary: Unlike proceedings on the merits, where the Statement of defence shall include a Counterclaim for revocation (R. 25.1 RoP) if there is an assertion that the patent alleged to be infringed is invalid...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院在Merz案中裁定，临时措施程序中的无效抗辩放弃不构成变更诉请或撤诉，法院可基于剩余争议继续审理。确认仿制药定价报销完成即具紧迫性，但仅公布价格不足证六个月内上市。考量竞争、价格侵蚀及患者利益后授予禁令，并给予48小时及一周宽限期。
items.ai_summary_en: In the Merz case, the UPC Court of Appeal ruled that waiving an invalidity defense in provisional measures proceedings does not constitute a claim change or withdrawal under the Rules of Procedure. The Court may proceed based on remaining issues and order cost compensation for withdrawn arguments. The decision confirms that obtaining pricing and reimbursement approval does not automatically establish imminent infringement within six months. However, considering factors such as product shortages, price erosion, and SPC expiry, the Court balanced the parties' interests and granted a provisional injunction with a 48-hour grace period. This highlights the nuanced approach to urgency and necessity in pharmaceutical patent disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确UPC临时措施程序中无效抗辩的处理规则，降低被告通过程序性动作拖延风险。同时细化了医药领域“紧迫性”的认定标准，强调定价报销完成的关键性。实务中需关注法院在平衡患者利益与专利权保护时的具体考量因素，特别是供应链短缺对禁令 granted 的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC provisional measures should carefully manage invalidity defenses to avoid unnecessary cost liabilities, as waivers are treated distinctly from formal withdrawals. Mere regulatory approvals like pricing do not guarantee safety from injunctions; defendants must comprehensively assess market dynamics. Factors such as existing product shortages, impending Supplementary Protection Certificate expiries, and broader public health impacts significantly influence the Court's assessment of urgency and necessity. Robust evidence regarding actual launch timelines and competitive conditions is essential to effectively counter claims of imminent infringement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["临时措施中放弃无效抗辩非变更诉请或撤诉，法院可依剩余争议继续审理并判承担费用", "确认完成定价报销程序即构成紧迫侵权风险，但仅公布价格不足以证明六个月内必然上市", "综合竞争态势、价格侵蚀及患者利益等因素，法院认定具备必要性并授予临时禁令及宽限期"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Waiving invalidity defenses in provisional measures is not a procedural withdrawal; courts may continue hearing remaining issues and award costs.", "Securing pricing and reimbursement approval does not inherently prove imminent launch within six months, requiring specific evidence of market entry certainty.", "Courts weigh patient interests, potential price erosion, and supply shortages when deciding on provisional injunctions, potentially granting short grace periods."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确临时措施阶段放弃无效抗辩的程序性质，避免被误读为撤诉或变更诉请", "细化医药专利禁令中“紧迫性”证据标准，区分定价报销完成与单纯价格公布", "观察法院在权衡禁令必要性时，对患者利益、供应短缺及价格侵蚀的具体评估逻辑"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Carefully evaluate the strategic implications of waiving invalidity defenses in provisional measures to mitigate unexpected cost orders.", "Practice note: Do not rely solely on pricing approvals to demonstrate lack of urgency; prepare detailed evidence on actual market launch probabilities.", "What to monitor: How Courts balance patient access and supply chain stability against patent rights when determining the necessity of provisional relief."]
core_points_en: Waiving invalidity defenses in provisional measures is not a procedural withdrawal; courts may continue hearing remaining issues and award costs.
Securing pricing and reimbursement approval does not inherently prove imminent launch within six months, requiring specific evidence of market entry certainty.
Courts weigh patient interests, potential price erosion, and supply shortages when deciding on provisional injunctions, potentially granting short grace periods.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Carefully evaluate the strategic implications of waiving invalidity defenses in provisional measures to mitigate unexpected cost orders.
Practice note: Do not rely solely on pricing approvals to demonstrate lack of urgency; prepare detailed evidence on actual market launch probabilities.
What to monitor: How Courts balance patient access and supply chain stability against patent rights when determining the necessity of provisional relief.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Order Disposing Of Provisional Measures Application, ACT_551054/2023
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基地院：临时措施申请失去目的时予以处置
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki: Disposing of Provisional Measures Applications Devoid of Purpose Under Rule 360
items.summary: If a provisional measures application has become devoid of purpose and hence there is no longer need to adjudicate on it, the Court shall dispose of it based on R. 360 RoP...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月29日，UPC赫尔辛基地方法院就ACT_551054/2023案发布命令。法院依据《程序规则》第360条裁定，若临时措施申请已失去目的且无需再行裁决，应予以处置。若当事人请求，法院可作出费用决定，并酌情考虑实体案件已知结果。本案涉及AIM Sport Development AG与多家TGI Sport实体关于EP 3 295 663号专利的争议。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Helsinki Local Division issued an order on April 29, 2026, regarding application ACT_551054/2023. The Court held that if a provisional measures application becomes devoid of purpose, it must be disposed of pursuant to Rule 360 of the Rules of Procedure. Upon request by the parties, the Court may issue a cost decision, potentially considering the known outcome of the case on the merits. This proceeding involved AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities concerning European Patent EP 3 295 663.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC临时禁令时，需密切关注程序进展。若主案胜诉或和解，应及时主张临时措施失去目的以终止程序，避免不必要的费用支出。建议结合实体判决结果，积极向法院申请费用分担，优化跨境知识产权诉讼的成本控制策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the procedural mechanism for terminating provisional measures proceedings under the Unified Patent Court framework when the underlying urgency or purpose dissipates. For rights holders and defendants, understanding when an application is deemed 'devoid of purpose' is critical for managing litigation timelines and potential cost liabilities. The ability to link cost decisions to the merits outcome provides a pathway for resolving ancillary disputes efficiently once the primary legal questions are settled or mooted.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["临时措施申请若失去目的，UPC法院依R.360条应予处置", "法院可依当事人请求裁定诉讼费用，参考实体案已知结果", "本案涉及EP 3 295 663号专利，原告为AIM Sport，被告为多家公司"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 360 RoP mandates disposal of provisional measures applications that have become devoid of purpose and no longer require adjudication.", "The Court may decide on costs upon party request, taking into account the known outcome of the main proceedings if available.", "The order addresses procedural efficiency in UPC local divisions when interim relief is no longer necessary due to case developments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注临时措施目的丧失情形，及时申请终止程序", "利用实体案结果争取有利费用裁定，降低诉讼成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties should assess whether changes in factual circumstances or settlement progress render provisional measures applications obsolete to avoid unnecessary proc…", "Practice note: When requesting cost decisions after disposal under Rule 360, parties should explicitly reference any known outcomes on the merits to support their position.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations by other UPC Local Divisions regarding what constitutes an application being 'devoid of purpose' under Rule 360."]
core_points_en: Rule 360 RoP mandates disposal of provisional measures applications that have become devoid of purpose and no longer require adjudication.
The Court may decide on costs upon party request, taking into account the known outcome of the main proceedings if available.
The order addresses procedural efficiency in UPC local divisions when interim relief is no longer necessary due to case developments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties should assess whether changes in factual circumstances or settlement progress render provisional measures applications obsolete to avoid unnecessary proc…
Practice note: When requesting cost decisions after disposal under Rule 360, parties should explicitly reference any known outcomes on the merits to support their position.
What to monitor: Future interpretations by other UPC Local Divisions regarding what constitutes an application being 'devoid of purpose' under Rule 360.

items.title_zh: 合作委员会启动强化EUIPN协作框架
items.title_en: EUIPO Launches Cooperation Board to Strengthen EUIPN Collaboration Framework
items.summary: Cooperation Board launch strengthens EUIPN framework for collaboration
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）启动了合作委员会，旨在加强欧洲知识产权局网络（EUIPN）的协作框架。原文摘要与正文仅重复该事实，未提供关于委员会具体职能、成员构成或后续工作计划的详细背景信息。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has officially launched a new Cooperation Board. This initiative is designed to strengthen the collaborative framework of the European Union Intellectual Property Network (EUIPN). The announcement confirms the establishment of this body to enhance coordination among member offices, though specific operational details regarding its functions or composition are not provided in the initial brief.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note / What to monitor：鉴于正文极度简略，目前无法评估该委员会对商标/外观设计注册程序或执法协调的具体影响。需持续观察EUIPO后续发布的官方指南或新闻稿，以获取关于新协作机制运作细节及对各成员国局实际影响的明确信息。
items.ai_insight_en: This development signals an ongoing effort by the EUIPO to refine administrative and procedural alignment across the EU intellectual property landscape. For rights holders and practitioners, the formation of this board indicates a continued focus on harmonizing practices among national offices. However, the immediate practical impact on registration procedures or enforcement remains unclear pending further official guidance.

items.title: AI Act Omnibus: EU institutions agree political deal
items.title_zh: 欧盟机构就《人工智能法案》综合修正案达成政治协议
items.title_en: EU Institutions Reach Political Deal on AI Act Omnibus Amendments
items.summary: The EU Commission, Parliament and Council have each announced this morning that a political deal has been reached overnight on the AI Act Omnibus. The ...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会、议会和理事会宣布就《人工智能法案》综合修正案（AI Act Omnibus）达成政治协议。此前最终三方会谈未果，此次协议旨在消除不确定性。关键变更清单将快速纳入最终文本并于数周内生效。尽管草案文本尚待公布，但已披露关键点，为企业规划合规提供更清晰基础。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Commission, Parliament, and Council have announced a political agreement on the AI Act Omnibus amendments. This breakthrough follows a failed final trilogue session just one week prior, salvaging the legislative process after significant uncertainty regarding the timeline. The agreed changes are expected to be fast-tracked into the final legal text, with entry into force anticipated within the coming weeks. Although the full draft text remains unpublished, this substantive accord clarifies key modifications to the original regulation. For enterprises, this development provides a much-needed foundation for compliance planning by resolving major ambiguities concerning the scope of application and implementation schedules. The agreement marks a critical step in stabilizing the regulatory environment for artificial intelligence across the European Union.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此协议标志着欧盟AI监管框架的关键进展，减少了此前因谈判失败带来的不确定性。企业应关注即将公布的最终文本细节，特别是关于合规义务的具体条款。目前阶段需密切监测正式法律文本的发布及其对现有AI系统合规要求的具体影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should closely monitor how the Omnibus revisions refine the definition of high-risk AI systems and adjust associated compliance obligations. It is advisable to immediately assess whether current products align with the updated framework, particularly regarding transparency mandates for general-purpose AI models. Establishing dynamic compliance mechanisms during the pre-enforcement window allows organizations to optimize data governance and algorithmic documentation. Proactive adaptation mitigates risks of market access barriers or substantial penalties resulting from sudden regulatory shifts, ensuring smoother integration into the EU digital market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟三大机构宣布就《人工智能法案》综合修正案达成政治协议，结束此前谈判僵局", "关键变更将快速纳入最终文本并预计在数周内生效，以明确实施时间表", "虽草案全文未出，但已披露关键点，为企业规划AI法案合规提供依据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU institutions secured a pivotal political deal on the AI Act Omnibus, overcoming previous deadlock.", "Key amendments addressing implementation challenges will be rapidly incorporated into the final legal text.", "The agreement reduces regulatory uncertainty, offering clearer compliance expectations and timelines."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["政治协议达成消除了AI法案实施范围和时间表的不确定性，利于企业长期规划", "需等待最终草案文本发布，以区分实质性合规要求与政策噪音", "后续应重点关注法案生效后的具体执行指南及对企业现有AI系统的合规冲击"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess the impact of revised high-risk AI definitions on product portfolios and adjust compliance strategies accordingly.", "Practice note: Leverage the pre-enforcement window to enhance data governance frameworks and mitigate market access risks.", "What to monitor: Final publication of the amended text and specific guidance on general-purpose AI model transparency requirements."]
core_points_en: EU institutions secured a pivotal political deal on the AI Act Omnibus, overcoming previous deadlock.
Key amendments addressing implementation challenges will be rapidly incorporated into the final legal text.
The agreement reduces regulatory uncertainty, offering clearer compliance expectations and timelines.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess the impact of revised high-risk AI definitions on product portfolios and adjust compliance strategies accordingly.
Practice note: Leverage the pre-enforcement window to enhance data governance frameworks and mitigate market access risks.
What to monitor: Final publication of the amended text and specific guidance on general-purpose AI model transparency requirements.

items.title: Partial success for Millenium Pharma in battle over Velcade in Paris
items.title_zh: 巴黎法院裁定：Millennium Pharma在Velcade专利战中部分胜诉
items.title_en: Paris Court Rules Partially in Favor of Millennium Pharma in Velcade Patent Dispute
items.summary: Millennium Pharmaceuticals and Cilag accuse several generic drug companies of infringing patents EP 2 251 344 B1 and EP 3 078 667 B1. Both protect a formulation of boronic acid compounds. They form the basis of Millennium’s drug Velcade with the active ingredient bortezomib. The drug treats multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma. Since the patents expired […] The post Partial success for Millenium Pharma in battle over Velcade in Paris appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: Millennium Pharmaceuticals与Cilag指控多家仿制药企侵犯其保护硼酸化合物制剂的欧洲专利EP 2 251 344 B1和EP 3 078 667 B1，该专利是抗癌药Velcade的基础。巴黎司法法院作出裁决，原告获得部分胜诉。鉴于相关专利已过期，此案主要涉及过往侵权行为的法律责任认定及赔偿问题，体现了原研药企在专利到期后仍通过诉讼维护市场利益的策略。
items.ai_summary_en: Millennium Pharmaceuticals and Cilag initiated proceedings against several generic manufacturers alleging infringement of European patents EP 2 251 344 B1 and EP 3 078 667 B1. These patents cover formulations of boronic acid compounds used in the anticancer drug Velcade (bortezomib). The Paris Judicial Court delivered a ruling resulting in partial success for the patent holders. The case highlights ongoing litigation dynamics regarding pharmaceutical formulations even as patent protections reach their expiration.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，法国后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision illustrates the continued enforcement activity by originator companies regarding formulation patents near or after expiry. Rights holders may pursue claims based on past activities or specific formulation aspects distinct from the active ingredient itself. Market participants should track how national courts interpret infringement boundaries for such secondary patents when primary composition-of-matter protections expire.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Millennium指控仿制药企侵犯Velcade基础专利，巴黎法院裁定部分胜诉", "涉案专利保护硼酸化合物制剂，虽已过期但侵权追责仍在进行中", "案件凸显原研药企利用剩余法律手段延缓仿制药全面进入市场的策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Millennium Pharmaceuticals and Cilag accused multiple generic companies of infringing two European patents covering boronic acid compound formulations.", "The disputed patents protect the formulation of Velcade, an active treatment for multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma.", "The Paris Judicial Court issued a judgment granting partial success to the plaintiffs in this patent infringement dispute."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕原研药企专利到期后的历史侵权追溯诉讼风险", "加强FTO分析与无效证据储备，应对潜在高额索赔"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Originator firms continue leveraging formulation patents to address market entry challenges post-expiry of core compound rights.", "Practice note: Courts assess infringement claims for specific pharmaceutical formulations separately from the validity status of the underlying active ingredient patents.", "What to monitor: Future rulings in France concerning the scope of protection for boronic acid formulations and similar secondary patent assets."]

The disputed patents protect the formulation of Velcade, an active treatment for multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma.

items.title: UPC update: 3D printing first | motorcycle dispute rumbles on
items.title_zh: UPC更新：3D打印首案与摩托车纠纷持续
items.title_en: UPC Update: First 3D Printing Patent Case and Ongoing Motorcycle Dispute
items.summary: Publication of the UPC’s annual report and adoption of the procedural rules of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre were also among major developments
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）发布年度报告，并采纳专利调解与仲裁中心的程序规则。标题显示UPC涉及3D打印首例案件，且一起摩托车相关争议仍在进行中。这些构成了UPC近期的主要发展动态。
items.ai_summary_en: This update covers significant recent developments at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). A landmark case involving 3D printing technology has been initiated, marking the first time this specific technical field has appeared in UPC litigation. Concurrently, a high-profile patent dispute within the motorcycle industry continues to proceed through the court system, highlighting the sustained nature of complex cross-border IP conflicts. Additionally, the UPC published its annual report, providing transparency on its operational progress. Crucially, the procedural rules for the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre were formally adopted. These measures collectively enhance the court’s alternative dispute resolution framework, offering parties more flexible mechanisms beyond traditional litigation. These updates reflect the UPC’s ongoing efforts to establish robust jurisprudence and optimize procedural efficiency for stakeholders across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC在3D打印等新兴技术领域的司法实践倾向，以及新采纳的调解仲裁规则对争议解决效率的实际影响。摩托车纠纷的后续进展亦值得观察，以评估UPC对传统机械领域专利争议的审理尺度。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should closely monitor UPC jurisprudence regarding emerging technologies like 3D printing to anticipate potential infringement risks and define claim scopes accurately. The adoption of formal procedural rules for mediation and arbitration provides a viable, cost-effective alternative to full litigation for resolving cross-border patent disputes. Companies operating in Europe are advised to integrate these alternative mechanisms into their broader IP enforcement strategies. Furthermore, maintaining rigorous compliance with European patent standards and leveraging the UPC’s diversified dispute resolution framework can help protect commercial interests while managing legal exposure efficiently in a multi-jurisdictional environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC发布了其年度报告，披露法院运作情况", "UPC采纳了专利调解与仲裁中心的程序规则", "标题显示UPC处理了首例3D打印相关案件"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC对3D打印侵权认定的司法标准形成", "监测专利调解与仲裁中心程序规则的实际应用效果", "跟踪摩托车纠纷案件的后续审理进程与裁决结果"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor new UPC precedents in emerging tech sectors like 3D printing to assess infringement exposure and guide R&D compliance.", "Practice note: Utilize the newly adopted mediation and arbitration rules as strategic, lower-cost alternatives to resolve cross-border patent conflicts.", "What to monitor: Track the progression of the ongoing motorcycle dispute and the implementation of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre's procedures."]
core_points_en: The UPC is hearing its first patent case involving 3D printing technology, establishing initial judicial practice in this emerging sector.
A major motorcycle industry patent dispute remains active before the UPC, demonstrating the prolonged timeline characteristic of such litigation.
The UPC released its annual report and adopted procedural rules for the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre to strengthen dispute resolution options.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor new UPC precedents in emerging tech sectors like 3D printing to assess infringement exposure and guide R&D compliance.
Practice note: Utilize the newly adopted mediation and arbitration rules as strategic, lower-cost alternatives to resolve cross-border patent conflicts.
What to monitor: Track the progression of the ongoing motorcycle dispute and the implementation of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre's procedures.

items.title: New MPEG-DASH disputes add heat to streaming patent licensing wars
items.title_zh: 新MPEG-DASH纠纷加剧流媒体专利许可战
items.title_en: New MPEG-DASH disputes add heat to streaming patent licensing wars
items.summary: Huawei sues RTL at the UPC, following assertions by fellow Avanci Video licensor Helios Streaming against other streamers
items.ai_summary_zh: 华为在统一专利法院（UPC）起诉RTL，紧随Avanci视频许可池成员Helios Streaming对其他流媒体服务商的专利主张。此举标志着基于MPEG-DASH标准的流媒体专利许可争端升级，反映了标准必要专利（SEP）持有者与内容分发平台间激烈的许可谈判与诉讼博弈，凸显了欧洲市场在视频技术IP合规方面的高压态势。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国流媒体及硬件出口企业需高度关注Avanci等许可池的动态。面对UPC诉讼激增，建议提前进行FTO分析，评估SEP许可必要性，并建立快速响应机制以应对潜在禁令风险，避免高额赔偿与市场准入受阻。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华为在UPC起诉RTL，引发MPEG-DASH标准许可新争议", "Avanci视频许可池成员加强针对流媒体平台的专利维权", "欧洲流媒体行业面临日益严峻的标准必要专利诉讼风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕Avanci许可池在UPC发起的集中诉讼行动", "尽快评估MPEG-DASH相关SEP许可成本与合规风险"]

items.title: UPC revokes Chinese military medical research institute’s remdesivir patent
items.title_zh: UPC米兰分部撤销中国军科院瑞德西韦专利
items.title_en: UPC Milan Division Revokes AMMS Remdesivir Patent and Rejects Amendments
items.summary: According to the judgment, the value of the patent dispute is €20 million. However, following the defeat, the Chinese Academy of Military Medical Sciences (AMMS) only has to pay €800,000, which it had previously agreed with Gilead. This week, the central division Milan completely revoked one of AMMS’ patents and rejected amendments (case ID: UPC_CFI_552/2025). […] The post UPC revokes Chinese military medical research institute’s remdesivir patent appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）米兰中央分部作出判决，完全撤销中国人民解放军军事医学科学院（AMMS）的一项瑞德西韦专利，并驳回其修改请求。本案争议标的额为2000万欧元，但依据此前与吉利德科学达成的协议，AMMS败诉后仅需支付80万欧元诉讼费用。该案件编号为UPC_CFI_552/2025，标志着中国科研机构在UPC体系下的专利维权遭遇重大挫折，凸显了欧洲专利无效程序的高风险性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Central Division in Milan has issued a judgment completely revoking a patent held by the Chinese Academy of Military Medical Sciences (AMMS) related to remdesivir. The court also rejected proposed amendments to the patent claims in case UPC_CFI_552/2025. The dispute involved a value of €20 million. Under a prior agreement with Gilead, AMMS is liable for €800,000 following this adverse decision.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision illustrates the UPC's authority to centrally invalidate patents across participating states. For rights holders, particularly those from non-European jurisdictions, the outcome underscores the importance of robust claim drafting and validity assessments before engaging in UPC litigation. The rejection of amendments highlights the strict standards applied during central division proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC米兰分部完全撤销中国军科院瑞德西韦专利并驳回修改", "争议标的2000万欧元，依协议败诉方仅付80万欧元诉讼费用", "案件编号UPC_CFI_552/2025，体现UPC对专利有效性严格审查"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Central Division in Milan fully revoked an AMMS remdesivir patent and rejected amendment requests.", "The case ID is UPC_CFI_552/2025, involving a disputed value of €20 million between the parties.", "AMMS must pay €800,000 pursuant to a pre-existing agreement with Gilead following the patent revocation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC判决具全域效力，专利被撤将导致全境权利丧失", "建议在欧诉讼前加强FTO分析及预设无效抗辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Central division rulings can lead to immediate, broad invalidation of patent rights across multiple jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Applicants should ensure strong foundational validity support, as post-grant amendment opportunities may be limited or rejected.", "What to monitor: Future UPC Central Division decisions regarding patent validity challenges and the treatment of auxiliary requests."]
core_points_en: The UPC Central Division in Milan fully revoked an AMMS remdesivir patent and rejected amendment requests.
The case ID is UPC_CFI_552/2025, involving a disputed value of €20 million between the parties.
AMMS must pay €800,000 pursuant to a pre-existing agreement with Gilead following the patent revocation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Central division rulings can lead to immediate, broad invalidation of patent rights across multiple jurisdictions.
Practice note: Applicants should ensure strong foundational validity support, as post-grant amendment opportunities may be limited or rejected.
What to monitor: Future UPC Central Division decisions regarding patent validity challenges and the treatment of auxiliary requests.
