items.title: Recent Developments In Intellectual Property Legislation Regarding Law 5271/2026
items.title_zh: 希腊第5271/2026号法律强化艺术品知识产权保护
items.title_en: Greece Enacts Law 5271/2026 Strengthening Criminal Protection for Artworks and Collectibles
items.summary: On 30 January 2026, the New Law 5271/2026 (the Law) was published, establishing a dedicated legal framework to strengthen the protection of Works of Art and Collectible Items through enhanced criminal liability, expanded institutional mechanisms and increased penalties for forgery, fraud and intentional damage to cultural property.
items.ai_summary_zh: 希腊于2026年1月30日发布第5271/2026号法律，建立专门框架以加强艺术品和收藏品的保护。该法通过增强刑事责任、扩大机构机制及提高对伪造、欺诈和故意损坏文化财产行为的处罚力度，旨在打击涉及真实性、作者身份或来源的误导行为，并新增针对故意破坏艺术品的刑事条款。
items.ai_summary_en: On 30 January 2026, Greece published Law 5271/2026, establishing a specialized legal framework to enhance the protection of artworks and collectible items. The legislation introduces distinct criminal offenses for forgery, fraud, and intentional damage to cultural property, significantly increasing potential penalties. It expands institutional mechanisms and imposes stricter liability for acts involving misrepresentation of authenticity or origin. Penalties include imprisonment and substantial fines, with aggravated sanctions for organized crime or high-value items. The law also mandates confiscation, restitution, or destruction of infringing goods and introduces professional bans for offenders. This comprehensive approach aims to deter illicit activities in the art market and safeguard cultural heritage through enhanced criminal enforcement and regulatory oversight.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该立法显著提高了希腊境内艺术品交易的合规门槛与刑事风险。从业者需重点关注真伪鉴定流程及来源证明文件，以规避因“意图误导”而引发的严厉刑事处罚及职业禁止令。
items.ai_insight_en: Entities operating in the European art market, including traders, galleries, and auction houses, must ensure strict compliance with Greece's new criminal framework. Robust due diligence regarding provenance and authenticity documentation is critical to mitigate risks of inadvertent involvement in forgery or fraud. Companies should implement internal compliance controls to verify item origins and avoid classifications of commercial-scale infringement. Monitoring legislative updates and judicial interpretations will be essential for maintaining operational integrity and avoiding severe criminal liabilities, including professional bans and asset forfeiture under the new regime.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新法将制造、销售或持有意图误导真实性或来源的艺术品定为刑事犯罪，扩展了原有欺诈规定", "基础刑罚为六个月至五年监禁及五千至十二万欧元罚款；情节严重者可处最高十年监禁及三十万欧…", "刑法典新增第378A条，专门惩处故意或过失损坏、破坏公共机构或文化场所内艺术品的行为"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Establishes independent criminal offenses for artwork forgery and fraud, covering the entire chain from manufacturing to circulation.", "Significantly raises maximum penalties to ten years imprisonment and fines up to €300,000 for aggravated cases.", "Introduces new crimes for intentional vandalism, alongside professional bans and mandatory confiscation or destruction measures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["艺术品交易商及收藏家需重新评估尽职调查标准，确保 authenticity 证明链条完整", "故意损坏文化财产的刑事责任独立于一般财产损害，特别针对公共机构藏品提供更强保护", "法院有权在定罪前没收或销毁涉案物品，即使被告最终无罪，物品处置风险依然存在"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict verification of provenance and authenticity documents is required to avoid criminal liability for forgery or fraud.", "Practice note: Implement internal compliance mechanisms to prevent exposure to heavy penalties associated with commercial-scale infringement.", "What to monitor: Judicial applications of aggravated penalties and professional ban provisions in high-value art transactions."]
core_points_en: Establishes independent criminal offenses for artwork forgery and fraud, covering the entire chain from manufacturing to circulation.
Significantly raises maximum penalties to ten years imprisonment and fines up to €300,000 for aggravated cases.
Introduces new crimes for intentional vandalism, alongside professional bans and mandatory confiscation or destruction measures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict verification of provenance and authenticity documents is required to avoid criminal liability for forgery or fraud.
Practice note: Implement internal compliance mechanisms to prevent exposure to heavy penalties associated with commercial-scale infringement.
What to monitor: Judicial applications of aggravated penalties and professional ban provisions in high-value art transactions.

items.title: No infringement without fixation – The UPC Court of Appeal on functional claim construction and entering an appearance in electronic proceedings
items.title_zh: 无固定即无侵权：UPC上诉院论功能性权利要求与电子应诉
items.title_en: UPC Appeal Court: No Infringement Without Fixation of Technical Operation in Functional Claims
items.summary: HUROM v. NUC/WARMCOOK: UPC_CoA_409/2025, 410/2025, 420/2025 – decision of 27 March 2026 In this decision, the Court of Appeal of the Unified Patent Court clarifies that functional claim features must not be construed in isolation from the technical operation disclosed... Der Beitrag No infringement without fixation – The UPC Court of Appeal on functional claim construction and entering an appearance in electronic proceedings erschien zuerst auf BOEHMERT & BOEHMERT .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉院在HUROM诉NUC/WARMCOOK案中作出判决。法院澄清，功能性权利要求特征不得脱离披露的技术操作孤立解释。案件还涉及电子诉讼程序中的出庭登记问题。该判决明确了功能性限定的解释规则及程序要求。
items.ai_summary_en: In the HUROM v. NUC/WARMCOOK case, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal clarified that functional claim features must be interpreted in light of the specific technical operation disclosed in the patent specification. The court emphasized that infringement cannot be established if the alleged infringing product does not embody or fixate this particular technical mechanism. This ruling establishes a strict interpretation standard for functional limitations within the UPC system. Additionally, the decision addresses procedural requirements for entering an appearance in electronic proceedings. These findings provide significant guidance for future patent infringement litigation before the UPC, highlighting the necessity of aligning product functionality with the specific technical solutions described in the patent to avoid liability.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC对功能性权利要求的解释标准，强调技术操作的关联性。实务中需关注该解释方法对侵权判定的影响，以及电子诉讼程序合规性。后续应观察该判例在类似机械或功能限定专利案件中的引用情况。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the importance of precise claim construction regarding functional features in UPC proceedings. Parties should focus defense strategies on demonstrating that their products utilize different technical pathways, thereby avoiding equivalence findings based solely on similar functions. Furthermore, strict adherence to procedural rules in electronic filings is critical, as procedural errors can jeopardize substantive rights. Legal teams must ensure robust documentation of technical implementations to support non-infringement arguments effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉院裁定功能性权利要求特征须结合披露的技术操作进行解释，不可孤立看待", "判决涉及HUROM诉NUC/WARMCOOK案，案号包括UPC_CoA_409/202…", "案件审理内容涵盖电子诉讼程序中出庭登记（entering an appearance）…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Functional claim features require holistic interpretation based on the disclosed technical operation rather than isolated analysis.", "No patent infringement is found if the accused product fails to fixate or embody the specific technical operation defined in the claims.", "The judgment clarifies procedural standards for formally entering an appearance within the UPC's electronic litigation framework."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["功能性权利要求解释需结合具体技术操作，避免孤立解读导致保护范围偏差", "电子诉讼程序中的出庭登记合规性成为程序争议点，需严格遵循UPC程序规则", "持续监测该判决在后续UPC一审及上诉案件中对功能性特征侵权比对的指导作用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use the absence of fixed technical operation as a primary defense to counter functional equivalence claims.", "Practice note: Master UPC electronic procedure rules to prevent procedural defects from undermining substantive legal positions.", "What to monitor: Future UPC rulings on the boundary between functional language and specific technical implementations in patent claims."]
core_points_en: Functional claim features require holistic interpretation based on the disclosed technical operation rather than isolated analysis.
No patent infringement is found if the accused product fails to fixate or embody the specific technical operation defined in the claims.
The judgment clarifies procedural standards for formally entering an appearance within the UPC's electronic litigation framework.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use the absence of fixed technical operation as a primary defense to counter functional equivalence claims.
Practice note: Master UPC electronic procedure rules to prevent procedural defects from undermining substantive legal positions.
What to monitor: Future UPC rulings on the boundary between functional language and specific technical implementations in patent claims.

items.title: Arrêt Sud de France : le marketing territorial en matière vinicole ne résiste pas au droit européen
items.title_zh: 南法案：葡萄酒地域营销难抵欧盟法
items.title_en: France's Highest Court Bars 'Sud de France' Wine Labeling Over EU Law Conflicts
items.summary: Par Maître Pierre PÉROT, Avocat counsel, August Debouzy et Maître Camille ABBA, Avocate, August Debouzy Par un arrêt du 3 décembre 2025, le Conseil d’État rejette le recours formé contre la décision ministérielle interdisant l’usage de la mention de la marque « Sud de France » pour l’étiquetage des vins produits en Occitanie. Derrière un […] L’article Arrêt Sud de France : le marketing territorial en matière vinicole ne résiste pas au droit européen est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年12月3日，法国最高行政法院作出判决，驳回针对部长级决定的上诉。该决定禁止在奥克西塔尼产区葡萄酒标签上使用“Sud de France”品牌标识。法院认定此类地域性市场营销行为不符合欧盟法律规定。
items.ai_summary_en: On December 3, 2025, the French Conseil d'État dismissed an appeal against a ministerial decision prohibiting the use of the 'Sud de France' regional brand on wine labels from the Occitanie region. The court upheld the ban, determining that this territorial marketing strategy violated European Union regulations governing wine labeling and geographical indications. This ruling reinforces the supremacy of EU law over national or regional commercial branding initiatives when they conflict with established frameworks for protecting origins and preventing consumer confusion. The decision highlights the strict boundaries within which regional promotional marks must operate, ensuring they do not undermine the integrity of protected designations or mislead consumers regarding the specific provenance of agricultural products.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显欧盟法对成员国葡萄酒地域品牌营销的严格约束。实务中需关注集体商标与地理标志保护边界，避免因地域性宣传用语触犯欧盟单一市场规则，建议持续监测此类执法趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting agricultural goods to Europe must carefully assess label compliance to avoid legal risks associated with non-official regional names. Using broad geographic terms for marketing purposes can trigger conflicts with protected designations of origin and lead to market access barriers. Companies should implement robust internal review mechanisms to verify that all product labeling aligns with current EU regulations on geographical indications and food information standards. Proactive compliance checks are essential to prevent potential litigation, product recalls, or bans that could disrupt supply chains and damage brand reputation in the European single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国最高行政法院于2025年12月3日驳回相关上诉，维持禁令", "禁止在奥克西塔尼葡萄酒标签使用“Sud de France”品牌标识", "法院裁定该地域营销方式违反欧盟法律相关规定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Conseil d'État confirmed the prohibition of using the 'Sud de France' mark on wine labels produced in Occitanie.", "Territorial marketing strategies must strictly comply with the European Union's regulatory framework for wine labeling.", "EU law takes precedence over conflicting national or regional geographical indications and trademark usage rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟法优先原则在地域品牌营销中的具体适用界限", "集体商标使用权与欧盟葡萄酒标签合规性的冲突风险", "法国行政法院对欧盟法解释的最新司法动向监测"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters must avoid unauthorized regional branding that conflicts with EU protected designations.", "Practice note: Establish rigorous label compliance reviews aligned with EU geographical indication regulations.", "What to monitor: Evolving case law on the intersection of regional marketing marks and EU wine labeling rules."]
core_points_en: The Conseil d'État confirmed the prohibition of using the 'Sud de France' mark on wine labels produced in Occitanie.
Territorial marketing strategies must strictly comply with the European Union's regulatory framework for wine labeling.
EU law takes precedence over conflicting national or regional geographical indications and trademark usage rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters must avoid unauthorized regional branding that conflicts with EU protected designations.
Practice note: Establish rigorous label compliance reviews aligned with EU geographical indication regulations.
What to monitor: Evolving case law on the intersection of regional marketing marks and EU wine labeling rules.

items.title: How trade marks and licensing protect the Olympic and Paralympic brand
items.title_zh: 商标与许可如何保护奥运及残奥品牌
items.title_en: How Trademarks and Licensing Protect the Olympic and Paralympic Brand at Milano Cortina 2026
items.summary: From the Olympic rings to official mascots and merchandise, trade marks and licensing play a critical role in protecting the Olympic and Paralympic brand. Drawing on first‑hand experience at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Games, this blog explores how IP
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局博客以2026年米兰-科尔蒂纳冬奥会为例，阐述商标与许可在保护奥运品牌中的核心作用。文章指出吉祥物Tina和Milo已在意大利注册特定商标，通过结构化许可计划覆盖官方商品与零售，并结合反假冒执法与消费者教育，确保品牌质量与商业收益。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office blog examines the critical role of trademarks and licensing in safeguarding the Olympic and Paralympic brands, using the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Games as a primary case study. The article highlights how the International Olympic Committee and International Paralympic Committee manage official merchandise and supplier authorizations through structured global licensing programs. These mechanisms ensure strict quality control and facilitate anti-counterfeiting enforcement. Official mascots are identified as key commercial assets, requiring distinctive design for trademark registration across multiple classes to support extensive merchandising. By combining robust legal protection with consumer education, this framework effectively preserves brand value and secures legitimate commercial interests against unauthorized use.
items.ai_insight_zh: 大型体育赛事通过“商标注册+集中许可+主动执法”构建品牌护城河。吉祥物等核心资产需提前在多类商品上进行商标布局，以支撑商业化授权。权利人不仅依赖注册，更强调事后质量控制与反假冒监测，这对高价值IP的商业化运营具有参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies participating in the Olympic supply chain or developing event-related merchandise must strictly adhere to licensing agreements to avoid infringing on protected identifiers. Unauthorized use of brand elements poses significant legal and reputational risks. Practitioners should conduct thorough freedom-to-operate searches prior to product launch, paying particular attention to the registration status of dynamic IP assets such as official mascots. Maintaining compliance is not merely a risk mitigation strategy but a fundamental prerequisite for accessing high-value international sports markets. Stakeholders must remain vigilant regarding evolving IP rights associated with each specific Games edition to ensure lawful commercial exploitation.

items.title: Panasonic’s chief IP counsel on licensing philosophy, shifting landscape and UPC lessons
items.title_zh: 松下首席IP顾问谈许可哲学及UPC经验
items.title_en: Panasonic IP Counsel on SEP Licensing Shifts, UPC Lessons, and In-House Strategy
items.summary: Speaking at IAM's SEP Summit Asia, Andrew Yen also highlighted the more proactive role in-house counsel can play to drive licensing outcomes
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅包含标题与摘要片段。松下首席知识产权顾问Andrew Yen在IAM SEP亚洲峰会上发言，强调了内部法律顾问在推动许可结果方面可发挥更积极作用，并提及统一专利法院（UPC）的相关经验教训。
items.ai_summary_en: At the IAM SEP Summit Asia, Andrew Yen, Chief Intellectual Property Counsel at Panasonic, provided critical insights into evolving standard-essential patent licensing philosophies amidst a rapidly changing global landscape. He detailed practical operational experiences and strategic lessons derived from navigating litigation within the Unified Patent Court system. Yen emphasized that in-house legal teams must adopt a more proactive stance to effectively drive licensing outcomes rather than reacting passively. His commentary underscores the necessity for corporate counsel to adapt to Europe’s new judicial environment by integrating robust litigation strategies with commercial licensing goals, ensuring organizations remain resilient against shifting enforcement dynamics and maintain leverage in complex negotiations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式：原文未提供关于UPC具体经验、许可哲学细节或欧洲市场影响的实质内容。仅能确认演讲者身份及宏观主题，需等待全文发布以获取具体实务观点。
items.ai_insight_en: With SEP litigation becoming increasingly routine in Europe, companies should study the strategic approaches of major industry players like Panasonic to strengthen internal control over licensing negotiations. It is essential to conduct thorough research into Unified Patent Court case law to optimize risk management mechanisms for European patent portfolios. Organizations must develop flexible strategies for portfolio management and dispute resolution to avoid being placed in a defensive position during litigation. Proactive engagement with emerging judicial precedents allows firms to better anticipate enforcement trends and align their intellectual property strategies with the realities of the unified European market.

items.title: Deutsche Patentanmeldungen in Digitaltechnologien legen stark zu
items.title_zh: 德国数字技术专利申请量大幅增长
items.title_en: German Patent Filings in Digital Technologies Surge, Driven by AI and Computer Engineering
items.summary: Deutsche Patentanmeldungen in Digitaltechnologien legen stark zu - Aktuelle DPMA-Analyse: Künstliche Intelligenz und digitale Vernetzung – Deutsche Unternehmen holen bei Anmeldungen für ihren Heimatmarkt in wichtigen Bereichen auf. DPMA-Präsidentin: Es geht um Wohlstand und digitale Souveränität – Konsequenter Schutz geistigen Eigentums Basis für den wirtschaftlichen Erfolg - Die Analyse zu Digitaltechnologien im Detail - Die Technologiefelder im Detail:Computertechnik - Digitale Kommunikationst
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国专利商标局（DPMA）2026 年分析显示，2025 年德国本土数字关键技术领域专利申请公开量达 5051 件，同比增长 12.3%。其中计算机技术领域增长最快，增幅达 20.5%，主要涉及人工智能发明。德国在本土市场申请量排名全球第五，但增速仅次于中国，凸显其在数字主权和经济繁荣方面的战略投入。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) reports a significant increase in domestic patent applications within key digital technology sectors. In 2025, 5,051 applications with effect for the German market were published, representing a 12.3% year-on-year rise. German applicants rank fifth globally on their home market, trailing the US, China, South Korea, and Japan. However, Germany recorded the second-highest growth rate among these top five jurisdictions. The 'Computer Engineering' sector led this expansion with a 20.5% increase, largely attributed to inventions related to artificial intelligence. DPMA President Eva Schewior emphasized that robust intellectual property protection is fundamental to economic prosperity and digital sovereignty.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应关注德国数字技术专利布局加速趋势，特别是在 AI 和计算机技术领域。建议加强在德专利申请策略，评估潜在侵权风险，并利用当地知识产权制度保护自身创新成果，提升在欧洲市场的竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: This data highlights a strategic intensification of IP activity in Germany's digital sector, particularly in AI. For rights holders operating in Europe, understanding the competitive density in computer engineering is crucial for portfolio positioning. The DPMA's emphasis on digital sovereignty suggests continued institutional support for domestic innovation, potentially influencing future enforcement priorities and examination practices in high-growth tech fields.

Germany ranks fifth globally in domestic filings but achieved the second-highest growth rate among the top five nations.

items.title: Unpacking Dolby v Roku – the first HEVC SEP-based European preliminary injunction
items.title_zh: 解析杜比诉罗库案：欧洲首例基于HEVC SEP的临时禁令
items.title_en: Dolby v Roku: Analyzing the First HEVC SEP Preliminary Injunction in Europe
items.summary: The ruling by the Munich court gives insight into the requirements that govern the availability of interim relief in Germany in the SEP/FRAND context – particularly the standards for secured validity and infringement
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑法院在杜比诉罗库案中作出裁决，这是欧洲首例基于HEVC标准必要专利（SEP）的临时禁令。该判决揭示了在德国SEP/FRAND语境下获得临时救济的要求，特别是关于确保证据有效性和侵权认定的标准。此案例为理解德国法院在处理SEP纠纷时的司法倾向提供了重要参考，强调了权利人在申请禁令时需满足的高标准举证责任。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich court has issued a landmark ruling in Dolby v Roku, marking the first preliminary injunction granted in Europe based on High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) standard-essential patents (SEPs). This decision provides critical insights into the stringent requirements for obtaining interim relief within the German SEP and FRAND landscape. Specifically, it clarifies the high thresholds regarding secured validity and infringement that rights holders must satisfy. The judgment serves as a significant reference for understanding judicial tendencies in German SEP disputes, emphasizing the rigorous burden of proof placed on applicants seeking injunctive relief to prevent market disruption.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应警惕德国法院在SEP诉讼中倾向于快速颁发禁令的趋势。需提前评估HEVC等专利包的许可风险，建立完善的FRAND合规体系。在遭遇诉讼时，应重点从专利有效性入手进行抗辩，并密切关注德国法院对“ secured validity”标准的最新司法解释，以争取谈判主动权或避免业务中断。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Germany should remain vigilant regarding the courts' tendency to grant rapid preliminary injunctions in SEP disputes. It is essential to proactively assess licensing risks associated with patent portfolios such as HEVC and establish robust FRAND compliance frameworks. When facing litigation, parties should prioritize challenging patent validity as a primary defense strategy. Additionally, closely monitoring evolving judicial interpretations of 'secured validity' by German courts is crucial for maintaining negotiation leverage and mitigating the risk of severe business interruptions due to enforced injunctions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["慕尼黑法院裁定杜比胜诉，颁发欧洲首例HEVC SEP临时禁令", "明确德国SEP/FRAND案件中获取临时救济需满足严格的有效性标准", "该案确立了侵权认定与专利有效性证明在临时禁令中的关键作用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Munich court ruled in favor of Dolby, granting the first European preliminary injunction based on HEVC standard-essential patents.", "The decision establishes strict validity standards that must be met to secure interim relief in German SEP and FRAND litigation cases.", "Proof of infringement and demonstrated patent validity are confirmed as pivotal elements for successfully obtaining preliminary injunctions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕德国SEP诉讼禁令风险，提前布局HEVC专利许可合规", "强化专利无效抗辩策略，应对法院对有效性的严苛审查要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess early licensing exposure for HEVC and similar SEP packages to mitigate sudden injunction risks in Germany.", "Practice note: Strengthen invalidity defenses to meet the rigorous validity scrutiny required for opposing preliminary injunctions.", "What to monitor: Track German judicial developments regarding the specific evidentiary standards for secured validity in SEP cases."]
core_points_en: Munich court ruled in favor of Dolby, granting the first European preliminary injunction based on HEVC standard-essential patents.
The decision establishes strict validity standards that must be met to secure interim relief in German SEP and FRAND litigation cases.
Proof of infringement and demonstrated patent validity are confirmed as pivotal elements for successfully obtaining preliminary injunctions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess early licensing exposure for HEVC and similar SEP packages to mitigate sudden injunction risks in Germany.
Practice note: Strengthen invalidity defenses to meet the rigorous validity scrutiny required for opposing preliminary injunctions.
What to monitor: Track German judicial developments regarding the specific evidentiary standards for secured validity in SEP cases.

items.title: LD Hamburg, April 7, 2026, Final Order, UPC_CFI_2255/2025
items.title_zh: UPC汉堡法院就戴森诉追觅案作出管辖权与权利要求解释终令
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg Confirms Universal Jurisdiction Over EU Defendants and Proportional Cost Allocation
items.summary: Dyson Technology Limited has filed an application for provisional measures at the UPC Hamburg against multiple Dreame entities and distributors, involving European Patent 3,119,235. The case raises questions about jurisdiction under the Brussels I recast regulation and the interplay between UPC procedural rules and EU regulations in cross-border patent disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC汉堡地方法院在戴森诉追觅案中发布临时措施终令。法院确认UPC对住所位于成员国的被告拥有普遍管辖权，无需分析侵权地。同时明确欧盟代表不能作为英国行为的锚定被告。法院修正了此前对涉案专利权利要求的解释，采用更宽泛的功能性解读，并确立按比例而非按当事人数量分摊诉讼费用的规则。
items.ai_summary_en: Dyson Technology Limited sought provisional measures at the UPC Hamburg against multiple Dreame entities regarding European Patent 3,119,235. The panel consolidated the universal jurisdiction doctrine, ruling that the UPC acts as a court of a Member State under Article 71a of the Brussels I recast Regulation. Consequently, it possesses jurisdiction under Article 4 over any defendant domiciled in a Contracting Member State, eliminating the need to analyze specific places of infringement for those parties. The decision also clarified limits on the anchor defendant mechanism under Article 8, noting that an EU Representative cannot serve as an anchor for UK-related acts due to lack of foreseeability. Furthermore, the panel adopted a broader functional interpretation of claim terms and established that recoverable cost ceilings apply per instance, requiring proportional rather than per-party cost splitting.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案巩固了UPC的普遍管辖权原则，降低了跨境维权门槛。但限制了通过欧盟代表建立管辖的连接点。权利要求解释的动态调整显示UPC遵循上诉法院指导，倾向于功能性解读。费用分摊规则明确为按比例计算，影响多方被告案件的诉讼成本预期。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly impacts litigation strategy by confirming that domicile in any single UPCA state subjects a defendant to the UPC’s universal jurisdiction. Companies must reassess their European corporate structures, particularly the role of EU Representatives, to mitigate risks of being used as anchors for cross-border disputes. Additionally, the clarification on proportional cost splitting offers a strategic avenue for managing legal expenditures in multi-defendant cases. Parties should leverage this rule to optimize defense budgets, ensuring that cost recovery claims are calculated based on shared instances rather than individual party counts, thereby reducing the financial burden of joint litigation efforts across Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC被视为成员国法院，对住所位于成员国的被告拥有普遍管辖权，无需另行分析侵权地", "欧盟代表不能作为英国行为的锚定被告，因在英国设立代表非法律强制，缺乏可预见性", "法院修正权利要求解释，确认界定纵向槽的任何壁边界均构成“壁的端部”，采用功能性解读"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC exercises universal jurisdiction under Article 4 BR over defendants domiciled in any Contracting Member State without separate infringement analysis.", "An EU Representative cannot serve as an anchor defendant for UK acts under Article 8 BR because such jurisdiction lacks necessary foreseeability for the defendant.", "Cost ceilings apply to representation costs per procedural instance, mandating proportional cost splitting among parties rather than allocation per party."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC普遍管辖权确立，简化了对多国被告的起诉流程，无需逐一证明侵权地", "利用欧盟代表作为锚定被告的策略受限，需确保被告对受诉法院有合理预见性", "关注UPC在权利要求解释上与上诉法院的一致性，以及按比例分摊费用的实务影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Domicile in one UPCA state exposes entities to pan-European injunctions, necessitating a review of European entity layouts to limit jurisdictional reach.", "Practice note: Leverage the proportional cost-splitting rule in multi-defendant scenarios to optimize defense spending and reduce individual financial exposure.", "What to monitor: How courts define 'foreseeability' for anchor defendants in future cases, especially concerning non-EU market activities linked via local representatives."]
core_points_en: The UPC exercises universal jurisdiction under Article 4 BR over defendants domiciled in any Contracting Member State without separate infringement analysis.
An EU Representative cannot serve as an anchor defendant for UK acts under Article 8 BR because such jurisdiction lacks necessary foreseeability for the defendant.
Cost ceilings apply to representation costs per procedural instance, mandating proportional cost splitting among parties rather than allocation per party.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Domicile in one UPCA state exposes entities to pan-European injunctions, necessitating a review of European entity layouts to limit jurisdictional reach.
Practice note: Leverage the proportional cost-splitting rule in multi-defendant scenarios to optimize defense spending and reduce individual financial exposure.
What to monitor: How courts define 'foreseeability' for anchor defendants in future cases, especially concerning non-EU market activities linked via local representatives.

items.title: Regional Court Munich puts decision in ZTE vs Samsung on hold
items.title_zh: 慕尼黑地院中止 ZTE 诉三星案以待无效裁决
items.title_en: Munich Regional Court Suspends ZTE v Samsung SEP Infringement Case Pending Validity Ruling
items.summary: The Regional Court Munich has handed down another decision in the complex dispute between ZTE and Samsung over SEPs.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院在 ZTE 与三星的 4G/5G 标准必要专利纠纷中，因联邦专利法院对涉案专利有效性存疑，决定中止侵权诉讼程序。该案原被视为检验慕尼黑法院不同审判庭对 FRAND 抗辩新立场的风向标，现需等待 2027 年无效审理结果，导致案件显著延期。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Regional Court has suspended infringement proceedings in the ongoing standard-essential patent dispute between ZTE and Samsung. The decision follows a preliminary opinion from the Federal Patent Court expressing doubts regarding the validity of ZTE’s patent EP 2 375 827. Consequently, the infringement suit is stayed until the nullity action is resolved, with hearings scheduled for mid-2027. This case was closely watched as a potential indicator of how different chambers within the Munich court would align on recent FRAND defense approaches established by another chamber. The suspension highlights the continued impact of Germany’s bifurcated system, where validity challenges can significantly delay infringement judgments. Both parties are engaged in eight parallel actions across the Unified Patent Court and German courts concerning 4G and 5G technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，应充分利用德国双轨制特点，积极发起无效宣告以阻滞侵权判决。同时需警惕诉讼周期拉长带来的市场不确定性，提前规划许可谈判或备选技术方案。
items.ai_insight_en: In Germany’s bifurcated patent system, initiating parallel nullity proceedings remains a critical strategy for defendants facing SEP infringement claims. By challenging validity early, rights holders or alleged infringers can secure stays that prevent immediate injunctions, thereby buying time for licensing negotiations or technical workarounds. However, stakeholders must anticipate prolonged uncertainty and increased legal costs associated with extended timelines. Effective management requires robust financial planning and proactive engagement in FRAND discussions during these delays. Monitoring how different judicial chambers interpret FRAND obligations alongside validity challenges will be essential for predicting case trajectories and managing portfolio risks effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国双轨制下，侵权诉讼因专利有效性存疑被慕尼黑法院中止", "本案涉及八起平行诉讼，原定澄清 FRAND 审理新思路的计划落空", "无效程序听证定于明年六月，最终裁决预计推迟至 2027 年中旬"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Munich Regional Court suspends infringement proceedings due to Federal Patent Court doubts about patent validity.", "The stay delays resolution until mid-2027, disrupting expectations for clarifying FRAND defense consistency.", "This development underscores the strategic leverage of nullity actions within Germany's bifurcated litigation framework."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用无效程序中止侵权诉讼，是应对德国 SEP 禁令的关键策略", "诉讼延期虽缓解即时压力，但要求企业做好长期合规与资金准备"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leveraging validity challenges to stay infringement suits mitigates immediate injunction risks in SEP disputes.", "Practice note: Prepare for extended litigation cycles and budget accordingly while using delays to negotiate licenses.", "What to monitor: Judicial alignment on FRAND defenses across different Munich chambers and Federal Patent Court trends."]
core_points_en: Munich Regional Court suspends infringement proceedings due to Federal Patent Court doubts about patent validity.
The stay delays resolution until mid-2027, disrupting expectations for clarifying FRAND defense consistency.
This development underscores the strategic leverage of nullity actions within Germany's bifurcated litigation framework.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leveraging validity challenges to stay infringement suits mitigates immediate injunction risks in SEP disputes.
Practice note: Prepare for extended litigation cycles and budget accordingly while using delays to negotiate licenses.
What to monitor: Judicial alignment on FRAND defenses across different Munich chambers and Federal Patent Court trends.

items.title: EU General Court Confirms IMPRESS Is A Laudatory Trademark
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院确认IMPRESS为褒义商标
items.title_en: EU General Court Confirms IMPRESS Is a Laudatory Trademark Lacking Distinctiveness
items.summary: On 11 March 2026, the EU General Court ruled in case T326/25, concerning the assessment of the laudatory nature of the IMPRESS word trademark application filed...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月11日，欧盟普通法院在T-326/25号案件中维持对“IMPRESS”商标的驳回决定。该商标由美国Kiss Nail Products公司申请，涵盖第3类和第8类美容美甲产品。法院认定该词对相关公众而言仅是对产品质量或效果的褒义描述，缺乏区分商品来源的显著性，不符合欧盟商标条例注册要求。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 March 2026, the EU General Court upheld the refusal of the word mark IMPRESS in case T-326/25. The application, filed by Kiss Nail Products, Inc., covered cosmetics and manicure tools in Classes 3 and 8. The Court confirmed that IMPRESS is perceived by the relevant public as a laudatory message promising strong or favorable results regarding appearance improvement, rather than as an indicator of commercial origin. Consequently, the mark lacks the distinctive character required under Article 7(1)(b) of the EU Trademark Regulation. The judgment reinforces that purely promotional signs are unregistrable unless they possess sufficient originality or semantic depth to transcend ordinary advertising language.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案重申了褒义性词汇在欧盟商标注册中的高门槛。即便非纯粹广告语，若无法证明其具有超越常规描述的原创性或需消费者额外解读，仍难获注册。企业应避免使用直接暗示质量或效果的通用词汇作为主商标，或通过长期使用获取后天显著性证据。
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants seeking EU trademark protection should avoid terms that directly describe product effects or quality, as these face high rejection risks based on absolute grounds. When utilizing laudatory language, brands must ensure the sign possesses distinct creative elements or metaphors that elevate it above generic advertising slogans. Conducting thorough distinctiveness searches and assessments prior to filing is essential to mitigate refusal risks. This approach helps secure stronger legal protection and improves overall registration success rates within the European Union market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院维持EUIPO驳回决定，认定IMPRESS在第3、8类商品上缺乏显著性", "法院指出该词被公众视为对美容效果的褒义承诺，而非商业来源标识", "申请人主张虽具褒义但仍存弱显著性，但法院未予采纳，强调需具备原创性或共鸣"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court determined that IMPRESS functions as a laudatory term in the beauty sector, failing to distinguish the commercial origin of the specified goods.", "Purely promotional trademarks remain unregistrable unless they demonstrate unique creativity or interpretive depth beyond standard advertising phrases.", "Distinctiveness is a substantive registration requirement assessed strictly against the perception of the relevant public and the specific goods involved."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["褒义性商标在欧盟面临严格的显著性审查，需证明其非普通广告信息", "单纯暗示产品效果（如“印象好”）的词汇难以直接注册，除非具备独特语境", "后续应关注法院对“最小解读努力”标准在具体案件中的适用尺度变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid direct descriptive claims for cosmetic effects to prevent refusals based on lack of distinctiveness at the EUIPO.", "Practice note: Enhance trademark design with creative or metaphorical elements to ensure the sign transcends ordinary advertising language.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of laudatory marks and the threshold for distinctiveness in Class 3 and Class 8 applications."]
core_points_en: The Court determined that IMPRESS functions as a laudatory term in the beauty sector, failing to distinguish the commercial origin of the specified goods.
Purely promotional trademarks remain unregistrable unless they demonstrate unique creativity or interpretive depth beyond standard advertising phrases.
Distinctiveness is a substantive registration requirement assessed strictly against the perception of the relevant public and the specific goods involved.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid direct descriptive claims for cosmetic effects to prevent refusals based on lack of distinctiveness at the EUIPO.
Practice note: Enhance trademark design with creative or metaphorical elements to ensure the sign transcends ordinary advertising language.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of laudatory marks and the threshold for distinctiveness in Class 3 and Class 8 applications.

items.title: Brussels LD orders production of evidence and samples in UPC soft tissue implant case
items.title_zh: UPC布鲁塞尔法院责令在软组织植入物案中提交证据及样品
items.title_en: Brussels UPC Division Orders Evidence Production in Soft Tissue Implant Dispute
items.summary: Applications in the UPC for Requests to Produce Evidence under r.190 RoP are rare beasts. An Order following one such application was made on 14 April 2026 by the Brussels LD (full panel) in the case of Establishment Labs S.A v GC Aesthetics (UPC_CFI_1357/2025 / UPC_CFI_629/2026).
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月14日，统一专利法院（UPC）布鲁塞尔地方法院全席庭在Establishment Labs诉GC Aesthetics案中，依据程序规则第190条发布命令，责令被告提交证据和样品。被告以原告产品作为先用权抗辩并申请证据开示，法院认为被告已满足初步举证要求，且为推进程序效率，驳回了原告关于申请过早的主张，但指出若原告配合承认相关事实，部分证据可免于提交。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Brussels Local Division, sitting as a full panel, issued an order on 14 April 2026 granting a rare request for evidence production under Rule 190 of the Rules of Procedure. In Establishment Labs S.A v GC Aesthetics, the defendant challenged patent validity by alleging prior use based on the plaintiff’s own breast implant product. The court ordered the plaintiff to produce samples and technical documents. It emphasized that parties cannot merely deny allegations when controlling relevant evidence. The decision prioritizes procedural efficiency, allowing evidence orders before the written procedure concludes if prima facie grounds exist, unless the holder provides admissions regarding the alleged prior use.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了UPC在证据开示方面的程序工具运用，强调程序效率与当事人协作。与EPO或德国国内诉讼相比，UPC更倾向于在早期阶段解决证据争议。实务中需关注法院对“初步举证”标准的把握，以及通过自认简化证据提交的策略空间，这影响了UPC诉讼中的证据准备节奏。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling highlights the UPC’s robust powers regarding evidence preservation and production, contrasting with more restrictive practices in some national jurisdictions or the EPO. Litigants should proactively assess risks where their own products might constitute prior art. Early evaluation allows for strategic admissions to limit disclosure burdens or prepares defenses against compulsory production. Understanding these procedural tools is essential for managing litigation strategy effectively within the new European patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC布鲁塞尔地方法院依据程序规则第190条，责令当事人提交涉案产品及技术监管文件作为…", "法院强调持有相关证据的一方不得消极否认指控，需配合程序效率推进，驳回了申请过早的抗辩", "法院采取务实态度，指示若当事人就先用权指控提供相关自认，则可免除部分证据的提交义务"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Brussels granted a rare Rule 190 evidence production order requiring sample and document disclosure.", "Parties controlling relevant evidence must cooperate and cannot rely solely on passive denial of allegations.", "Courts may order interim evidence production to ensure procedural efficiency without waiting for final written submissions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC法院在证据开示申请中重视程序效率，倾向于在书面程序结束前处理关键证据争议", "持有证据方若消极应对可能面临不利推定，法院鼓励通过自认等方式简化证据提交流程", "需持续观察UPC各地方法院在适用程序规则第190条时的具体标准差异及后续上诉动向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess exposure to compulsory evidence production when own products are cited as prior art.", "Practice note: Consider strategic admissions to limit scope of mandatory document and sample production.", "What to monitor: How UPC divisions balance procedural efficiency with proportionality in future evidence requests."]
core_points_en: UPC Brussels granted a rare Rule 190 evidence production order requiring sample and document disclosure.
Parties controlling relevant evidence must cooperate and cannot rely solely on passive denial of allegations.
Courts may order interim evidence production to ensure procedural efficiency without waiting for final written submissions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess exposure to compulsory evidence production when own products are cited as prior art.
Practice note: Consider strategic admissions to limit scope of mandatory document and sample production.
What to monitor: How UPC divisions balance procedural efficiency with proportionality in future evidence requests.

items.title: Antitrust fault lines: will licensing negotiation groups reshape SEP licensing?
items.title_zh: 反垄断分歧：许可谈判组会重塑SEP许可吗？
items.title_en: Antitrust Fault Lines: Will Licensing Negotiation Groups Reshape SEP Licensing?
items.summary: LNGs have received support from European competition officials, however, US scepticism – now crystallising in a DOJ investigation – looks set to hinder global adoption
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨许可谈判组（LNGs）在标准必要专利（SEP）许可中的角色。尽管欧盟竞争官员支持LNGs以平衡谈判地位，但美国司法部正对此展开调查，表现出强烈怀疑。这种跨大西洋的监管分歧可能阻碍LNGs在全球范围内的广泛采用，进而影响SEP许可市场的格局与效率。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the emerging role of licensing negotiation groups (LNGs) within the standard essential patent (SEP) licensing ecosystem. While European competition officials have expressed support for LNGs as a mechanism to balance negotiating power between licensors and implementers, significant skepticism persists across the Atlantic. The US Department of Justice has launched an investigation into these collective bargaining structures, signaling strong regulatory caution. This growing transatlantic divergence in antitrust enforcement creates substantial uncertainty for market participants. Consequently, the widespread global adoption of LNGs may be hindered, potentially fragmenting the international SEP licensing landscape. The resulting regulatory inconsistency threatens to impact both the efficiency of licensing negotiations and the overall stability of the global intellectual property market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国SEP实施企业应密切关注美欧监管差异。利用欧盟对LNGs的支持，可探索联合谈判以降低许可成本；同时需警惕美国司法部的反垄断风险，避免在美业务因参与此类组织而面临调查或处罚，制定差异化合规策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in SEP licensing should closely monitor the evolving regulatory divergence between the EU and US jurisdictions. While the EU framework offers potential pathways for collective negotiation to optimize licensing costs, companies must carefully assess antitrust risks associated with such arrangements under US law. Engaging in licensing negotiation groups could expose entities to investigations or penalties if deemed anti-competitive by US authorities. Therefore, rights holders and implementers alike should develop differentiated compliance strategies that account for these conflicting legal standards. Proactive risk assessment is essential to navigate the complex intersection of competition policy and intellectual property rights in cross-border transactions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟竞争官员支持许可谈判组以改善SEP许可环境", "美国司法部启动调查，对许可谈判组持怀疑态度", "美欧监管分歧可能阻碍许可谈判组的全球普及与应用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["European competition authorities support licensing negotiation groups to improve fairness and balance within the SEP licensing environment.", "The US Department of Justice has initiated an investigation into licensing negotiation groups, reflecting deep skepticism regarding their antitrust implications.", "Divergent regulatory approaches between the EU and US may obstruct the global adoption of licensing negotiation groups and affect market efficiency."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟对LNGs的支持，探索联合谈判降低许可成本", "警惕美国司法部调查风险，避免在美业务受反垄断牵连"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate opportunities for cost-efficient collective licensing under supportive EU frameworks while assessing global operational impacts.", "Practice note: Implement robust antitrust compliance measures to mitigate risks of DOJ investigations when participating in joint negotiation structures.", "What to monitor: Track developments in the US Department of Justice inquiry and corresponding EU policy guidance on licensing negotiation groups."]
core_points_en: European competition authorities support licensing negotiation groups to improve fairness and balance within the SEP licensing environment.
The US Department of Justice has initiated an investigation into licensing negotiation groups, reflecting deep skepticism regarding their antitrust implications.
Divergent regulatory approaches between the EU and US may obstruct the global adoption of licensing negotiation groups and affect market efficiency.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate opportunities for cost-efficient collective licensing under supportive EU frameworks while assessing global operational impacts.
Practice note: Implement robust antitrust compliance measures to mitigate risks of DOJ investigations when participating in joint negotiation structures.
What to monitor: Track developments in the US Department of Justice inquiry and corresponding EU policy guidance on licensing negotiation groups.

items.title: HEVC licensing chaos: who holds the leverage and what it costs
items.title_zh: HEVC许可乱象：谁掌握杠杆及成本几何
items.title_en: HEVC Licensing Chaos: Assessing Leverage, Hidden SEPs, and Rising Royalty Stacks
items.summary: New GreyB research shows a fractured patent landscape, partial pool coverage and hidden SEPs are driving litigation risk and pushing the true HEVC royalty stack higher than many implementers expect
items.ai_summary_zh: GreyB最新研究揭示HEVC专利格局破碎，部分专利池覆盖不全且存在隐藏的标准必要专利（SEP）。这导致诉讼风险显著增加，并推高了实际版税堆栈，远超许多实施者的预期。文章分析了当前许可环境中的权力动态，指出由于缺乏透明度和统一的许可机制，实施者面临更高的法律不确定性和财务负担，需警惕潜在的侵权指控与高额赔偿风险。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent research by GreyB highlights a fragmented patent landscape for High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC), characterized by incomplete coverage from existing patent pools and the presence of undisclosed standard-essential patents (SEPs). This structural opacity significantly elevates litigation risks for implementers. Consequently, the actual royalty stack is proving substantially higher than many market participants anticipated. The analysis underscores how the lack of transparency and unified licensing mechanisms creates profound legal uncertainty and financial burdens. Rights holders and implementers alike face increased exposure to infringement claims and potential damages, necessitating a reevaluation of current licensing strategies to navigate this complex and costly environment effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国科技企业应重新评估HEVC许可策略，不仅依赖主流专利池，还需通过FTO分析排查隐藏SEP风险。建议建立动态监控机制，预留应对突发诉讼的预算，并在谈判中争取更透明的费率结构，以规避因信息不对称导致的超额支付或禁令风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should adopt a holistic approach to HEVC compliance, moving beyond reliance on major patent pools to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses that identify hidden SEP exposures. Establishing dynamic monitoring systems and maintaining contingency budgets are critical to managing unexpected litigation shocks. Furthermore, negotiating for transparent rate structures can help mitigate information asymmetry, reducing the risk of excessive payments or injunctive relief. Proactive strategy adjustment is essential to safeguard against the escalating financial and legal challenges inherent in the current licensing ecosystem.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["HEVC专利景观破碎，部分专利池覆盖不足引发许可漏洞", "隐藏标准必要专利加剧诉讼风险，推高实际版税成本", "实施者面临高于预期的许可费用与法律不确定性挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A fractured HEVC patent landscape and partial pool coverage create significant licensing gaps for implementers.", "Undisclosed standard-essential patents intensify litigation risks and drive up the effective royalty burden.", "Implementers confront higher-than-expected licensing costs alongside substantial legal uncertainties."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["超越单一专利池，全面排查隐藏SEP以规避隐性侵权风险", "建立动态监控与预算储备，应对许可成本上升与诉讼冲击"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct comprehensive FTO analyses beyond single patent pools to uncover hidden SEP liabilities.", "Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring frameworks and reserve budgets to absorb sudden litigation costs.", "What to monitor: Negotiate for transparent fee structures to prevent overpayment and avoid injunction risks."]
core_points_en: A fractured HEVC patent landscape and partial pool coverage create significant licensing gaps for implementers.
Undisclosed standard-essential patents intensify litigation risks and drive up the effective royalty burden.
Implementers confront higher-than-expected licensing costs alongside substantial legal uncertainties.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct comprehensive FTO analyses beyond single patent pools to uncover hidden SEP liabilities.
Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring frameworks and reserve budgets to absorb sudden litigation costs.
What to monitor: Negotiate for transparent fee structures to prevent overpayment and avoid injunction risks.

items.title: Rapport Douanebalans 2025: Wat Houders Van Intellectuele Eigendomsrechten Moeten Onthouden
items.title_zh: 2025法国海关平衡报告：IP权利人需知要点
items.title_en: Rapport Douanebalans 2025: Wat Houders Van Intellectuele Eigendomsrechten Moeten Onthouden
items.summary: Voor houders van intellectuele eigendomsrechten maken deze cijfers duidelijk dat merkbescherming niet langer uitsluitend reactief kan blijven.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国海关发布2025年平衡报告，显示假冒产品仍呈大规模、跨行业趋势。2025年查获超2000万件假货，近三年累计超6000万件。1490名权利人在法提出海关干预申请，法国在欧盟排名第二。电商小包裹成主要渠道，89%来自中国，Shein等平台占比较高。玩具体育类占比65%，凸显主动战略协作海关的重要性。
items.ai_insight_zh: 报告揭示假货流通向碎片化、电商化转型，传统大宗集装箱运输减少。权利人需从被动反应转向与海关建立 proactive 战略协作，以应对高频次、小批量的侵权风险，特别是在玩具与体育用品等高仿品类领域。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年法国海关查获超2000万件假冒产品，近三年累计拦截量突破6000万件", "名知识产权权利人在法提交海关干预申请，法国位列欧盟第二，仅次于德国", "电商小包裹成主流，89%源自中国，Shein、Temu和AliExpress占据近四分…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["假货物流形态转变：从大宗集装箱转向低于150欧元的电商小包裹，监管难度增加", "平台集中度风险：少数大型跨境电商平台成为主要货源通道，需关注特定平台合规动态", "执法协作必要性：单纯依靠事后维权不足，需强化与法国及欧盟各国海关的前端数据共享与干预机制"]

items.title: Candidate Support Programme expands access to EPAC certification in Europe
items.title_zh: EPO启动候选人支持计划，扩大欧洲专利行政认证准入
items.title_en: EPO Launches First Candidate Support Programme Cycle for EPAC Certification
items.summary: Candidate Support Programme expands access to EPAC certification in Europe
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）联合各国专利局启动首届“候选人支持计划”（CSP），旨在提升欧洲专利行政认证（EPAC）的可及性。该计划为入选者提供培训、辅导及考试费用资助。2026年首周期吸引16个成员国参与，收到62份申请，其中女性占比73%。EPAC采用模块化考试形式，涵盖欧洲及国际专利程序管理技能，旨在构建更具包容性的专利专业队伍，促进性别与地域平衡。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has initiated the first cycle of its Candidate Support Programme (CSP) dedicated to the European Patent Administration Certification (EPAC). Implemented with national patent offices, the initiative aims to foster a more inclusive and geographically balanced patent profession. Sixteen member states participated, nominating candidates who receive comprehensive support including training, tutoring, exam fees, and bursaries. The programme attracted 62 applications, with women comprising 73% of applicants. Selected candidates are now undergoing preparation for the modular EPAC examinations scheduled for November 2026, reflecting growing professional interest in standardized certification for patent administration.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注EPAC认证在提升专利流程管理专业性方面的价值。利用CSP等支持机制培养具备国际视野的专利行政人才，有助于优化对欧专利布局与维护效率，降低合规风险，并增强在欧洲创新生态系统中的专业影响力。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the EPO's strategic push toward standardizing professional competencies in patent administration across Europe. For rights holders and corporate IP teams, the expansion of certified professionals may enhance the consistency and quality of procedural management within the European patent system. The high participation rate among women and diverse sectors suggests a shift towards broader accessibility in professional qualifications, potentially influencing hiring and training standards in the European IP sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO启动首届CSP计划，为EPAC候选人提供全方位培训与资金支持", "首周期获16个成员国响应，女性申请人占比高达73%，体现多样性", "EPAC实行模块化考试，验证专利行政人员处理欧专程序的专业能力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO launched the first CSP cycle for EPAC, offering selected candidates full financial and educational support from sixteen participating member states.", "Sixty-two applications were received, with women representing 73% of applicants across patent attorney offices, industry, and academia.", "Modular EPAC examinations are scheduled for 5 November 2026, validating skills required to manage European and international patent procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EPAC认证价值，提升对欧专利流程管理的专业性与合规性", "利用支持计划培养国际化专利行政人才，优化欧洲专利布局维护"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Standardized EPAC certification may improve the reliability of patent administration services, benefiting companies managing large European portfolios.", "Practice note: Monitor the uptake of EPAC-certified professionals in service providers to assess potential shifts in market expectations for procedural expertise.", "What to monitor: Future CSP cycles and nomination criteria by national offices to track evolving diversity and inclusion metrics in the European patent profession."]
core_points_en: The EPO launched the first CSP cycle for EPAC, offering selected candidates full financial and educational support from sixteen participating member states.
Sixty-two applications were received, with women representing 73% of applicants across patent attorney offices, industry, and academia.
Modular EPAC examinations are scheduled for 5 November 2026, validating skills required to manage European and international patent procedures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Standardized EPAC certification may improve the reliability of patent administration services, benefiting companies managing large European portfolios.
Practice note: Monitor the uptake of EPAC-certified professionals in service providers to assess potential shifts in market expectations for procedural expertise.
What to monitor: Future CSP cycles and nomination criteria by national offices to track evolving diversity and inclusion metrics in the European patent profession.

items.title: EPO introduces automated live captions in oral proceedings by VICO
items.title_zh: 欧专局在视频口审中引入自动实时字幕
items.title_en: EPO Introduces Automated Live Captions for Opposition Oral Proceedings via VICO
items.summary: Accessibility and inclusion enhanced by option for opposition hearings via Zoom
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局在异议程序的视频会议口审中引入自动实时字幕功能，以支持听障人士平等参与。该功能基于Zoom平台，需当事人提前申请，无需提供医疗证明。字幕由语音识别生成，可显示为审理语言或自动翻译。欧专局不保证准确性，且禁止保存或分享字幕，以确保程序机密性与效率。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office now offers automated real-time captions for oral proceedings in opposition cases conducted via video conference. This accessibility feature, available on Zoom, supports parties with hearing impairments by providing live subtitles generated through speech recognition. Requests must be submitted in advance, ideally before the final date under Rule 116 EPC, without requiring medical evidence. Participants can view captions in the proceeding’s language or have them automatically translated into other EPO official languages. The EPO emphasizes that captions are machine-generated, not guaranteed for accuracy, and strictly confidential. They must not be saved or shared, and the division may deactivate them if they disrupt procedural efficiency.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此举措体现欧专局数字化与包容性战略，优化听障当事人参与体验。实务中需注意申请时限与技术限制，字幕仅供参考而非官方记录。当事人应规范使用，避免违规保存导致程序风险，关注后续技术稳定性及可能的规则细化。
items.ai_insight_en: This update enhances procedural accessibility for rights holders with hearing impairments during EPO opposition hearings. While beneficial for inclusion, practitioners must treat captions as auxiliary aids rather than official records. Strict adherence to confidentiality rules and advance request timelines is essential to avoid procedural complications or technical denials.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议程序视频口审可申请自动实时字幕，需在最终期限前提前提出请求", "字幕基于语音识别技术生成，支持显示原语言或自动翻译为欧专局官方语言", "欧专局不保证字幕准确性，禁止保存或向第三方分享，违者可能影响程序进行"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Automated real-time captions are available on request for opposition oral proceedings held via Zoom video conference.", "Requests must be filed in advance, ideally before the Rule 116 EPC deadline, without need for medical proof.", "Captions are confidential, non-binding, and must not be recorded or shared with third parties."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["体现欧专局提升程序无障碍性的政策导向，优化特殊需求当事人体验", "实务需严格遵守申请时限，理解字幕非官方记录且不得外传的程序纪律", "观察自动翻译技术在多语言口审中的实际表现及潜在的技术局限性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Improves equitable access for participants with hearing impairments during remote opposition oral proceedings.", "Practice note: Submit caption requests well before the Rule 116 EPC deadline; do not rely on captions for legal accuracy.", "What to monitor: Technical performance of auto-translation features and any future EPO guidance on caption usage protocols."]
core_points_en: Automated real-time captions are available on request for opposition oral proceedings held via Zoom video conference.
Requests must be filed in advance, ideally before the Rule 116 EPC deadline, without need for medical proof.
Captions are confidential, non-binding, and must not be recorded or shared with third parties.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Improves equitable access for participants with hearing impairments during remote opposition oral proceedings.
Practice note: Submit caption requests well before the Rule 116 EPC deadline; do not rely on captions for legal accuracy.
What to monitor: Technical performance of auto-translation features and any future EPO guidance on caption usage protocols.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 7, 2026, Order, UPC_CoA_21/2026
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭关于诉讼费用担保的裁定
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Security for Costs Under Article 69(4) UPCA Is Strictly One-Way
items.summary: An “applicant” is defined as the person who initiates legal proceedings by filing an application. This one-directional mechanism ensures that the party...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Suinno诉微软案中裁定，依据《统一专利法院协议》第69(4)条，诉讼费用担保仅可针对启动程序的申请人下令，旨在保护被动卷入诉讼的一方。即使双方均提起上诉，各方仅能就对方的上诉请求担保。主诉讼撤回不影响撤销反请求及附属费用担保请求的独立性。
items.ai_summary_en: In the Suinno v. Microsoft case, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal issued an order clarifying that security for costs under Article 69(4) of the UPC Agreement operates as a strictly one-directional mechanism. The ruling establishes that only the party involuntarily drawn into proceedings may request such security from the applicant initiating the action. This principle protects respondents from unrecoverable legal expenses. Even if the original first-instance claimant becomes the respondent in an appeal, they cannot seek security unless specific conditions regarding manifest errors are met. In cases involving cross-appeals, each party’s ability to request security is limited to the other party’s specific appeal. Furthermore, the court confirmed that withdrawing the main infringement action does not invalidate pending counterclaims for revocation or associated cost security requests, preserving their autonomous procedural status.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定明确了UPC程序中费用担保的单向性原则，强调对被动应诉方的保护。实务中需关注“申请人”定义在交叉上诉中的具体适用，以及主从诉讼请求分离对成本风险控制的影响，特别是当被告提起撤销反请求时的策略考量。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners must carefully assess procedural posture to determine eligibility for security for costs, as the mechanism is designed to protect passive parties rather than active litigants. When facing infringement claims, defendants filing counterclaims for revocation should recognize that these actions maintain independent legal existence. Consequently, even if the claimant withdraws the primary suit, the defendant can continue pursuing the counterclaim and potentially secure cost protections. This autonomy allows for strategic management of litigation exposure and cost recovery risks. Legal teams should monitor how courts define 'applicant' status in complex multi-party scenarios to effectively leverage these procedural safeguards during both first instance and appellate stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["费用担保仅针对启动程序的申请人，不得有利于申请人，以保护被动应诉方", "上诉程序中仅被上诉方可请求费用担保；若双方上诉，仅能就对方上诉部分请求", "主侵权诉讼终结或撤回，不导致 pending 的撤销反请求及相关费用担保请求无效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Security for costs under Article 69(4) UPCA applies exclusively against the applicant initiating proceedings and never in their favor.", "In appeals, only the respondent may request security; cross-appeals restrict each party to seeking costs solely for the opponent's appeal.", "Withdrawal of the main infringement action does not render pending revocation counterclaims or related cost security requests inadmissible."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确费用担保机制旨在保护非自愿卷入诉讼方，限制申请人利用担保施压", "需监控交叉上诉情形下，双方各自仅能针对对方上诉部分请求担保的规则适用", "注意主诉讼撤回后，撤销反请求及其附属费用担保请求仍具独立存续效力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Accurately defining procedural status is critical to avoiding unexpected cost liabilities and securing financial protections.", "Practice note: Leverage the autonomous nature of revocation counterclaims to maintain cost security arguments after main action withdrawals.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'applicant' definitions in cross-appeal scenarios to refine litigation cost strategies."]
core_points_en: Security for costs under Article 69(4) UPCA applies exclusively against the applicant initiating proceedings and never in their favor.
In appeals, only the respondent may request security; cross-appeals restrict each party to seeking costs solely for the opponent's appeal.
Withdrawal of the main infringement action does not render pending revocation counterclaims or related cost security requests inadmissible.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Accurately defining procedural status is critical to avoiding unexpected cost liabilities and securing financial protections.
Practice note: Leverage the autonomous nature of revocation counterclaims to maintain cost security arguments after main action withdrawals.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'applicant' definitions in cross-appeal scenarios to refine litigation cost strategies.

items.title: CJEU delivers pastiche-style judgment on … pastiche
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院就Pelham II案中的“模仿”概念作出判决
items.title_en: CJEU Grand Chamber Clarifies Pastiche Exception Under InfoSoc Directive in Pelham II
items.summary: Moses PelhamToday, the Grand Chamber of the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) issued its judgment in Pelham II, C-590/23, concerning the notion of pastiche in Article 5(3)(k) of the InfoSoc Directive.To understand the genesis of the referral, reference must be made to the Opinions of two Advocates General (AGs): AG Cruz Villalón in Deckmyn and AG Szpunar in Pelham I. The former
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院大法庭在Pelham II案（C-590/23）中作出判决，阐释了《信息社会指令》第5(3)(k)条中“模仿”（pastiche）的概念。该案背景需参考此前两位总法律顾问在Deckmyn案和Pelham I案中的意见。
items.ai_summary_en: The Grand Chamber of the Court of Justice of the European Union issued its judgment in Pelham II, Case C-590/23, addressing the scope of the pastiche exception under Article 5(3)(k) of the InfoSoc Directive. This referral originated from German courts seeking to distinguish between pastiche and parody within copyright law. The judgment builds upon previous legal opinions by Advocates General Cruz Villalón in Deckmyn and Szpunar in Pelham I. By establishing new judicial standards, the Court provides binding guidance for member states on handling disputes involving sampling and secondary creations. This decision significantly impacts how national courts interpret copyright exceptions, ensuring a more uniform application of EU copyright principles across jurisdictions regarding transformative uses of protected works.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注欧盟法院对“模仿”例外的具体界定标准，及其与先前总法律顾问意见的异同。该判决将影响成员国对版权例外情形的司法适用，建议持续追踪判决全文以明确法律边界。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders engaged in music sampling, short-form video creation, or game mod development within the EU must reassess the risks associated with invoking the pastiche defense. It is critical to strictly differentiate between homage and infringement, avoiding direct replication of core expressive elements. Legal teams should closely monitor subsequent case law developments in key markets like Germany to ensure operational compliance. Understanding these clarified boundaries helps mitigate litigation risks and supports the development of robust compliance strategies for digital content products operating under harmonized EU copyright rules.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院大法庭发布Pelham II案判决，案件编号为C-590/23", "判决核心在于解释《信息社会指令》中关于“模仿”的版权例外条款"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court defines the specific scope and conditions for applying the pastiche exception within EU copyright law frameworks.", "The judgment clarifies the conceptual differences between pastiche and parody to unify judicial standards across member states.", "National courts must follow this new interpretive path when adjudicating cases involving music sampling and derivative works."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟法院对版权指令中“模仿”例外的最终法律定义", "观察该判决如何协调或区分此前总法律顾问在相关案件中的意见", "后续需监测成员国法院如何依据此判决处理类似版权侵权纠纷"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate whether secondary content qualifies for the pastiche exception to mitigate infringement litigation risks.", "Practice note: Adjust compliance strategies for digital content products based on the newly unified EU judicial standards.", "What to monitor: Track post-judgment interpretations in national courts, particularly in Germany, regarding sampling disputes."]
core_points_en: The Court defines the specific scope and conditions for applying the pastiche exception within EU copyright law frameworks.
The judgment clarifies the conceptual differences between pastiche and parody to unify judicial standards across member states.
National courts must follow this new interpretive path when adjudicating cases involving music sampling and derivative works.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate whether secondary content qualifies for the pastiche exception to mitigate infringement litigation risks.
Practice note: Adjust compliance strategies for digital content products based on the newly unified EU judicial standards.
What to monitor: Track post-judgment interpretations in national courts, particularly in Germany, regarding sampling disputes.

items.title: Fisher & Paykel and WBH successful in revoking Flexicare’s patent in Milan
items.title_zh: 费雪派克与WBH在米兰成功撤销Flexicare专利
items.title_en: Fisher & Paykel and WBH Successfully Revoke Flexicare Patent in Milan Court
items.summary: At the centre of the revocation claim filed by Fisher & Paykel is Flexicare’s EP 4 185 356 relating to a nasal cannula designed to improve patient comfort and usability during high-flow oxygen therapy. The nasal cannula comprises a manifold, non-sealing nasal prongs and a connector for a gas tube. Fisher & Paykel claims the […] The post Fisher & Paykel and WBH successful in revoking Flexicare’s patent in Milan appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 费雪派克（Fisher & Paykel）和WBH在意大利米兰成功撤销了Flexicare持有的欧洲专利EP 4 185 356。该专利涉及一种用于高流量氧疗的鼻导管，旨在提高患者舒适度和可用性，包含歧管、非密封鼻叉和气体管连接器。
items.ai_summary_en: Fisher & Paykel, represented by WBH, successfully initiated revocation proceedings in Milan against Flexicare’s European patent EP 4 185 356. The patent concerns a nasal cannula designed to enhance patient comfort and usability during high-flow oxygen therapy, featuring a manifold, non-sealing nasal prongs, and a gas tube connector. The Milan court upheld the plaintiff's invalidity arguments, ruling that the technical solution lacked patentability or met other grounds for invalidation. This decision effectively eliminates potential infringement risks for related products, marking a significant victory for Fisher & Paykel in clearing market barriers and securing freedom to operate within the Italian jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注米兰法院在该医疗器械领域专利有效性审查中的具体理由。鉴于正文信息有限，暂无法评估该判决对同类专利稳定性的广泛影响或后续上诉情况，建议持续追踪案件法律文书细节。
items.ai_insight_en: Medical device manufacturers exporting to Europe should prioritize thorough patent stability analyses under target national jurisdictions. When facing patents that hinder market access, companies are advised to actively utilize local invalidation procedures as a strategic countermeasure. Furthermore, it is crucial to monitor variations in judicial scrutiny standards across different member states regarding similar technical solutions. Proactively developing tailored response strategies based on these regional legal nuances can significantly mitigate litigation risks and ensure smoother market entry and operational continuity for healthcare technology providers.

items.title: UPC issues Spanish injunction in latest long-arm jurisdiction decision
items.title_zh: UPC在最新长臂管辖案中发布西班牙禁令
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg Division Issues Spanish Injunction, Clarifying Long-Arm Jurisdiction Boundaries in Dyson Case
items.summary: Hamburg Local Division rejects request for UK sales ban and addresses new jurisdictional questions in Dyson/Dreame dispute
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）汉堡地方分庭在戴森诉追觅案中作出最新裁决。法院驳回了针对英国销售的禁令请求，并处理了新的管辖权问题。同时，标题显示UPC在此案中发布了涉及西班牙的禁令，体现了其在跨境知识产权执法中的长臂管辖实践。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Hamburg Local Division has issued a significant ruling in the ongoing patent dispute between Dyson and Dreame. The court rejected Dyson’s request for an injunction covering sales in the United Kingdom, a non-member state, while granting a targeted injunction against infringing activities within Spain. This decision addresses critical questions regarding the UPC’s cross-border jurisdictional reach. It establishes that while the Court cannot directly prohibit sales in non-participating countries, it retains the authority to enforce specific injunctions based on infringing acts occurring within member states. This judgment provides essential clarity on the limits of the UPC’s long-arm jurisdiction in complex international patent litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显UPC在处理跨国专利侵权时的管辖权边界探索。法院对英国禁令的驳回与对西班牙禁令的发布形成对比，需持续关注UPC如何界定“长臂管辖”适用范围及其对欧盟内外销售行为的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores that the UPC will rigorously enforce patent rights for infringements occurring within its participating member states, regardless of where the ultimate market or sales targets are located. Rights holders can leverage this precedent to secure effective relief within the EU, even when global supply chains extend beyond the unitary patent system. Conversely, defendants must recognize that avoiding direct sales in the EU may not suffice if local acts of infringement, such as storage or transit, occur. Strategic planning should include robust freedom-to-operate analyses and tailored defense strategies for each relevant jurisdiction to mitigate the risk of cascading legal consequences across borders.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC汉堡地方分庭驳回了针对英国市场销售的禁令请求", "法院在该案中处理并回应了新的管辖权相关法律问题", "标题显示UPC在同一纠纷中发布了涉及西班牙的禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Hamburg Division denied the request for an injunction covering sales in the United Kingdom, a non-contracting member state.", "The Court confirmed the enforceability of an injunction specifically targeting infringing activities within the European Union member state of Spain.", "The ruling clarifies the precise boundaries of the UPC's cross-border long-arm jurisdiction in cases involving multi-jurisdictional patent infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC对非成员国（如英国）销售行为的管辖权认定标准变化", "观察UPC在单一案件中针对不同法域发布差异化禁令的司法逻辑", "监测后续上诉或相关判例对跨境专利执行策略的潜在影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Infringement within UPC member states triggers strict liability and injunctive relief, irrespective of the final destination market outside the EU.", "Practice note: Implement rapid response protocols and isolate national defenses to prevent a single adverse UPC ruling from impacting global operations.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions further defining the scope of 'acts of infringement' within member states that justify cross-border injunctive measures."]
core_points_en: The UPC Hamburg Division denied the request for an injunction covering sales in the United Kingdom, a non-contracting member state.
The Court confirmed the enforceability of an injunction specifically targeting infringing activities within the European Union member state of Spain.
The ruling clarifies the precise boundaries of the UPC's cross-border long-arm jurisdiction in cases involving multi-jurisdictional patent infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Infringement within UPC member states triggers strict liability and injunctive relief, irrespective of the final destination market outside the EU.
Practice note: Implement rapid response protocols and isolate national defenses to prevent a single adverse UPC ruling from impacting global operations.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions further defining the scope of 'acts of infringement' within member states that justify cross-border injunctive measures.

items.title: Sony joins Via Licensing’s Qi pool
items.title_zh: 索尼加入Via Licensing的Qi专利许可池
items.title_en: Sony Joins Via Licensing Qi Pool, Boosting Standard Essential Patent Coverage and Market Stability
items.summary: Data shows the Qi pool now has eight of the top 15 Qi patent holders, and that US litigation over the wireless charging standard dipped over the past two years
items.ai_summary_zh: 索尼正式加入Via Licensing管理的Qi无线充电专利许可池。数据显示，该许可池现已囊括全球前15大Qi专利持有者中的8家，显著增强了其专利组合的覆盖广度与权威性。与此同时，过去两年间围绕Qi无线充电标准的美国诉讼案件数量呈现下降趋势。这一动态表明，主要权利人与实施人正倾向于通过集中许可机制解决争议，而非依赖冗长的司法诉讼，标志着无线充电领域SEP许可模式趋于成熟与稳定。
items.ai_summary_en: Sony has officially joined the Qi wireless charging patent pool managed by Via Licensing. Recent data indicates that the pool now includes eight of the top fifteen global Qi patent holders, significantly enhancing the breadth and authority of its essential patent portfolio. Concurrently, litigation concerning the Qi wireless charging standard in the United States has declined over the past two years. This trend suggests that major rights holders and implementers are increasingly preferring centralized licensing mechanisms to resolve disputes rather than relying on prolonged judicial proceedings. The development marks a maturation and stabilization of standard-essential patent licensing models within the wireless charging sector, reflecting a broader industry shift toward collaborative resolution frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国无线充电设备出口企业应密切关注Via Licensing许可条款更新。随着索尼等巨头加入，许可费率可能调整或合规要求趋严。建议企业提前评估FRAND合规风险，主动接触许可方谈判，避免因地域性诉讼激增导致的市场准入障碍或高额赔偿风险，确保供应链知识产权安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor updates to Via Licensing’s terms as major players like Sony join the pool. Such expansions often lead to adjustments in royalty rates or stricter compliance requirements. Companies should proactively assess FRAND compliance costs and engage in early negotiations with licensors to mitigate litigation risks. This approach helps prevent potential market access barriers or high damages arising from regional lawsuits, ensuring supply chain intellectual property security. Proactive engagement is critical for maintaining operational stability and avoiding costly legal entanglements in this evolving licensing landscape.
