items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Paris Court of Appeal overturned an INPI decision on December 19, 2025, cancelling the TOGG trademark.", "The court found the individual applicant acted in bad faith at the time of filing the trademark application.", "This ruling reinforces strict judicial scrutiny of applicant intent and prevents abuse of the registration system."]

The court found the individual applicant acted in bad faith at the time of filing the trademark application.
This ruling reinforces strict judicial scrutiny of applicant intent and prevents abuse of the registration system.

Practice note: Gather comprehensive evidence of the applicant's knowledge of prior rights and absence of legitimate commercial purpose.
What to monitor: New trademark filings that mirror established international brands, especially those filed by individuals or shell entities.

items.title: SEP wars: coalitions form as UK Supreme Court prepares for Tesla v Avanci
items.title_zh: SEP博弈：英国最高法院准备审理特斯拉诉Avanci案
items.title_en: SEP Litigation Intensifies: Coalitions Form Ahead of UK Supreme Court Tesla v Avanci Hearing
items.summary: Lobbies and interest groups are among the interveners in a major dispute over whether courts can set patent pool rates
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，游说团体和利益集团已作为干预者介入特斯拉与Avanci之间的重大纠纷。该案即将由英国最高法院审理，核心争议点在于法院是否拥有设定专利池许可费率的权力。各方势力正围绕此法律权限问题形成联盟。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court is preparing to hear the landmark dispute in Tesla v Avanci, a case centered on the critical question of whether national courts possess the authority to determine global licensing rates for standard-essential patents (SEPs). This pivotal proceeding has attracted significant attention from various lobbies and interest groups, which have intervened in the litigation to advocate for their respective positions. The involvement of these diverse stakeholders highlights the escalating strategic competition regarding global jurisdiction over SEP rate-setting. As alliances form among industry participants seeking to influence the final judgment, the outcome is expected to fundamentally reshape the landscape of SEP licensing across Europe and globally. The case represents a decisive moment in defining the scope of judicial power in complex international intellectual property disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案将明确英国法院在SEP费率裁定上的管辖权边界。需密切关注最高法院对专利池费率设定权的最终认定，这将影响未来SEP许可谈判策略及全球费率裁决格局。
items.ai_insight_en: This case carries substantial implications for rights holders and implementers operating within European jurisdictions. If the UK Supreme Court affirms its jurisdiction to set global SEP rates, the resulting precedent could establish a de facto pricing benchmark for international licensing agreements. Stakeholders should closely monitor the formation of intervening coalitions, as these alliances may signal shifts in regulatory expectations. Companies involved in SEP-intensive industries are advised to assess potential litigation risks in the UK and consider active participation in relevant industry forums to help shape favorable licensing frameworks and maintain leverage in ongoing negotiations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院正准备审理特斯拉诉Avanci的标准必要专利纠纷案件", "多个游说团体和利益集团已作为干预者介入该重大法律争议", "案件核心争议在于法院是否有权直接设定专利池的许可费率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court will determine whether it holds the legal authority to set global licensing rates for standard-essential patents.", "Multiple lobbying groups and industry interests have intervened, forming distinct coalitions to support opposing legal viewpoints.", "The final ruling will decisively influence future strategies for SEP licensing negotiations and related litigation worldwide."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国最高法院对法院设定专利池费率权限的认定将具风向标意义", "多方利益集团介入表明行业对SEP费率裁定机制的高度关注", "需持续跟踪案件审理进展及最终判决对FRAND原则解释的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor whether the UK judgment establishes a global benchmark for SEP royalty rates, impacting cross-border licensing costs.", "Practice note: Engage with industry alliances and intervene where possible to influence rule-making and protect commercial interests.", "What to monitor: The specific legal reasoning regarding extraterritorial jurisdiction and its adoption by other major patent jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court will determine whether it holds the legal authority to set global licensing rates for standard-essential patents.
Multiple lobbying groups and industry interests have intervened, forming distinct coalitions to support opposing legal viewpoints.
The final ruling will decisively influence future strategies for SEP licensing negotiations and related litigation worldwide.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor whether the UK judgment establishes a global benchmark for SEP royalty rates, impacting cross-border licensing costs.
Practice note: Engage with industry alliances and intervene where possible to influence rule-making and protect commercial interests.
What to monitor: The specific legal reasoning regarding extraterritorial jurisdiction and its adoption by other major patent jurisdictions.

items.title: Why global FRAND rate-setting must require SEP owner consent
items.title_zh: 全球FRAND费率设定为何需SEP权利人同意
items.title_en: Why Global FRAND Rate-Setting Requires SEP Owner Consent to Protect Rights
items.summary: Without SEP owner consent, rate-setting risks undermining property rights, distorting FRAND obligations and producing unenforceable, conflicting outcomes
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评论指出，若缺乏标准必要专利（SEP）权利人的明确同意，单方面设定全球FRAND费率将严重削弱财产权基础。这种做法不仅扭曲了FRAND承诺的本质，还可能导致不可执行且相互冲突的裁决结果。文章强调在跨国许可谈判中尊重权利人意愿对于维持公平、可预测的知识产权生态至关重要，反对司法过度干预商业谈判自主权。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary argues that imposing global FRAND rates without the explicit consent of standard-essential patent (SEP) owners fundamentally undermines the legal basis of property rights. Such unilateral judicial interventions distort the core nature of FRAND commitments, potentially leading to unenforceable and conflicting international rulings. The analysis emphasizes that respecting the voluntary will of rights holders in cross-border licensing negotiations is essential for maintaining a fair and predictable intellectual property ecosystem. It strongly opposes excessive judicial interference in commercial negotiation autonomy, warning that bypassing owner consent creates significant legal uncertainty and disrupts the balance required for effective global technology licensing frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对海外SEP诉讼时，应警惕法院单方裁定全球费率的趋势。建议积极主张程序权利，强调自愿谈判的重要性，避免被动接受不利判决。同时，加强内部专利布局与许可策略研究，利用“同意”机制作为谈判筹码，争取更公平的全球许可条件，降低合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding judicial trends toward determining global FRAND rates without holder consent. Actively asserting procedural rights and emphasizing the necessity of voluntary negotiations can help avoid passive acceptance of unfavorable rulings. Strengthening internal patent portfolio strategies and leveraging the 'consent' mechanism as a strategic bargaining chip may secure more equitable global licensing terms. This approach helps mitigate compliance risks and ensures that licensing outcomes align with established property rights principles rather than imposed judicial mandates.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单方设定全球FRAND费率损害SEP财产权基础与法律确定性", "缺乏权利人同意导致FRAND义务扭曲及裁决冲突不可执行", "主张尊重许可谈判自主权以维护公平稳定的SEP生态系统"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unilateral global FRAND rate-setting severely damages the foundation of SEP property rights and reduces overall legal certainty for market participants.", "Lacking explicit owner consent distorts FRAND obligations, often resulting in conflicting judgments that are difficult or impossible to enforce globally.", "Respecting negotiation autonomy is crucial for maintaining a stable, fair SEP ecosystem and preventing excessive judicial intervention in commercial deals."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕单方裁定全球费率，积极主张自愿谈判程序权利", "利用权利人同意机制作为谈判筹码，争取公平许可条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Vigilance against unilateral global rate determinations protects commercial autonomy and prevents exposure to unenforceable, conflicting legal outcomes.", "Practice note: Leverage the requirement for owner consent as a strategic negotiation tool to secure fairer global licensing conditions and assert procedural rights.", "What to monitor: Judicial shifts towards overriding voluntary negotiation processes and the increasing use of anti-suit injunctions in cross-border SEP disputes."]
core_points_en: Unilateral global FRAND rate-setting severely damages the foundation of SEP property rights and reduces overall legal certainty for market participants.
Lacking explicit owner consent distorts FRAND obligations, often resulting in conflicting judgments that are difficult or impossible to enforce globally.
Respecting negotiation autonomy is crucial for maintaining a stable, fair SEP ecosystem and preventing excessive judicial intervention in commercial deals.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Vigilance against unilateral global rate determinations protects commercial autonomy and prevents exposure to unenforceable, conflicting legal outcomes.
Practice note: Leverage the requirement for owner consent as a strategic negotiation tool to secure fairer global licensing conditions and assert procedural rights.
What to monitor: Judicial shifts towards overriding voluntary negotiation processes and the increasing use of anti-suit injunctions in cross-border SEP disputes.

items.title: IP FAQ: Which Trademark Symbol Should I Use?
items.title_zh: 知识产权问答：应使用哪种商标符号？
items.title_en: Trademark Symbols Guide: Distinguishing Registered and Unregistered Marks in the EU
items.summary: Trademarks are the cornerstone of a business identity, and successfully registering them for your organization's products or services is a cause for celebration.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析商标符号含义：™用于未注册商品商标，℠用于未注册商标服务，®确认已在国家或区域机构（如EUIPO）正式注册。注册比未注册提供更强的法律权利和更广的地理保护范围，是商业战略中展示保护状态的关键。
items.ai_summary_en: This article clarifies the legal meanings and appropriate usage scenarios for common trademark symbols. It distinguishes between unregistered marks, indicated by ™ or ℠, and officially registered trademarks marked with ®. The analysis emphasizes that in jurisdictions such as the European Union, the ® symbol may only be used after formal registration with the EUIPO. Correctly displaying protection status is a critical component of commercial strategy, helping to deter infringement and establish clear brand rights boundaries. Understanding these distinctions ensures compliance and strengthens brand enforcement capabilities across different markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需严格区分符号使用场景，避免在未获注册时误用®导致合规风险。企业应关注EUIPO注册状态以确立权利边界，未注册商标仅能在特定运营区域提供有限的普通法保护，需持续监测权利固化进程。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe must strictly differentiate between registered and unregistered trademark statuses when selecting symbols. Using the ® symbol before obtaining official registration from the EUIPO constitutes a compliance risk, potentially leading to allegations of misleading advertising or administrative penalties. Companies should prioritize accelerating their EU trademark registration processes to secure full legal protections. Once registered, utilizing the ® symbol enhances brand deterrence against infringers and supports robust enforcement actions, thereby establishing a compliant and defensible international brand presence.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["™符号通常表示未在国家或区域知识产权局注册的商标，用于声明知识产权主张", "®符号确认商标已获官方注册，区别于版权符号，代表更强的法律保护权利", "注册商标经EUIPO等机构批准后，比未注册商标拥有更广泛的地理范围和法律效力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The ™ symbol indicates an unregistered mark where the user claims intellectual property rights for goods or services.", "The ® symbol is strictly reserved for trademarks officially approved and registered with the relevant national or regional office.", "Registered trademarks provide stronger legal validity and broader territorial protection compared to unregistered counterparts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["准确使用商标符号是展示保护状态及商业战略的基础环节", "未注册商标法律保护受限，仅能防止特定区域内相同商品的混淆", "需持续观察EUIPO等机构的注册审批进度以确立完整权利"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using the ® symbol prior to EUIPO registration to prevent compliance risks and potential administrative sanctions.", "Practice note: Accelerate EU trademark registration strategies to leverage statutory symbols for enhanced brand enforcement efficacy.", "What to monitor: Ensure consistent alignment between actual registration status and displayed trademark symbols across all commercial materials."]
core_points_en: The ™ symbol indicates an unregistered mark where the user claims intellectual property rights for goods or services.
The ® symbol is strictly reserved for trademarks officially approved and registered with the relevant national or regional office.
Registered trademarks provide stronger legal validity and broader territorial protection compared to unregistered counterparts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using the ® symbol prior to EUIPO registration to prevent compliance risks and potential administrative sanctions.
Practice note: Accelerate EU trademark registration strategies to leverage statutory symbols for enhanced brand enforcement efficacy.
What to monitor: Ensure consistent alignment between actual registration status and displayed trademark symbols across all commercial materials.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 30, 2026, Order, UPC_CoA_12/2026
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭关于私人庭审笔录的命令
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Order on Private Transcripts from Audio Recordings in Amazon v InterDigital
items.summary: A party or its representative may prepare a private transcript of an oral hearing, based on an audio recording pursuant to R. 115 RoP. When producing a private transcript, a party or its representative may obtain support of an assistant or support staff...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Amazon与InterDigital案件中发布命令，明确当事人可依据《程序规则》第115条，基于庭审录音制作私人笔录。制作时可由助理或速记员在监督下协助。若满足特定条件，该笔录可用于UPC之外的相关诉讼程序。
items.ai_summary_en: In the appeal proceedings between Amazon.com, Inc. and InterDigital VC Holdings, Inc., the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal issued a procedural order clarifying the rules for private transcripts. The Court confirmed that parties or their representatives may prepare private transcripts of oral hearings based on audio recordings permitted under Rule 115 of the Rules of Procedure. Such transcription work may be assisted by staff, including stenographers, provided they operate strictly under the direct supervision and presence of the party or representative. Furthermore, the ruling establishes that these private transcripts may be utilized in related court proceedings outside the UPC jurisdiction, subject to specific conditions. This decision balances the practical need for accurate internal records with the imperative of maintaining procedural confidentiality within the UPC system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此命令澄清了UPC程序中私人笔录的制作合法性及跨程序使用条件，为当事人保留庭审记录提供了明确依据。需关注后续实践中对“特定条件”的具体认定标准，以及该做法对其他欧洲司法辖区的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling provides clarity on evidentiary documentation strategies for litigants before the UPC. Parties can now confidently maintain detailed internal records of oral hearings by leveraging Rule 115, ensuring better case management and strategy development. However, strict adherence to supervision requirements is critical to avoid procedural violations. Additionally, the permission to use these transcripts in parallel global proceedings offers strategic advantages for coordinating cross-border disputes. Legal teams should establish robust protocols for transcript creation and usage to maximize evidentiary value while mitigating risks associated with confidentiality breaches or procedural defects in multi-jurisdictional contexts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["当事人可依程序规则第115条，基于录音制作私人庭审笔录", "制作笔录时，助理或速记员可在当事人监督下提供协助", "满足特定条件时，私人笔录可用于UPC外的相关诉讼程序"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Parties may create private transcripts of oral hearings using audio recordings authorized under Rule 115 of the Rules of Procedure.", "Assistants like stenographers may help produce transcripts but must remain under the direct supervision of the party or representative.", "Private transcripts may be used in related external litigation outside the UPC, provided specific regulatory conditions are satisfied."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确了UPC庭审录音转录的合规操作路径，降低程序违规风险", "允许在监督下使用外部辅助人员，提升了庭审记录的准确性与效率", "需持续观察法院对跨程序使用私人笔录的具体审查标准与限制条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enables precise internal record-keeping of UPC hearings while permitting controlled use in parallel global litigation.", "Practice note: Implement strict supervisory protocols for third-party transcription services to ensure compliance with Rule 115.", "What to monitor: Specific conditions governing the admissibility of private transcripts in non-UPC jurisdictions to prevent procedural challenges."]
core_points_en: Parties may create private transcripts of oral hearings using audio recordings authorized under Rule 115 of the Rules of Procedure.
Assistants like stenographers may help produce transcripts but must remain under the direct supervision of the party or representative.
Private transcripts may be used in related external litigation outside the UPC, provided specific regulatory conditions are satisfied.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Enables precise internal record-keeping of UPC hearings while permitting controlled use in parallel global litigation.
Practice note: Implement strict supervisory protocols for third-party transcription services to ensure compliance with Rule 115.
What to monitor: Specific conditions governing the admissibility of private transcripts in non-UPC jurisdictions to prevent procedural challenges.

items.title: LD Hamburg, March 31, 2026, Alignment Of Deadlines, UPC_CFI_360/2026
items.title_zh: 汉堡地方法院裁定对齐多被告答辩期限
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg Aligns Defence Deadlines for Multiple Defendants to Enhance Procedural Efficiency
items.summary: A minor deviation in deadlines for multiple defendants makes a request to align them reasonable for procedural efficiency, as per the court’s discretion under R. 9.3(a) RoP.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院汉堡地方法院在Infoblox案中裁定，鉴于多被告答辩期限仅偏差20天，为提升程序效率，依据《程序规则》第9.3(a)条批准对齐期限。原告虽无义务主动安排美国送达费用，但需承担因延迟安排导致的送达日期偏差风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The Local Division of the Unified Patent Court in Hamburg exercised its discretion under Rule 9.3(a) of the Rules of Procedure to grant a request aligning the deadlines for filing Statements of Defence among multiple defendants in Infoblox v. Nixu. The Court determined that although the existing deadlines differed by only twenty days, harmonizing them was reasonable to ensure procedural efficiency and fairness. Furthermore, the judgment clarified that while a claimant is not strictly obligated to proactively arrange payment for service of process in the United States, they bear the risk of resulting deviations in service dates if such arrangements are delayed following a sub-registry request. This decision underscores the importance of timely administrative actions in cross-border litigation to prevent unnecessary procedural complications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC在多被告诉讼中对程序期限管理的裁量标准，强调程序效率优先。同时界定了跨境送达中原告的风险边界，即虽无主动付费义务，但需承受时间偏差后果。实务中应关注法院对轻微期限差异的容忍度及送达策略对程序进度的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: In multi-defendant UPC proceedings, parties should carefully assess how variations in service timelines impact overall litigation strategy. While claimants manage service logistics, defendants must remain vigilant regarding potential deadline discrepancies arising from cross-border service delays. Proactive monitoring of service progress allows parties to anticipate procedural risks and file timely applications for deadline adjustments when necessary. This approach helps safeguard procedural rights and ensures that defense strategies are not compromised by administrative delays outside their direct control, promoting a more predictable and efficient litigation environment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院依据《程序规则》第9.3(a)条行使裁量权，批准对齐多被告的答辩期限以提升效率", "各被告原定答辩期限偏差仅为20天，法院认定被告提出的对齐请求具有合理性", "原告不承担主动安排美国送达费用的义务，但需自行承担由此产生的送达日期偏差风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court used its discretionary power under Rule 9.3(a) RoP to approve aligned defence deadlines for multiple defendants to improve procedural efficiency.", "A minor twenty-day deviation in existing deadlines was deemed sufficient justification for the Court to order alignment of the time limits.", "Claimants bear the risk of deviating service dates if they fail to promptly arrange payment for service in the United States after notification."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC法院倾向于通过对齐期限来优化多被告案件的程序效率，即使差异较小", "跨境送达的时间风险由原告承担，即便其无主动安排支付的强制性义务", "需持续观察UPC各地方法院在程序规则适用上的裁量一致性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess how service timeline variations in multi-defendant cases impact defense strategy and procedural rights.", "Practice note: Monitor cross-border service progress closely to anticipate deadline discrepancies and file adjustment requests promptly.", "What to monitor: Administrative delays in arranging international service payments that may lead to uneven procedural timelines."]
core_points_en: The Court used its discretionary power under Rule 9.3(a) RoP to approve aligned defence deadlines for multiple defendants to improve procedural efficiency.
A minor twenty-day deviation in existing deadlines was deemed sufficient justification for the Court to order alignment of the time limits.
Claimants bear the risk of deviating service dates if they fail to promptly arrange payment for service in the United States after notification.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess how service timeline variations in multi-defendant cases impact defense strategy and procedural rights.
Practice note: Monitor cross-border service progress closely to anticipate deadline discrepancies and file adjustment requests promptly.
What to monitor: Administrative delays in arranging international service payments that may lead to uneven procedural timelines.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 30, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_899/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭裁定：一审未提管辖权异议则上诉禁反言
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Failure to Contest Jurisdiction at First Instance Forecloses Appeal
items.summary: Failing to contest jurisdiction in first instance forecloses the objection on appeal (Art. 26(1) Brussels I Recast, R. 19.7 RoP).
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Sinocare等诉Abbott案中裁定，被告若未在一审阶段质疑法院管辖权，则在上诉中不得再以此为由推翻原判。同时确认，即便被告行为发生在成员国境外，只要制造旨在进入UPC领土的侵权产品，即构成损害发生地，确立UPC管辖权。上诉人需证明一审错误导致判决不当。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal ruled that defendants who fail to contest jurisdiction in first instance proceedings are precluded from raising this objection on appeal, citing Article 26(1) Brussels I Recast and Rule 19.7 RoP. The court affirmed that manufacturing products intended for the UPC territory establishes jurisdiction under Article 71b(2) and Article 7(2) Brussels I Recast, even if acts occur outside Contracting Member States. Additionally, appellants must demonstrate that alleged procedural or substantive errors by the Court of First Instance directly resulted in an incorrect decision. The necessity of provisional measures was supported by evidence of direct competition and risk of permanent price erosion in reimbursement markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This order reinforces strict procedural timelines for jurisdictional challenges and clarifies the territorial scope of UPC jurisdiction for cross-border supply chains. Rights holders should note that targeting the UPC market can establish jurisdiction regardless of physical location. Appellants face a heightened burden to prove causation between alleged errors and the final outcome, limiting successful appeals on procedural grounds alone.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["一审未质疑管辖权即视为放弃，上诉阶段不得再提出管辖权异议", "制造旨在进入UPC成员国的产品，即使行为在境外，亦确立UPC管辖权", "上诉人须证明一审程序或实体错误实际导致了错误的判决结果"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Failure to contest jurisdiction at first instance forecloses the objection on appeal under Rule 19.7 RoP.", "Manufacturing products intended for UPC territory establishes jurisdiction even if defendant acts outside Contracting Member States.", "Appellants must prove that alleged first instance errors actually resulted in an incorrect decision."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["程序合规：需在一审阶段及时提出管辖权异议，否则上诉阶段将丧失该抗辩权利", "管辖确立：针对UPC市场的制造与销售行为，即便发生在境外，仍可能受UPC管辖", "上诉举证：上诉方必须具体论证一审错误与判决结果之间的因果关系，而非仅指出错误"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Defendants must raise jurisdictional objections immediately in first instance proceedings to preserve them for appeal.", "Practice note: Manufacturing goods intended for the UPC market creates a jurisdictional nexus, even if production occurs outside member states.", "What to monitor: The appellate standard requiring proof that procedural errors directly caused an incorrect decision."]
core_points_en: Failure to contest jurisdiction at first instance forecloses the objection on appeal under Rule 19.7 RoP.
Manufacturing products intended for UPC territory establishes jurisdiction even if defendant acts outside Contracting Member States.
Appellants must prove that alleged first instance errors actually resulted in an incorrect decision.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Defendants must raise jurisdictional objections immediately in first instance proceedings to preserve them for appeal.
Practice note: Manufacturing goods intended for the UPC market creates a jurisdictional nexus, even if production occurs outside member states.
What to monitor: The appellate standard requiring proof that procedural errors directly caused an incorrect decision.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 27, 2026, Order, UPC_CoA_898/2025
items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭：主张未注册权利要求版本不自动增加被上诉人负担
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Asserting Non-Registered Claims Does Not Automatically Burden Appellee
items.summary: There is no automatic additional burden on the Appellee from assertion of a non-registered version.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在ONWARD Medical诉Niche Biomedical案中裁定，在临时措施程序中主张未注册的权利要求版本并非被绝对排除，其可采性取决于个案情况。法院指出，此举不会自动给被上诉人带来额外负担。若辩护权受到不当限制，可通过程序规则予以解决。新辅助请求的可采性受法院裁量权约束，需确保被上诉人未遭受实质性损害。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal issued an order in ONWARD Medical N.V. v. Niche Biomedical, Inc., clarifying the admissibility of non-registered claim versions in provisional measures proceedings. The Court held that asserting such versions is not categorically excluded; rather, admissibility depends on the specific circumstances under Rule 211.2 RoP. Crucially, there is no automatic additional burden on the Appellee when a non-registered version is asserted. Suitability for provisional measures requires a case-by-case assessment, with safeguards available under Rule 211.3 if defense rights are unduly curtailed. Additionally, the decision addresses new auxiliary requests on appeal under Rule 222, balancing procedural efficiency against potential prejudice. The ruling also elaborates on establishing intended use under Article 26 UPCA through objective circumstances.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC临时措施程序中处理非注册权利要求版本的灵活性标准。实务中需关注法院如何平衡程序效率与辩护权保障，特别是在评估“实质性损害”时的具体考量因素。后续应观察该裁定对类似紧急禁令申请中权利要求策略选择的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should note that opponents may strategically assert non-registered claim versions in urgent proceedings without triggering automatic procedural penalties. Parties facing such assertions must focus on demonstrating whether their defense rights are materially prejudiced rather than challenging the mere existence of the unregistered claims. It is essential to scrutinize the objective evidence supporting alleged 'intended use' under Article 26 UPCA. Defense strategies should actively utilize Rule 211.3 to address any undue curtailment of rights. Furthermore, when introducing new requests on appeal, counsel must anticipate judicial restraint based on the summary nature of proceedings while arguing against material prejudice to ensure admissibility.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["临时措施中主张未注册权利要求版本不被绝对排除，可采性依个案情形判定", "主张未注册版本不自动构成对被上诉人的额外负担，需具体评估是否适宜", "上诉阶段新辅助请求的可采性由法院裁量，以被上诉人未受实质损害为前提"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Asserting non-registered claim versions in provisional measures is not absolutely excluded but depends on individual case circumstances.", "Claiming a non-registered version does not automatically impose an additional evidentiary burden or procedural obstacle on the Appellee.", "Admissibility of new auxiliary requests on appeal is subject to judicial discretion, balancing summary nature and party rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确临时措施中非注册权利要求版本的可采性标准，避免绝对化排除", "强调法院在接纳上诉新请求时的裁量权及对被上诉人权益的保护平衡", "需持续观察UPC在界定“实质性损害”及“辩护权受限”时的具体司法实践"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Opponents can assert non-registered claims without automatic penalty; challenge only if defense rights are materially prejudiced.", "Practice note: Scrutinize objective evidence for 'intended use' and utilize Rule 211.3 to protect defense rights against undue curtailment.", "What to monitor: Judicial discretion regarding new auxiliary requests on appeal, balancing procedural efficiency with fair trial guarantees."]
core_points_en: Asserting non-registered claim versions in provisional measures is not absolutely excluded but depends on individual case circumstances.
Claiming a non-registered version does not automatically impose an additional evidentiary burden or procedural obstacle on the Appellee.
Admissibility of new auxiliary requests on appeal is subject to judicial discretion, balancing summary nature and party rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Opponents can assert non-registered claims without automatic penalty; challenge only if defense rights are materially prejudiced.
Practice note: Scrutinize objective evidence for 'intended use' and utilize Rule 211.3 to protect defense rights against undue curtailment.
What to monitor: Judicial discretion regarding new auxiliary requests on appeal, balancing procedural efficiency with fair trial guarantees.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 30, 2026, Order, UPC_CoA_302/2025, CoA_305/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭裁定费用确定须由一审法院启动
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Rules Cost Determination Must Start at First Instance
items.summary: Cost determination proceedings must always be initiated at the Court of First Instance, regardless of whether costs arise from first instance or appeal (R. 150 et seq. RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在涉及 Rematec 与 Europe Forestry 的案件中作出命令，明确依据《程序规则》第 150 条及以下条款，费用确定程序必须向一审法院提起。即便费用产生于上诉阶段，上诉庭也无权直接受理或转交该申请，因规则未设特别程序且此举将剥夺当事人的上诉权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal clarified that cost determination proceedings under Rules 150 et seq. RoP constitute a separate procedural track that must always be initiated before the Court of First Instance. This requirement applies irrespective of whether the costs were incurred during first instance or appellate proceedings. The Court emphasized that initiating such applications at the Court of Appeal would undermine the statutory right to appeal cost decisions under Rules 157 and 221 RoP. Furthermore, the Court of Appeal lacks the legal authority to refer cost applications to the Court of First Instance, as referrals are strictly limited to scenarios expressly provided for in the UPCA or Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 诉讼中需严格遵循程序管辖，胜诉后务必向一审法院而非上诉庭申请费用裁定，避免因程序错误导致权利落空，建议提前布局一审阶段的费用证据保全。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling establishes strict jurisdictional boundaries for recovering litigation expenses within the Unified Patent Court system. Rights holders and litigants must ensure that any application for cost determination is directed exclusively to the Court of First Instance, even following an appellate judgment. Misdirecting these applications to the Court of Appeal risks procedural dismissal or delay, as the appellate division has confirmed it lacks the competence to process or transfer such requests. Proper adherence to this procedural pathway is essential for effective enforcement of cost awards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["费用确定程序属独立诉讼，必须向统一专利法院一审法院提起", "无论费用源于一审还是上诉，上诉庭均无权直接受理费用裁定申请", "程序规则未授权上诉庭将费用申请转交一审，仅限法定情形可转交"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Cost determination is a distinct proceeding requiring filing at the Court of First Instance under Rules 150 et seq. RoP.", "Applications must target the Court of First Instance regardless of whether costs stem from first instance or appeal stages.", "The Court of Appeal cannot refer cost applications to the Court of First Instance absent explicit statutory authorization."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["费用申请须精准投递至一审法院，切勿误向上诉庭提交", "需在一审阶段即完善费用证据链，以備后续独立程序使用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensures correct forum selection for cost recovery, preventing procedural errors that could delay or jeopardize expense reimbursement.", "Practice note: File all cost determination applications with the Court of First Instance immediately after final judgments, avoiding the Court of Appeal.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of Rule 150 et seq. regarding evidence submission timelines and potential amendments to referral mechanisms."]
core_points_en: Cost determination is a distinct proceeding requiring filing at the Court of First Instance under Rules 150 et seq. RoP.
Applications must target the Court of First Instance regardless of whether costs stem from first instance or appeal stages.
The Court of Appeal cannot refer cost applications to the Court of First Instance absent explicit statutory authorization.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensures correct forum selection for cost recovery, preventing procedural errors that could delay or jeopardize expense reimbursement.
Practice note: File all cost determination applications with the Court of First Instance immediately after final judgments, avoiding the Court of Appeal.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of Rule 150 et seq. regarding evidence submission timelines and potential amendments to referral mechanisms.

items.title: CD Paris, March 30, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_258/2025
items.title_zh: 巴黎中央分院：独立权利要求撤销不自动影响从属权利要求效力
items.title_en: UPC Paris: Revoking Independent Claim Does Not Invalidate Unchallenged Dependent Claims
items.summary: The revocation of an independent claim does not automatically affect the validity of unchallenged dependent claims, as the latter may possess autonomous patentability due to additional technical features.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎中央分院在Emporia诉Seoul Viosys案中裁定，独立权利要求被撤销不自动导致未受挑战的从属权利要求无效，因其可能具备自主可专利性。专利权人可将幸存从属权利要求重写为独立形式，但须符合清楚性和单一性要求。法院视此为强制性形式调整而非需许可的实质性修改。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Central Division Paris ruled that revoking an independent claim does not automatically invalidate unchallenged dependent claims, which may retain autonomous patentability. In Emporia v Seoul Viosys, the Court allowed recasting surviving dependent claims into independent form, provided they meet clarity and unity requirements under Articles 84 and 82 EPC. This adaptation was deemed a mandatory formal step rather than a substantive amendment requiring leave. The Court further held that mere claim deletion does not necessitate a consolidated document. Requests to amend under Rule 30(2) RoP were rejected where parallel appeal proceedings lacked a direct logical nexus to the proposed changes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC程序中权利要求删除后的形式调整边界，区分了强制性适应与实质性修改。实务中需关注法院对“清楚性与单一性”的形式审查标准，以及平行诉讼对修改请求的影响限制，这对应对UPC撤销行动具有程序参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies procedural boundaries for claim adaptation during UPC revocation actions. It distinguishes between mandatory formal adjustments and substantive amendments, offering rights holders a pathway to preserve patent scope via dependent claims. Practitioners should note the strict threshold for linking amendment requests to parallel appellate outcomes, limiting tactical delays based on foreseeable legal developments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["独立权利要求撤销不自动否定从属权利要求效力，后者可因附加技术特征具备自主可专利性", "专利权人可将幸存从属权利要求重写为独立形式，但须符合欧洲专利公约第84条及82条", "若无直接逻辑关联或新进展可预见，平行上诉程序判决不构成依据程序规则30(2)条修改专利…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Revocation of an independent claim does not automatically invalidate unchallenged dependent claims with autonomous patentability.", "Recasting dependent claims into independent form is a mandatory formal adaptation subject to EPC clarity and unity rules.", "Amendment requests under Rule 30(2) RoP require a direct logical nexus to new procedural developments in parallel cases."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确从属权利要求在独立项撤销后的存续逻辑，降低专利整体被无效风险", "界定权利要求重写的形式属性，无需额外许可即可进行适应性修改", "限制基于平行诉讼结果的修改请求，强调程序间的直接逻辑必要性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Dependent claims can survive independent claim revocation, preserving potential enforcement avenues if they possess distinct technical features.", "Practice note: Recasting dependent claims is treated as a formal compliance step with EPC Articles 82 and 84, not a discretionary substantive amendment.", "What to monitor: The Court’s strict interpretation of 'direct logical nexus' for Rule 30(2) amendments limits reliance on parallel appeal decisions for procedural adjustments."]
core_points_en: Revocation of an independent claim does not automatically invalidate unchallenged dependent claims with autonomous patentability.
Recasting dependent claims into independent form is a mandatory formal adaptation subject to EPC clarity and unity rules.
Amendment requests under Rule 30(2) RoP require a direct logical nexus to new procedural developments in parallel cases.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Dependent claims can survive independent claim revocation, preserving potential enforcement avenues if they possess distinct technical features.
Practice note: Recasting dependent claims is treated as a formal compliance step with EPC Articles 82 and 84, not a discretionary substantive amendment.
What to monitor: The Court’s strict interpretation of 'direct logical nexus' for Rule 30(2) amendments limits reliance on parallel appeal decisions for procedural adjustments.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 27, 2026, Decision, UPC_CoA_409/2025, UPC_CoA_410/2025, UPC_CoA_420/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院裁定登录系统不构成应诉管辖
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: CMS Login Does Not Constitute Entering an Appearance Under Brussels Ia
items.summary: Logging into the UPC's Case Management System does not constitute an "entering of an appearance" to establish jurisdiction under the Brussels Ia Regulation.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在涉及 NUC Electronics 与 Hurom 的案件中裁定，被告仅登录案件管理系统查看文件，不构成《布鲁塞尔条例 Ia》意义上的“出庭应诉”。法院指出，确立管辖权需被告采取实质性步骤，如提交初步异议或答辩状，单纯访问不足以视为对法院管辖权的自愿接受或争议确认。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal ruled that merely logging into the Case Management System to access case files does not amount to 'entering an appearance' under Article 26(1) of the Brussels Ia Regulation. The decision clarifies that passive file access lacks the deliberate choice required to establish jurisdiction. Jurisdiction is only confirmed when a defendant takes active procedural steps, such as filing a Preliminary Objection regarding jurisdiction or submitting a Statement of Defence on the merits. This distinction prevents accidental submission to UPC jurisdiction through routine administrative access.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对 UPC 诉讼时，切勿因登录查看案情而误判为已应诉。应严格把控首次提交文件的时机与内容，避免因程序性操作不当导致丧失管辖权异议权利，需由专业律师评估后采取行动。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical clarity for rights holders and defendants managing cross-border patent disputes within the UPC framework. It confirms that preliminary case assessment activities, such as reviewing documents in the digital system, do not inadvertently waive jurisdictional objections. Legal teams can safely monitor proceedings without fear of accidental submission, provided they refrain from filing substantive responses before deciding on their jurisdictional strategy.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅登录 UPC 系统查看文件不视为法律意义上的出庭应诉行为", "确立管辖权需提交初步异议或实体答辩等实质性诉讼步骤", "明确区分被动查阅与主动抗辩，防止管辖权被意外确立"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Passive access to UPC case files via the Case Management System does not constitute entering an appearance under Brussels Ia Regulation.", "Establishing jurisdiction requires active procedural steps, specifically filing a Preliminary Objection or a Statement of Defence.", "The ruling distinguishes between mere file inspection and the deliberate legal choice to submit to the court's international jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["登录系统查阅卷宗不会触发管辖权，企业可安全进行初步评估", "首次提交异议或答辩状是确立管辖的关键节点，须谨慎决策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders can review UPC case materials without risking accidental acceptance of the court's jurisdiction under EU regulations.", "Practice note: Ensure no substantive filings are made until a formal decision on jurisdictional challenges or defense strategy is finalized.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes an 'active step' beyond standard pleadings that might trigger jurisdictional consent."]
core_points_en: Passive access to UPC case files via the Case Management System does not constitute entering an appearance under Brussels Ia Regulation.
Establishing jurisdiction requires active procedural steps, specifically filing a Preliminary Objection or a Statement of Defence.
The ruling distinguishes between mere file inspection and the deliberate legal choice to submit to the court's international jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders can review UPC case materials without risking accidental acceptance of the court's jurisdiction under EU regulations.
Practice note: Ensure no substantive filings are made until a formal decision on jurisdictional challenges or defense strategy is finalized.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes an 'active step' beyond standard pleadings that might trigger jurisdictional consent.

items.title: InterDigital, Dolby, Nokia fuelling spike in AV1 litigation
items.title_zh: InterDigital、杜比和诺基亚推高AV1专利诉讼激增
items.title_en: InterDigital, Dolby, Nokia fuelling spike in AV1 litigation
items.summary: US district court lawsuits over the so-called royalty-free video codec specification reached an all-time high in 2025 and these cases come with intriguing legal arguments including lack of FRAND, higher damages and potential injunctions
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年针对所谓免版税视频编码标准AV1的美国地区法院诉讼达到历史新高。InterDigital、杜比和诺基亚等权利人发起诉讼，主张缺乏FRAND许可、要求更高损害赔偿并寻求潜在禁令。尽管AV1标榜免版税，但权利人的法律行动揭示了标准实施中的知识产权冲突，凸显了即使在被视为开放的标准中，SEP持有者仍积极通过司法途径维护其商业利益。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国音视频企业需警惕“免版税”标准的法律陷阱。AV1诉讼激增表明SEP持有人正利用FRAND争议扩大索赔范围。建议出口企业重新评估AV1合规策略，提前进行FTO分析，并在许可谈判中做好应对禁令和高额赔偿的准备，避免被动卷入跨国IP纠纷。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年AV1相关美国诉讼创历史新高，打破免版税预期", "InterDigital等巨头主张缺乏FRAND许可及高额赔偿", "案件涉及潜在禁令，凸显SEP在开放标准中的博弈"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕AV1“免版税”背后的SEP诉讼风险", "加强FRAND合规审查以应对高额赔偿诉求"]

items.title: Licensing negotiation groups under scrutiny: cartel risk or analytical drift?
items.title_zh: 许可谈判组受审视：卡特尔风险还是分析偏差？
items.title_en: Scrutinizing SEP Licensing Groups: Cartel Risk or Analytical Drift in Competition Law?
items.summary: Saturday opinion: Treating implementer coordination in SEP licensing as cartel conduct risks “analytical drift”, where antitrust becomes a proxy for trade or industrial policy rather than a disciplined competition analysis
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评论指出，将实施者在标准必要专利（SEP）许可中的协调行为直接视为卡特尔行为，可能导致“分析偏差”。这种偏差使得反垄断执法沦为贸易或产业政策的代理工具，而非基于严谨的竞争分析。文章强调应区分合法的集体谈判与非法的共谋，避免过度干预市场机制，确保反垄断法在SEP领域的适用保持专业性和纪律性，以平衡专利权人与实施者的利益。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary argues that classifying implementer coordination in standard-essential patent licensing as cartel conduct risks significant analytical drift. Such an approach may transform antitrust enforcement into a proxy for trade or industrial policy, rather than maintaining a disciplined competition analysis. The article emphasizes the critical need to distinguish between legitimate collective bargaining and illegal collusion. By avoiding excessive intervention in market mechanisms, regulators can ensure that antitrust laws remain professional and rigorous within the SEP sector. This balanced perspective is essential for protecting the interests of both patent holders and implementers, ensuring that competition law serves its intended purpose without being distorted by broader policy objectives.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临SEP许可时，可探索联合谈判以增强议价能力，但需严格合规以避免反垄断调查。建议建立内部合规审查机制，确保谈判策略符合欧盟竞争法，避免被认定为价格固定或市场分割。同时，密切关注欧盟相关立法动态，灵活调整应对策略，维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in joint negotiations for SEP licenses must navigate strict compliance requirements to avoid triggering EU antitrust investigations. Establishing robust internal compliance review mechanisms is essential to ensure that negotiation strategies align with European competition law standards. Parties should carefully structure their interactions to avoid any appearance of price-fixing or market allocation. Furthermore, continuous monitoring of evolving EU legislative dynamics regarding SEP regulation is necessary. This allows stakeholders to adapt their strategies proactively, safeguarding their legal rights while maintaining operational flexibility in complex licensing environments.

items.title: Accord PPH avec le Canada
items.title_zh: 法国INPI与加拿大OPIC启动PPH合作，符合条件的专利申请可申请加速审查
items.title_en: INPI France and CIPO Launch Bilateral Patent Prosecution Highway Agreement
items.summary: Accord PPH avec le Canada llegrand@inpi.fr 03 avril 2026 Les conditions pour utiliser le PPH Les déposants peuvent demander l'accélération du traitement de leur demande auprès de l'un des deux offices à partir du moment où elle aura été examinée par l'autre office, indépendamment de l'ordre chronologique des dépôts et des revendications de priorité. De manière classique, après dépôt d'une requête PPH, le traitement d'une demande de brevet déposée à l'INPI sous priorité d'une demande déposée à l'
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国工业产权局宣布与加拿大知识产权局建立专利审查高速路（PPH）合作。根据说明，只要对应申请已在另一局被认定部分权利要求具备可专利性，申请人即可在满足条件时请求加速审查。INPI页面还指出，在加拿大一侧，自提出PPH加速请求起，整体授权流程平均可加快约23个月。该安排将影响法加之间的专利申请节奏、优先权运用和跨局审查协同。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) and the Canadian Intellectual Property Office (CIPO) have implemented a bilateral Patent Prosecution Highway (PPH). Applicants may request accelerated examination at either office once corresponding claims are found patentable by the other, regardless of filing order or priority claims. Acceleration requires sufficiently similar or narrower claim scope in the second application. INPI data indicates that PPH requests at CIPO reduce the average total prosecution timeline by approximately 23 months. Eligibility mandates matching earliest dates for national or PCT applications where CIPO served as the International Searching or Preliminary Examining Authority.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这类双边PPH安排对有法加双边布局需求的申请人具有直接实务价值。企业和代理人在制定巴黎公约优先权、PCT进入国家阶段及加速审查策略时，可更主动地利用一局的可专利性结论缩短另一局进度，但仍需关注权利要求对应关系和提交时点。
items.ai_insight_en: This bilateral arrangement offers procedural efficiency for rights holders managing parallel portfolios in France and Canada. By leveraging positive examination outcomes from one jurisdiction, applicants can potentially expedite grant proceedings in the other. Practitioners should prioritize aligning claim scopes early to satisfy correspondence requirements. Monitoring actual examination timelines at INPI will be necessary to quantify local benefits, complementing the reported efficiencies observed at CIPO.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INPI宣布与加拿大知识产权局建立PPH合作机制。", "满足条件的对应专利申请可依据另一局的审查结果请求加速审查。", "INPI页面称加拿大一侧整体授权流程平均可加快约23个月。"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["INPI and CIPO enable mutual PPH acceleration based on positive examination results from either office.", "Accelerated processing applies regardless of chronological filing order, provided claims are sufficiently corresponding.", "CIPO reports an average twenty-three month reduction in total prosecution time following PPH requests."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["法加双边专利申请可把PPH纳入时间压缩工具箱。", "权利要求对应关系将成为能否顺利用PPH的关键。", "跨局加速应与优先权和国家阶段进入节奏一起规划。"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enables faster patent grants in France and Canada by leveraging positive examination results from the counterpart office.", "Practice note: Ensure claims in the second filing are identical or narrower than those allowed in the first office to maintain eligibility.", "What to monitor: Track INPI-specific processing times post-implementation to validate acceleration benefits compared to standard examination queues."]
core_points_en: INPI and CIPO enable mutual PPH acceleration based on positive examination results from either office.
Accelerated processing applies regardless of chronological filing order, provided claims are sufficiently corresponding.
CIPO reports an average twenty-three month reduction in total prosecution time following PPH requests.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Enables faster patent grants in France and Canada by leveraging positive examination results from the counterpart office.
Practice note: Ensure claims in the second filing are identical or narrower than those allowed in the first office to maintain eligibility.
What to monitor: Track INPI-specific processing times post-implementation to validate acceleration benefits compared to standard examination queues.

items.title: Ericsson, InterDigital, Nokia and Qualcomm provide insights into 6G – and what it could mean for patent licensing
items.title_zh: 爱立信等解析6G专利许可前景
items.title_en: Ericsson, Nokia, InterDigital, Qualcomm Discuss 6G Impact on SEP Licensing at IPBC Europe
items.summary: New services, devices and verticals – 6G is set to stir up SEP value creation said panellists at IPBC Europe 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 在IPBC Europe 2026会议上，爱立信、InterDigital、诺基亚和高通专家探讨6G技术对标准必要专利（SEP）价值创造的影响。与会者指出，随着新服务、设备及垂直行业的涌现，6G将显著改变SEP许可格局。会议聚焦于技术演进如何驱动新的许可模式及价值分配，为行业提供关于未来通信标准知识产权商业化路径的关键洞察。
items.ai_summary_en: At IPBC Europe 2026, experts from Ericsson, InterDigital, Nokia, and Qualcomm examined how sixth-generation (6G) technology will reshape standard-essential patent (SEP) value creation. Panelists emphasized that the emergence of new services, devices, and vertical industries will significantly alter the current licensing landscape. The discussions focused on how technological evolution drives novel licensing models and value distribution mechanisms. These insights provide critical guidance for the industry regarding the commercialization pathways of intellectual property in future communication standards. Stakeholders are advised to consider these shifts as they prepare for the complex interplay between technical innovation and legal frameworks governing FRAND commitments in the next generation of connectivity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注6G SEP许可范式变化，提前布局相关专利组合以增强谈判筹码。鉴于新垂直行业涌入，建议评估跨界许可风险，建立灵活的FRAND应对策略，避免在下一代技术标准中陷入被动局面。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving paradigm of 6G SEP licensing to anticipate changes in negotiation dynamics. Proactively building robust patent portfolios is essential to strengthen bargaining positions in future licensing discussions. Given the influx of new vertical industries into the telecommunications ecosystem, companies must assess cross-industry licensing risks carefully. Establishing flexible FRAND response strategies will help mitigate potential disputes and avoid passive positioning in next-generation technical standards. This proactive approach ensures better alignment with emerging commercial realities and legal expectations surrounding standard-essential patents.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["G新技术与垂直应用将重塑SEP价值创造逻辑", "主流通信巨头在IPBC Europe探讨未来许可趋势", "新服务模式可能引发SEP许可规则的重大调整"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New 6G technologies and vertical applications are expected to fundamentally reshape the logic of SEP value creation.", "Major communication giants discussed future licensing trends and strategic implications during IPBC Europe sessions.", "Emerging service models may trigger significant adjustments to existing SEP licensing rules and frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提前布局6G专利以增强未来许可谈判筹码", "评估跨界许可风险并建立灵活FRAND应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Build early 6G patent portfolios to enhance leverage in future licensing negotiations.", "Practice note: Evaluate cross-industry licensing risks and develop flexible FRAND compliance strategies.", "What to monitor: Shifts in SEP licensing paradigms driven by new verticals and service models."]
core_points_en: New 6G technologies and vertical applications are expected to fundamentally reshape the logic of SEP value creation.
Major communication giants discussed future licensing trends and strategic implications during IPBC Europe sessions.
Emerging service models may trigger significant adjustments to existing SEP licensing rules and frameworks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Build early 6G patent portfolios to enhance leverage in future licensing negotiations.
Practice note: Evaluate cross-industry licensing risks and develop flexible FRAND compliance strategies.
What to monitor: Shifts in SEP licensing paradigms driven by new verticals and service models.

items.title: European patent offices raise official fees
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利机构上调官方费用
items.title_en: EPO and UKIPO Implement Official Fee Increases, Ending Long-Standing Price Freezes
items.summary: Two of Europe’s most important IP offices have implemented fee increases this month, with some long-standing UK charges, dating back to the late 1990s, finally seeing a rise.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲两家重要知识产权机构本月实施费用上调。其中，英国部分长期未变的收费标准（可追溯至20世纪90年代末）此次迎来上涨。原文未提供具体费率数值或详细调整清单，仅确认了费用增加的事实及涉及的主要区域。
items.ai_summary_en: Two of Europe’s most significant intellectual property offices, the European Patent Office (EPO) and the United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), have officially implemented increases to their official fees this month. Notably, certain UK charges that had remained unchanged since the late 1990s are seeing their first adjustment in over two decades. These fee revisions reflect the growing operational costs required to maintain the intellectual property system. Consequently, applicants and rights holders will face higher expenditures for patent applications and maintenance within the European region. Market participants operating in or targeting Europe should closely monitor these changes, as they directly impact budget planning and portfolio management strategies across both jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认欧英两地专利官费上涨事实。实务中需关注后续官方发布的详细费率表，以评估对专利申请及维持成本的具体影响，建议持续监测各局官网公告。
items.ai_insight_en: The simultaneous fee hikes by major European offices signal a structural shift in IP administration costs. Rights holders should immediately review existing patent portfolios to assess cost-benefit ratios, particularly for non-core or low-value assets where abandonment may optimize budgets. For new filings, teams must recalculate return on investment projections to account for sustained higher fees. Integrating these increased costs into long-term strategic planning is essential to manage financial exposure effectively while maintaining robust protection across key European markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲两家重要知识产权机构已于本月正式实施官方费用上调措施", "英国部分自20世纪90年代末以来长期保持不变的收费标准此次出现上涨"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO and UKIPO have simultaneously implemented a new round of official fee increases for patent procedures.", "Specific UK patent fee items have ended a freeze lasting more than twenty years with formal price adjustments.", "These adjustments will directly increase corporate costs for patent applications and maintenance in Europe."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["确认欧洲主要专利局及英国知识产权局已启动新一轮官费调整机制", "英国部分历史悠久的收费项目结束长期冻结状态，呈现上涨趋势", "需等待官方发布完整费率细则，以便准确核算后续业务成本变动"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Immediate screening of European patent portfolios is needed to identify and drop low-value patents to offset rising costs.", "Practice note: Re-evaluate new application budgets and incorporate official fee growth into long-term intellectual property strategic planning.", "What to monitor: Future announcements from EPO and UKIPO regarding potential further adjustments or transitional provisions for pending cases."]
core_points_en: The EPO and UKIPO have simultaneously implemented a new round of official fee increases for patent procedures.
Specific UK patent fee items have ended a freeze lasting more than twenty years with formal price adjustments.
These adjustments will directly increase corporate costs for patent applications and maintenance in Europe.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Immediate screening of European patent portfolios is needed to identify and drop low-value patents to offset rising costs.
Practice note: Re-evaluate new application budgets and incorporate official fee growth into long-term intellectual property strategic planning.
What to monitor: Future announcements from EPO and UKIPO regarding potential further adjustments or transitional provisions for pending cases.

items.summary: Accord PPH avec les États-Unis llegrand@inpi.fr 02 avril 2026 Les conditions pour utiliser le PPH Les déposants peuvent demander l'accélération du traitement de leur demande auprès de l'un des deux offices à partir du moment où elle aura été examiné par l'autre office, indépendamment de l'ordre chronologique des dépôts et des revendications de priorité. De manière classique, après dépôt d'une requête PPH, le traitement d'une demande de brevet déposée à l'INPI et revendiquant la priorité d'une de
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国工业产权局（INPI）宣布与美国专利商标局（USPTO）建立专利审查高速路（PPH）机制。申请人若其申请已获一方办公室认定为可授权，可请求另一方加速审查，不受申请顺序限制。该程序在法国免费，旨在缩短审查周期，美国平均九个月内发出审查意见，要求权利要求范围相同或更窄。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) has announced a Patent Prosecution Highway (PPH) agreement with the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). This mechanism allows applicants to request accelerated examination in either jurisdiction once the corresponding application has been examined by the other office, regardless of filing chronology or priority claims. Specifically, if the USPTO deems certain claims patentable, an INPI application with similar or narrower claims may qualify for fast-track processing. Conversely, INPI can accelerate examination for USPTO applications claiming priority from an INPI filing deemed patentable. The PPH request is free of charge in France, while the USPTO typically issues a patentability opinion within nine months under this program.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在法美同时布局专利时，应充分利用此免费加速通道。建议优先在美或法获取有利审查结果，随即启动 PPH 请求，以低成本快速获得双方法域专利权，提升海外维权与市场准入效率。
items.ai_insight_en: This agreement provides a cost-effective pathway for rights holders managing parallel patent portfolios in France and the United States. By leveraging positive examination outcomes from one office, applicants can potentially shorten the pendency period in the other jurisdiction without incurring additional official fees for the acceleration request. This alignment supports more predictable prosecution timelines for transatlantic intellectual property strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法美双向加速：任一局先行审查合格后，另一局可依请求加速处理", "权利要求一致性：加速申请需基于已被认定可授权的相同或更窄权利要求", "零成本高效率：法国境内提交 PPH 请求免费，显著缩短获权等待时间"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Accelerated examination is available bidirectionally between INPI and USPTO once the counterpart office determines claims are patentable.", "Claims in the second-filed application must be sufficiently similar or narrower than those accepted as patentable by the first examining office.", "PPH requests at INPI are free of charge, aiming to reduce procedural delays compared to standard examination timelines."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用单方授权结果，低成本实现法美双地快速确权", "优化申请策略，通过 PPH 机制缩短产品上市前的法律不确定期"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders with dual filings in France and the US can leverage positive examination results to expedite grant procedures in the counterpart jurisdiction.", "Practice note: Ensure that claims submitted to the second office are drafted to match or restrict the scope of claims already found patentable by the first office.", "What to monitor: Track the specific procedural requirements and timing expectations for PPH requests at both INPI and USPTO to optimize docketing strategies."]
core_points_en: Accelerated examination is available bidirectionally between INPI and USPTO once the counterpart office determines claims are patentable.
Claims in the second-filed application must be sufficiently similar or narrower than those accepted as patentable by the first examining office.
PPH requests at INPI are free of charge, aiming to reduce procedural delays compared to standard examination timelines.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders with dual filings in France and the US can leverage positive examination results to expedite grant procedures in the counterpart jurisdiction.
Practice note: Ensure that claims submitted to the second office are drafted to match or restrict the scope of claims already found patentable by the first office.
What to monitor: Track the specific procedural requirements and timing expectations for PPH requests at both INPI and USPTO to optimize docketing strategies.

items.title: Samsung brands $1 billion InterDigital patent deal ‘outlandish’, ‘unsustainable’
items.title_zh: 三星斥 InterDigital 十亿美元专利交易荒谬且不可持续
items.title_en: Samsung Labels $1 Billion InterDigital SEP Deal Outlandish Amid Rising Royalty Costs
items.summary: The South Korean phone maker issued a rare public statement about the SEP licensing environment, as its financial reports reveal hundreds of millions of dollars in royalty payments each year
items.ai_summary_zh: 韩国手机制造商三星发布罕见公开声明，抨击与 InterDigital 达成的十亿美元专利许可协议“荒谬”且“不可持续”。此举背景是三星财报显示其每年需支付数亿美元特许权使用费。该事件凸显了当前标准必要专利（SEP）许可环境中，实施者与权利人之间关于 FRAND 费率及累积许可成本的激烈博弈，可能引发后续法律或谈判行动。
items.ai_summary_en: Samsung has issued a rare public statement criticizing the standard essential patent licensing environment, specifically branding its recent one billion dollar agreement with InterDigital as outlandish and unsustainable. This move follows revelations in Samsung’s financial reports that it pays hundreds of millions of dollars annually in royalty fees. The dispute highlights the intense friction between implementers and rights holders regarding fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory rates and cumulative licensing costs. As major technology firms face escalating financial pressures from patent portfolios, this high-profile conflict underscores the growing challenges in enforcing FRAND principles globally. It signals potential for further legal actions or renegotiations, reflecting broader market tensions over the valuation and aggregation of SEP royalties in the telecommunications sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国手机厂商在欧洲常面临类似高额 SEP 索偿。应建立专利费累积预警机制，积极参与全球费率诉讼以确立合理基准，避免被动接受不合理条款，同时加强自身专利储备以增加谈判筹码。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe and the US should closely monitor how high-value SEP disputes influence judicial interpretations of FRAND commitments. Implementers facing cumulative royalty demands must establish robust internal mechanisms to track and forecast total licensing liabilities, preventing cost overruns. Active participation in global rate-setting litigation is crucial to establishing favorable precedents and avoiding passive acceptance of potentially unreasonable terms. Strengthening proprietary patent portfolios remains a key strategy to enhance bargaining power during negotiations. Rights holders should also anticipate increased scrutiny on aggregate royalty burdens, which may shape future licensing strategies and regulatory approaches across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["三星公开谴责十亿美元级 SEP 许可交易缺乏合理性", "财报揭示巨头每年巨额专利费支出，凸显成本压力", "全球 SEP 许可环境紧张，FRAND 原则执行面临新挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Samsung publicly condemned the one billion dollar SEP licensing transaction with InterDigital as lacking commercial reasonableness and sustainability.", "Financial disclosures reveal massive annual royalty expenditures, highlighting significant cost pressures faced by major device manufacturers.", "Global SEP licensing tensions are rising, presenting new challenges to the consistent application and enforcement of FRAND principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立专利费累积预警，防范多重收费导致成本失控", "主动参与全球费率诉讼，争取有利 FRAND 裁决基准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implementers must develop early warning systems for cumulative patent fees to prevent uncontrolled cost escalation from multiple licensors.", "Practice note: Proactively engage in global rate-setting litigation to secure favorable FRAND benchmarks rather than accepting unilateral demands.", "What to monitor: Watch for shifts in judicial attitudes toward aggregate royalty caps and the impact of public corporate statements on negotiation dynamics."]
core_points_en: Samsung publicly condemned the one billion dollar SEP licensing transaction with InterDigital as lacking commercial reasonableness and sustainability.
Financial disclosures reveal massive annual royalty expenditures, highlighting significant cost pressures faced by major device manufacturers.
Global SEP licensing tensions are rising, presenting new challenges to the consistent application and enforcement of FRAND principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implementers must develop early warning systems for cumulative patent fees to prevent uncontrolled cost escalation from multiple licensors.
Practice note: Proactively engage in global rate-setting litigation to secure favorable FRAND benchmarks rather than accepting unilateral demands.
What to monitor: Watch for shifts in judicial attitudes toward aggregate royalty caps and the impact of public corporate statements on negotiation dynamics.

items.title: UKIPO fee rises from 1 April 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局自2026年4月1日起上调官方费用
items.title_en: UKIPO Official Fees Increase by Approximately 25 Percent Effective 1 April 2026
items.summary: The UKIPO’s official fees have increased from today and further guidance has now been provided on how the new fees will apply.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）已将其官方费用上调约25%，并发布了新费用适用指南。此次调整旨在应对自上次商标费用调整（1998年）以来估计32%的通胀增长，并为UKIPO计划中的新系统投资提供资金支持。完整修订费用表可在UKIPO官网查询。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office has implemented an approximate twenty-five percent increase in its official fees, effective 1 April 2026. This adjustment addresses an estimated thirty-two percent rise in inflation since the last fee update for trade marks in 1998. The revenue generated aims to support the UKIPO’s planned investment in new operational systems. The office has published additional guidance detailing how these new fees apply to pending and future filings. Rights holders and practitioners should consult the complete schedule of revised fees available on the UKIPO website to ensure accurate budgeting and compliance with the updated payment requirements for patents, trade marks, and designs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类政策更新给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This significant fee adjustment impacts portfolio maintenance costs for rights holders with substantial UK holdings. The link to system modernization suggests potential procedural changes may follow. Practitioners should verify current fee structures for all pending transactions and review annual renewal budgets. items.title: Sisvel launches new point-of-sales device pool with Nokia, Huawei, LG Electronics on board
items.title_zh: Sisvel联合诺基亚华为LG推出销售点设备专利池
items.title_en: Sisvel Launches New Point-of-Sale Patent Pool with Nokia, Huawei, and LG Electronics
items.summary: New offering adds to growing number of multilateral IoT licensing efforts
items.ai_summary_zh: Sisvel宣布启动新的销售点（POS）设备专利池，诺基亚、华为和LG电子已加入该计划。此举旨在应对物联网领域日益增长的多边许可需求，为相关设备制造商提供一站式许可解决方案。
items.ai_summary_en: Licensing administrator Sisvel has announced the launch of a new patent pool specifically targeting point-of-sale (POS) devices. Major industry participants, including Nokia, Huawei, and LG Electronics, have joined the initiative. This development aims to consolidate standard-essential patents from multiple stakeholders, thereby simplifying the complex multilateral licensing processes prevalent in the Internet of Things sector, particularly for retail terminal equipment. The move reflects a broader trend within the European market toward more integrated and specialized SEP licensing models, addressing the growing need for efficient access to essential technologies in connected retail environments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注该专利池涵盖的具体专利范围及费率结构。随着物联网设备碎片化加剧，此类多边许可模式可能成为行业主流，建议持续跟踪后续被许可人签约情况及潜在的反垄断合规动态。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting IoT terminals to Europe should carefully assess potential exposure to this new patent pool. It is advisable to evaluate standard-essential patent risks associated with POS devices early in the product lifecycle. Companies should proactively monitor licensing terms and consider engagement strategies to mitigate risks of injunctions or litigation. Furthermore, rights holders may leverage the participation of major entities like Huawei to optimize their global licensing negotiations and align with evolving industry standards for efficient IP clearance.

items.title: Statutory guidance: Directions for the use of the One IPO Digital Services
items.title_zh: UKIPO发布One IPO数字服务使用指引，明确电子提交文件的形式要求
items.title_en: UKIPO Issues Statutory Directions for One IPO Digital Services Document Delivery
items.summary: Directions under section 124A of the Patents Act 1977 for the form and manner in which documents can be delivered using the One IPO Digital Services.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布《One IPO Digital Services 使用指引》。官方页面说明，《1977年专利法》第124A条允许局长指定向知识产权局递交电子文件的形式和方式，而该指引具体列出了使用 One IPO 数字服务时应满足的要求。该页面于 2026 年 4 月 1 日发布，并附有补充说明文件。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published statutory guidance detailing the form and manner for delivering documents via One IPO Digital Services. Issued under section 124A of the Patents Act 1977, these directions establish the technical and procedural requirements for electronic submissions. This update formalizes the digital interface standards for patent proceedings, ensuring that filings made through the unified platform comply with statutory delivery obligations. Rights holders and representatives must adhere to these specified formats to maintain valid service and avoid procedural defects in their interactions with the Office.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这项指引属于数字化提交流程的基础性程序更新。对通过英国在线系统办理专利事务的申请人和代理人而言，合规重点已从单纯会不会线上提交，转向是否按照 One IPO 新流程和文件格式要求完成递交与留痕。
items.ai_insight_en: This guidance clarifies the procedural baseline for digital interactions with the UKIPO. For European and US practitioners, understanding these specific delivery mechanics is essential to ensure that electronic filings constitute valid service under UK law. Compliance with the stated form and manner prevents administrative rejections or delays arising from non-conforming submission formats within the One IPO ecosystem.

This comprehensive document outlines the standard administrative steps required from the initial filing of a patent application through to the initiation of search and substantive examination processes. Furthermore, it provides specific operational norms for legally adding or correcting inventor details on existing applications. The guidance aims to offer applicants clear direction on navigating these essential bureaucratic requirements, ensuring that all procedural aspects are handled correctly to maintain the validity and enforceability of their intellectual property rights within the UK jurisdiction.

Familiarity with this updated guidance is essential to prevent formal defects that could compromise rights stability or cause unnecessary examination delays. Ensuring strict compliance with these administrative standards helps safeguard intellectual property assets and maintains the robustness of patent portfolios against potential validity challenges arising from procedural non-compliance during the prosecution phase.

items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has officially launched a new suite of digital services designed to streamline the administration of UK patents. These online tools enable applicants and rights holders to apply for new patents, manage existing portfolio items, and process renewals digitally. This launch marks a shift toward fully digital interaction for core patent lifecycle events at the UKIPO. The announcement confirms the immediate availability of these platforms for public use, aiming to modernize the interface between the office and users. No detailed technical specifications or transitional arrangements were provided in this initial brief notice.

items.title: CJEU on continuity, heritage, and deceptive trademarks – Fauré Le Page Paris 1717
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定商标标注虚构年份可能构成欺骗
items.title_en: CJEU Clarifies Deception Threshold for Heritage Marks: Fauré Le Page Paris 1717
items.summary: To which extent can a sign invoke heritage, age, and prestige before it crosses into deception? In the luxury sector, a trademark’s reference to a long history can signal continuity, know-how, and quality, making the line between genuine legacy and misleading branding especially thin. On 26 March 2026, the CJEU drew that boundary by stating that the difference lay in whether a business had been acquired as a whole or whether only the trademark had changed hands (C‑412/24).
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 Fauré Le Page 案中明确，若企业仅受让商标而非整体业务，却在商标中加入暗示悠久历史的年份（如 1717），可能因误导消费者关于商品连续性、技艺及声望而构成欺骗。法院强调，欺骗须涉及商品特征，但在奢侈品领域，声誉形象属于关键特征。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C‑412/24, the Court of Justice of the European Union addressed whether a trademark incorporating a historical date constitutes deception under Article 3(1)(g) of Directive 2008/95/EC. The case concerned 'Fauré Le Page Paris 1717', filed by an entity that acquired only the trademark rights after the original business ceased operations in 1992. The CJEU held that using a date implying centuries of continuity can mislead consumers regarding the goods' characteristics, specifically heritage and know-how, when no actual business transfer occurred. The Court emphasized that while deception must relate to product characteristics, reputation and prestige are intrinsic features in the luxury sector, making such misleading indications sufficient to establish a risk of deception.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧注册含历史元素的奢侈品商标时，需确保商业实体与历史传承的真实性关联。若仅收购商标而无业务延续，应避免使用暗示悠久历史的年份或描述，以防被认定为欺骗性商标导致无效或被禁。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the boundary between legitimate brand heritage marketing and deceptive practices in the EU. Rights holders acquiring legacy marks must ensure that any reference to historical dates or longevity accurately reflects the continuity of the business entity and its know-how. Misrepresenting the origin or history of production capabilities, particularly in sectors where reputation drives consumer choice, poses significant validity risks. Practitioners should carefully assess the factual basis of historical claims embedded in new filings or rebranding efforts following asset-only acquisitions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅受让商标未承接整体业务时，标注历史年份易致欺骗", "奢侈品商标的声望形象被视为商品本身的重要特征", "虚构年份暗示技艺传承若无事实依据则违反商标指令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A trademark including a historical date may be deceptive if it falsely implies continuity of know-how from an original undertaking to the current owner.", "Deception under EU law requires misrepresentation of goods' characteristics; however, prestige and heritage are key characteristics in the luxury market.", "Acquiring only trademark rights without the underlying historic business does not justify claims of long-standing tradition or inherited craftsmanship."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["收购旧品牌商标时需核实是否包含整体业务资产", "避免在无实际传承时使用历史年份营销以防法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands acquiring legacy trademarks must verify if historical references in new marks align with actual business continuity to avoid invalidity for deception.", "Practice note: When filing marks with historical elements post-acquisition, distinguish between mere trademark ownership and the transfer of the underlying undertaking's goodwill…", "What to monitor: How national courts apply this CJEU standard to other luxury sectors where brand prestige and perceived heritage significantly influence consumer purchasing decis…"]
core_points_en: A trademark including a historical date may be deceptive if it falsely implies continuity of know-how from an original undertaking to the current owner.
Deception under EU law requires misrepresentation of goods' characteristics; however, prestige and heritage are key characteristics in the luxury market.
Acquiring only trademark rights without the underlying historic business does not justify claims of long-standing tradition or inherited craftsmanship.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands acquiring legacy trademarks must verify if historical references in new marks align with actual business continuity to avoid invalidity for deception.
Practice note: When filing marks with historical elements post-acquisition, distinguish between mere trademark ownership and the transfer of the underlying undertaking's goodwill…
What to monitor: How national courts apply this CJEU standard to other luxury sectors where brand prestige and perceived heritage significantly influence consumer purchasing decis…
