items.title: WIPO Launches Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange
items.title_zh: WIPO 启动人工智能基础设施交流平台
items.title_en: WIPO Launches Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange for Expert Dialogue
items.summary: WIPO today launched the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange (AIII), a new initiative for expert dialogue on IP and AI issues that will focus on technical and operational issues and complement ongoing discussions at other WIPO forums.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）正式推出“人工智能基础设施交流平台”（AIII）。该倡议旨在建立专家对话机制，聚焦知识产权与人工智能领域的技术及运营议题。作为现有 WIPO 论坛讨论的补充，AIII 将专注于解决实际操作层面的问题，促进全球范围内关于 AI 技术架构与 IP 规则衔接的专业交流，为后续政策制定提供技术支撑。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has officially launched the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange (AIII). This new initiative establishes a dedicated platform for expert dialogue focusing specifically on technical and operational issues at the intersection of intellectual property and artificial intelligence. The AIII is designed to complement ongoing discussions within other WIPO forums by addressing practical infrastructure matters, thereby supporting broader policy conversations with specialized technical insights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，国际后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and technology companies, this development signals a continued institutional focus on the operational realities of AI deployment within the IP framework. While the immediate output is dialogue rather than binding regulation, the technical definitions and operational standards discussed may influence future global harmonization efforts. Stakeholders should track how these technical discussions evolve alongside broader policy debates to anticipate shifts in international IP norms regarding AI infrastructure.

items.title: L’EUIPO enregistre le plus grand nombre de dépôts de son histoire
items.title_zh: 欧盟知识产权局商标注册量创历史新高
items.title_en: EUIPO Records Highest Volume of Trademark Applications in Its History
items.summary: L’Office de l’Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO) est l’agence de l’Union européenne (UE) chargée de la gestion des marques de l’Union européenne, des dessins et modèles enregistrés de l’Union européenne, des indications géographiques pour les produits artisanaux et industriels, ainsi que de la coopération européenne et internationale dans le domaine de la (suite) L’article L’EUIPO enregistre le plus grand nombre de dépôts de son histoire est apparu en premier sur Lavoix -
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）记录的商标申请数量达到其历史最高水平。摘要仅重申EUIPO作为欧盟机构，负责管理欧盟商标、注册外观设计及手工艺和工业产品地理标志的职能，未提供具体统计数据或背景分析。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has announced that it recorded the highest number of trademark applications in its history. As the agency responsible for managing EU trademarks, registered Community designs, and geographical indications for craft and industrial products, EUIPO’s data reflects robust demand for brand protection within the internal market. This trend indicates that companies are actively consolidating their legal standing in the single market through EU trademark registrations to address intensifying commercial competition and challenges associated with global expansion strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文内容缺失，仅能确认申请量创新高这一事实。需等待完整报告发布，以观察具体增长幅度、主要申请人类别及行业分布，从而评估欧盟市场品牌保护活跃度的实际变化趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: A sharp increase in application volumes typically elevates the risk of examination backlogs at the EUIPO, potentially extending authorization timelines. Stakeholders should proactively plan brand portfolios rather than delaying filings until just before product launches. Adopting a combined strategy utilizing both EU-wide and national member state trademarks may offer strategic advantages for securing timely protection. Monitoring EUIPO procedural updates is essential to ensure core brands receive stable and efficient legal coverage in key markets amidst growing administrative pressure.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题明确指出EUIPO记录的商标申请数量创下该机构历史上的最高纪录", "原文摘要确认EUIPO是负责管理欧盟商标及注册外观设计的欧盟官方机构"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO achieves a historic record high in trademark filings, signaling a significant surge in registration demand across the region.", "The agency's mandate encompasses the administration of EU trademarks, registered designs, and geographical indications for various products.", "Data highlights heightened corporate prioritization of brand protection strategies within the European Union single market."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需关注后续完整数据报告中关于申请量同比增长的具体数值", "观察主要申请来源国及热门商品服务类别的分布变化", "监测EUIPO是否同步发布关于审查积压或处理效率的配套说明"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Surging application volumes may cause examination delays, necessitating earlier brand filing schedules to avoid market entry risks.", "Practice note: Consider combining EU trademark applications with national filings to optimize protection speed and scope where appropriate.", "What to monitor: Track EUIPO operational metrics and examination trends to adjust filing strategies for maintaining timely legal coverage."]

The agency's mandate encompasses the administration of EU trademarks, registered designs, and geographical indications for various products.

items.title: Court of Appeal tests the UPC’s long arm: the UPC’s first CJEU referral
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院测试长臂管辖：首次向欧盟法院提请裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Tests Long-Arm Jurisdiction in First CJEU Referral
items.summary: Dyson Technology Ltd v Dreame International (Hong Kong) Ltd and others – Court of Appeal, 6 March 2026, UPC_CoA_789/2025, UPC_CoA_813/2025
items.ai_summary_zh: 在戴森诉追觅专利侵权案中，UPC上诉法院确认并扩展了汉堡地方法院颁发的临时禁令。针对涉及西班牙部分及非UPC成员国管辖权争议，法院首次向欧盟法院提请初步裁决，询问关于第三国被告及中介概念的法律解释，以界定UPC对欧盟境外被告及非UPC成员国行为的管辖边界。
items.ai_summary_en: In Dyson Technology Ltd v Dreame International (Hong Kong) Ltd, the UPC Court of Appeal confirmed and extended a preliminary injunction across UPC territory. Simultaneously, it issued the first referral to the CJEU regarding the limits of the UPC’s international jurisdiction and the legal definition of an intermediary. The case addresses whether the UPC can exercise jurisdiction over non-EU defendants and acts occurring in EU Member States not party to the UPC Agreement, such as Spain. By splitting the proceedings, the Court stayed appeals concerning Spanish relief while deciding on UPC-wide measures. This landmark referral seeks clarity on applying Brussels I bis Regulation provisions to common court structures, potentially defining the scope of cross-border patent enforcement against entities outside the European Union.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案作为UPC上诉法院首次向欧盟法院提请裁决，其核心在于厘清UPC长臂管辖权的法律边界。需密切关注欧盟法院对涉及第三国被告及非UPC成员国侵权行为管辖权的最终解释，这将确立UPC跨境执法的关键先例。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the expanding reach of the Unified Patent Court, signaling that entities established outside the EU may face jurisdictional exposure through local distributors or authorized representatives. Companies should carefully evaluate their European distribution architectures to mitigate risks of being classified as liable intermediaries in infringement cases. Legal teams must monitor the upcoming CJEU ruling closely, as its interpretation of jurisdictional links will significantly influence cross-border litigation strategies and defense planning for multinational corporations operating within the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院确认并扩展了针对UPC领土范围内的临时禁令效力", "法院就UPC对欧盟境外被告及非UPC成员国行为的管辖权向欧盟法院提请首次初步裁决", "案件涉及对《布鲁塞尔条例》中“不可调和判决”及“中介”概念的欧盟法律解释问题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal confirmed and extended the preliminary injunction across the entire UPC territory for the patent holder.", "This marks the first CJEU referral by the UPC Court of Appeal, focusing on the boundaries of international jurisdiction.", "Core disputes involve liability attribution for non-EU defendants and acts within non-UPC EU Member States like Spain."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC首次启动向欧盟法院的初步裁决程序，具有里程碑式的程序意义", "裁决结果将直接决定UPC能否对位于欧盟境外的被告行使管辖权", "需观察欧盟法院如何界定《布鲁塞尔条例》在UPC框架下的适用范围"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-EU entities risk UPC long-arm jurisdiction via local sales channels or representative appointments.", "Practice note: Review European supply chains to prevent classification as infringing intermediaries or joint tortfeasors.", "What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment will define critical thresholds for international jurisdiction and intermediary liability."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal confirmed and extended the preliminary injunction across the entire UPC territory for the patent holder.
This marks the first CJEU referral by the UPC Court of Appeal, focusing on the boundaries of international jurisdiction.
Core disputes involve liability attribution for non-EU defendants and acts within non-UPC EU Member States like Spain.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-EU entities risk UPC long-arm jurisdiction via local sales channels or representative appointments.
Practice note: Review European supply chains to prevent classification as infringing intermediaries or joint tortfeasors.
What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment will define critical thresholds for international jurisdiction and intermediary liability.

items.title: [Blog] Révision du programme PACE : effet au 1er février 2026
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局修订 PACE 计划：2026 年 2 月起仅限加速审查
items.title_en: EPO Revises PACE Program: Accelerated Search Option Removed as of February 2026
items.summary: Depuis 2015, l'Office européen des brevets (OEB) vise à améliorer durablement les délais dans lesquels le rapport de recherche européen...
items.ai_summary_zh: 自 2015 年起，欧洲专利局致力于缩短检索报告发出时间，2024 年平均耗时已降至 5.5 个月。鉴于此效率提升，欧专局决定自 2026 年 2 月 1 日起修订 PACE 加速程序，将其适用范围限制在实质审查阶段。自此，申请人将无法再请求加速检索过程，但仍可加速获取首次审查意见及授权通知。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has revised the Programme for Accelerated prosecution of European patent applications (PACE), effective 1 February 2026. Following sustained improvements in search efficiency, with the average time for issuing extended or partial European search reports dropping to 5.5 months in 2024, the EPO determined that accelerated search processing is no longer necessary. Consequently, the mechanism to request acceleration during the search phase has been discontinued. The updated PACE program now exclusively facilitates accelerated substantive examination, allowing applicants to receive the first examination report and Rule 71(3) communications more rapidly.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请需调整策略，不再能依赖 PACE 加速检索。建议利用现有检索速度优势，提前布局实审阶段的加速请求，以平衡整体获权周期，避免因规则变更导致的时间预期偏差。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing European portfolios, the removal of the accelerated search option requires an adjustment in procedural expectations. Since the EPO cites improved baseline search timelines as the rationale, applicants should anticipate standard search durations rather than relying on PACE for early results. Strategic focus must now shift entirely to leveraging PACE during the examination phase to maintain overall prosecution speed. Monitoring future EPO communications regarding search timeline metrics will help determine if further procedural adjustments are warranted.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧专局因检索效率显著提升，决定收缩 PACE 计划适用范围", "自 2026 年 2 月 1 日起，PACE 程序不再支持加速检索阶段请求", "修订后 PACE 仅保留加速实质审查及后续授权通知的功能"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Effective 1 February 2026, the EPO removed the option to request accelerated processing during the European search phase under the PACE program.", "This revision follows a strategic decision to limit PACE acceleration mechanisms solely to the substantive examination stage of proceedings.", "Applicants may still utilize PACE to expedite the issuance of the first examination report and subsequent grant notifications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["取消检索加速选项，要求企业重新规划欧专申请的时间表", "应聚焦实审阶段加速策略，以应对新规则下的审查节奏变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Portfolio managers must update prosecution timelines to reflect that search phases will proceed at standard speeds, removing previous acceleration options.", "Practice note: Ensure Form 1005 requests submitted after 1 February 2026 are strictly limited to accelerating substantive examination, as search acceleration is no longer availabl…", "What to monitor: Watch for EPO updates on average search report issuance times to assess whether the baseline efficiency justifies the permanent removal of the search acceleration…"]
core_points_en: Effective 1 February 2026, the EPO removed the option to request accelerated processing during the European search phase under the PACE program.
This revision follows a strategic decision to limit PACE acceleration mechanisms solely to the substantive examination stage of proceedings.
Applicants may still utilize PACE to expedite the issuance of the first examination report and subsequent grant notifications.

Practice note: Ensure Form 1005 requests submitted after 1 February 2026 are strictly limited to accelerating substantive examination, as search acceleration is no longer availabl…

items.title: Droit d'auteur et étiquettes de Champagne : confirmation du rejet de la théorie de l'accessoire et de la cession tacite
items.title_zh: 香槟标签版权案：法法院拒认附属理论及默示转让
items.title_en: Douai Court Rejects Accessory Theory and Implied License in Champagne Label Copyright Dispute
items.summary: La Cour d'appel de Douai confirme qu'une œuvre reproduite sur des bouteilles de champagne sans autorisation constitue une contrefaçon. La décision du 20 novembre 2025 rappelle que ni la théorie...
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国杜埃上诉法院于2025年11月20日确认，未经授权使用作品构成版权侵权。法院驳回香槟生产商关于“附属理论”及“默示权利转让”的抗辩，维持里尔法院原判。涉案作品被用于瓶标、包装及网站，法院认定其直接参与产品视觉识别与商业增值，非次要或偶然出现，故不适用附属理论例外。
items.ai_summary_en: The Douai Court of Appeal upheld a finding of copyright infringement against a Champagne producer for reproducing an artist's work on labels, packaging, and digital media without authorization. The November 20, 2025 decision reinforces the strict interpretation of the author’s exploitation monopoly. The court explicitly rejected the 'accessory theory' defense, ruling that the artwork was central to the product's commercial appeal rather than a secondary element. Furthermore, it denied the existence of an implied license, emphasizing that property rights cannot be transferred without a clear written contract. This case illustrates that purchasing the physical support of a sculpture does not grant reproduction rights, highlighting significant risks for companies using artistic works in branding without explicit licensing agreements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案重申法国法院对作者专有权的严格解释。若受保护作品贡献于产品经济吸引力或差异化战略，即被视为主要元素而非附属品。企业需注意，仅购买实物载体不等于获得复制权，缺乏书面许可时，附属理论与默示转让难以作为有效抗辩理由。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe must avoid relying on oral agreements or assumptions that background artistic elements do not require specific authorization. French courts strictly limit the 'accessory theory,' meaning even seemingly auxiliary designs can trigger liability if they enhance commercial attractiveness. Rights holders should ensure comprehensive written licensing agreements are executed before using any third-party artistic material. These contracts must clearly define the scope, duration, and media channels of use. Failure to secure explicit rights exposes companies to high infringement damages and injunctions, as mere ownership of a physical artwork does not imply reproduction rights for commercial branding purposes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["杜埃上诉法院确认未经授权在香槟标签使用作品构成侵权，驳回附属理论抗辩", "法院认定作品用于商业标识与增值，非次要偶然出现，故不适用附属理论例外", "法院拒绝承认默示权利转让，强调无清晰合同时企业不受保护，维持侵权定罪"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Court rejects accessory theory, confirming the artwork is a core commercial differentiation element rather than a minor background feature.", "Implied license claim denied; transfer of property rights requires a clear, explicit written contract under French copyright law.", "Unauthorized use of artistic works on packaging and digital platforms constitutes confirmed copyright infringement."]

items.title: [Blog] Propriété Intellectuelle : Moteur De Croissance, D’emplois Et D’exportations En Europe
items.title_zh: 知识产权：欧洲增长、就业与出口引擎
items.title_en: EPO and EUIPO Study: IP-Intensive Sectors Drive EU Economic Growth, Employment, and Exports
items.summary: Une nouvelle étude de l'Office européen des brevets (OEB) et de l'Office de l'Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO) s’est intéressée...
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO与EUIPO发布2021-2023年研究报告，显示知识产权密集型行业占欧盟GDP的48%，提供30.6%的就业岗位，且薪资高出平均水平40.9%。此类行业主导对外贸易，占欧盟进口76.4%及非欧盟出口78.3%。研究还揭示IP强度与初创企业融资呈正相关，EPO为此推出Deep Tech Finder工具以辅助投资对接。
items.ai_summary_en: A joint study by the European Patent Office (EPO) and the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) analyzes intellectual property rights usage from 2021 to 2023. The research identifies 361 IP-intensive sectors, which collectively generate 48% of the European Union’s gross domestic product and account for 30.6% of all jobs within the bloc. These industries offer salaries approximately 40.9% higher than the broader European economy average. Patent-intensive sectors are predominantly found in technology and healthcare, while trademark-intensive areas focus on fashion, luxury goods, and creative industries reliant on copyright. Furthermore, these sectors dominate external trade, representing 76.4% of EU imports and 78.3% of exports to non-EU countries. The study also highlights a strong positive correlation between IP intensity and startup financing, prompting the EPO to launch the Deep Tech Finder tool to facilitate investment connections for high-potential innovative enterprises across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本研究量化了IP对欧盟宏观经济的贡献，证实高IP强度与高薪资、高出口及强融资能力的关联。实务中可关注EPO新推出的Deep Tech Finder工具，其为识别高价值专利初创企业提供了官方数据支持，有助于投资者与合作伙伴进行尽职调查与市场定位。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating within the European market, robust intellectual property strategies are essential for accessing high-value supply chains and securing competitive advantages. Aligning patent and trademark portfolios with identified IP-intensive sectors can enhance market positioning and facilitate integration into lucrative technological and creative ecosystems. Leveraging official data tools, such as the EPO’s Deep Tech Finder, allows firms to demonstrate technical credibility to potential investors, thereby optimizing fundraising outcomes. Stakeholders should closely monitor compliance requirements and competitive dynamics within these high-density IP environments. Proactive portfolio management and strategic use of available intelligence resources enable businesses to capitalize on policy incentives and expand their commercial footprint effectively across the European Union.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["IP-intensive sectors contribute nearly half of the EU's GDP and over thirty percent of total employment opportunities.", "These industries command significantly higher wages and dominate the European Union's import and export trade volumes.", "Strong IP portfolios correlate with increased startup funding, supported by new EPO tools for investor matching."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["IP密集型产业在欧盟经济中占据近半壁江山，是政策保护与资源倾斜的核心领域", "EPO推出的Deep Tech Finder工具为评估初创企业技术资产价值提供了新的官…", "需持续观察后续基于此数据的政策调整，特别是在促进创新融资与国际贸易方面的具体措施"]

These industries command significantly higher wages and dominate the European Union's import and export trade volumes.
Strong IP portfolios correlate with increased startup funding, supported by new EPO tools for investor matching.

Practice note: Utilize official data tools to enhance technical endorsement and optimize financing strategies.
What to monitor: Compliance trends and competitive landscapes within high IP-density industry sectors.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-258/24 Katholische Schwangerschaftsberatung
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：天主教协会不得仅因员工退教而将其解雇
items.title_en: CJEU: Catholic Association Cannot Dismiss Employee Solely for Leaving Church
items.summary: A Catholic association cannot dismiss an employee on the sole ground that the employee concerned has left the Catholic Church
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 C-258/24 案中裁定，基于宗教理念的协会不能仅因员工退出教会而解雇该员工。法院指出，此类解雇需证明该要求对特定活动是真实、合法且正当的。若协会雇佣非信徒从事相同工作，则员工退教本身不构成对协会理念的挑战，最终由成员国法院评估具体情形。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-258/24, the Court of Justice ruled that a Catholic association cannot dismiss an employee solely because they left the Catholic Church. Such dismissal requires proving the religious requirement is genuine, legitimate, and justified by the nature of the activities. The Court emphasized that national courts, not the religious organization, must assess this balance. If non-Catholics perform identical roles, departure from the church does not inherently undermine the employer's ethos. The German Federal Labour Court retains final authority to evaluate the specific circumstances of the case.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中资企业若涉及宗教背景或特定价值观管理，需谨慎制定用工标准。解雇理由必须具备客观正当性，避免因文化差异引发歧视诉讼，建议咨询当地法律顾问合规审查。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the limits of religious autonomy in employment within the EU, requiring objective justification for loyalty clauses. Organizations with ethos-based hiring practices must ensure consistency in role requirements across all employees. Failure to demonstrate why religious adherence is essential for specific tasks may render dismissals discriminatory under EU law.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅因员工退出教会而解雇，原则上不符合欧盟反歧视法律框架", "职业要求的真实性须由国内法院而非教会组织自行评估认定", "若协会雇佣非信徒从事同类工作，退教行为不必然损害其宗教理念"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Dismissal based solely on leaving the Catholic Church is prohibited unless the occupational requirement is proven genuine and justified.", "National courts, rather than religious organizations, hold the authority to assess whether such requirements are legitimate and justified.", "Employing non-Catholics for identical roles suggests that leaving the church does not necessarily challenge the association's ethos."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["用工解雇理由需具备客观正当性，避免触碰反歧视红线", "跨国经营应尊重当地司法对职业要求真实性的最终裁量权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ethos-based employers must review employment contracts to ensure religious loyalty clauses are objectively justified by job functions.", "Practice note: National courts will scrutinize whether non-religious employees perform similar duties, impacting the validity of faith-based dismissal grounds.", "What to monitor: How Member State courts apply the 'genuine, legitimate and justified' test to various religious and philosophical organizations."]
core_points_en: Dismissal based solely on leaving the Catholic Church is prohibited unless the occupational requirement is proven genuine and justified.
National courts, rather than religious organizations, hold the authority to assess whether such requirements are legitimate and justified.
Employing non-Catholics for identical roles suggests that leaving the church does not necessarily challenge the association's ethos.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ethos-based employers must review employment contracts to ensure religious loyalty clauses are objectively justified by job functions.
Practice note: National courts will scrutinize whether non-religious employees perform similar duties, impacting the validity of faith-based dismissal grounds.
What to monitor: How Member State courts apply the 'genuine, legitimate and justified' test to various religious and philosophical organizations.

items.title: Author’s own personality reflected in works of applied art? CJEU rules on subsistence and infringement in mio/konektra
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定实用艺术品版权存续与侵权
items.title_en: CJEU mio/konektra: Copyright Subsistence and Infringement Standards for Applied Art
items.summary: The question of copyright subsistence in EU copyright law will never get old. In the InfoSoc Directive, the EU legislature harmonised some of the economic rights, but left entirely unaddressed the question of what requirements a copyright work should exhibit to be protected in the first place.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在mio/konektra案中裁定，实用艺术品的版权保护不依附于外观设计法，二者无规则与例外关系。只要满足“作者智力创作”的原创性标准及表达要素，即可受版权保护。该案由瑞典和德国法院转呈，涉及Palais Royal桌子等家具的版权侵权争议，重申了Cofemel案确立的累积保护原则。
items.ai_summary_en: In the joined mio/konektra cases referred by Swedish and German courts, the Court of Justice of the EU clarified copyright protection standards for works of applied art. The Court reaffirmed that copyright and design rights operate as independent, parallel regimes without a rule-exception hierarchy. It established that such works qualify for copyright protection solely by meeting two cumulative criteria: constituting an original subject matter and expressing the author's intellectual creation. No additional requirements apply. This ruling resolves longstanding legal tensions regarding overlapping rights by defining how an author’s personality must be reflected in functional objects. The decision provides critical guidance on subsistence and infringement, emphasizing that distinct objectives govern each protection system while ensuring consistent application of originality standards across member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案澄清了实用艺术品在欧盟层面的版权门槛，确认其与外观设计权可累积保护。企业需关注原创性判断标准在工业设计中的应用，避免仅依赖外观设计注册而忽视版权风险。后续应监测 pending 案件对原创性具体认定的细化影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants producing or exporting functional designs to the EU should reassess IP risks, recognizing that design registration does not preclude copyright claims. Products meeting originality thresholds may face dual liability under both regimes. Rights holders must maintain robust documentation proving independent creation and distinctive creative choices during development phases. Legal teams should conduct comprehensive audits covering both design and copyright compliance to mitigate infringement exposure. Given the CJEU’s strict interpretation of originality, demonstrating the author’s personal touch remains essential for defense or enforcement strategies involving furniture and similar applied art categories.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院明确外观设计与版权保护无规则例外关系，二者目标不同且规则独立", "实用艺术品若满足作者智力创作的原创性及表达要素，即符合版权保护标准", "案件源于瑞典和德国法院转呈，涉及家具设计相似性的版权侵权与存续认定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright and design protection systems are independent and parallel, with no hierarchical relationship between them.", "Applied art requires only originality and expression of intellectual creation for copyright protection, excluding extra standards.", "Originality mandates reflecting the author's unique intellectual creation and personality characteristics within the work."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["实用艺术品版权与外观设计权可并行存在，不因获得设计保护而排除版权", "原创性标准统一适用“作者智力创作”，不因作品类型提高或降低门槛", "需持续观察Gândul Media等未决案件对原创性具体要素的进一步阐释"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Functional designs may face cumulative copyright and design infringement risks requiring dual IP strategy.", "Practice note: Establish rigorous evidence chains documenting originality and creative decisions to withstand judicial scrutiny.", "What to monitor: Evolving CJEU interpretations of 'author's personality' in applied art affecting subsistence determinations."]
core_points_en: Copyright and design protection systems are independent and parallel, with no hierarchical relationship between them.
Applied art requires only originality and expression of intellectual creation for copyright protection, excluding extra standards.
Originality mandates reflecting the author's unique intellectual creation and personality characteristics within the work.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Functional designs may face cumulative copyright and design infringement risks requiring dual IP strategy.
Practice note: Establish rigorous evidence chains documenting originality and creative decisions to withstand judicial scrutiny.
What to monitor: Evolving CJEU interpretations of 'author's personality' in applied art affecting subsistence determinations.

items.title: Just out of reach of the long arm of the UPC: Court of Appeal Confirms Limits of Long-Arm Jurisdiction
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院确认长臂管辖权限制
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Confirms Long-Arm Jurisdiction Does Not Extend to Non-Member States
items.summary: The Court of Appeal of the Unified Patent Court ("UPC") issued an important order on 13 March 2025 clarifying the territorial scope of...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年3月13日，统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院发布命令，澄清其国际管辖权的地域范围。在Keeex诉Adobe等案中，法院确认若仅依据损害发生地确立管辖，UPC无权审理非成员国境内的侵权行为。即使侵权活动在法国发生，管辖权也不能延伸至瑞士、英国等非UPC成员国。
items.ai_summary_en: On 13 March 2025, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal issued a significant order clarifying the territorial limits of its jurisdiction under Article 7(2) of Regulation 1215/2012. The decision addresses an infringement action involving Keeex SAS and defendants including Adobe and OpenAI entities. The Court confirmed that the UPC’s 'long-arm' jurisdiction cannot be invoked for alleged infringements occurring in non-UPC member states solely because harm was suffered there, provided no defendant is domiciled within a UPC contracting state. This ruling rejects attempts to establish jurisdiction over damages in countries such as Switzerland and the United Kingdom based merely on asset connections or the location of infringing acts within the UPC territory. It reinforces that without a defendant domiciled in a member state, the UPC lacks authority to adjudicate cross-border claims extending beyond its own geographic scope.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定限制了原告利用UPC作为单一论坛进行多地域专利执法的能力，特别是针对非成员国市场的侵权索赔。实务中需严格区分被告住所地管辖与损害地管辖的适用范围，避免在非成员国侵权主张上出现管辖瑕疵。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores that the UPC cannot serve as a universal forum for global patent enforcement. Rights holders must recognize that litigation strategies relying exclusively on the UPC will leave gaps in protection for markets outside the UPC system, such as the UK, Switzerland, and other non-contracting states. Consequently, enforcing patents across these jurisdictions requires separate national proceedings. Determining the domicile of potential defendants remains the critical first step in assessing whether multi-territorial relief can be sought centrally at the UPC or if parallel national actions are necessary to ensure comprehensive coverage.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院确认，仅依损害地确立管辖时，权限限于UPC领土内损害", "管辖权不能仅因法国境内有侵权行为，就延伸至瑞士、英国等非成员国", "被告住所地在UPC成员国时，BSH框架仍允许管辖域外侵权，本案未动摇此原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC long-arm jurisdiction does not extend to non-member states unless at least one defendant is domiciled within a UPC contracting member state.", "The mere occurrence of infringing acts within the UPC territory is insufficient to establish jurisdiction over damage sustained in non-UPC countries.", "Plaintiffs' attempts to leverage asset connections to expand UPC jurisdiction to territories outside the EU were explicitly rejected by the Court."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC长臂管辖权不覆盖非成员国，即便损害由成员国侵权行为引发", "依据损害地起诉时，须确保索赔范围严格限定在UPC成员国领土内", "需持续关注法院对第71b(3)条关于欧盟外损害管辖权的后续解释"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigants must accept that UPC proceedings do not automatically cover non-member states like the UK or Switzerland.", "Practice note: Assessing defendant domicile is essential to determine if centralized UPC action is viable for cross-border claims.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'domicile' and potential legislative adjustments regarding the UPC's international reach."]
core_points_en: UPC long-arm jurisdiction does not extend to non-member states unless at least one defendant is domiciled within a UPC contracting member state.
The mere occurrence of infringing acts within the UPC territory is insufficient to establish jurisdiction over damage sustained in non-UPC countries.
Plaintiffs' attempts to leverage asset connections to expand UPC jurisdiction to territories outside the EU were explicitly rejected by the Court.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigants must accept that UPC proceedings do not automatically cover non-member states like the UK or Switzerland.
Practice note: Assessing defendant domicile is essential to determine if centralized UPC action is viable for cross-border claims.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'domicile' and potential legislative adjustments regarding the UPC's international reach.

items.title: From Braunschweig to Luxembourg: When local hospitality constitutes genuine use
items.title_zh: 从布伦瑞克到卢森堡：本地餐饮服务构成欧盟商标真实使用
items.title_en: From Braunschweig to Luxembourg: Local Hospitality Services Constitute Genuine EU Trademark Use
items.summary: Can a single steakhouse in a small German city establish genuine use of an international trade mark designating the EU for hotel and catering services? In Altendorfer v EUIPO (T-297/25), the General Court confirmed that local use combined with online presence and an international reputation can satisfy the requirements for genuine use under EU trade mark law.   Background In 2012, Haus zur Hanse
items.ai_summary_zh: 在Altendorfer诉EUIPO案（T-297/25）中，欧盟普通法院确认，位于德国小城市的单一牛排馆，若结合线上展示及国际声誉，可确立指定欧盟的酒店和餐饮服务的国际商标真实使用。该判决明确了局部地域使用在特定条件下符合欧盟商标法要求。
items.ai_summary_en: In Altendorfer v EUIPO (T-297/25), the General Court clarified that a single steakhouse in a small German city can establish genuine use of an international trademark designating the EU for hotel and catering services. The judgment confirms that local physical use, when combined with an online presence and international reputation, satisfies the requirements for genuine use under EU trademark law. This decision dispels the misconception that pure localized use cannot cover the entire EU territory. It establishes that maintaining trademark validity does not require extensive physical operations across all member states. Instead, partial geographic use supported by digital visibility is sufficient to demonstrate real commercial exploitation within the internal market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了“本地使用+线上辐射+国际声誉”作为认定欧盟商标真实使用的复合标准。实务中需关注法院对“国际声誉”证据门槛的界定，以及纯本地服务如何通过数字渠道证明其跨境影响力，这对维持欧盟商标有效性具有参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: Trademark holders operating in Europe are not required to maintain physical coverage across all member states to preserve their rights. A strategy focusing on high-quality flagship locations, supported by robust digital exposure and international marketing, can effectively meet genuine use requirements at a lower cost. This approach helps mitigate the risk of cancellation for non-use. Rights holders should prioritize building a strong digital footprint and accumulating evidence of international reputation to substantiate the reach of their localized commercial activities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院在Altendorfer案中裁定单一本地实体店可构成欧盟商标真实使用", "法院认定本地使用结合线上存在及国际声誉可满足欧盟商标法下的使用要求", "案件涉及指定欧盟的国际商标在酒店和餐饮服务类别上的有效性维持问题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Single-location physical services combined with online presence can justify genuine use of an EU trademark.", "The General Court overturned the rigid view that purely localized use fails to cover the entire EU market scope.", "International reputation and online channels serve as key evidence elements to compensate for geographic limitations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["本地实体经营结合数字足迹可作为对抗商标撤销的有效证据组合", "需持续观察法院对“国际声誉”在非大规模跨国企业中的具体举证要求", "关注该判例对仅在单一成员国运营但持有欧盟商标企业的合规影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage a single physical location paired with online presence to maintain EU trademark validity cost-effectively.", "Practice note: Focus on accumulating evidence of international reputation to substantiate the radiation range of localized use.", "What to monitor: Monitor how courts assess the interplay between digital visibility and physical service locations in future disputes."]
core_points_en: Single-location physical services combined with online presence can justify genuine use of an EU trademark.
The General Court overturned the rigid view that purely localized use fails to cover the entire EU market scope.
International reputation and online channels serve as key evidence elements to compensate for geographic limitations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage a single physical location paired with online presence to maintain EU trademark validity cost-effectively.
Practice note: Focus on accumulating evidence of international reputation to substantiate the radiation range of localized use.
What to monitor: Monitor how courts assess the interplay between digital visibility and physical service locations in future disputes.

items.title: UPC Court of Appeal overturns long-arm jurisdiction in suit against OpenAI and Adobe
items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭推翻针对OpenAI和Adobe的长臂管辖权
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Narrows Long-Arm Jurisdiction Over Non-Member State Entities
items.summary: Appellate judges narrow the circumstances in which the Unified Patent Court has jurisdiction over alleged infringement beyond its member states
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在针对OpenAI和Adobe的诉讼中作出裁决，推翻了此前关于长臂管辖权的认定。上诉法官缩小了UPC对其成员国以外涉嫌侵权行为的管辖情形，明确了法院跨境行使管辖权的限制条件。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has issued a significant ruling in litigation involving OpenAI and Adobe, overturning a previous finding of long-arm jurisdiction. The appellate judges explicitly narrowed the circumstances under which the UPC exercises authority over alleged patent infringements occurring outside its member states. This decision establishes stricter standards for establishing jurisdiction over entities based in non-member countries. By limiting the court's capacity for cross-border enforcement against foreign defendants, the judgment fundamentally alters the procedural landscape. It restricts the ability of patent holders to leverage the UPC for claims primarily directed at activities or entities located beyond the participating member states, thereby creating a more defined boundary for the court's reach.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注该裁决对UPC跨境管辖标准的具体法律论证及后续影响。鉴于正文信息有限，暂无法评估其对非欧盟实体应诉策略的具体改变，建议持续追踪判决书全文以明确管辖权测试的新标准。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly impacts litigation strategies for companies operating within and outside Europe. Rights holders must carefully assess whether their claims fall within the UPC's strict jurisdictional boundaries before filing suit, particularly when targeting non-EU entities. Defendants can now leverage these clarified rules to challenge jurisdiction more effectively, potentially avoiding costly multi-jurisdictional disputes. Legal teams should review ongoing cases to identify potential jurisdictional vulnerabilities. Furthermore, this development necessitates a recalibration of European patent enforcement strategies, ensuring that forum selection aligns with the actual location of infringing activities rather than relying on expansive interpretations of long-arm jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭在涉OpenAI和Adobe案中推翻长臂管辖权认定", "上诉法官缩小了UPC对成员国境外涉嫌侵权行为的管辖范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal overturned the lower court's decision granting long-arm jurisdiction over the defendants.", "The ruling strictly limits the UPC's jurisdiction regarding alleged patent infringement acts occurring outside member states.", "New judicial standards are established for handling patent litigation involving entities domiciled in non-member countries."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC上诉庭对跨境侵权管辖权界限的最新司法解释", "观察该裁决是否成为后续类似跨国专利侵权案件的先例", "需获取判决全文以确认管辖权收窄的具体法律依据和适用条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use clarified jurisdictional limits to challenge liability for non-member state entities in UPC proceedings.", "Practice note: Reassess European patent litigation defense strategies and forum selection to align with stricter jurisdictional standards.", "What to monitor: Subsequent UPC case law developments regarding cross-border infringement claims and jurisdictional thresholds."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal overturned the lower court's decision granting long-arm jurisdiction over the defendants.
The ruling strictly limits the UPC's jurisdiction regarding alleged patent infringement acts occurring outside member states.
New judicial standards are established for handling patent litigation involving entities domiciled in non-member countries.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use clarified jurisdictional limits to challenge liability for non-member state entities in UPC proceedings.
Practice note: Reassess European patent litigation defense strategies and forum selection to align with stricter jurisdictional standards.
What to monitor: Subsequent UPC case law developments regarding cross-border infringement claims and jurisdictional thresholds.

items.title: Monday Miscellany
items.title_zh: 英国议会发布AI、版权与创意产业报告
items.title_en: UK Parliament Committee Publishes Report on AI, Copyright and Creative Industries
items.summary: The IPKat brings you the latest IP news, events, and opportunities!IPKat newsPermaKat Eleonora Rosati contributes to the UK Parliament report on AI, copyright, and the creative industries.The Communications and Digital Committee has published its report ‘AI, copyright and the creative industries’. The inquiry was launched to feed into the government's work on copyright and Artificial
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国议会通信和数字委员会发布了题为《人工智能、版权和创意产业》的报告。该调查旨在为政府在版权和人工智能领域的工作提供参考。IPKat专栏作者Eleonora Rosati为此报告做出了贡献。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Parliament’s Communications and Digital Committee has published its report titled ‘AI, copyright and the creative industries’. This inquiry was launched to inform the government’s ongoing work regarding copyright law and artificial intelligence. IPKat contributor Eleonora Rosati provided input to the report. The publication marks a significant step in the legislative scrutiny of how AI technologies intersect with intellectual property rights within the UK creative sector, offering recommendations that may shape future policy directions and statutory adjustments in this rapidly evolving field.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类政策更新给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This report signals continued parliamentary focus on balancing AI innovation with creator rights in the UK. Rights holders and technology developers should review the findings to anticipate potential regulatory shifts. While not binding, the committee’s recommendations often influence subsequent government consultations and legislative drafts, making them a key indicator for future compliance requirements and licensing frameworks in the AI and content sectors.

core_points_en: The Communications and Digital Committee published its report on AI, copyright, and the creative industries.
The inquiry aims to support the UK government’s policy development on copyright and artificial intelligence.
IPKat contributor Eleonora Rosati provided expert input to the parliamentary committee during this inquiry.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Stakeholders in creative industries and AI development should assess the report’s recommendations for potential impacts on licensing models and data usage rights.
Practice note: The report serves as a primary reference for understanding the UK legislative intent regarding copyright exceptions and AI training data obligations.
What to monitor: Track the UK government’s formal response to the committee’s findings and any subsequent proposals for statutory reform or guidance.

items.title: Court of Appeal denies UPC long-arm jurisdiction based on Art. 7(2) Brussels I Recast
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院驳回基于《布鲁塞尔条例》的长臂管辖权
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Denies Long-Arm Jurisdiction Over Non-UPC States
items.summary: Keeex v Adobe, OpenAI and others – Court of Appeal, 13 March 2026 (UPC_CoA_922/2025, UPC_CoA_923/2025, UPC_CoA_924/2025, UPC_CoA_925/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院在Keeex诉Adobe等案中裁定，依据《布鲁塞尔条例I》重铸版第7(2)条确立的管辖权仅限于UPC领土内发生的损害，不延伸至非UPC成员国（如英国、瑞士等）。法院撤销巴黎地方分院对非UPC领土部分的管辖权认定，强调原告需在起诉书中充分证明国际管辖权依据，澄清了UPC跨境侵权案件的管辖边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal ruled that jurisdiction under Article 7(2) of the Brussels I Recast Regulation is strictly limited to damage occurring within UPC member states. In Keeex v Adobe and others, the Court set aside a Paris Local Division decision that had asserted jurisdiction over patent parts in non-UPC countries like the UK and Switzerland. The ruling clarifies that Article 7(2) cannot extend to infringements outside the UPC territory, reinforcing territorial limits. Claimants must now substantiate international jurisdiction grounds explicitly in their statements of claim, particularly when relying on auxiliary jurisdiction provisions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision restricts the UPC's reach, preventing it from adjudicating infringement of national patent parts in non-contracting states via tort jurisdiction. Rights holders must segment claims carefully, ensuring actions for non-UPC territories are filed in appropriate national courts. Procedural rigor in establishing jurisdiction at the outset is now critical to avoid partial dismissals.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院裁定第7(2)条管辖权仅限UPC领土内损害，不涵盖非UPC国家", "法院撤销一审对瑞士、英国等非UPC成员国专利部分的管辖权认定", "原告须在起诉书中充分 substantiate 国际管辖权依据，特别是援引第71b(3…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Article 7(2) Brussels I Recast jurisdiction is limited to damage within UPC territory.", "Court of Appeal rejected jurisdiction over patent parts in non-UPC states like UK.", "Claimants must substantiate international jurisdiction grounds clearly in initial pleadings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确UPC管辖权地域边界，排除对非UPC成员国专利侵权的直接管辖", "强化程序要求，原告需在起诉阶段提供充分的国际管辖权证据", "需持续观察上诉法院对《布鲁塞尔条例》第71b(3)条辅助管辖权的后续解释"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patent enforcement strategies must separate UPC and non-UPC territories, as the UPC cannot rule on infringement in states like the UK or Switzerland under this b…", "Practice note: Draft statements of claim with precise jurisdictional facts, distinguishing between UPC-based and national-based causes of action to meet heightened substantiation…", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of Article 71b(3) Brussels I Recast by the UPC Court of Appeal regarding auxiliary jurisdiction in mixed-territory disputes."]
core_points_en: Article 7(2) Brussels I Recast jurisdiction is limited to damage within UPC territory.
Court of Appeal rejected jurisdiction over patent parts in non-UPC states like UK.
Claimants must substantiate international jurisdiction grounds clearly in initial pleadings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patent enforcement strategies must separate UPC and non-UPC territories, as the UPC cannot rule on infringement in states like the UK or Switzerland under this b…
Practice note: Draft statements of claim with precise jurisdictional facts, distinguishing between UPC-based and national-based causes of action to meet heightened substantiation…
What to monitor: Future interpretations of Article 71b(3) Brussels I Recast by the UPC Court of Appeal regarding auxiliary jurisdiction in mixed-territory disputes.

items.title: UK confidentiality approach poses challenges for SEP owners/licensees, despite Optis redaction win
items.title_zh: 尽管 Optis 胜诉，英国保密规则仍给 SEP 双方带来挑战
items.title_en: UK Confidentiality Rules Challenge SEP Parties Despite Optis Redaction Win
items.summary: The Court of Appeal has eased worries about confidential material, but SEP-licensing companies must remain proactive in their defence of commercially valuable information
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在 Optis 案件中虽允许对部分机密材料进行编辑处理，缓解了业界担忧，但整体保密制度仍对标准必要专利（SEP）权利人和被许可人构成挑战。文章指出，企业不能仅依赖法院裁决，必须主动采取防御措施，以保护具有商业价值的敏感信息不被过度披露，确保持续的竞争优势。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has provided some relief regarding the handling of confidential material in litigation. However, standard essential patent (SEP) owners and licensees face ongoing challenges in protecting commercially sensitive information. Market participants must remain proactive in defending their data against excessive disclosure requirements inherent in the current legal framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights that while judicial precedents like Optis offer procedural tools for redacting sensitive data, they do not eliminate the fundamental tension between open justice principles and commercial confidentiality in UK SEP disputes. Rights holders should anticipate rigorous scrutiny of information classification requests and prepare robust justifications for non-disclosure early in proceedings to mitigate exposure risks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院放宽机密材料处理，但未彻底解决披露风险", "SEP 诉讼中商业敏感信息的保护仍需企业主动构建防御体系", "Optis 案件的编辑胜诉为先例，但不足以消除所有保密隐患"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal eased concerns about confidential material handling in recent proceedings.", "SEP licensors and licensees still face significant risks regarding commercial information protection.", "Proactive measures are required to defend valuable business data from broad disclosure obligations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在英诉讼需提前规划保密分级，主动申请保护令以防数据外泄", "借鉴 Optis 案经验，积极主张对敏感商业信息进行编辑处理"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: SEP stakeholders must balance litigation transparency with the need to protect trade secrets and licensing terms from public exposure.", "Practice note: Parties should proactively identify commercially sensitive documents and seek appropriate redactions or confidentiality rings at the earliest procedural stages.", "What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal decisions clarifying the scope of permissible redactions and the threshold for proving commercial harm from disclosure."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal eased concerns about confidential material handling in recent proceedings.
SEP licensors and licensees still face significant risks regarding commercial information protection.
Proactive measures are required to defend valuable business data from broad disclosure obligations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: SEP stakeholders must balance litigation transparency with the need to protect trade secrets and licensing terms from public exposure.
Practice note: Parties should proactively identify commercially sensitive documents and seek appropriate redactions or confidentiality rings at the earliest procedural stages.
What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal decisions clarifying the scope of permissible redactions and the threshold for proving commercial harm from disclosure.

items.title: DPMAnutzerforum 2026: Quantentechnologie nimmt Fahrt auf
items.title_zh: 2026 年用户论坛：量子技术加速发展
items.title_en: DPMA Forum 2026: Quantum Technology Patent Filings Surge Sevenfold in Germany
items.summary: DPMAnutzerforum 2026: Quantentechnologie nimmt Fahrt auf - DPMA-Analyse: Siebenmal mehr Patentanmeldungen mit quantentechnologischem Bezug als noch vor zehn Jahren – Deutsche Anmelder auf Rang 2 hinter den Vereinigten Staaten – DPMA-Präsidentin: Deutschland könnte entscheidende Innovationen mitprägen – Potenzial von Quantentechnologie für Deutschland Leitthema beim DPMAnutzerforum in München - Komplexe Berechnungen, abhörsichere Kommunikation, ultrapräzise Messungen - Asiatische Konzerne führen
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国专利商标局（DPMA）在 2026 年用户论坛上发布分析显示，过去十年德国市场量子技术相关专利申请量增长七倍。德国申请者数量位居全球第二，仅次于美国。DPMA 主席强调德国具备塑造关键创新的潜力，指出知识产权保护是将研究成果转化为商业模型的前提，亚洲企业在该领域亦处于领先地位。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) reported at its 2026 User Forum that patent applications related to quantum technology for the German market have increased nearly sevenfold over the past decade. German applicants currently rank second globally, trailing only the United States. DPMA President Eva Schewior emphasized that while the field is still developing, Germany possesses significant potential to shape key innovations through its strong research institutions and industrial base. The agency highlighted that robust intellectual property protection, particularly via patents, is a critical prerequisite for translating these technological advancements into viable commercial products and business models.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局量子技术时需关注德国强劲的创新势头与专利壁垒。建议加强在该领域的专利挖掘与申请，利用 DPMA 数据监测竞争对手动态，并重视知识产权合规以规避潜在侵权风险，确保在欧洲市场的技术转化安全。
items.ai_insight_en: This data indicates a maturing innovation ecosystem in Germany for quantum technologies, suggesting increasing competitive density in this sector. For rights holders and portfolio managers, the rapid growth in filings highlights the importance of monitoring German patent landscapes to identify emerging technical trends and potential freedom-to-operate considerations. The emphasis on IP protection as a driver for commercialization underscores the strategic value of securing robust patent positions early in the development cycle within this jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国量子技术专利申请十年间增长近七倍，创新动态显著", "德国申请者数量居全球第二，具备塑造关键创新潜力", "DPMA 强调专利保护对转化量子技术商业价值至关重要"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Quantum technology patent filings for the German market have grown nearly seven times compared to levels recorded ten years ago.", "German applicants hold the second position globally in quantum technology patents, following only the United States in volume.", "DPMA leadership identifies patent protection as essential for converting quantum research into successful commercial applications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德国量子专利激增趋势，提前布局核心技术研发", "利用官方数据监测竞争态势，强化知识产权合规管理"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: High growth in German quantum patents signals a competitive landscape requiring active portfolio monitoring and strategic positioning.", "Practice note: Leverage DPMA analytical data to assess competitor activity and identify white spaces or crowded areas in quantum tech sub-sectors.", "What to monitor: Track filing trends by major corporate and academic entities to anticipate future enforcement risks and licensing opportunities."]
core_points_en: Quantum technology patent filings for the German market have grown nearly seven times compared to levels recorded ten years ago.
German applicants hold the second position globally in quantum technology patents, following only the United States in volume.
DPMA leadership identifies patent protection as essential for converting quantum research into successful commercial applications.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: High growth in German quantum patents signals a competitive landscape requiring active portfolio monitoring and strategic positioning.
Practice note: Leverage DPMA analytical data to assess competitor activity and identify white spaces or crowded areas in quantum tech sub-sectors.
What to monitor: Track filing trends by major corporate and academic entities to anticipate future enforcement risks and licensing opportunities.

items.title: Breitling for women? More like bad faith for women, says the EUIPO
items.title_zh: 百年灵女性系列？欧盟知识产权局称系女性商标恶意注册
items.title_en: EUIPO Board Affirms Invalidity of Breitling-Related Mark for Bad Faith Filing
items.summary: Recently, the Fifth Board of Appeal (BoA) of the EUIPO affirmed the Cancellation Division’s declaration of invalidity of a trade mark under Article 59(1)(b) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation (EUTMR). While the BoA’s assessment serves as a useful summary and a concise application of the Court of Justice of the EU (CJEU) case law on trade mark applications made in bad faith, the defences submitted by
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局第五上诉委员会维持撤销部门裁定，依据《欧盟商标条例》第59(1)(b)条宣告某商标无效。该裁定是对欧盟法院关于恶意申请商标判例法的简明适用与总结，驳回了申请人提出的抗辩理由。
items.ai_summary_en: The Fifth Board of Appeal of the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has upheld the Cancellation Division’s decision to declare a trade mark invalid under Article 59(1)(b) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation. The ruling confirms that the application was filed in bad faith, aligning with established case law from the Court of Justice of the European Union regarding dishonest intentions at the time of filing. The Board provided a concise application of these legal principles, rejecting the defences submitted by the rights holder. This decision underscores the strict enforcement of good faith requirements in EU trade mark registration procedures and highlights the significant legal risks associated with applications lacking genuine commercial intent or aiming to exploit the reputation of existing brands.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了欧盟机构在认定商标恶意注册时对既有判例法的严格遵循。实务中需关注上诉委员会如何具体界定“恶意”要素，以及申请人抗辩失败的具体事实背景，以评估类似商标布局的合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking EU trade mark protection must demonstrate a clear, genuine intention to use the sign for the specified goods or services. Applications perceived as strategic hoarding or attempts to mimic well-known brands are increasingly scrutinized and likely to be invalidated. Establishing robust internal compliance reviews before filing is essential to mitigate the risk of bad faith declarations. Such measures help prevent costly litigation, potential loss of registered rights, and wasted resources, ensuring that IP portfolios remain secure and legally defensible against third-party challenges.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟知识产权局第五上诉委员会维持了撤销部门关于商标无效的宣告", "无效宣告依据为《欧盟商标条例》第59(1)(b)条，即商标申请存在恶意", "上诉委员会的评估被视为对欧盟法院恶意申请判例法的简洁应用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO Board of Appeal affirmed the invalidity of the contested mark based on findings of bad faith under Article 59(1)(b).", "The decision applies CJEU jurisprudence to confirm that applications made without honest intent face severe legal consequences.", "Rights holders must ensure genuine use intent to avoid invalidation risks stemming from accusations of malicious registration."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟知识产权局在恶意注册认定上保持与欧盟法院判例的高度一致性", "需持续观察上诉委员会在具体案件中对恶意主观意图的证据采信标准", "商标申请人在面对无效宣告时，其抗辩策略的有效性在此类案件中受到严格限制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure all filings reflect genuine commercial intent to withstand bad faith challenges.", "Practice note: Implement pre-filing compliance checks to detect and eliminate risks of imitation or hoarding.", "What to monitor: Evolving EUIPO and CJEU standards defining bad faith indicators in trade mark applications."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO Board of Appeal affirmed the invalidity of the contested mark based on findings of bad faith under Article 59(1)(b).
The decision applies CJEU jurisprudence to confirm that applications made without honest intent face severe legal consequences.
Rights holders must ensure genuine use intent to avoid invalidation risks stemming from accusations of malicious registration.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure all filings reflect genuine commercial intent to withstand bad faith challenges.
Practice note: Implement pre-filing compliance checks to detect and eliminate risks of imitation or hoarding.
What to monitor: Evolving EUIPO and CJEU standards defining bad faith indicators in trade mark applications.

items.title: Dyson v Dreame: The long-arm of the UPC, and its limits – the UPC's first CJEU referral
items.title_zh: 戴森诉追觅：UPC长臂管辖及其界限
items.title_en: Dyson v Dreame: UPC Court of Appeal Makes First CJEU Referral on Jurisdiction
items.summary: On 6 March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal issued a landmark order in Dyson v Dreame & Eurep referring four questions concerning the extent of the UPC’s l...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月6日，统一专利法院上诉部就戴森诉追觅案作出里程碑式裁定，就UPC“长臂管辖权”范围向欧盟法院提请四个问题，此为UPC首次向欧盟法院转介。汉堡地方法院此前颁发初步禁令并延伸至西班牙，上诉法院部分支持戴森，但将关键欧盟法律问题提交欧盟法院裁决。
items.ai_summary_en: On 6 March 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal referred four questions to the CJEU regarding the extent of the UPC’s long-arm jurisdiction in Dyson v Dreame. This marks the UPC’s first referral to the CJEU. The dispute involves a preliminary injunction against Dreame, its EU Authorized Representative Eurep, and distributors. The Hamburg Local Division had extended the injunction to Spain, a non-UPC state. While the Court of Appeal resolved appeals concerning UPC territory defendants, it suspended proceedings on issues involving non-UPC states and the liability of regulatory representatives pending CJEU guidance on these specific points of EU law.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral clarifies the procedural interface between the UPC and the CJEU. It highlights uncertainties regarding cross-border enforcement against entities in non-UPC states and the patent liability of parties acting solely as regulatory compliance contacts. Rights holders must assess how these jurisdictional boundaries affect multi-state enforcement strategies and the selection of defendants in complex supply chains involving non-EU manufacturers.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal issued its first ever referral to the CJEU concerning jurisdictional limits.", "Four questions address the UPC's reach into non-member states and liability of regulatory representatives.", "The Hamburg Local Division had previously extended a preliminary injunction to cover Spain and Eurep."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟法院对UPC管辖权延伸至非成员国的法律界定", "观察纯行政性质的欧盟授权代表是否承担专利侵权责任", "监测此案对跨境电子商务平台卖家合规义务的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Determines whether UPC injunctions can effectively reach assets or activities in non-UPC EU states like Spain.", "Practice note: Clarifies if appointing an EU Authorized Representative for product safety creates exposure to patent infringement claims.", "What to monitor: The CJEU’s interpretation of the UPC Agreement’s territorial scope and the legal status of regulatory representatives."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal issued its first ever referral to the CJEU concerning jurisdictional limits.
Four questions address the UPC's reach into non-member states and liability of regulatory representatives.
The Hamburg Local Division had previously extended a preliminary injunction to cover Spain and Eurep.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Determines whether UPC injunctions can effectively reach assets or activities in non-UPC EU states like Spain.
Practice note: Clarifies if appointing an EU Authorized Representative for product safety creates exposure to patent infringement claims.
What to monitor: The CJEU’s interpretation of the UPC Agreement’s territorial scope and the legal status of regulatory representatives.

items.title: Huawei launches another major battle over streaming against Disney
items.title_zh: 华为就流媒体技术对迪士尼发起新一轮重大专利战
items.title_en: Huawei Shifts to Offensive Strategy, Launching Major UPC Patent Litigation Against Disney Over Streaming
items.summary: Since the launch of the UPC, Huawei has increasingly changed its role from defendant to claimant in major patent disputes. With several new lawsuits against Disney over HEVC technology, the Chinese tech group once again shows its determination to defend its own patents in Europe. Huawei filed a lawsuit against twelve companies of the Walt […] The post Huawei launches another major battle over streaming against Disney appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 自统一专利法院（UPC）启动以来，华为在重大专利纠纷中逐渐从被告转变为原告。华为就HEVC技术对华特迪士尼公司的十二家实体提起诉讼，显示其在欧洲维护自身专利的决心。
items.ai_summary_en: Since the Unified Patent Court (UPC) began operations, Huawei has strategically transitioned from a defendant to an active claimant in significant European patent disputes. The technology group recently initiated multiple lawsuits against twelve entities within the Walt Disney Company group, alleging infringement of its High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) patents related to streaming technologies. This legal action underscores Huawei’s determined effort to enforce its intellectual property rights across Europe using the new centralized judicial framework. By targeting major media and entertainment conglomerates, Huawei demonstrates a clear shift in its European IP strategy, moving away from passive defense toward proactive enforcement. This development highlights the increasing willingness of major tech firms to leverage the UPC’s jurisdiction to protect their standard-essential patent portfolios and secure licensing revenues in the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注华为在UPC框架下针对内容提供商的SEP执法趋势。此案反映了标准必要专利权利人在欧洲司法辖区日益积极的诉讼策略，可能影响流媒体行业的许可谈判格局。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize the UPC as a powerful tool for centralizing enforcement actions, allowing rights holders to address infringement across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously. Companies operating in Europe should consider transitioning from purely defensive postures to proactive portfolio management, leveraging unified rulings to establish industry standards and secure licensing agreements. It is advisable to conduct regular audits of high-value patent assets and establish robust monitoring systems to identify potential infringers early. By integrating litigation into broader business strategies, firms can transform intellectual property into a competitive advantage and negotiation leverage within the European digital media and technology sectors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华为角色转变：自UPC启动后，华为在主要专利纠纷中已从被告转为原告", "新诉讼行动：华为就HEVC技术向迪士尼旗下十二家公司提起多项新诉讼", "战略意图：此举表明华为致力于在欧洲积极行使其专利权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Huawei has strategically shifted from a defensive posture to actively initiating patent litigation as a claimant before the newly established Unified Patent Court.", "The lawsuit targets twelve Disney subsidiaries for alleged infringement of HEVC streaming technology patents, marking a significant enforcement action.", "This case illustrates how major technology companies are utilizing the UPC's centralized jurisdiction to strengthen overseas patent protection strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["观察华为在UPC针对非传统科技巨头（如媒体公司）的SEP诉讼策略变化", "监测HEVC相关专利在欧洲的司法认定及对流媒体服务的影响", "关注迪士尼等被诉方在UPC程序中的抗辩理由及后续法律应对"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the UPC's unified jurisdiction to reduce multi-country litigation costs and enhance enforcement efficiency for SEP portfolios.", "Practice note: Transition from defensive responses to proactive litigation strategies to establish market position and generate licensing revenue.", "What to monitor: Track the procedural developments in this high-profile dispute to assess the UPC's approach to damages and injunctions in streaming cases."]
core_points_en: Huawei has strategically shifted from a defensive posture to actively initiating patent litigation as a claimant before the newly established Unified Patent Court.
The lawsuit targets twelve Disney subsidiaries for alleged infringement of HEVC streaming technology patents, marking a significant enforcement action.
This case illustrates how major technology companies are utilizing the UPC's centralized jurisdiction to strengthen overseas patent protection strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the UPC's unified jurisdiction to reduce multi-country litigation costs and enhance enforcement efficiency for SEP portfolios.
Practice note: Transition from defensive responses to proactive litigation strategies to establish market position and generate licensing revenue.
What to monitor: Track the procedural developments in this high-profile dispute to assess the UPC's approach to damages and injunctions in streaming cases.

items.title: Preliminary opinion of the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal, G1/25: Adaptation of the Description
items.title_zh: 欧专局扩大上诉委员会G1/25案初步意见：说明书适应性修改
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board G1/25 Preliminary Opinion: Legal Basis for Description Adaptation
items.summary: On 11 March 2026 , the Enlarged Board of Appeal (EBoA) issued its p reliminary opinion in G 1/25 , following a referral from case T 0697/22. The case concerns whether, and on what legal basis, the description must be adapted to amended claims during EPO proceedings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月11日，欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会就G1/25案发布初步意见。该案源于T0697/22号案件的移交，核心争议在于欧洲专利局程序中，说明书是否必须以及基于何种法律依据需适应经修改的权利要求进行调整。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 March 2026, the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal issued a preliminary opinion in case G 1/25, following a referral from technical board of appeal case T 0697/22. The central issue concerns whether and on what legal basis patent applicants must adapt the description to match amended claims during European patent proceedings. This opinion aims to resolve divergent practices among various technical boards of appeal regarding the consistency between claims and the description. By clarifying the legal boundaries of this adaptation requirement, the decision seeks to unify examination standards across the EPO. The outcome is expected to have significant implications for future patent grant and opposition proceedings, influencing how applicants manage claim amendments and description updates throughout the prosecution lifecycle.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案初步意见直接回应长期存在的说明书修改义务争议。需持续监测最终决定对审查实践中“一致性”要求的界定，及其对专利申请维持策略的潜在程序影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should closely monitor the final decision in G 1/25, as it will define the strictness of description adaptation requirements. Pending applications may require immediate review to ensure descriptions align with current claim sets, mitigating risks of refusal or invalidity due to inconsistencies. Applicants should prepare to adjust drafting and response strategies to comply with clarified rules. Proactive assessment of existing portfolios is advisable to identify potential discrepancies. Engaging specialized counsel may help optimize prosecution strategies and ensure robust patent protection under the evolving procedural landscape established by this landmark referral.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["扩大上诉委员会于2026年3月11日发布G1/25案初步意见", "案件核心探讨说明书是否须及依据何法条适应修改后的权利要求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO Enlarged Board addresses whether descriptions must be adapted to amended claims and identifies the specific legal basis for such obligations.", "The preliminary opinion aims to harmonize inconsistent examination practices across technical boards regarding description-claim consistency requirements.", "The final decision will significantly influence amendment strategies and compliance requirements in future European patent applications and oppositions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注最终决定对说明书修改法律基础的明确界定", "监测该意见对后续欧洲专利审查实践的一致性影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Review pending European cases to ensure strict consistency between descriptions and amended claims, minimizing refusal or invalidity risks.", "Practice note: Adjust patent drafting and prosecution strategies to align with the clarified legal standards for description adaptation upon claim changes.", "What to monitor: Await the final EBoA decision in G 1/25 to update internal guidelines and response protocols for European patent office communications."]
core_points_en: The EPO Enlarged Board addresses whether descriptions must be adapted to amended claims and identifies the specific legal basis for such obligations.
The preliminary opinion aims to harmonize inconsistent examination practices across technical boards regarding description-claim consistency requirements.
The final decision will significantly influence amendment strategies and compliance requirements in future European patent applications and oppositions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Review pending European cases to ensure strict consistency between descriptions and amended claims, minimizing refusal or invalidity risks.
Practice note: Adjust patent drafting and prosecution strategies to align with the clarified legal standards for description adaptation upon claim changes.
What to monitor: Await the final EBoA decision in G 1/25 to update internal guidelines and response protocols for European patent office communications.

items.title: 'Tang Gold' is not essentially derived from 'Nadorcott' plant variety, finds Patents Court
items.title_zh: 英法院裁定Tang Gold柑橘非Nadorcott本质衍生品种
items.title_en: UK Patents Court Rules Tang Gold Not Essentially Derived From Nadorcott Plant Variety
items.summary: In a rare plant variety rights decision, Asda supermarkets were (mostly) victorious in yesterday's judgment from the Patents Court: Nador Cott Protection SAS v Asda Stores Limited [2026] EWHC 553 (Pat). Mr Justice Mellor held that the 'Tang Gold' mandarin that was sold by Asda was not an essentially derived variety (EDV) of the 'Nadorcott' variety. But if it were, then the owners
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国专利法院在Nador Cott Protection诉Asda案中作出罕见植物新品种权判决。Mellor法官裁定，Asda销售的“Tang Gold”柑橘并非“Nadorcott”品种的本质衍生品种（EDV）。因此，Asda在主要争议点上胜诉，未构成对植物新品种权的侵犯。
items.ai_summary_en: In a rare plant variety rights decision, the UK Patents Court ruled in favor of Asda Stores Limited in Nador Cott Protection SAS v Asda Stores Limited [2026] EWHC 553 (Pat). Mr Justice Mellor determined that the 'Tang Gold' mandarin sold by Asda does not constitute an essentially derived variety (EDV) of the protected 'Nadorcott' variety. Consequently, the court found no infringement of plant variety rights. This judgment provides critical judicial clarity on the legal standards for determining essential derivation under UK law. It establishes important boundaries for the scope of protection afforded to breeders and defines the specific criteria for infringement. The ruling offers significant guidance for the horticultural industry regarding the interpretation of EDV status and the limits of proprietary rights over new plant varieties.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了英国法下植物新品种本质衍生品种的认定界限。需关注法院对品种独立性的事实认定标准，以及该判例对后续农业育种权利边界界定的参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the importance of rigorous freedom-to-operate analyses for entities operating within the European plant breeding sector. Stakeholders should conduct thorough due diligence to assess whether new cultivars might be deemed essentially derived from existing protected varieties. Emphasizing independent breeding innovation is crucial to mitigate infringement risks associated with established European plant variety rights. In the event of litigation, parties should strategically leverage the specific judicial standards for essential derivation to construct robust defenses. Monitoring evolving case law regarding EDV interpretations will remain vital for managing intellectual property portfolios and ensuring compliance with regional regulatory frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国专利法院裁定Tang Gold柑橘不构成Nadorcott品种的本质衍生品种", "零售商Asda在该植物新品种权侵权诉讼中获得主要胜诉"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court determined that Tang Gold lacks the core legal characteristics required to be classified as an essentially derived variety of Nadorcott.", "The judgment clarifies the scope of plant variety rights protection and establishes specific boundaries for infringement determinations.", "Retailer Asda achieved victory on the primary claims, thereby avoiding liability for damages in this plant variety dispute."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["植物新品种权侵权判定中本质衍生品种的法律界定标准", "英国法院在农业知识产权纠纷中的事实认定倾向", "后续类似品种权纠纷对该判例的引用与遵循情况"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct comprehensive pre-market screenings to evaluate potential essential derived variety risks for target cultivars in Europe.", "Practice note: Strengthen independent research and development capabilities to avoid disputes involving European plant variety right infringements.", "What to monitor: Track judicial interpretations of essential derivation criteria to inform breeding strategies and litigation defense approaches."]
core_points_en: The court determined that Tang Gold lacks the core legal characteristics required to be classified as an essentially derived variety of Nadorcott.
The judgment clarifies the scope of plant variety rights protection and establishes specific boundaries for infringement determinations.
Retailer Asda achieved victory on the primary claims, thereby avoiding liability for damages in this plant variety dispute.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct comprehensive pre-market screenings to evaluate potential essential derived variety risks for target cultivars in Europe.
Practice note: Strengthen independent research and development capabilities to avoid disputes involving European plant variety right infringements.
What to monitor: Track judicial interpretations of essential derivation criteria to inform breeding strategies and litigation defense approaches.

items.title: Albania is First to Join WIPO’s Riyadh Design Law Treaty
items.title_zh: 阿尔巴尼亚率先加入 WIPO 利雅得外观设计法条约
items.title_en: Albania Becomes First State to Ratify WIPO Riyadh Design Law Treaty
items.summary: Albania today became the first member state to join WIPO’s Riyadh Design Law Treaty (RDLT), depositing its instrument of ratification with Director General Daren Tang and marking a major milestone in bringing into force the new international framework for design protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 阿尔巴尼亚今日成为首个加入世界知识产权组织《利雅得外观设计法条约》的成员国，并向总干事邓鸿森交存了批准书。此举标志着建立新的国际外观设计保护框架进程中的重要里程碑，该条约旨在简化和协调全球外观设计注册程序，降低申请人成本。
items.ai_summary_en: Albania has become the first member state to join the WIPO Riyadh Design Law Treaty (RDLT) by depositing its instrument of ratification with Director General Daren Tang. This action represents a significant milestone in the process of bringing the new international framework for design protection into force, marking the initial step toward establishing harmonized global standards for industrial design registration.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，阿尔巴尼亚后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This development signals the early stages of implementing the RDLT, which aims to streamline and harmonize procedural requirements for industrial designs globally. Rights holders should track the treaty's entry into force conditions to understand potential future impacts on international design application procedures and administrative consistency across contracting parties.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["阿尔巴尼亚成为全球首个批准该条约的国家", "条约旨在简化国际外观设计注册流程与形式要求", "此举推动条约生效并完善国际知识产权保护网络"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Albania is the first country to deposit its instrument of ratification for the WIPO Riyadh Design Law Treaty.", "The ratification marks a key milestone in the effort to bring the new international design protection framework into force."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用新条约简化程序降低阿尔巴尼亚申请成本", "关注条约生效进度以优化欧洲市场布局策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor Albania's accession as an indicator of the treaty's progress toward entering into force and affecting international design frameworks.", "Practice note: Track WIPO announcements regarding additional ratifications required to trigger the treaty's operational status.", "What to monitor: Future updates on the number of contracting parties and the expected timeline for the RDLT's formal entry into force."]
core_points_en: Albania is the first country to deposit its instrument of ratification for the WIPO Riyadh Design Law Treaty.
The ratification marks a key milestone in the effort to bring the new international design protection framework into force.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor Albania's accession as an indicator of the treaty's progress toward entering into force and affecting international design frameworks.
Practice note: Track WIPO announcements regarding additional ratifications required to trigger the treaty's operational status.
What to monitor: Future updates on the number of contracting parties and the expected timeline for the RDLT's formal entry into force.
