items.title: EPO President visits Bulgaria to discuss the future of Europe’s patent system
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局局长访保加利亚探讨专利体系未来
items.title_en: EPO President Visits Bulgaria to Discuss Future of European Patent System
items.summary: On 11 and 12 March, European Patent Office (EPO) President António Campinos travelled to Sofia, Bulgaria, and took part in the high-level IP conference “Innovation empowered: protect, compete, succeed”
items.ai_summary_zh: 3月11日至12日，欧洲专利局（EPO）局长António Campinos访问索非亚，出席“创新赋能”高层知识产权会议。期间与保加利亚经济部长及专利局局长会晤，就欧洲专利体系未来、数字化转型及加强合作交换意见。局长强调专利在提升竞争力中的作用，并向三位保加利亚杰出发明人颁发金书奖，表彰其在农业、机器人及能源领域的贡献。
items.ai_summary_en: European Patent Office President António Campinos visited Sofia, Bulgaria, on 11 and 12 March to attend the high-level IP conference “Innovation empowered: protect, compete, succeed.” The event convened business leaders, researchers, and officials from the EPO, EUIPO, and WIPO. Campinos emphasized patents as critical assets for legal certainty and competitiveness. He held bilateral discussions with Bulgarian Minister for Economy and Industry Irina Shtonova and Patent Office President Olya Dimitrova. Topics included the future of the European patent system, responses to emerging technologies, and modernization of the European Patent Network. The visit forms part of the EPO’s ongoing consultation on its Scenarios for the Future foresight study, aiming to support micro and small entities through digital transformation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次访问是EPO“未来情景”前瞻研究咨询过程的一部分，显示EPO正加强与成员国在政策层面的协调。关注EPO如何通过数字化和网络现代化提升服务效率，以及其对中小企业创新支持的具体举措落地情况。
items.ai_insight_en: This engagement highlights the EPO’s strategic focus on aligning national offices with broader European innovation goals. For rights holders, the emphasis on digital transformation and support for smaller entities suggests upcoming service enhancements. Monitoring the outcomes of the Scenarios for the Future study is essential for anticipating procedural or policy shifts in the European patent landscape.

items.title: LD The Hague, Infringement Action, February 24, 2026, UPC_CFI_619/2025
items.title_zh: UPC海牙分院：广义禁令涵盖未来变体及辅助请求程序规范
items.title_en: UPC Hague Ruling: Broad Injunctions Cover Future Variants and Auxiliary Request Feasibility
items.summary: Following the CoA's Abbott/Sibio ruling (UPC_CoA_328/2024), a claimant requesting general injunctive relief covering patent claims...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院海牙地方法院在GSK诉Mode案中裁定，依据上诉法院先例，广义禁令可涵盖未修改权利要求的未来产品变体。若EMA批准晚于起诉，新增产品修正案可被接纳。法院质疑14个月审限内大量辅助请求的可行性，要求提交表格概览但未设数量上限。被告关于剔除迟交证据的申请被驳回，以保障听证权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in The Hague issued a significant ruling in the GSK v. Mode case, aligning with the Court of Appeal’s Abbott/Sibio precedent. The court confirmed that broadly drafted requests for general injunctive relief can encompass future product variants without requiring claim amendments. Furthermore, adding a new product to the infringement action is admissible if EMA approval occurs after the Statement of Claim is filed, as parties are not obligated to anticipate regulatory timelines in other jurisdictions like the US. While the court expressed serious concerns regarding the feasibility of handling numerous auxiliary requests within the strict fourteen-month procedural timeline, it declined to impose a hard numerical cap. Instead, the claimant was ordered to provide a comprehensive tabular overview clarifying features and invalidity attacks. The decision also affirmed that submitting new evidence in reply does not violate front-loaded proceedings, thereby safeguarding the right to be heard.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确认了UPC程序中禁令范围的延展性及证据提交的灵活性。实务中需关注法院对辅助请求形式规范的强化，以及在前置程序中平衡审理速度与当事人听证权的倾向。后续应观察 interim conference 对辅助请求的最终限制尺度。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies strategic boundaries for patent enforcement and defense at the UPC. Rights holders can leverage broad injunctive language to capture evolving product lines, reducing the need for iterative claim amendments when regulatory approvals shift during litigation. For defendants, the ruling highlights the importance of robust technical comparison and invalidity preparations early in proceedings. While courts remain wary of excessive auxiliary requests, they prioritize substantive fairness over rigid numerical limits. Parties should focus on structured presentations of arguments to navigate the expedited fourteen-month timeline effectively, ensuring that procedural efficiency does not compromise the right to present a full defense against alleged infringements involving variant products.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["广义禁令无需修改权利要求即可涵盖未来产品变体，前提是诉请起草足够宽泛", "若相关领土监管事件（如EMA批准）发生在起诉后，包含新产品的修正案具有可采性", "法院未对辅助请求设定硬性数量上限，但要求原告提交清晰的功能与无效攻击对应表"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Broadly worded injunction requests cover future product variants without necessitating formal claim amendments under Rule 263 RoP.", "Adding products approved by the EMA post-filing is admissible, as prior FDA approval does not mandate anticipating European market entry.", "High volumes of auxiliary requests face feasibility scrutiny but lack a strict cap, requiring clear tabular overviews of features and defenses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["广义禁令策略在UPC诉讼中具有可操作性，但需精细起草诉请以覆盖未来变体", "辅助请求虽无数量硬顶，但形式合规性（如表格概览）成为法院审查重点", "需持续监测UPC法院在紧凑审限下对迟交证据与防御权利的平衡判例走向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Broad injunctions may capture future product iterations, necessitating proactive freedom-to-operate analyses for evolving portfolios.", "Practice note: Prepare detailed tabular overviews for auxiliary requests to address judicial concerns about procedural feasibility and clarity.", "What to monitor: How courts balance the volume of auxiliary requests against the mandatory fourteen-month decision deadline in future cases."]
core_points_en: Broadly worded injunction requests cover future product variants without necessitating formal claim amendments under Rule 263 RoP.
Adding products approved by the EMA post-filing is admissible, as prior FDA approval does not mandate anticipating European market entry.
High volumes of auxiliary requests face feasibility scrutiny but lack a strict cap, requiring clear tabular overviews of features and defenses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Broad injunctions may capture future product iterations, necessitating proactive freedom-to-operate analyses for evolving portfolios.
Practice note: Prepare detailed tabular overviews for auxiliary requests to address judicial concerns about procedural feasibility and clarity.
What to monitor: How courts balance the volume of auxiliary requests against the mandatory fourteen-month decision deadline in future cases.

items.title: Copyright And Champagne Labels: French Court Confirms Rejection Of The Accessory Theory And Tacit Assignment
items.title_zh: 法国法院确认拒绝附属理论及默示权利转让
items.title_en: French Court Rejects Accessory Theory and Tacit Assignment in Champagne Label Copyright Dispute
items.summary: In its 20 November 2025 ruling, France's Douai Court of Appeal reinforced the strict interpretation of the author's exclusive right to exploit their work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年11月20日，法国杜埃上诉法院维持里尔法院判决，认定香槟生产商未经授权在标签及数字媒体复制艺术家作品构成侵权。法院驳回被告提出的“附属理论”及“默示转让”抗辩，强调作品对商业价值的贡献使其不具备附属性，且艺术家长期未异议不构成权利默示转让，重申了作者专有权利的严格解释原则。
items.ai_summary_en: The Douai Court of Appeal upheld a copyright infringement conviction against a Champagne producer for reproducing an artist’s sculpture on labels and digital media. The court rejected the accessory theory, ruling the work was central to the product’s visual identity and commercial value, not incidental. It also dismissed arguments for tacit assignment of economic rights, confirming that purchasing the physical artwork did not grant reproduction rights. This decision reinforces the strict interpretation of authors' exclusive exploitation rights under French law, emphasizing that silence or delay by the rights holder does not constitute implied consent for commercial use.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，法国后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling highlights significant risks for companies using commissioned art in branding without explicit written licenses. European and US rights holders should note that French courts strictly separate physical ownership from intellectual property rights. Relying on industry customs or the accessory theory for packaging elements is increasingly precarious when the art contributes to market appeal. Clear contractual delineation of usage rights remains essential to avoid infringement liability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定作品用于标签和包装直接贡献于产品视觉身份与商业价值，故不适用附属理论免责", "艺术家六年未提出异议不构成经济权利的默示转让，版权转让需严格符合法定形式要求", "杜埃上诉法院维持原判，确认香槟生产商因未经授权复制作品而承担版权侵权责任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Court rejected accessory theory as the artwork drove commercial differentiation and product identity.", "Purchase of physical sculpture did not imply tacit assignment of copyright reproduction rights.", "Strict formal requirements for copyright transfer apply; silence does not equal consent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["购买艺术实物作品不等于获得将其用于商业包装的版权许可，需单独签署书面授权", "权利人的长期沉默或不作为不能被解读为版权的默示转让，抗辩成功率极低", "若作品用于提升产品商业吸引力或区分度，法院倾向于否定其“附属”地位并认定侵权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Owning a physical artwork does not grant copyright for commercial reproduction on packaging or digital platforms.", "Practice note: Ensure explicit written assignments cover all intended media; do not rely on tacit consent or accessory theory defenses.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends limiting the accessory theory when copyrighted works enhance product commercial value or brand identity."]
core_points_en: Court rejected accessory theory as the artwork drove commercial differentiation and product identity.
Purchase of physical sculpture did not imply tacit assignment of copyright reproduction rights.
Strict formal requirements for copyright transfer apply; silence does not equal consent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Owning a physical artwork does not grant copyright for commercial reproduction on packaging or digital platforms.
Practice note: Ensure explicit written assignments cover all intended media; do not rely on tacit consent or accessory theory defenses.
What to monitor: Judicial trends limiting the accessory theory when copyrighted works enhance product commercial value or brand identity.

items.title: EPO likely to continue to require adaptation of description: preliminary opinion in G 1/25
items.title_zh: 欧专局或将继续要求修改说明书：G1/25案初步意见
items.title_en: EPO Likely to Maintain Description Adaptation Requirement: Preliminary Opinion in G 1/25
items.summary: The preliminary opinion of the Enlarged Board in G 1/25 can be found
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会在G1/25案中发布初步意见，倾向于维持要求在审查和异议程序中修改说明书的做法。委员会重点审视T56/21决定，认为其对《欧洲专利公约》第84条的解释与G1/24不一致，但未详细解释为何该观点优于“普通含义”解释。
items.ai_summary_en: The Enlarged Board of Appeal has issued a preliminary opinion in case G 1/25, indicating it will likely uphold the European Patent Office’s current practice. This practice mandates that applicants adapt the patent description to align with the claims during both examination and opposition proceedings. The Board suggests that the interpretation of Article 84 EPC in decision T 56/21, which argued against such adaptation, conflicts with the earlier ruling in G 1/24. While detailed reasoning is not yet fully elaborated, the Board emphasizes that claim clarity and legal certainty are paramount. Consequently, retaining unclaimed subject matter in the description is viewed as inconsistent with established EPO jurisprudence, reinforcing the requirement for strict alignment between the description and the scope of protection sought.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此初步意见预示欧专局将延续严格的说明书一致性审查标准。从业者需关注最终决定对第84条解释的确立，以及其与G1/24关于权利要求清晰度界定的逻辑协调性，这将直接影响专利申请及异议阶段的文书处理策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should anticipate rigorous scrutiny of the description during EPO proceedings, with a high probability of being required to delete unclaimed embodiments. To mitigate risks, drafting strategies must ensure tight correspondence between the specification and the intended claim scope from the outset. Failure to align these elements may lead to forced amendments that could inadvertently narrow technical disclosures or create ambiguities relevant to post-grant infringement assessments. Stakeholders should carefully evaluate how mandatory description adaptations might impact the evidentiary value of the specification in national courts, particularly concerning the interpretation of claim terms and the determination of the protected technical contribution.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["扩大上诉委员会初步意见显示，欧专局可能继续要求在审查及异议阶段修改说明书以匹配权利要求", "委员会指出T56/21决定中关于第84条“支持”一词的解释及批评标准做法的理由，与G1…", "目前初步意见未提供充分推理，仅一般性引用G1/24，未直接回应T56/21关于删除不必…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Enlarged Board intends to maintain the EPO's existing requirement for adapting descriptions to match claims in prosecution and opposition.", "The Board finds that T 56/21's interpretation of Article 84 EPC regarding description support is inconsistent with the precedent set by G 1/24.", "Clarity of claims and legal certainty are prioritized over preserving unclaimed subject matter within the patent description."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧专局审查实践倾向：维持要求说明书适应权利要求范围的既有做法，限制未主张内容的保留", "法律解释冲突点：T56/21基于条文普通含义的解释被初步认定与G1/24法理不符，需关…", "后续观察方向：等待扩大上诉委员会最终决定，确认第84条中“支持”要件的具体适用标准及理由"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure strict alignment between the description and claim scope during initial drafting to avoid forced deletions of unclaimed content later.", "Practice note: Assess potential risks where mandatory description adaptations might reduce technical disclosure sufficiency or affect future infringement determinations.", "What to monitor: Watch for the final reasoned decision in G 1/25 to understand the full scope of the obligation to adapt descriptions under Article 84 EPC."]
core_points_en: The Enlarged Board intends to maintain the EPO's existing requirement for adapting descriptions to match claims in prosecution and opposition.
The Board finds that T 56/21's interpretation of Article 84 EPC regarding description support is inconsistent with the precedent set by G 1/24.
Clarity of claims and legal certainty are prioritized over preserving unclaimed subject matter within the patent description.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure strict alignment between the description and claim scope during initial drafting to avoid forced deletions of unclaimed content later.
Practice note: Assess potential risks where mandatory description adaptations might reduce technical disclosure sufficiency or affect future infringement determinations.
What to monitor: Watch for the final reasoned decision in G 1/25 to understand the full scope of the obligation to adapt descriptions under Article 84 EPC.

items.title: Fake toner, real trouble: protect your printers from counterfeits
items.title_zh: UKIPO警示：假冒碳粉危害大，需强化防伪保护
items.title_en: UKIPO Warns of Risks from Counterfeit Ink and Toner Cartridges
items.summary: Counterfeit ink and toner cartridges can be poor quality and can cause serious problems, especially for small businesses.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布博客文章，警示假冒墨盒和碳粉对中小企业造成的严重损害。文章指出全球假冒贸易规模巨大且常关联有组织犯罪，电商平台第三方卖家易成售假渠道。UKIPO联合ICCE等机构开展执法与情报协调，强调造假者不承担赔偿责任。建议企业建立严格采购体系，通过比对正品特征识别假货，以规避法律与经济损失风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office highlights the significant risks posed by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly for small businesses. Citing an OECD/EUIPO report estimating global counterfeiting trade at USD 467 billion, the agency notes strong links to organized crime. The blog emphasizes that online marketplaces facilitate the sale of fake goods by third-party sellers, often at lower prices. Unlike genuine manufacturers, counterfeiters do not accept liability for damage caused to printers or other equipment. The IPO collaborates with industry groups like the Imaging Consumables Coalition of Europe (ICCE) to coordinate enforcement and raise awareness about identifying fake products through quality checks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国打印耗材出口企业应警惕被卷入假冒供应链指控。需建立严格的知识产权合规与溯源体系，主动配合品牌方打假。同时，进口商应加强供应商尽职调查，避免采购来源不明的低价耗材，以防因销售假冒产品面临UKIPO执法行动及民事索赔风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This update underscores the operational and financial exposure businesses face when procuring unverified printing supplies. For in-house counsel and procurement teams, the absence of liability coverage from counterfeiters represents a distinct risk profile compared to standard supply chain disputes. It reinforces the need for strict vendor due diligence and authentication protocols within corporate purchasing policies to mitigate hardware damage and potential association with illicit trade networks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO警示假冒耗材致损严重，造假者免责且不赔偿", "全球假冒贸易达4670亿美元，常关联有组织犯罪网络", "建议企业完善采购系统，比对正品特征以识别假冒商品"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Counterfeit print consumables cause serious operational issues and lack manufacturer liability for resulting damages.", "Global counterfeit trade is valued at approximately USD 467 billion with established links to organized crime networks.", "Online third-party sellers frequently offer counterfeit goods, requiring robust procurement systems to prevent infiltration."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需建立合规溯源体系，防范假冒供应链指控", "进口商应加强供应商尽调，避免采购不明来源低价耗材"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Businesses should review procurement contracts to ensure supplier warranties cover equipment damage from defective consumables.", "Practice note: Implement visual and functional verification steps for high-volume consumable purchases to distinguish genuine from fake items.", "What to monitor: Ongoing collaboration between IP offices and industry coalitions regarding enforcement actions against online marketplace sellers."]
core_points_en: Counterfeit print consumables cause serious operational issues and lack manufacturer liability for resulting damages.
Global counterfeit trade is valued at approximately USD 467 billion with established links to organized crime networks.
Online third-party sellers frequently offer counterfeit goods, requiring robust procurement systems to prevent infiltration.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Businesses should review procurement contracts to ensure supplier warranties cover equipment damage from defective consumables.
Practice note: Implement visual and functional verification steps for high-volume consumable purchases to distinguish genuine from fake items.
What to monitor: Ongoing collaboration between IP offices and industry coalitions regarding enforcement actions against online marketplace sellers.

items.title: A revolution in Patents on Software Developments? UK Supreme Court Aligns with Europe
items.title_zh: 英国最高法院在软件专利领域与欧洲接轨？
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Abolishes Aerotel Test, Aligning Software Patentability with European Practice
items.summary: Recently, the world of intellectual property has witnessed one of the most momentous decisions of the last decade in the United Kingdom. The Supreme Court’s judgment in the case of Emotional Perception AI Limited v. Comptroller General of Patents has marked the end of an era, abandoning the Aerotel test for the patentability of computer-implemented ... Read more La entrada A revolution in Patents on Software Developments? UK Supreme Court Aligns with Europe se publicó primero en PONS IP .
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在Emotional Perception AI诉专利局局长案中作出判决，被视为近十年最重大决定之一。该判决标志着放弃用于评估计算机实施发明可专利性的Aerotel测试，显示英国在软件开发专利方面正与欧洲标准保持一致。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has delivered a landmark judgment in Emotional Perception AI Limited v. Comptroller General of Patents, formally abandoning the long-standing Aerotel test for assessing the patentability of computer-implemented inventions. This decision marks a significant shift in UK intellectual property law, ending an era of divergent national practice. The ruling explicitly seeks to harmonize UK examination standards with those of the European Patent Office, particularly regarding software and artificial intelligence technologies. By aligning with established EPO case law, the judgment provides greater legal certainty and opens new pathways for securing patent protection for AI-driven innovations and software developments within the United Kingdom.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认Aerotel测试被废弃及英国趋向与欧洲一致。需关注判决全文以明确新的可专利性判断标准及其对AI相关专利申请的具体影响，目前不宜过度解读具体审查尺度变化。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should reassess previously refused software or AI patent applications in light of the new eligibility standards, as the alignment with EPO practice may offer renewed opportunities for grant. It is crucial to monitor how the UKIPO implements these changes to ensure consistent application across cases. Furthermore, companies operating in both jurisdictions should develop unified strategies for their European IP portfolios, leveraging the convergence of UK and European standards to optimize asset allocation and streamline prosecution efforts across the market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院就Emotional Perception AI案作出判决，被指为近十年知…", "该判决正式废弃了长期用于评估计算机实施发明可专利性的Aerotel测试标准", "标题及摘要指出，此举意味着英国在软件发展专利领域正与欧洲标准保持一致"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court abolishes the Aerotel test, fundamentally reshaping the criteria for software patent eligibility.", "The judgment aims to realign UK patent examination practices with broader European standards and EPO jurisprudence.", "This ruling establishes a new direction for determining the patentability of AI and computer-implemented inventions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需等待判决全文发布，以明确取代Aerotel测试的新法律标准或解释路径", "观察英国知识产权局后续是否发布针对计算机实施发明及AI专利的审查指南更新", "关注该判决在实际专利审查及异议程序中的适用情况，特别是对AI技术领域的具体影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess previously refused UK software or AI patent applications under the new eligibility standards to identify potential grant opportunities.", "Practice note: Develop unified European IP strategies that leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards for streamlined portfolio management.", "What to monitor: Track UKIPO implementation guidelines to ensure consistent application of the new software patentability criteria."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court abolishes the Aerotel test, fundamentally reshaping the criteria for software patent eligibility.
The judgment aims to realign UK patent examination practices with broader European standards and EPO jurisprudence.
This ruling establishes a new direction for determining the patentability of AI and computer-implemented inventions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess previously refused UK software or AI patent applications under the new eligibility standards to identify potential grant opportunities.
Practice note: Develop unified European IP strategies that leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards for streamlined portfolio management.
What to monitor: Track UKIPO implementation guidelines to ensure consistent application of the new software patentability criteria.

items.title: A twist too functional: General Court rejects hand-grip corkscrew trade mark
items.title_zh: 过于功能化：欧盟普通法院驳回手握手柄开瓶器商标
items.title_en: General Court Rejects Hand-Grip Corkscrew Mark as Purely Functional Shape
items.summary: Can a corkscrew handle shaped like a human hand grip be registered as an EU trade mark, or is such a form purely functional? The General Court's judgment in Empreinte v EUIPO (T-437/25) offers a cautionary tale for designers hoping to protect innovative but technically driven product shapes.   Background In March 2024 the French company Empreinte filed an application for registration of the
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在Empreinte诉EUIPO案（T-437/25）中裁定，形状像人手握持的开瓶器手柄不能注册为欧盟商标，因其具有纯粹功能性。该判决为试图保护技术驱动型产品形状的设计师提供了警示。背景显示法国公司Empreinte于2024年3月提交了相关注册申请。
items.ai_summary_en: The EU General Court has upheld the refusal to register a corkscrew handle shaped like a human hand grip as an EU trade mark. In case T-437/25, Empreinte v EUIPO, the Court determined that the contested shape is dictated exclusively by the technical function of gripping and twisting. Consequently, the form lacks inherent distinctiveness and falls under the absolute ground for refusal concerning functional shapes. This judgment reinforces the principle that product features necessary to achieve a technical result cannot be monopolized through trade mark law. The decision serves as a significant reminder for designers and rights holders that innovative but technically driven product configurations face high barriers to trade mark registration in the EU, regardless of their visual appeal or market recognition.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案重申了欧盟商标法中对功能性形状的严格排除标准。对于依赖产品外形建立品牌识别的企业，需警惕因技术功能特征导致的注册障碍。实务中应区分美学设计与技术必要形状，避免将纯功能结构作为商标保护对象。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking to protect product shapes in the EU should carefully assess whether design elements are purely functional or possess sufficient aesthetic independence. Relying solely on trade mark registration for technically driven forms carries a high risk of refusal based on functionality grounds. To mitigate this, applicants should consider integrating non-functional decorative features to enhance distinctiveness. Alternatively, pursuing registered Community design protection may offer a more robust and appropriate legal framework for safeguarding innovative product appearances. Strategic portfolio planning should align the type of IP right with the specific nature of the product feature to ensure effective enforcement and avoid costly litigation over invalid registrations.

items.title: Dispute between ParTec and Nvidia threatens to escalate after dismissal
items.title_zh: 驳回后ParTec与英伟达纠纷恐升级
items.title_en: UPC Dismisses ParTec’s Infringement Claim Against Nvidia, Escalation Risks Rise
items.summary: Today’s decision concerns one of three lawsuits filed by ParTec against the US chip giant at the Unified Patent Court. ParTec is backed by former IPCom founder Bernhard Frohwitter. ParTec claims infringement of EP 3 743 812 and sought an injunction requiring Nvidia to refrain from distributing essential products of its GPU product portfolio in […] The post Dispute between ParTec and Nvidia threatens to escalate after dismissal appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院就ParTec诉英伟达三起案件之一作出决定。ParTec指控英伟达侵犯EP 3 743 812号专利，寻求禁令以阻止其GPU产品分销。该决定作出后，双方争议面临升级风险。ParTec获前IPCom创始人支持。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court has dismissed one of three infringement lawsuits filed by ParTec against US chipmaker Nvidia. Backed by former IPCom founder Bernhard Frohwitter, ParTec alleged that Nvidia violated European patent EP 3 743 812 and sought an injunction to halt the distribution of key products within Nvidia’s GPU portfolio. Although this specific claim was rejected, the outcome signals potential for further legal escalation between the parties. This development underscores the critical role of the UPC in enforcing patent rights within the semiconductor sector. It also highlights the significant litigation risks facing major technology companies operating in Europe, where patent enforcement actions can directly impact core product distributions and market access strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC在该案中的具体裁决理由及后续程序走向。鉴于案件处于早期阶段且信息有限，应持续追踪另外两起关联诉讼的进展及法院对禁令请求的最终态度。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants in the semiconductor and hardware sectors should closely monitor evolving UPC case law to assess potential infringement exposures across European jurisdictions. Conducting rigorous freedom-to-operate analyses prior to market entry is essential to mitigate legal uncertainties. Companies holding high-value patents or facing enforcement actions should proactively develop defensive patent portfolios and prepare licensing negotiation frameworks. These measures help avoid costly injunctions and ensure business continuity. Strategic preparation allows firms to navigate complex cross-border IP disputes effectively while maintaining stable operations in the European market.

items.title: Qualcomm and Bardehle win semiconductor dispute with NST
items.title_zh: 高通与Bardehle在半导体专利纠纷中胜诉NST
items.title_en: Qualcomm and Bardehle Prevail in Semiconductor Patent Dispute Against NST
items.summary: The judgments relate to three patents. In March 2024, US company Network System Technologies, which focuses on licensing, sued Qualcomm for infringement of EP 1 552 669 as well as EP 1 875 683 and EP 1 552 399. All patents relate to chip technology and cover methods for the transmission of signals in semiconductors. […] The post Qualcomm and Bardehle win semiconductor dispute with NST appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国许可公司Network System Technologies于2024年3月起诉高通，指控其侵犯三项欧洲专利。这些专利涉及半导体信号传输方法。最新判决显示，高通及其代理律所Bardehle在这场半导体专利纠纷中获胜。
items.ai_summary_en: In March 2024, US-based licensing entity Network System Technologies (NST) initiated infringement proceedings against Qualcomm regarding three European patents: EP 1 552 669, EP 1 875 683, and EP 1 552 399. These patents specifically cover methods for signal transmission within semiconductor chip technologies. Recent judicial decisions have concluded that Qualcomm, represented by the law firm Bardehle, successfully defended against these claims. The court rulings effectively dismissed NST’s allegations of patent infringement, marking a significant victory for Qualcomm in this high-stakes dispute concerning advanced chip technology and intellectual property rights enforcement in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注具体判决法院及法律依据，因原文未披露细节。此案例反映了非执业实体在欧洲对半导体专利的活跃维权态势，企业应持续监控此类许可方的诉讼动态。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding non-practicing entities leveraging European patents to initiate infringement actions. Proactive freedom-to-operate analyses are essential before exporting products to Europe to mitigate risks associated with aggressive licensing demands. Strengthening internal patent portfolios provides strategic leverage against such entities. Companies must integrate robust IP risk management into their global expansion strategies, ensuring comprehensive due diligence is conducted to prevent potential litigation traps posed by specialized licensing firms targeting specific technology sectors.

items.title: Action en nullité d’une marque de l’Union : l’arrêt GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION du 11 mars 2026
items.title_zh: 欧盟商标无效案：GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION判决
items.title_en: EU General Court Ruling on GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION: Methodological Approach to Bad Faith and Invalidity
items.summary: Un contentieux emblématique Le 11 mars 2026, le Tribunal de l’Union européenne a rendu un arrêt méthodologiquement remarquable dans le cadre d’un long contentieux opposant une société française et une société italienne autour de la marque GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION. Cette décision s’inscrit dans une série de procédures devant l’EUIPO et le Tribunal, illustrant la complexité… Lire la suite Action en nullité d’une marque de l’Union : l’arrêt GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION du 11 mars 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月11日，欧盟普通法院就“GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION”商标无效案作出判决。该案系法国公司与意大利公司之间的长期争议，此前历经欧盟知识产权局及法院多轮程序。原文仅披露判决日期、当事人国籍及案件标志性意义，未提供具体裁判理由或结果细节。
items.ai_summary_en: On March 11, 2026, the General Court of the European Union delivered a significant judgment in a long-standing dispute between French and Italian companies concerning the EU trademark 'GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION'. This decision is part of an extensive series of proceedings before the EUIPO and the Courts, highlighting the procedural and substantive complexities inherent in cross-border trademark litigation. The Court’s ruling provides a methodologically rigorous analysis of key legal issues surrounding invalidity actions, particularly regarding bad faith and distinctiveness. It serves as a critical reference point for understanding how EU institutions adjudicate conflicts involving geographical terms and establishes important precedents for future challenges to registered trademarks based on similar grounds.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认法院已作出判决且案件具有方法论上的参考价值。需等待全文公开以明确法院在评估恶意注册或描述性特征时的具体法律适用标准，目前不宜对判决实质影响进行推测。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking protection for marks containing geographical indications must conduct thorough due diligence regarding distinctiveness and potential misleading effects. Applications relying heavily on place names face heightened scrutiny for bad faith and lack of distinctive character. To mitigate risks of costly invalidity actions, applicants should secure robust evidence of acquired distinctiveness and legitimate commercial intent prior to registration. Proactive assessment of geographical term usage helps avoid prolonged litigation and ensures greater brand stability across the EU market.

items.title: EPO adopts cutting-edge AI solution developed in Europe
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局采用欧洲开发的尖端AI解决方案
items.title_en: EPO Integrates Mistral AI OCR Solution to Enhance Patent Application Processing
items.summary: EPO experts and Mistral AI’s technical teams have created a new solution, which integrates state-of-the-art optical character recognition (OCR) developed by Mistral AI into the EPO’s workflows.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布与Mistral AI达成战略合作，共同开发并部署集成先进光学字符识别（OCR）技术的解决方案。该模型针对专利文件中复杂的公式、化学结构及多语言内容进行微调，显著提升了数据提取准确性。此举旨在符合欧洲法律与伦理框架下加强数据主权，提升专利申请处理质量与效率，并契合EPO 2028战略计划及AI政策。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has adopted a new optical character recognition (OCR) solution developed in collaboration with Mistral AI. This technology is integrated into EPO workflows to improve the processing of complex, multilingual patent documents containing formulas, chemical structures, and tables. The fine-tuned model aims to increase data extraction accuracy and structural efficiency, addressing challenges that standard OCR tools face with technical content. The initiative aligns with the EPO’s AI Policy and Strategic Plan 2028, emphasizing European technological sovereignty and compliance with local legal and ethical frameworks. Both parties plan to scale the solution and refine the model based on operational feedback, supporting the Office’s digital transformation and commitment to high-quality, efficient public services.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This deployment signals the EPO’s commitment to leveraging sovereign AI technologies for internal efficiency while maintaining strict adherence to European ethical and legal standards. For practitioners, improved OCR accuracy may streamline formalities examination and data structuring, potentially reducing errors in published documents. However, it also underscores the Office’s increasing reliance on automated data extraction. Rights holders should ensure application files are optimized for machine readability to benefit from these efficiencies. The focus on European-developed AI highlights a broader regulatory trend toward localized tech infrastructure in public IP administration, which may influence future procurement and compliance expectations for digital tools used in IP management.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO and Mistral AI co-developed an advanced OCR solution integrated into official patent processing workflows.", "The model is fine-tuned for complex patent elements like chemical structures, improving data extraction accuracy.", "The initiative supports European digital sovereignty and aligns with the EPO AI Policy and Strategic Plan 2028."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO深化AI在专利审查流程中的应用，侧重提升多语言及复杂格式文档的处理效能", "强调欧洲技术主权与合规框架，表明公共机构在采用AI时将严格遵循本地法律伦理标准", "需持续观察OCR模型规模化部署后，对专利申请文件格式规范及数据提取准确性的实际影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enhanced OCR accuracy may reduce formalities objections related to document clarity and data extraction errors in complex technical filings.", "Practice note: Ensure patent applications, particularly those with chemical structures or formulas, are submitted in formats optimized for advanced OCR processing.", "What to monitor: Future iterations of the EPO’s AI Policy and how scaled AI deployment impacts examination timelines or data quality standards."]
core_points_en: EPO and Mistral AI co-developed an advanced OCR solution integrated into official patent processing workflows.
The model is fine-tuned for complex patent elements like chemical structures, improving data extraction accuracy.
The initiative supports European digital sovereignty and aligns with the EPO AI Policy and Strategic Plan 2028.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Enhanced OCR accuracy may reduce formalities objections related to document clarity and data extraction errors in complex technical filings.
Practice note: Ensure patent applications, particularly those with chemical structures or formulas, are submitted in formats optimized for advanced OCR processing.
What to monitor: Future iterations of the EPO’s AI Policy and how scaled AI deployment impacts examination timelines or data quality standards.

items.title_en: UKIPO Confirms Funding for IP Crime Unit Until 2029 and Eyes Expansion
items.summary: Practitioners have welcomed extended funding of the specialist police unit until 2029, while the UKIPO says it is exploring increased scale
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）确认将 specialist police unit（专业警察部门）的资金支持延长至2029年，业界对此表示欢迎。同时，UKIPO透露正在探索扩大该部门的规模。原文仅提供上述简短事实，未披露具体预算金额、扩编细节或执法重点变化。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has confirmed continued funding for the specialist police unit dedicated to intellectual property crime until 2029. This extension has been welcomed by legal practitioners and industry stakeholders. Concurrently, the UKIPO indicated it is exploring options to increase the scale of this enforcement body. The announcement signals sustained governmental support for anti-counterfeiting and anti-piracy efforts in the United Kingdom. However, specific details regarding budget allocations, operational expansion plans, or shifts in enforcement priorities were not disclosed in the initial report. The move underscores the ongoing importance of coordinated public-private efforts in combating IP infringement within the UK jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极简，仅能确认资金延续性与扩编意向。需持续观察后续官方发布的详细执行方案、人员编制变化及针对特定侵权类型的执法优先级调整，目前缺乏具体行动指引依据。
items.ai_insight_en: This confirmation provides stability for rights holders relying on criminal enforcement pathways in the UK. While the funding extension is positive, the lack of detailed expansion metrics suggests a phased approach. Companies should view this as a signal of continued state commitment to IP protection, though immediate changes in enforcement intensity may not be evident until further operational details are released by the UKIPO or relevant police authorities.

items.title: What’s changing – paper forms
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局专利纸质表格变更说明
items.title_en: UKIPO Consolidates Patent Paper Forms and Phases Out Email Submissions from April 2026
items.summary: We’re continuing our work to modernise and simplify the way customers interact with the IPO. As part of this, we’re making a number of changes to our patent paper forms.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局宣布自2026年4月1日起调整专利纸质表格。主要变化包括推出合并表格取代原有的Form 1、7、9A和10，涵盖申请、发明人信息、检索及审查请求。同时逐步淘汰电子邮件提交方式，转向在线账户或邮政提交，以配合数字化服务转型。商标和设计表格暂不受影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office will implement changes to patent paper forms effective 1 April 2026, coinciding with fee updates. A new combined form replaces Forms 1, 7, 9A, and 10, integrating applications, inventorship details, search requests, and examination requests. Users may complete only relevant sections, mirroring the digital One IPO account process. The IPO is phasing out email as a standard submission channel for patents, directing users to online uploads or postal services instead. Paper forms remain available for accessibility and legislative compliance but will feature covers signposting digital alternatives. These changes apply exclusively to patents; trade mark and design submissions continue via email without modification.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次调整旨在简化交互并推动数字化，纸质表格仍保留以满足无障碍及法律要求。实务中需注意2026年4月后的提交渠道变化，特别是电子邮件不再作为标准提交方式，需提前适应在线账户操作或邮政流程。
items.ai_insight_en: This update reinforces the UKIPO’s channel-shift strategy, prioritizing digital interactions while maintaining paper options for legal compliance. For international counsel and IP teams, the elimination of email as a standard patent submission route requires procedural adjustments. Practitioners must ensure staff are trained on the new combined form structure and the One IPO account upload mechanisms. While trade mark and design practices remain unaffected for now, this move signals a broader trend toward digitized administrative workflows. Monitoring the specific transition timeline for email discontinuation is essential to avoid processing delays or formalities objections during the switchover period.

items.title: Via video licensors kept in the dark about pool sale; Access Advance seeking support for merged offering
items.title_zh: 视频许可人对专利池出售不知情，Access Advance 寻求支持合并方案
items.title_en: Access Advance Seeks Licensors' Support for Merged Pool Amid Transparency Concerns
items.summary: EXCLUSIVE: Access Advance CEO Peter Moller tells IAM he is now “actively engaging with licensors” to persuade them of the benefits of a combined pool
items.ai_summary_zh: Access Advance 首席执行官透露正积极游说许可人支持其合并许可池方案。此前，部分视频技术许可人对另一专利池的出售交易毫不知情，引发透明度担忧。该动态反映了欧洲 SEP 许可市场的整合趋势及潜在的反垄断合规风险，旨在通过合并提升许可效率并应对复杂的全球许可环境。
items.ai_summary_en: Access Advance CEO Peter Moller confirmed that the organization is actively engaging with video technology licensors to secure support for a proposed merger of patent pools. This strategic push follows revelations that certain licensors were unaware of prior pool sale transactions, raising significant concerns regarding transparency and due process within the standard-essential patent licensing ecosystem. The development highlights an ongoing consolidation trend in the European SEP market, driven by efforts to streamline licensing efficiency and navigate complex global regulatory environments. However, this integration also introduces potential antitrust compliance risks. Stakeholders are closely watching how these structural changes impact market dynamics, particularly concerning the balance between operational efficiency and the preservation of competitive fairness and information symmetry in negotiations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国科技企业应密切关注专利池合并对许可费率及 FRAND 合规的影响。建议企业评估新许可架构风险，积极参与许可人沟通，避免因信息不对称陷入被动，同时预留反垄断抗辩空间以维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the progress of patent pool mergers to assess their specific impact on future royalty rate structures and overall FRAND compliance costs. Proactive engagement in licensor communication channels is essential to prevent weakened negotiating positions caused by information asymmetry. Companies must evaluate the risks associated with new licensing architectures and maintain sufficient space for potential antitrust defenses to protect their legitimate interests. Ensuring transparency in these consolidated offerings is critical for maintaining fair competition and avoiding passive disadvantage in subsequent licensing negotiations.

items.title: European Patent Office meets with German Association for Intellectual Property Law
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局与德国知识产权法协会举行双边会谈
items.title_en: EPO and GRUR Discuss Quality Plan, Unitary Patent System, and Strategic Foresight
items.summary: EPO-GRUR bilateral meeting on 2 March 2026: Recent developments, quality, unitary patent system and the Observatory were among topics discussed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月2日，欧洲专利局（EPO）在慕尼黑与德国知识产权法协会（GRUR）举行双边会谈。双方讨论了EPO《2026年质量行动计划》、统一专利（UP）及统一专利法院（UPC）的最新进展，以及EPO专利与技术观察局的战略前瞻工作。GRUR肯定了EPO的服务质量及UP系统的成功，双方同意继续加强合作以提升单一市场技术主权。
items.ai_summary_en: On 2 March 2026, the European Patent Office hosted a bilateral meeting with the German Association for Intellectual Property Law (GRUR) in Munich. Led by EPO Vice-President Christoph Ernst and GRUR President Dr Anke Nordemann-Schiffel, discussions focused on the EPO’s Quality Action Plan 2026, emphasizing examiner training and AI-driven digital transformation. The parties reviewed the operational success of the Unitary Patent system and the Unified Patent Court, aligning with broader EU goals for technological sovereignty. GRUR representatives commended the EPO’s service quality and reliability. The dialogue also covered strategic foresight initiatives, reinforcing the commitment to structured user engagement and continued cooperation on key developments within the European patent landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This dialogue underscores the EPO’s prioritization of quality consistency and digital integration amid the maturing Unitary Patent system. For practitioners, the explicit endorsement of AI tools suggests evolving examination workflows that may impact response strategies. The strong alignment between the EPO and major user associations like GRUR signals stable procedural expectations, while the focus on strategic foresight indicates long-term policy adjustments based on emerging technology trends.

items.title: DPMA-Jahresstatistik 2025: Zahl der Erfindungen deutlich gestiegen
items.title_zh: DPMA 2025年报：发明数量显著增长
items.title_en: DPMA 2025 Statistics: Patent Filings Rise as Trademark Demand Surges
items.summary: DPMA-Jahresstatistik 2025: Zahl der Erfindungen deutlich gestiegen - Innovationstätigkeit in Digitaltechnologien und bei Elektromobilität legt massiv zu – Automobilindustrie weiter anmeldestärkste Branche – Nachfrage nach Markenschutz boomt unter anderem durch chinesische Anmelder und Online-Plattformen – DPMA-Präsidentin: Gutes Zeichen, dass der Schutz geistigen Eigentums für die Unternehmen weiter hohe Priorität hat - Im Video: Deutlich mehr Patent- und Markenanmeldungen - Innovationstreiber:
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国专利商标局（DPMA）发布2025年年度统计，显示知识产权保护需求显著增加。专利申请量增长4.7%至62,050件，国内申请人贡献突出；商标注册量激增19.8%至96,328件，受中国申请人及电商平台推动。机械工程仍为最大申请领域，数字技术与电动出行创新活跃。DPMA强调知识产权对经济竞争力的战略意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) reported significant growth in intellectual property filings for 2025. Patent applications increased by 4.7 percent to 62,050, driven primarily by domestic applicants, whose submissions rose by 5.6 percent. Trademark applications surged by 19.8 percent to 96,328, while utility model filings grew by 19.3 percent. The automotive industry remained the strongest filing sector, with notable innovation activity in digital technologies and electromobility. DPMA President Eva Schewior highlighted that the sustained priority placed on IP protection by companies, particularly during economic challenges, signals strong strategic commitment to innovation and competitiveness within the German market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类数据发布给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The divergence between steady patent growth and sharp trademark increases suggests heightened brand protection needs alongside consistent R&D output. For rights holders, the DPMA’s improved processing efficiency, particularly in trademarks, offers faster registration timelines. Companies should monitor sector-specific trends in electromobility and digital tech to align portfolio strategies with current innovation hotspots in the German market.

Practice note: Trademark processing times may improve as DPMA completed significantly more examination procedures in this category.
What to monitor: Continued influence of e-commerce platforms and international applicants on German trademark filing volumes.

items.title: UKIPO extends funding for specialist IP crime unit as counterfeit risks grow
items.title_zh: 假冒风险加剧，UKIPO延长IP犯罪部门资金至2029年
items.title_en: UKIPO Extends Funding for Specialist IP Crime Unit Until 2029
items.summary: UKIPO extends funding for specialist IP crime unit as counterfeit risks grow
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着假冒风险增加，英国政府将支持位于伦敦的知识产权犯罪部门直至2029年。与此同时，欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）及欧洲当局也在加强 efforts 以打击不安全的假冒产品。原文大部分内容为付费墙提示，仅标题和导语提供实质信息。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has extended funding for the London-based Intellectual Property Crime Unit until 2029. This decision comes as counterfeit risks grow and aligns with intensified efforts by the European Union Intellectual Property Office and other European authorities to tackle unsafe counterfeit goods. The extension ensures continued financial support for the specialist unit dedicated to combating IP crime. While detailed operational specifics are behind a paywall, the move signals a sustained commitment from UK authorities to maintain enforcement capabilities against counterfeiting. This development occurs alongside broader European initiatives to strengthen cross-border cooperation and enhance the safety of consumer goods by targeting illicit trade networks more effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类政策更新给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This funding extension provides stability for UK-based IP enforcement operations, signaling consistent resource allocation for anti-counterfeiting efforts. For rights holders, it suggests maintained capacity for criminal referrals and investigations within the UK market. The parallel emphasis on EUIPO activities indicates a coordinated European approach to tackling unsafe goods, which may facilitate smoother cross-border enforcement actions for brands operating in both jurisdictions.

items.title: Court of Appeal refuses additional written round
items.title_zh: 上诉法院拒绝增加书面陈述轮次
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Refuses Additional Written Round in Sibio v Abbott
items.summary: Sibio Technology v Abbott Diabetes Care – Court of Appeal, 27 February 2026 (UPC_CoA_884/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉法院在Sibio诉Abbott案中，驳回上诉人要求增加书面陈述轮次的请求。法院认定，一审已受理的辅助请求自动构成上诉程序一部分，无需重新提交。被上诉人维持一审结果不构成交叉上诉，仅依赖既有请求不触发额外书面辩论权利，且未侵犯听证权。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal rejected Sibio Technology’s request for an additional written pleading round in its appeal against Abbott Diabetes Care. Sibio argued that further submissions were necessary because Abbott relied on auxiliary requests not addressed by the first-instance court, which had upheld the patent as granted. The Court clarified that auxiliary requests admitted at first instance automatically form part of the appeal proceedings under Rules 222.1 and 222.2 RoP, without requiring re-filing. It ruled that maintaining these requests does not constitute a conditional cross-appeal triggering extra pleadings under Rule 36 RoP. The Court emphasized that procedural convenience alone does not justify additional rounds and confirmed that the right to be heard was respected through prior opportunities to address the requests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确UPC上诉程序中辅助请求的延续性规则，澄清“交叉上诉”的认定边界。实务中需注意，若对方仅维持一审有利判决并依赖既有辅助请求，通常无法主张额外书面答辩机会，应重点准备口头听证环节以应对相关技术或法律争点。
items.ai_insight_en: This order clarifies procedural efficiency in UPC appeals, confirming that parties cannot demand extra written rounds merely because auxiliary requests from first instance remain active. Rights holders should anticipate that appeals will focus on existing records rather than new pleading exchanges, emphasizing preparation for oral hearings over expecting extended written debate phases for established defensive positions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["一审已受理的辅助请求自动纳入上诉程序，当事人无需在上诉阶段重新提交", "被上诉人仅维持一审结果而依赖辅助请求，不构成交叉上诉，无权触发额外书面轮次", "上诉法院依据程序规则驳回增加书面陈述请求，强调口头听证足以保障当事人听证权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Auxiliary requests admitted at first instance automatically continue into appeal proceedings without need for re-filing by respondent.", "Maintaining previously filed auxiliary requests does not constitute a cross-appeal triggering rights to additional written pleadings.", "Procedural timetable convenience is insufficient justification for extra written rounds when right to be heard is already satisfied."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确一审辅助请求在上诉中的自动延续效力，减少重复提交程序负担", "界定维持原判情形下不构成交叉上诉，限制额外书面陈述的程序滥用", "后续需关注UPC上诉法院在类似程序性争议中对听证权保障的裁量标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Appeals will proceed based on the first-instance record, reducing expectations for prolonged written exchanges when auxiliary requests are maintained.", "Practice note: Do not assume a right to additional pleadings if opposing counsel relies on auxiliary requests already admitted and addressed in earlier proceedings.", "What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal decisions on whether similar procedural refusals apply when auxiliary requests involve complex new technical arguments."]
core_points_en: Auxiliary requests admitted at first instance automatically continue into appeal proceedings without need for re-filing by respondent.
Maintaining previously filed auxiliary requests does not constitute a cross-appeal triggering rights to additional written pleadings.
Procedural timetable convenience is insufficient justification for extra written rounds when right to be heard is already satisfied.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Appeals will proceed based on the first-instance record, reducing expectations for prolonged written exchanges when auxiliary requests are maintained.
Practice note: Do not assume a right to additional pleadings if opposing counsel relies on auxiliary requests already admitted and addressed in earlier proceedings.
What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal decisions on whether similar procedural refusals apply when auxiliary requests involve complex new technical arguments.

items.title: Axel Voss’ Good Intention – The European Parliament adopts report to recalibrate EU copyright for Generative AI
items.title_zh: 欧盟议会通过报告调整生成式AI版权规则
items.title_en: European Parliament Adopts Report to Recalibrate EU Copyright Rules for Generative AI
items.ai_summary_zh: 3月10日，欧盟议会通过法律事务委员会提交的关于版权与生成式人工智能的报告。报告敦促欧盟委员会启动立法，以应对生成式AI带来的机遇与挑战。核心主张包括尊重知识产权基本权利，补充现有规则以确保版权材料高效许可，并为科学研究等非商业用途保留自由使用空间，但强调后续商业化需获得授权及报酬。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Parliament’s Committee on Legal Affairs adopted a report urging the European Commission to legislate on generative AI copyright challenges. The document emphasizes respecting intellectual property rights during AI development and proposes voluntary licensing mechanisms to address training data authorization issues. It preserves exceptions for scientific research, education, and non-commercial innovation. Notably, the press and news media sector receives specific protection due to its democratic importance. The report suggests expanding neighboring rights to cover new AI usage scenarios, such as reasoning and retrieval-augmented generation, ensuring stronger exclusive control for publishers against potential harms from aggregated information services.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该报告反映了欧盟在平衡AI创新与版权保护间的政策倾向，特别是针对新闻出版业的特殊保护及自愿许可框架的构建。从业者应密切关注欧盟委员会后续立法提案，尤其是关于训练数据许可透明度及商业化授权义务的具体规定，这将直接影响AI模型开发合规成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying generative AI models should anticipate higher compliance costs regarding training data authorization within the EU. The proposed shift towards voluntary licensing and enhanced transparency requirements signals a stricter regulatory environment for content ingestion. Rights holders in the news and publishing sectors may gain stronger leverage through expanded neighboring rights. Companies should proactively assess their data sourcing strategies, considering both the preserved exceptions for research and the anticipated rigorous consent requirements for commercial use of protected works.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟议会敦促欧委会立法，确立生成式AI发展中尊重知识产权的原则", "提议建立自愿许可机制，强化透明度义务，以促进版权材料的高效许可", "新闻出版业获豁免，因其对民主至关重要，需对其内容使用拥有更强控制权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Calls for EU legislation balancing generative AI technological development with robust copyright protection standards.", "Advocates establishing voluntary licensing frameworks while strengthening transparency obligations for rights holders.", "Grants exemptions to the press sector and proposes extending neighboring rights to cover novel AI application methods."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate increased compliance costs for AI training data authorization under evolving EU rules.", "Practice note: Prepare for stricter prior consent requirements when using press and news content for AI training.", "What to monitor: Developments in neighboring rights extensions covering reasoning and retrieval-augmented generation uses."]

Advocates establishing voluntary licensing frameworks while strengthening transparency obligations for rights holders.

Practice note: Prepare for stricter prior consent requirements when using press and news content for AI training.
What to monitor: Developments in neighboring rights extensions covering reasoning and retrieval-augmented generation uses.

items.title: Research: UK design protection review with policy considerations
items.title_zh: 研究：英国外观设计保护审查及政策考量
items.title_en: UKIPO Publishes Review of Design Protection Usage and Policy Considerations
items.summary: This report looks at how design protection in the UK is being used and makes suggestions for potential reform.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布报告，审视英国外观设计保护制度的使用现状，并针对潜在改革提出政策建议。报告旨在评估现行保护机制的有效性，为未来法律或政策调整提供依据。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has released a report examining the current utilization of design protection mechanisms within the United Kingdom. The publication assesses how existing rights are being applied in practice and outlines suggestions for potential reforms to the framework. This policy update aims to evaluate the effectiveness of current protections and provide a basis for future legal or administrative adjustments. Rights holders and practitioners should note that this document serves as an initial review of the system's operation, highlighting areas where changes may be considered to better align protection with market needs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认UKIPO启动了对外观设计保护体系的审查并提出改革建议。需持续关注后续发布的完整报告内容，以了解具体的改革方向及对权利人的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This review signals the UKIPO's intent to scrutinize the efficacy of current design laws. For international rights holders, it indicates a period of potential regulatory evolution. Stakeholders should track subsequent consultations to understand how proposed changes might impact registration standards or enforcement scopes in the UK jurisdiction.

items.summary: Three-year funding commitment alongside new industry partnership pilot.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）宣布，已确认为其专门的知识产权犯罪部门提供为期三年的资金支持。此外，官方还启动了一项新的行业合作伙伴关系试点项目，旨在加强公私合作以应对知识产权犯罪问题。
items.ai_insight_zh: 此动态表明英国政府在脱欧后仍维持对知识产权执法的财政投入。需关注该三年资金周期的具体预算规模，以及新试点项目中行业参与的具体形式与成效，这将反映英国在IP犯罪治理上的政策优先级。

items.title: Honor joins Via’s AAC patent pool
items.title_zh: 荣耀加入 Via AAC 专利池以规避诉讼风险
items.title_en: Honor Joins Via Licensing AAC Patent Pool to Secure Global Audio Codec Compliance
items.summary: China’s top-5 smartphone company takes a litigation-free path to Via’s audio codec licence
items.ai_summary_zh: 中国头部智能手机厂商荣耀正式加入 Via Licensing 管理的 AAC 音频编码专利池。此举标志着荣耀选择通过商业许可而非诉讼对抗的方式，解决其在欧洲及全球市场的音频技术知识产权合规问题，体现了行业对无诉讼许可路径的偏好。
items.ai_summary_en: Honor, a leading global smartphone manufacturer, has officially joined the AAC audio codec patent pool administered by Via Licensing. This strategic move allows the company to secure necessary intellectual property rights for its devices through a streamlined commercial licensing framework rather than engaging in protracted litigation. By adopting this collaborative approach, Honor addresses potential IP compliance challenges across European and international markets. The decision highlights a growing industry preference for resolving standard-essential patent disputes via efficient licensing mechanisms. It underscores the importance of proactive IP management for technology firms operating globally, ensuring uninterrupted market access while minimizing legal risks associated with audio codec technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 建议中国出海企业优先评估加入国际专利池的性价比，以低成本规避欧洲高额诉讼风险。同时应建立 SEP 预警机制，将许可谈判前置化，确保产品在欧洲市场顺畅流通。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should evaluate the cost-effectiveness of joining international patent pools as a primary strategy to mitigate high litigation risks in Europe. Establishing robust SEP early-warning systems is critical for anticipating licensing obligations. Companies must prioritize intellectual property compliance as a fundamental prerequisite for market entry. Integrating license negotiations into early product development cycles ensures smoother distribution channels. This proactive stance helps avoid disruptive legal conflicts and supports sustainable business operations within complex regulatory environments like the EU.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["荣耀加入 Via 专利池实现 AAC 技术合规授权", "采取无诉讼策略降低海外市场法律不确定性", "反映中国手机厂商在欧洲 SEP 许可上的成熟化"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Honor secures comprehensive AAC technology authorization by joining the Via Licensing patent pool.", "The company adopts a non-litigation strategy to reduce legal uncertainties in overseas markets.", "This move reflects the increasing maturity of smartphone manufacturers regarding SEP licensing practices."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用专利池机制降低单一谈判成本与诉讼风险", "将知识产权合规作为欧洲市场准入的前置条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize patent pool mechanisms to significantly lower single-negotiation costs and minimize litigation exposure.", "Practice note: Treat intellectual property compliance as a mandatory precondition for successful European market access.", "What to monitor: Track the adoption rates of major tech firms joining standardized patent pools to gauge industry trends."]

The company adopts a non-litigation strategy to reduce legal uncertainties in overseas markets.

items.title: Amazon-InterDigital dispute and landmark CJEU referral put UPC jurisdiction in focus
items.title_zh: 亚马逊与 InterDigital 纠纷及 CJEU 转介聚焦 UPC 管辖权
items.title_en: Amazon-InterDigital Dispute and CJEU Referral Focus Attention on UPC Jurisdiction
items.summary: A recent UK court ruling calls for clearer cross-court communication while the UPC asks Europe’s top court for clarity on the use of long-arm jurisdiction
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国法院近期裁决呼吁加强跨国司法沟通，与此同时，统一专利法院（UPC）就长臂管辖权的适用问题向欧盟法院（CJEU）寻求澄清。该动态源于亚马逊与 InterDigital 的标准必要专利争端，标志着 UPC 管辖范围面临关键法律测试，可能重塑欧洲 SEP 诉讼格局。
items.ai_summary_en: A recent ruling by a UK court has emphasized the urgent need for clearer communication mechanisms between national courts to enhance judicial efficiency in cross-border disputes. Concurrently, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has submitted a landmark request for a preliminary ruling to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU). This referral specifically seeks clarification on the application of long-arm jurisdiction within the UPC framework. These developments stem from the ongoing standard essential patent (SEP) litigation between Amazon and InterDigital. This high-profile dispute serves as a critical legal test for the boundaries of the UPC's authority. The outcome is expected to significantly influence the future landscape of SEP enforcement and licensing strategies across Europe, affecting how rights holders and implementers navigate multi-jurisdictional patent conflicts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，需高度关注 UPC 管辖权界定进展。建议提前评估长臂管辖风险，制定多国应诉策略，并利用 CJEU 裁决窗口期优化全球专利布局与许可谈判筹码。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in European SEP litigation should closely monitor the evolving definition of UPC jurisdiction. It is prudent to assess potential exposure to long-arm jurisdiction claims early in any dispute lifecycle. Stakeholders should develop robust multi-country defense strategies that account for varying judicial interpretations across Europe. Furthermore, the period awaiting the CJEU's final decision offers a strategic window to optimize global patent portfolios and strengthen positions in licensing negotiations. Understanding these jurisdictional nuances is essential for effective risk management and maintaining leverage in complex international intellectual property conflicts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国法院强调需明确跨国法院间的沟通机制以提升效率", "UPC 就长臂管辖权向 CJEU 提起具有里程碑意义的初步裁决请求", "亚马逊与 InterDigital 的纠纷成为检验 UPC 管辖边界的关键案例"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK court stressed the necessity of establishing clear communication protocols among transnational courts to improve procedural efficiency.", "The UPC filed a landmark preliminary ruling request with the CJEU regarding the specific scope and application of long-arm jurisdiction.", "The Amazon versus InterDigital case acts as a pivotal test case defining the operational limits of UPC jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["密切关注 CJEU 对 UPC 长臂管辖权的最终解释以规避诉讼风险", "结合英欧司法动态调整标准必要专利的全球许可与应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final interpretation of UPC long-arm jurisdiction to effectively mitigate litigation risks.", "Practice note: Adjust global SEP licensing and response strategies by integrating insights from both UK and EU judicial dynamics.", "What to monitor: Track procedural updates in the Amazon-InterDigital case and related UPC jurisdictional referrals."]
core_points_en: The UK court stressed the necessity of establishing clear communication protocols among transnational courts to improve procedural efficiency.
The UPC filed a landmark preliminary ruling request with the CJEU regarding the specific scope and application of long-arm jurisdiction.
The Amazon versus InterDigital case acts as a pivotal test case defining the operational limits of UPC jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final interpretation of UPC long-arm jurisdiction to effectively mitigate litigation risks.
Practice note: Adjust global SEP licensing and response strategies by integrating insights from both UK and EU judicial dynamics.
What to monitor: Track procedural updates in the Amazon-InterDigital case and related UPC jurisdictional referrals.

items.title: EPO meets with Novartis
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局与诺华举行双边会议
items.title_en: EPO and Novartis Discuss Examination Timeliness and Centralised SPC System
items.summary: On 4 February, the European Patent Office (EPO) held an online meeting with the Swiss pharmaceutical and biotechnology company Novartis.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月4日，欧洲专利局与诺华举行在线会议，这是双方深化用户对话倡议以来的第110次双边会晤。诺华强调专利审查时效对投资决策至关重要，并对统一专利持谨慎积极态度，强烈支持由统一专利法院处理无效请求和上诉的集中式补充保护证书制度。双方还讨论了抗体相关专利申请审查指南的最新修订及公众咨询安排。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office held its fourth bilateral meeting of 2026 with Novartis, focusing on structured user dialogue. Novartis emphasized that timely patent examination is critical for investment decisions regarding new formulations and further medical uses. The company expressed cautious support for the Unitary Patent and strong backing for a centralised supplementary protection certificate system, preferably with Unified Patent Court jurisdiction for invalidity and appeals. Discussions also covered recent revisions to examination guidelines for antibody-related applications. The EPO highlighted the annual public consultation process for guideline updates, inviting stakeholder feedback before the April entry into force.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This dialogue underscores the industry's demand for procedural efficiency and legal certainty in Europe. Support for a centralised SPC system highlights ongoing efforts to streamline post-grant protections. Applicants should leverage public consultations to influence examination practices, particularly in complex biotechnology fields like antibodies, ensuring guidelines reflect practical prosecution realities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["诺华强调专利审查时效性对新制剂及医疗用途投资决策具有关键影响", "诺华支持建立集中式补充保护证书制度，并由统一专利法院处理相关无效及上诉", "双方讨论了抗体相关专利申请审查指南修订，EPO提示年度公众咨询机会"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Novartis identified timely patent examination as crucial for investment planning in pharmaceuticals.", "The company supports a centralised SPC system with UPC handling for invalidity and appeals.", "Stakeholders discussed antibody examination guidelines and the EPO’s annual public consultation process."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["审查时效已成为药企研发投资规划的关键考量因素，需密切关注EPO流程效率变化", "行业头部企业对统一专利法院管辖下的集中式SPC制度表现出明确支持倾向", "需持续跟踪EPO在抗体等领域审查指南的年度修订及公众咨询反馈机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Examination speed directly impacts commercial planning for new formulations and medical uses.", "Practice note: Engage with the EPO’s annual public consultation to shape upcoming examination guidelines.", "What to monitor: Developments in the proposed centralised SPC system and its interaction with the UPC."]
core_points_en: Novartis identified timely patent examination as crucial for investment planning in pharmaceuticals.
The company supports a centralised SPC system with UPC handling for invalidity and appeals.
Stakeholders discussed antibody examination guidelines and the EPO’s annual public consultation process.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Examination speed directly impacts commercial planning for new formulations and medical uses.
Practice note: Engage with the EPO’s annual public consultation to shape upcoming examination guidelines.
What to monitor: Developments in the proposed centralised SPC system and its interaction with the UPC.

items.title: Sisvel and Quectel go public with IoT patent licensing collaboration
items.title_zh: Sisvel与Quectel公开物联网专利许可合作
items.title_en: Quectel and Sisvel Launch Collaboration to Streamline IoT Patent Licensing Access
items.summary: World’s largest module maker to offer “streamlined” access to pool licence to its IoT customers
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球最大物联网模块制造商Quectel与专利池运营商Sisvel达成合作。Quectel将向其物联网客户提供对Sisvel专利池许可的“简化”访问渠道。此举旨在优化终端用户的专利合规流程，通过模块商层面整合许可资源，降低下游客户的授权复杂度。
items.ai_summary_en: Quectel, recognized as the world’s largest Internet of Things module manufacturer, has announced a strategic collaboration with Sisvel, a prominent patent pool administrator. This partnership aims to provide Quectel’s IoT customers with streamlined and simplified access to essential patent licenses managed by Sisvel. The initiative marks a significant shift in the supply chain, where upstream manufacturing giants are actively integrating standard-essential patent licensing resources. By facilitating this commercial cooperation, the parties seek to reduce compliance costs and litigation risks for downstream clients. This development is poised to reshape the patent licensing ecosystem within the IoT sector, offering a more efficient pathway for hardware manufacturers and device makers to secure necessary intellectual property rights without navigating complex individual negotiations.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note：模块商介入SEP许可分发成为新趋势，可能改变传统终端厂商直接谈判模式。需关注该“简化访问”的具体法律架构、覆盖专利范围及是否包含FRAND费率传导机制，以评估其对供应链责任分配的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor this emerging 'manufacturing plus licensing' model, as it may significantly influence existing FRAND negotiation strategies. Companies ought to evaluate how such integrated channels affect their current licensing frameworks and cost structures. It is advisable to proactively map product portfolios against relevant patent pools to leverage the collective bargaining power offered by leading manufacturers. However, stakeholders must also remain vigilant regarding potential legal risks associated with bundled licensing arrangements. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for maintaining compliance while optimizing intellectual property expenditure in the evolving IoT landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Quectel作为全球最大模块商，与Sisvel建立公开合作关系", "Quectel将向客户提供对Sisvel专利池许可的简化访问权限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Quectel drives simplification of patent pool license access processes for its extensive global customer base.", "Sisvel collaborates to optimize efficiency for IoT clients seeking standard-essential patent licenses through unified channels.", "The agreement reflects a proactive role shift for hardware manufacturers within the broader intellectual property licensing value chain."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["观察模块商在SEP许可链条中的角色转变及其对下游客户的影响", "监测Sisvel专利池在此合作模式下的许可条款透明度与覆盖范围", "关注此类合作是否引发新的反垄断或竞争法层面的监管审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess the impact of manufacturer-led licensing models on traditional FRAND negotiation dynamics and market positioning.", "Practice note: Leverage scalable licensing channels provided by major hardware vendors to reduce overall permissioning costs effectively.", "What to monitor: Watch for potential legal challenges or regulatory scrutiny concerning bundled licensing practices and anti-competitive concerns."]
core_points_en: Quectel drives simplification of patent pool license access processes for its extensive global customer base.
Sisvel collaborates to optimize efficiency for IoT clients seeking standard-essential patent licenses through unified channels.
The agreement reflects a proactive role shift for hardware manufacturers within the broader intellectual property licensing value chain.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess the impact of manufacturer-led licensing models on traditional FRAND negotiation dynamics and market positioning.
Practice note: Leverage scalable licensing channels provided by major hardware vendors to reduce overall permissioning costs effectively.
What to monitor: Watch for potential legal challenges or regulatory scrutiny concerning bundled licensing practices and anti-competitive concerns.

items.title: KPN takes Oppo dispute to India, expands UPC campaign
items.title_zh: KPN将OPPO争议诉至印度并扩大UPC行动
items.title_en: KPN Expands Global SEP Campaign Against Oppo with First WebRTC Case in India
items.summary: A WebRTC case – the first in India – is the latest action in a protracted spat between the Dutch telecoms company and India’s second-largest smartphone vendor
items.ai_summary_zh: 荷兰电信公司KPN与印度智能手机厂商OPPO之间的长期纠纷出现新动态。KPN在印度提起WebRTC案件，这是该国首例此类诉讼。同时，标题显示KPN正在统一专利法院（UPC）扩大其针对OPPO的诉讼行动。
items.ai_summary_en: The long-standing patent dispute between Dutch telecom operator KPN and Indian smartphone manufacturer Oppo has escalated significantly. Following initial litigation at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) in Europe, KPN has now initiated its first patent infringement action in India based on WebRTC technology. This strategic move extends the conflict from European jurisdictions into key Asian markets, demonstrating a coordinated multi-jurisdictional enforcement strategy. By leveraging a global network of lawsuits, the rights holder aims to increase pressure on the implementer to accept licensing terms. The case highlights the growing trend of SEP holders utilizing parallel proceedings across different regions to strengthen their negotiating position regarding standard essential patents and global royalty rates.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注KPN在UPC与印度法院的双线诉讼策略对全球SEP许可谈判的影响。观察UPC案件的具体指控范围及印度首例WebRTC案的审理走向，以评估跨法域诉讼协调趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the increasing complexity of managing SEP disputes across multiple legal systems. Rights holders are actively employing parallel litigation strategies to maximize leverage during negotiations. For market participants, particularly those operating globally, this signals the necessity of preparing for simultaneous enforcement actions in diverse jurisdictions. Companies must evaluate their exposure not just in traditional strongholds like Europe but also in emerging markets where procedural rules may differ. Effective IP management now requires a holistic view of global litigation risks and the potential for cross-border injunctions or damages claims that can impact overall business operations and supply chain stability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["KPN在印度提起WebRTC案件，系该国首例此类诉讼", "标题显示KPN正在统一专利法院（UPC）扩大对OPPO的行动", "此案是荷兰KPN与印度OPPO之间长期纠纷的最新进展"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注KPN在UPC的诉讼扩张动作及其具体法律主张", "监测印度首例WebRTC案件的程序进展及潜在裁决影响", "观察跨国SEP持有人在欧印两地并行诉讼的策略模式"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Multinational implementers face heightened risk of coordinated global SEP enforcement requiring integrated defense strategies.", "Practice note: Monitor parallel filings in non-traditional jurisdictions to anticipate shifts in negotiation leverage and litigation tactics.", "What to monitor: UPC decisions and Indian court rulings on WebRTC standards to assess impacts on global FRAND rate determinations."]
core_points_en: KPN initiates its first WebRTC-based patent lawsuit in India, establishing a new front in the ongoing dispute with Oppo.
The Indian filing serves as an extension of KPN's broader litigation campaign at the Unified Patent Court, reflecting a coordinated global strategy.
The central focus remains on securing favorable standard essential patent licensing terms and determining appropriate global royalty rates.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Multinational implementers face heightened risk of coordinated global SEP enforcement requiring integrated defense strategies.
Practice note: Monitor parallel filings in non-traditional jurisdictions to anticipate shifts in negotiation leverage and litigation tactics.
What to monitor: UPC decisions and Indian court rulings on WebRTC standards to assess impacts on global FRAND rate determinations.

items.title: Celebrating International Women’s Day
items.title_zh: 庆祝国际妇女节：EPO发布联合声明及最新研究
items.title_en: EPO and WIPO Issue Joint Statement on Women’s Representation in IP Systems
items.summary: Action for equality: joint statement and the latest study
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）在国际妇女节之际，与世界知识产权组织（WIPO）及82个国家局签署联合声明，强调女性在知识产权系统中的代表性不足。EPO观察站发布《推进STEM领域女性发展》研究，指出女性在发明人、创始人及专利专业人员中占比增长缓慢，存在结构性障碍。EPO还将标志改为紫色以示支持，并展示了四位杰出女性发明人的案例。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) joins the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) and eighty-two national offices in a joint statement for International Women’s Day, addressing women’s underrepresentation in intellectual property systems. The signatories affirm equal rights for women to protect inventions and navigate IP processes without barriers. Concurrently, the EPO Observatory published a study titled Advancing Women in STEM, which reveals slow growth in female participation across science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. The report highlights persistent structural barriers for women as inventors and patent professionals while profiling four award-winning female innovators to support evidence-based policy strategies for inclusive innovation within the European and global IP frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This initiative reflects institutional commitment to diversity rather than immediate procedural changes in patent law. For rights holders and in-house counsel, the data underscores the importance of internal diversity metrics in R&D teams. While no new filing rules are introduced, monitoring subsequent EPO policies derived from this study may reveal future support mechanisms or targeted programs aimed at reducing structural barriers for female innovators in European jurisdictions.
