items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO, WIPO, and eighty-two national offices signed a joint statement advocating for equal access to IP systems for women.", "New EPO Observatory study indicates slow growth in female representation among STEM inventors and patent professionals despite incremental gains.", "Report identifies structural barriers and profiles award-winning female inventors to inform diversity and inclusion strategies in innovation sectors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EPO是否基于研究结果推出针对女性发明人的具体援助计划或费用优惠政策", "监测联合声明签署国后续是否在各自国家局层面落实促进女性参与IP的具体行动", "留意EPO观察站未来是否发布更细分的国别或技术领域女性发明人数据分析报告"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Corporate IP teams should align internal diversity goals with broader European policy trends to enhance employer branding and innovation inclusivity.", "Practice note: Current announcements are policy-focused; no immediate changes to patent examination guidelines, fees, or procedural requirements are indicated by this release.", "What to monitor: Watch for follow-up EPO initiatives, such as mentorship programs or data-driven policy adjustments, resulting from the Advancing Women in STEM study findings."]
core_points_en: EPO, WIPO, and eighty-two national offices signed a joint statement advocating for equal access to IP systems for women.
New EPO Observatory study indicates slow growth in female representation among STEM inventors and patent professionals despite incremental gains.
Report identifies structural barriers and profiles award-winning female inventors to inform diversity and inclusion strategies in innovation sectors.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Corporate IP teams should align internal diversity goals with broader European policy trends to enhance employer branding and innovation inclusivity.
Practice note: Current announcements are policy-focused; no immediate changes to patent examination guidelines, fees, or procedural requirements are indicated by this release.
What to monitor: Watch for follow-up EPO initiatives, such as mentorship programs or data-driven policy adjustments, resulting from the Advancing Women in STEM study findings.

items.title: UPC monthly statistics publication
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院恢复发布月度统计数据
items.title_en: Unified Patent Court Resumes Monthly Statistics with Updated Format and New Data Sets
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court resumes the publication of its monthly statistics
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院宣布恢复发布月度统计数据，并推出更新格式以提供更清晰洞察。截至2026年2月28日，一审法院累计受理1130起案件。2026年前两月新立案72起，其中侵权诉讼30起，撤销反诉39起。杜塞尔多夫、慕尼黑和海牙分院案件量居前。英语为最主要诉讼语言，占比62.5%。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court has resumed publishing monthly statistics, introducing an updated format for clearer insights and incorporating new data sets. As of 28 February 2026, the Court of First Instance recorded 1,130 total cases since June 2023. In 2026, 72 new cases were filed, comprising 30 infringement actions and 39 revocation counterclaims. The data highlights continued activity across key divisions, with Düsseldorf, Munich, and The Hague showing significant caseloads. This relaunch aims to provide a more comprehensive overview of the Court’s operational trends and procedural distributions for stakeholders monitoring European patent litigation developments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类数据发布给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The resumption of regular statistical reporting enhances transparency for rights holders and counsel tracking UPC litigation trends. The data confirms the continued centrality of specific divisions, particularly in Germany and the Netherlands, for patent disputes. Practitioners should note the high ratio of revocation counterclaims relative to standalone actions, reflecting common defensive strategies. Monitoring these updated metrics helps inform forum selection and resource allocation for ongoing or prospective European patent enforcement and defense matters.

items.title: Upcoming changes to judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉院及巴黎分院法官组成变更
items.title_en: UPC Announces Judicial Composition Changes for Court of Appeal and Paris Central Division
items.summary: The Court informs on changes to the judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris, effective as of 1 April 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院宣布，自2026年4月1日起调整上诉法院及第一审法院巴黎中央分院的法官组成。因Emanuela Germano法官退休，Paolo Catallozzi法官调任上诉法院，Vincenzo Carnì法官接任巴黎中央分院。该决定由行政委员会于2025年7月作出。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court has announced changes to the judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris, effective 1 April 2026. These adjustments follow the retirement of legally qualified judge Ms Emanuela Germano from the Court of Appeal at the end of March 2026. The Administrative Committee, via decisions dated 2 July 2025, appointed Mr Paolo Catallozzi, previously a judge at the Central Division Paris, to the Court of Appeal. To fill the resulting vacancy, Mr Vincenzo Carnì has been appointed as a legally qualified judge at the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris. The Court presidents acknowledged Ms Germano’s contributions and welcomed the new appointees.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This administrative update confirms the continuity of judicial operations within key UPC divisions. For practitioners, noting the transition of Judge Catallozzi to the appellate level and the introduction of Judge Carnì in Paris is essential for tracking panel compositions in pending or future cases. While specific jurisprudential trends cannot be inferred from appointment notices alone, rights holders should update internal records to reflect these changes in judicial rosters for strategic case assessment.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ms Emanuela Germano retires from the UPC Court of Appeal effective late March 2026.", "Mr Paolo Catallozzi transfers from Paris Central Division to the Court of Appeal on 1 April 2026.", "Mr Vincenzo Carnì is appointed as legally qualified judge at Paris Central Division effective 1 April 2026."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注巴黎中央分院新晋法官Vincenzo Carnì的既往裁判风格或专业背景", "观察上诉法院新成员Paolo Catallozzi加入后对二审案件处理效率的影响", "后续需留意UPC其他分院是否跟随出现类似的人事结构调整动态"]

core_points_en: Ms Emanuela Germano retires from the UPC Court of Appeal effective late March 2026.
Mr Paolo Catallozzi transfers from Paris Central Division to the Court of Appeal on 1 April 2026.
Mr Vincenzo Carnì is appointed as legally qualified judge at Paris Central Division effective 1 April 2026.

Practice note: Verify panel compositions in upcoming hearings to account for the new appointments of Judges Catallozzi and Carnì.
What to monitor: Observe any procedural or stylistic shifts in the Paris Central Division under the new judicial composition over time.

items.title: The enchanted land of IP
items.title_zh: 知识产权的魔法之地：远行树案例解析
items.title_en: IP in the Enchanted Land: Copyright and Trademark Protection for The Faraway Tree
items.summary: A favourite childhood story is being adapted for the screen, and it got me thinking about the intellectual property behind it. From copyright and licensing to trade marks, The Magic Faraway Tree shows how IP helps creative worlds travel from
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局博客以伊妮德·布莱顿作品《远行树》改编电影为例，阐释知识产权在创意产业中的作用。文章指出，尽管该书出版于20世纪30年代，但依据英国法律，版权保护期为作者死后70年，故目前仍受保护。任何影视改编需获得权利人许可。此外，相关标题和角色已注册为商标，由出版商持有，用于保护品牌及周边商品开发。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office blog uses the film adaptation of The Magic Faraway Tree to illustrate intellectual property roles in creative industries. Although the original book was published in the 1930s, it remains under copyright protection because the author died less than seventy years ago. Consequently, UK law requires formal licensing for adaptations, ensuring respect for the original work. Additionally, rights holders have registered trademarks for Faraway Tree titles and characters across publishing, entertainment, and merchandise sectors. This strategy builds a brand moat by preventing unauthorized use. The case demonstrates how copyright, licensing, and trademarks work together to protect creator rights while facilitating commercial exploitation and safeguarding the integrity of cherished literary works in new media formats.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文作为官方科普，强调了经典文学作品改编中的权利清算逻辑。实务中需注意，即使作品年代久远，仍需核实作者死亡时间以确认版权状态。同时，商标权与版权并行保护是IP资产运营的常见策略，改编方需同时处理版权许可与商标合规问题，避免侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders managing literary IP for international markets must verify remaining copyright terms in target jurisdictions to avoid infringement liabilities. Establishing a comprehensive global trademark strategy is equally critical, particularly securing registrations for key IP names in core markets before launch. Combining copyright licensing with robust trademark protection creates a multi-dimensional defense system. This integrated approach safeguards commercial monetization efforts and prevents third-party squatting, ensuring that brand equity and creative assets remain secure during cross-border expansion and multimedia adaptation projects.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["依据英国法律，文学作品版权保护期为作者去世后70年，伊妮德·布莱顿作品尚未进入公有领域", "《远行树》的电影改编必须从版权持有人处获得许可，以确保改编的合法授权及对原作的尊重", "霍德与斯托顿有限公司持有“远行树”等相关注册商标，覆盖出版、娱乐及衍生品等领域"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK copyright law protects works for seventy years after the author's death, meaning The Faraway Tree has not yet entered the public domain.", "Film adaptations require formal authorization from rights holders to ensure legal compliance and proper treatment of the original literary source material.", "Registered trademarks covering character names and brand identifiers prevent infringement and expand commercial value across multiple product categories."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["经典作品改编需严格核算版权保护期，英国标准为作者死后70年，未届满前不得自由使用", "IP改编涉及多重权利类型，除版权许可外，还需排查角色名、书名等是否构成在先商标障碍", "出版商通常通过商标布局强化对系列作品的控制力，被许可方应审查完整的IP权利链条"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify copyright status in target export markets to mitigate infringement risks arising from unexpired protection periods.", "Practice note: Implement a combined copyright and trademark protection strategy, registering key IP names early in core markets to strengthen brand barriers.", "What to monitor: Track trademark filings and copyright expirations in key jurisdictions to proactively defend against unauthorized adaptations or brand squatting."]
core_points_en: UK copyright law protects works for seventy years after the author's death, meaning The Faraway Tree has not yet entered the public domain.
Film adaptations require formal authorization from rights holders to ensure legal compliance and proper treatment of the original literary source material.
Registered trademarks covering character names and brand identifiers prevent infringement and expand commercial value across multiple product categories.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify copyright status in target export markets to mitigate infringement risks arising from unexpired protection periods.
Practice note: Implement a combined copyright and trademark protection strategy, registering key IP names early in core markets to strengthen brand barriers.
What to monitor: Track trademark filings and copyright expirations in key jurisdictions to proactively defend against unauthorized adaptations or brand squatting.

This data release focuses exclusively on statistical trends regarding the presence of female inventors in the UK patent system. It does not address legal changes, litigation outcomes, or specific policy recommendations within the provided summary. The publication serves as an official record of demographic shifts in innovation activity over nearly three decades.

items.title: 1 April 2026: Update to the Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) fees
items.title_zh: 2026年4月1日：专利合作条约(PCT)费用更新
items.title_en: UKIPO Confirms PCT Fee Structure Changes Effective 1 April 2026
items.summary: The Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) fee structure will change on 1 April 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局官方公告显示，专利合作条约（PCT）的费用结构将于2026年4月1日发生变更。原文仅陈述了这一时间节点和事件事实，未提供具体的费率调整数值、增减幅度或受影响的具体费用项目清单。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office has issued an official notice confirming that the fee structure for the Patent Cooperation Treaty will undergo significant adjustments effective 1 April 2026. As a critical international patent application system administered by WIPO, these changes directly impact all entities filing international applications through the UK or other European receiving offices. The update aims to reflect evolving administrative costs within the international phase. Consequently, applicants must proactively plan their budgets to avoid procedural delays or unexpected expenditures caused by the new fee standards. Ensuring accurate financial preparation is essential for a smooth transition from the international phase into the national phase, maintaining the integrity and continuity of patent protection strategies across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants relying on the PCT pathway for European patent portfolios should anticipate increased costs for overseas rights confirmation and enforcement. It is advisable to recalculate intellectual property budgets for post-2026 filings. Strategic timing of applications around this date can optimize capital allocation. Stakeholders must closely monitor specific rate details to ensure efficient fund management and avoid unnecessary financial burdens during the international and national phases of patent prosecution.

items.title: AI-assisted minute-taking extended to Unitary Patent oral proceedings
items.title_zh: EPO将AI辅助笔录扩展至统一专利口头程序
items.title_en: EPO Extends AI-Assisted Minute-Taking to Unitary Patent Oral Proceedings
items.summary: EPO extends AI‑assisted minute‑taking to Unitary Patent proceedings, improving efficiency, accuracy and consistency while ensuring full human oversight and data protection
items.ai_summary_zh: 继2025年试点成功后，欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布将AI辅助笔录功能扩展至统一专利保护部门（UPPD）的口头程序。该工具此前已用于审查、异议等部门。流程包括录音、AI生成转录稿及草稿，最终由人工审核定稿并删除原始录音，以确保数据保护及符合《欧洲专利公约》第124(3)条规定。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office is extending AI-assisted minute-taking to oral proceedings before the Unitary Patent Protection Division, following a successful 2025 pilot. This tool, already utilized in examination and opposition proceedings, aims to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and consistency while maintaining full human oversight. Audio recordings are transcribed via AI, processed into draft minutes, and finalized by the division under Rule 124(3) EPC. To ensure data protection and procedural safeguards, all recordings and intermediate transcripts are deleted once final minutes are issued to the parties. This expansion further integrates AI support into the patent-granting process for Unitary Patents.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此举标志着AI工具在EPO核心确权及统一专利相关程序中的进一步整合。当事人应关注AI生成笔录的准确性对程序记录的影响，以及数据删除机制对后续证据固定的潜在影响。需持续观察该模式在UPPD实践中的具体运行效率及当事人反馈。
items.ai_insight_en: This development signals deeper integration of AI tools within EPO procedural workflows, specifically for Unitary Patent matters. While efficiency gains are noted, rights holders should recognize that human examiners retain final authority over the official record. The automatic deletion of source audio limits post-hoc verification options, making real-time attention during proceedings increasingly critical for accurate factual preservation.

Final minutes remain under full human oversight and responsibility, complying with Rule 124(3) EPC standards.

items.title: EPO and IP Australia PCT pilot programme enters into force starting 1 March 2026
items.title_zh: EPO与澳大利亚知识产权局PCT试点项目于2026年3月1日生效
items.title_en: EPO and IP Australia Launch Two-Year PCT Pilot Programme for Australian Applicants
items.summary: Under a two-year pilot programme between the EPO and IP Australia that started 1 March 2026, Australian applicants can designate the EPO as their International Searching Authority (ISA) under the PCT.
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2026年3月1日起，EPO与澳大利亚知识产权局启动为期两年的PCT试点项目。澳大利亚申请人可将EPO指定为国际检索单位及国际初步审查单位。此举旨在加速获得高质量欧洲专利保护，通过免除补充检索节省时间与成本，并在满足条件下享受欧洲阶段审查费75%的减免。该试点适用于向澳局或WIPO国际局提交申请的情形。
items.ai_summary_en: Effective 1 March 2026, a two-year pilot programme allows Australian applicants to designate the European Patent Office (EPO) as their International Searching Authority (ISA) and International Preliminary Examining Authority (IPEA) under the Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT). This option applies to applications filed with IP Australia or the WIPO International Bureau. By selecting the EPO, applicants can bypass the supplementary search upon entering the European regional phase, reducing procedural time and costs. Furthermore, requesting international preliminary examination with the EPO under PCT Chapter II qualifies applicants for a 75% reduction in European regional phase examination fees. The initiative aims to provide an accelerated route to high-quality European patent protection and supports informed decision-making regarding entry into European and national phases.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该试点为澳大利亚申请人提供了进入欧洲专利体系的快速通道，通过前置高质量检索降低后续不确定性。实务中需关注EPO作为ISA/IPEA的具体操作指引及费用减免的申请流程，评估其对整体专利申请策略的成本效益影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This pilot streamlines the path to European patent protection for Australian filers by leveraging EPO search quality early in the process. The waiver of supplementary searches and significant fee reductions offer tangible efficiency gains for portfolio management. Rights holders should evaluate whether designating the EPO as ISA aligns with their broader global filing strategies and budget constraints during this two-year window.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年3月1日起，澳申请人可指定EPO为PCT国际检索及初步审查单位", "使用EPO检索报告进入欧洲阶段时可免除补充检索，节省时间和成本", "若在EPO进行PCT第二章国际初步审查，欧洲阶段审查费减免75%"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Australian applicants may designate EPO as ISA and IPEA under new two-year PCT pilot starting March 2026.", "Using EPO search reports exempts applicants from supplementary search when entering the European regional phase.", "Requesting EPO preliminary examination yields a seventy-five percent reduction in European regional phase examination fees."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["试点项目为澳申请人提供加速进入欧洲专利体系的程序性便利", "免除补充检索及费用减免直接降低欧洲阶段获权成本", "需持续观察试点期间EPO检索质量对后续审查效率的实际影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Australian entities gain cost savings and faster access to European patent validation through streamlined PCT procedures.", "Practice note: Ensure PCT requests explicitly designate EPO as ISA/IPEA to secure supplementary search waivers and fee reductions.", "What to monitor: Track EPO processing times and search quality outcomes during the pilot to assess long-term strategic value."]
core_points_en: Australian applicants may designate EPO as ISA and IPEA under new two-year PCT pilot starting March 2026.
Using EPO search reports exempts applicants from supplementary search when entering the European regional phase.
Requesting EPO preliminary examination yields a seventy-five percent reduction in European regional phase examination fees.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Australian entities gain cost savings and faster access to European patent validation through streamlined PCT procedures.
Practice note: Ensure PCT requests explicitly designate EPO as ISA/IPEA to secure supplementary search waivers and fee reductions.
What to monitor: Track EPO processing times and search quality outcomes during the pilot to assess long-term strategic value.

items.title: EPO’s next-generation search tool for examiners now used in over 40 national patent offices worldwide
items.title_zh: 欧专局新一代检索工具已在超40国专利局启用
items.title_en: EPO Next-Generation Search Tool Deployed in Over Forty National Patent Offices
items.summary: A new search era has formally commenced: the next-generation tool ANSERA-based SEARCH was developed by the European Patent Office (EPO) in collaboration with national patent offices across the EPO member states.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）开发的基于ANSERA的新一代检索工具SEARCH已在全球40多个国家专利局投入使用，服务于2500多名审查员。该工具取代了旧的EPOQUE Net系统，支持更复杂的现有技术检索、智能排序及语义检索功能，旨在提升专利质量并 harmonize 全球检索实践，符合EPO《2028年战略计划》中关于数字化工具卓越性的承诺。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has deployed its next-generation ANSERA-based SEARCH tool across more than forty national patent offices globally. Serving over 2,500 examiners, this system replaces the legacy EPOQUE Net platform, aligning with the EPO Strategic Plan 2028. The tool facilitates advanced prior art searches across worldwide patent documents and extensive non-patent literature collections. It features intelligent result ranking, semantic-like retrieval, and structured workflow management to handle complex document volumes. This rollout aims to harmonize high-quality search practices internationally, reinforcing the EPO’s commitment to digital excellence and supporting global innovation ecosystems through shared standards and sophisticated examination infrastructure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This deployment signals a convergence of international search capabilities toward EPO standards. Rights holders should anticipate rigorous novelty and inventive step assessments, particularly regarding non-patent literature. Consistent tool usage across jurisdictions may reduce procedural divergence but raises the baseline for prior art disclosure. Applicants must ensure comprehensive pre-filing searches to address enhanced examiner capabilities. Monitoring how national offices integrate these tools into local examination guidelines will be essential for predicting prosecution outcomes and adapting global filing strategies accordingly.

items.title: President’s mission to Hungary
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局局长访问匈牙利
items.title_en: EPO President Visits Hungary to Discuss European Patent Network Modernization
items.summary: President Campinos attended the 130th anniversary of the Hungarian Intellectual Property Office (HIPO) and met with HIPO President Szabolcs Farkas to discuss the future of the European Patent Network.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局局长坎皮诺斯出席匈牙利知识产权局成立130周年庆典，并与该局主席法卡斯会晤。双方讨论了国家和欧洲层面的知识产权优先事项，重点涉及加强欧洲专利网络、通过统一专利体系深化整合、协调实践及共享数字工具，以提升欧洲技术市场的连贯性与韧性。
items.ai_summary_en: EPO President António Campinos attended the 130th anniversary of the Hungarian Intellectual Property Office (HIPO) and met with HIPO President Szabolcs Farkas. Discussions focused on national and European IP priorities, emphasizing the need for high quality and legal certainty amid technological change. The visit supported the EPO’s ongoing consultations for its new Scenarios for the Future study. Key topics included strengthening the European Patent Network through deeper integration via the Unitary Patent system, harmonized practices, and shared digital tools. Both leaders highlighted the importance of modernizing cooperation to enhance capacity-building and joint support for startups and SMEs, aiming to reinforce Europe as a coherent and resilient technology market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This engagement signals the EPO’s continued push for deeper integration within the European Patent Network, particularly leveraging the Unitary Patent system. For rights holders, the emphasis on harmonized practices and shared digital tools suggests potential improvements in procedural consistency and efficiency across member states. Monitoring the outcomes of the Scenarios for the Future study will be essential for understanding long-term strategic shifts in the European innovation ecosystem.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["局长出席匈牙利知识产权局130周年庆典并发表主旨演讲，强调高质量与法律确定性", "双方探讨加强欧洲专利网络，包括通过统一专利体系深化整合以强化欧洲技术市场", "会议聚焦现代化合作，涵盖数字协作、实践趋同及对初创企业和中小企业的支持"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO强调通过统一专利体系深化整合，预示欧洲专利网络协同性将增强", "共享数字工具与实践趋同是未来合作重点，可能提升跨国申请处理效率", "需持续观察EPO“未来情景”研究对创新生态系统及专利制度演变的长期影响"]

items.title: When milk turns sour: Oatly v Dairy UK
items.title_zh: 牛奶变酸：Oatly诉Dairy UK商标无效案终审
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Rules Oatly's 'Post Milk Generation' Trademark Invalid
items.summary: Back in 2021, Oatly, a Swedish manufacturer of oat drinks, obtained a registration for the trade mark POST MILK GENERATION in the UK for goods including milk substitutes, yoghurt substitute, and other food products, all being oat-based.
items.ai_summary_zh: 瑞典公司Oatly在英国注册“POST MILK GENERATION”商标，被Dairy UK以违反保留欧盟法规为由申请无效。该法规规定“牛奶”仅用于动物源产品。历经UKIPO、高等法院、上诉法院审理，英国最高法院最终裁定支持Dairy UK，认定商标中使用“milk”构成法规禁止的称谓，即便非描述性使用也无法规避监管禁令，宣告商标无效。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has upheld the invalidation of Oatly’s ‘POST MILK GENERATION’ trademark, concluding a four-year legal dispute with Dairy UK. The Swedish oat drink manufacturer originally secured registration in 2021 for plant-based food substitutes. Dairy UK challenged the mark, arguing it violated retained EU regulations restricting the term ‘milk’ to animal-derived products. While the High Court initially favored Oatly, the Court of Appeal reversed this decision, finding that the mark infringed regulatory prohibitions regardless of consumer deception. The Supreme Court ultimately agreed with Dairy UK, interpreting the regulation strictly to prohibit using ‘milk’ designations for non-animal products. This final ruling confirms that such trademarks are invalid under current UK law, reinforcing strict labeling standards for dairy alternatives.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了保留欧盟法规对植物基产品命名的严格限制，即使商标具有显著性而非通用描述，若包含受保护术语仍可能因监管合规问题被宣告无效。企业需在品牌创设阶段审查食品标签法规与商标法的交叉风险，避免使用受监管保护的特定行业术语。
items.ai_insight_en: This landmark decision clarifies the scope of protected designations for dairy terms under UK law, impacting all market participants in the plant-based sector. Rights holders must ensure trademark applications avoid prohibited terminology linked to regulated food categories, even if used metaphorically. The ruling emphasizes that regulatory compliance takes precedence over distinctiveness claims when specific statutory bans apply. Companies should conduct rigorous clearance searches focusing on sector-specific labeling laws rather than relying solely on traditional likelihood of confusion assessments. Future branding strategies for alternative proteins must navigate these strict definitional boundaries to avoid costly invalidation proceedings and potential enforcement actions by industry associations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院裁定Oatly商标因含“milk”一词违反保留欧盟法规而无效", "法院认定法规禁止在非动物源产品上使用“牛奶”称谓，无名义上的公平使用空间", "此前高等法院曾判Oatly胜诉，但上诉法院及最高法院均推翻该判决支持乳业协会"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court upheld the invalidation of Oatly's 'POST MILK GENERATION' trademark after a four-year legal battle.", "Retained EU regulations strictly prohibit using 'milk' designations for any product other than those derived from animal secretions.", "The Court rejected arguments that the mark was merely descriptive or that notional fair use could bypass regulatory prohibitions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["商标显著性不能豁免食品命名监管法规的强制性禁止规定", "植物基替代产品在英国市场使用“牛奶”相关词汇面临极高法律风险", "需持续关注英国脱欧后保留欧盟法规在知识产权与食品监管交叉领域的解释趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Plant-based food manufacturers must align trademark strategies with strict dairy labeling regulations to avoid invalidation risks.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive clearance searches that include regulatory compliance checks for restricted food terminology beyond standard conflicts.", "What to monitor: Potential legislative updates or further judicial interpretations regarding the definition of 'designation' in food labeling laws."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court upheld the invalidation of Oatly's 'POST MILK GENERATION' trademark after a four-year legal battle.
Retained EU regulations strictly prohibit using 'milk' designations for any product other than those derived from animal secretions.
The Court rejected arguments that the mark was merely descriptive or that notional fair use could bypass regulatory prohibitions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Plant-based food manufacturers must align trademark strategies with strict dairy labeling regulations to avoid invalidation risks.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive clearance searches that include regulatory compliance checks for restricted food terminology beyond standard conflicts.
What to monitor: Potential legislative updates or further judicial interpretations regarding the definition of 'designation' in food labeling laws.

items.title: Copyright and Champagne labels: French court confirms rejection of the accessory theory and tacit assignment
items.title_zh: 法国法院确认拒绝附属理论及默示转让抗辩
items.title_en: French Court Rejects Accessory Theory and Tacit Assignment in Champagne Label Copyright Dispute
items.summary: France's Douai Court of Appeal has confirmed that a work reproduced on Champagne bottles without authorisation constitutes copyright infringement.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国杜埃上诉法院于2025年11月20日裁定，香槟生产商未经授权在标签及包装上复制艺术作品构成版权侵权。法院重申，附属理论或默示转让均不能保护无明确合同而利用受版权保护作品的行为，维持了里尔法院有利于艺术家的原判，强调了对作者专有权利的严格解释。
items.ai_summary_en: The Douai Court of Appeal has upheld a ruling that a Champagne producer infringed copyright by reproducing an artist's work on bottle labels, packaging, and digital media without authorization. The court explicitly rejected the 'accessory theory' and the defense of tacit assignment of economic rights. Although the Champagne house purchased the physical sculpture for €4,000, the transfer of material ownership did not convey any intellectual property rights. The decision reinforces the strict interpretation of authors' exclusive exploitation rights under French law, confirming that absent a clear contractual agreement, economic rights remain with the creator regardless of the commercial context or the incidental nature of the use.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案重申法国版权法下作者权利的严格保护立场。企业需注意，购买物理艺术品并不自动获得将其用于商业包装的版权许可。附属理论作为抗辩理由在主要商业使用场景中难以成立，缺乏书面授权将直接导致侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants acquiring physical artworks or designs must secure separate, written copyright licenses to avoid infringement liabilities. Relying on the accessory theory or assuming tacit permissions from physical purchases carries significant legal risk in France. Rights holders should conduct thorough due diligence to verify the chain of title and ensure all necessary economic rights are explicitly assigned before commercial exploitation. This case underscores the critical distinction between owning a physical object and holding the underlying intellectual property rights.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["杜埃上诉法院确认未经授权在香槟瓶上复制作品构成版权侵权", "法院拒绝适用附属理论，认定该作品并非次要或附带出现", "法院否认存在经济权利的默示转让，强调需有明确合同约定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court rejected the accessory theory, finding the artwork was central to the branding rather than secondary or incidental.", "Physical purchase of an artwork does not imply tacit assignment of economic rights; explicit contracts are mandatory.", "Authors retain exclusive exploitation rights unless clearly transferred via written agreement, ensuring strict legal protection."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["购买实物艺术品不等于获得版权商业化使用权，需单独签署许可协议", "附属理论抗辩在作品作为主要营销元素时通常不被法院采纳", "默示转让在法国司法实践中极难被认定，书面合同不可或缺"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Acquiring physical art does not grant usage rights; separate written copyright licenses are essential for commercial exploitation.", "Practice note: Avoid relying on the accessory theory or tacit assignment defenses in France, as courts strictly require explicit contractual transfers.", "What to monitor: Ensure comprehensive due diligence on the chain of title for all artistic works used in branding, packaging, and digital media."]
core_points_en: The court rejected the accessory theory, finding the artwork was central to the branding rather than secondary or incidental.
Physical purchase of an artwork does not imply tacit assignment of economic rights; explicit contracts are mandatory.
Authors retain exclusive exploitation rights unless clearly transferred via written agreement, ensuring strict legal protection.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Acquiring physical art does not grant usage rights; separate written copyright licenses are essential for commercial exploitation.
Practice note: Avoid relying on the accessory theory or tacit assignment defenses in France, as courts strictly require explicit contractual transfers.
What to monitor: Ensure comprehensive due diligence on the chain of title for all artistic works used in branding, packaging, and digital media.

items.title: Avanci unveils Wi-Fi 6 Vehicle programme with Mercedes as first licensee
items.title_zh: Avanci 推出 Wi-Fi 6 车载计划，奔驰成首家被许可人
items.title_en: Avanci Launches Wi-Fi 6 Vehicle Licensing Platform with Mercedes-Benz as First Licensee
items.summary: New platform has “significantly lower” rates than cellular SEP offering, says programme lead Qudus Olaniran
items.ai_summary_zh: 专利池运营商 Avanci 正式推出面向汽车行业的 Wi-Fi 6 标准必要专利许可计划，德国车企梅赛德斯 - 奔驰成为首个签约被许可人。项目负责人强调，该新平台的许可费率显著低于其现有的蜂窝通信 SEP 许可方案，旨在为车联网领域提供更适配的知识产权授权框架。
items.ai_summary_en: Patent pool administrator Avanci has officially introduced a new licensing program for Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents tailored specifically for the automotive industry. German automaker Mercedes-Benz has become the first licensee to sign an agreement under this new framework. Qudus Olaniran, the program lead, emphasized that the royalty rates for this new platform are significantly lower than those of Avanci’s existing cellular SEP licensing offerings. This initiative aims to provide a more suitable intellectual property authorization framework for connected vehicle technologies. The launch marks a strategic expansion of Avanci’s portfolio beyond traditional cellular connectivity, addressing the growing integration of Wi-Fi standards in modern vehicles and offering market participants a distinct, cost-effective licensing pathway for non-cellular wireless technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及零部件出口商需关注 Wi-Fi 6 许可成本变化，评估加入新计划对整体合规成本的影响。建议提前梳理自身专利储备，利用费率差异优化谈判策略，避免重复付费。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving cost structures associated with Wi-Fi 6 licensing compared to established cellular SEP regimes. Understanding the rate differential is critical for optimizing overall compliance budgets and negotiating strategies. Companies must evaluate whether joining the new program offers tangible financial benefits or if it introduces unnecessary complexity. Furthermore, rights holders and implementers need to assess the risk of double charging as vehicle connectivity technologies converge. Proactive analysis of patent portfolios against both cellular and Wi-Fi standards will help ensure efficient licensing outcomes and prevent redundant payments in the connected car ecosystem.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Avanci 发布专为汽车行业设计的 Wi-Fi 6 专利许可新平台", "梅赛德斯 - 奔驰作为首家车企签署该 Wi-Fi 6 许可协议", "新计划费率据称显著低于现有蜂窝网络 SEP 许可收费标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Avanci has released a dedicated Wi-Fi 6 patent licensing platform designed specifically for the automotive sector.", "Mercedes-Benz is the first automotive manufacturer to execute a license agreement under the new Wi-Fi 6 program.", "Program leadership states that the new platform's royalty rates are significantly lower than existing cellular SEP fees."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注 Wi-Fi 6 与蜂窝 SEP 费率差异，优化整体许可成本结构", "评估新计划适用性，防止在车联网技术演进中面临双重收费"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Analyze Wi-Fi 6 versus cellular SEP rate differentials to optimize total licensing cost structures.", "Practice note: Evaluate the applicability of the new program to prevent potential double charging in connected vehicle tech.", "What to monitor: Changes in Wi-Fi 6 licensing costs and their impact on overall compliance expenses for manufacturers."]
core_points_en: Avanci has released a dedicated Wi-Fi 6 patent licensing platform designed specifically for the automotive sector.
Mercedes-Benz is the first automotive manufacturer to execute a license agreement under the new Wi-Fi 6 program.
Program leadership states that the new platform's royalty rates are significantly lower than existing cellular SEP fees.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Analyze Wi-Fi 6 versus cellular SEP rate differentials to optimize total licensing cost structures.
Practice note: Evaluate the applicability of the new program to prevent potential double charging in connected vehicle tech.
What to monitor: Changes in Wi-Fi 6 licensing costs and their impact on overall compliance expenses for manufacturers.

items.title: BYD sued at UPC over former LG Energy Solution battery patents
items.title_zh: 比亚迪在统一专利法院因电池专利被诉
items.title_en: BYD Sued at UPC Over Former LG Energy Solution Battery Patents
items.summary: BMS Innovations lawsuit marks first time BYD has been sued at UPC post-Avanci licence
items.ai_summary_zh: BMS Innovations 在统一专利法院起诉比亚迪，指控其侵犯原属 LG 能源解决方案的电池专利。此案标志比亚迪在获得 Avanci 许可后，首次成为统一专利法院的被告，凸显电动汽车电池领域知识产权争端在欧洲司法层面的升级趋势。
items.ai_summary_en: BMS Innovations has initiated litigation against BYD at the Unified Patent Court (UPC), alleging infringement of battery patents previously owned by LG Energy Solution. This action represents a significant development in European intellectual property enforcement, marking the first time BYD has been named as a defendant in UPC proceedings since securing its Avanci licensing agreement. The case underscores an escalating trend in patent disputes within the electric vehicle battery sector, highlighting that existing pool licenses do not necessarily immunize manufacturers from targeted enforcement actions involving specific patent portfolios outside those agreements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企即便获取部分许可，仍可能面临特定专利组合的单独诉讼。建议企业深化专利尽职调查，评估统一专利法院管辖风险，并制定针对性的无效或抗辩策略以应对潜在纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Securing a license through a major patent pool like Avanci does not grant immunity from separate infringement claims involving non-pool patents. Rights holders and manufacturers must recognize that individual entities may still pursue targeted litigation for specific technology assets excluded from broader licensing frameworks. Consequently, companies should conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses that extend beyond standard pool coverage. It is critical to assess potential exposure under the UPC’s jurisdiction early, preparing robust invalidity arguments and defensive strategies to address these distinct, high-stakes enforcement actions effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["BMS Innovations 就前 LG 电池专利在 UPC 起诉比亚迪", "这是比亚迪获 Avanci 许可后首次在 UPC 遭遇诉讼挑战", "案件反映电动汽车电池技术领域的专利博弈日益激烈"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["BMS Innovations filed a lawsuit at the UPC against BYD regarding former LG Energy Solution battery patents.", "This marks BYD's first appearance as a defendant at the UPC following its recent Avanci licensing agreement.", "The dispute reflects intensifying patent competition and legal challenges within the European EV battery market."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["获许可不代表免疫，需警惕非池内专利的单独诉讼风险", "应针对统一专利法院特性，提前布局证据与法律应对方案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pool licenses do not cover all relevant IP; standalone suits remain a material risk for manufacturers.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive due diligence on non-pool patents and prepare UPC-specific defense strategies.", "What to monitor: Enforcement trends by non-practicing entities or competitors targeting specific battery technologies."]
core_points_en: BMS Innovations filed a lawsuit at the UPC against BYD regarding former LG Energy Solution battery patents.
This marks BYD's first appearance as a defendant at the UPC following its recent Avanci licensing agreement.
The dispute reflects intensifying patent competition and legal challenges within the European EV battery market.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Pool licenses do not cover all relevant IP; standalone suits remain a material risk for manufacturers.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive due diligence on non-pool patents and prepare UPC-specific defense strategies.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends by non-practicing entities or competitors targeting specific battery technologies.

items.title: BREAKING: Imminent new referral to the EBA on the relevance of G1/24 to added matter
items.title_zh: T 873/24案就G1/24对新增事项影响向EBA提交新转介
items.title_en: Breaking: Board Refers Questions to EBA on G1/24 Impact on Added Matter Assessment
items.summary: The Board of Appeal in T 873/24 has decided to refer questions to the Enlarged Board of Appeal (EBA) on the application of G1/24 (claim interpretation) to the assessment of added matter. According to the minutes of oral proceedings, the Board of Appeal concluded that the case hinged on whether the order from G1/24 that the description and drawings should always be consulted for claim
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局上诉委员会在T 873/24案中决定向扩大上诉委员会（EBA）提交问题，询问G1/24号决定（关于权利要求解释）如何适用于新增事项（added matter）的评估。庭审记录显示，该案核心在于是否应遵循G1/24的命令，即在解释权利要求时始终参考说明书和附图。
items.ai_summary_en: The Technical Board of Appeal in case T 873/24 has decided to refer questions to the Enlarged Board of Appeal regarding the application of decision G1/24 to the assessment of added matter. The central legal issue concerns whether the principle established in G1/24, which mandates that claims must always be interpreted in light of the description and drawings, applies with equal force when determining if amendments introduce subject matter extending beyond the content of the original application. The Board concluded that the outcome of the case hinges on clarifying this specific scope of application. This referral seeks authoritative guidance on reconciling claim interpretation standards with the strict prohibition against adding new matter during prosecution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需密切关注EBA对此转介的受理及后续意见，这将澄清权利要求解释规则与新增事项审查标准的交互关系，对欧洲专利申请及异议程序的实务操作具有潜在重大影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This development signals a potential shift in how European patent applications are examined for compliance with added matter provisions. Rights holders should anticipate stricter scrutiny of amendments, particularly where claim language relies heavily on descriptive support. It is prudent to review current drafting strategies to ensure that specifications provide robust support for various potential claim modifications. Proactively strengthening the descriptive basis can mitigate risks associated with future rejections based on extended subject matter, ensuring greater resilience during examination and opposition phases.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉委员会在T 873/24案中决定向EBA提交关于G1/24适用性的法律问题", "争议焦点在于G1/24要求的“始终参考说明书和附图”是否影响新增事项评估"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Board determined that the case outcome depends entirely on defining the precise scope and applicability of the G1/24 interpretation principles.", "A formal referral has been submitted to the Enlarged Board of Appeal to resolve critical legal uncertainties concerning the assessment of added matter.", "The proceedings explicitly address the necessary connection between the description, drawings, and claims during both interpretation and amendment review."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EBA是否受理该转介及最终法律意见的发布节奏", "观察G1/24确立的解释原则在新增事项审查中的具体适用边界", "监测该法律不确定性对正在进行的欧洲专利上诉案件策略的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen specification support for claims to prevent rejection based on added matter issues during examination.", "Practice note: Monitor the final Enlarged Board of Appeal ruling closely to adjust European patent application drafting strategies accordingly.", "What to monitor: Track procedural updates in T 873/24 and subsequent EBA decisions impacting claim interpretation and amendment limits."]
core_points_en: The Board determined that the case outcome depends entirely on defining the precise scope and applicability of the G1/24 interpretation principles.
A formal referral has been submitted to the Enlarged Board of Appeal to resolve critical legal uncertainties concerning the assessment of added matter.
The proceedings explicitly address the necessary connection between the description, drawings, and claims during both interpretation and amendment review.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen specification support for claims to prevent rejection based on added matter issues during examination.
Practice note: Monitor the final Enlarged Board of Appeal ruling closely to adjust European patent application drafting strategies accordingly.
What to monitor: Track procedural updates in T 873/24 and subsequent EBA decisions impacting claim interpretation and amendment limits.

items.title: Injunction need not list products, but 40 auxiliary requests trigger tabular clarity requirements
items.title_zh: 禁令无需列明产品，但40项辅助请求触发表格化清晰度要求
items.title_en: UPC Ruling: Injunctions Need Not List Products, but Excessive Auxiliary Requests Require Tabular Clarity
items.summary: GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals v Moderna – The Hague Local Division, 18 February 2026, UPC_CFI_616/2025 and UPC_CFI_1439/2025
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院海牙地方分庭在GSK诉Moderna案中发布程序令。法院批准GSK依据规则263将新疫苗纳入侵权产品定义，重申禁令无需限定具体产品名称。针对GSK提交的40项条件性辅助请求，法院认为数量过多影响审理速度，未直接驳回，而是责令其在一周内提交详细表格，明确各请求的特征组合及对应的无效抗辩，以满足规则30.1的清晰度要求。
items.ai_summary_en: In GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals v Moderna, the UPC Hague Local Division issued a procedural order addressing key litigation strategies. First, the Court confirmed that injunction requests need not be limited to specific product names; relief covering variants and versions is sufficient. Consequently, adding Moderna’s updated vaccine mNEXSPIKE was not strictly necessary for scope, though permitted under Rule 263 RoP due to the recent EMA opinion. Second, regarding GSK’s forty conditional auxiliary requests filed under Rule 30.1 RoP, the Court found the volume excessive and potentially hindering speedy proceedings. It ordered GSK to submit a detailed table within one week, clearly mapping each request’s feature combinations to corresponding invalidity attacks. Failure to comply would restrict future submissions of combined features, ensuring procedural efficiency and clarity in complex patent disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC诉讼中禁令范围的宽泛解释倾向，同时强化了对大量辅助请求的程序管理。当事人需注意，虽无硬性数量上限，但须通过结构化表格确保请求清晰，否则面临被限制或驳回风险。这要求 litigants 在提交复杂权利要求修改时，必须提前优化呈现形式以符合“快速审理”原则。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties before the UPC should prioritize procedural efficiency when managing claim amendments and auxiliary requests. While broad injunction language effectively covers future product iterations, submitting numerous conditional claims requires rigorous organization. Courts may enforce tabular formats to ensure clarity and prevent delays. Litigants must balance comprehensive protection with judicial expectations for concise, convergent argumentation. Strategic drafting of statements of claim should anticipate potential product variations without relying on exhaustive lists, while maintaining readiness to present complex technical distinctions in a structured, court-friendly manner to avoid adverse procedural orders.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院批准将新疫苗纳入侵权定义，重申禁令救济无需绑定封闭的具体产品名单", "四十项辅助请求被指过量，法院责令提交表格以明确特征组合与无效抗辩对应关系", "法院认定在EMA积极意见出台前，原告无法合理预见并主张该欧洲相关产品上市"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Injunction requests do not require a closed list of specific product names, as coverage extends to relevant variants and versions.", "Forty auxiliary requests were deemed excessive, prompting the Court to mandate a structured table clarifying feature combinations.", "Unapproved additional feature combinations face strict procedural limits, preventing arbitrary submission without prior leave."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["禁令范围不必局限于具体产品名称，涵盖变体即可，降低了列举所有侵权版本的负担", "提交大量辅助请求时，必须提供清晰的映射表格，否则可能因违反程序效率被法院干预", "需持续观察UPC各地分庭对规则30.1下“合理性”判断的具体尺度及后续上诉确认情况"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Broad injunction wording allows flexible enforcement against new product variants without constant amendment applications.", "Practice note: Convert complex auxiliary requests into clear tables linking features to invalidity grounds to satisfy clarity requirements.", "What to monitor: Judicial tolerance for the number of auxiliary requests and strict adherence to deadlines for structured submissions."]
core_points_en: Injunction requests do not require a closed list of specific product names, as coverage extends to relevant variants and versions.
Forty auxiliary requests were deemed excessive, prompting the Court to mandate a structured table clarifying feature combinations.
Unapproved additional feature combinations face strict procedural limits, preventing arbitrary submission without prior leave.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Broad injunction wording allows flexible enforcement against new product variants without constant amendment applications.
Practice note: Convert complex auxiliary requests into clear tables linking features to invalidity grounds to satisfy clarity requirements.
What to monitor: Judicial tolerance for the number of auxiliary requests and strict adherence to deadlines for structured submissions.

items.title: R.262A applications required to maintain confidentiality in UPC Proceedings | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC诉讼中维持机密性须依R.262A条正式申请
items.title_en: R.262A applications required to maintain confidentiality in UPC Proceedings | EIP
items.summary: The Court of Appeal clarified the necessity of formal applications to maintain confidentiality in Unified Patent Court (UPC) proceedings when disclosing ordered information. This ruling arose from a dispute involving patent infringement and confidentiality claims between EOFlow and Insulet.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在EOFlow与Insulet专利纠纷中裁定，当事人若希望保持被命令披露信息的机密性，必须依据程序规则第262A条提交正式申请。因EOFlow未在规定的14天内提出申请，仅标记“高度机密”无效，导致相关信息失去机密保护，对方可自由使用。该案源于米兰中央分庭对违反临时禁令的处罚上诉。
items.ai_insight_zh: 此裁决明确了UPC程序中保密义务的严格形式要求，强调程序合规对保护商业秘密的关键作用。实务中需密切关注法院对逾期申请的裁量标准，以及该规则对跨境证据披露策略的影响，确保在强制披露环节及时履行程序义务以避免权利减损。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭裁定，维持披露信息机密性必须依程序规则第262A条提交正式申请，不存在隐含…", "当事人需在披露后14天内提出保密申请，EOFlow因逾期未申请且仅系统标记，导致信息丧…", "本案背景为Insulet获初步禁令后，要求EOFlow披露侵权设备来源及第三方身份引发…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC程序下保密保护非自动生效，必须主动依规则第262A条提起正式申请", "天申请窗口期为硬性约束，逾期将导致已披露商业信息彻底失去机密保护", "需观察法院对第三方访问受限信息的后续裁定，以完善证据披露风险管理"]

items.title: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
items.title_zh: EUIPO支持欧洲打击假冒药品重大行动
items.title_en: EUIPO Supports Europol-Led Operation SHIELD VI Against Counterfeit Medicines
items.summary: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO支持由Europol主导的“SHIELD VI”行动，30个欧洲国家参与，查获价值3200万欧元的假冒药品及非法兴奋剂。行动导致近500人被捕，关闭66个销售网站。EUIPO提供专业知识分析在线侵权趋势，并与EMA等机构联合发起宣传活动，警示从未经核实来源购买药品的风险，保护消费者及合法企业权益。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) supported Operation SHIELD VI, a Europe-wide enforcement action led by Europol between April and November 2025. Authorities from 30 countries seized EUR 32 million in counterfeit medicines and illicit doping substances, including Semaglutide-based products. The operation resulted in nearly 500 arrests, over 3,300 prosecutions, and the shutdown of 66 websites selling fake pharmaceuticals. EUIPO contributed expertise in analyzing online infringement trends. Concurrently, EUIPO, the European Medicines Agency, and Europol launched the "Stay on top – EU vs Fake Medicines" campaign to educate consumers on identifying authorized sellers and avoiding unverified online sources.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This operation highlights the increasing convergence of IP enforcement and public health protection within the EU. For rights holders in the pharmaceutical sector, it underscores the critical role of data sharing with agencies like EUIPO and Europol. The focus on online platforms and specific high-demand substances, such as Semaglutide, indicates that brand owners should prioritize monitoring digital marketplaces for lookalike products. Collaborative initiatives like the "Stay on top" campaign offer strategic opportunities for companies to align with regulatory bodies, enhancing consumer trust while mitigating reputational risks associated with counterfeit goods.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["SHIELD VI行动查获3200万欧元假冒药品，涉及30国，近500人被捕", "EUIPO提供专业知识支持，分析假冒药品相关的在线知识产权侵权趋势", "EUIPO联合EMA等发起宣传活动，提高公众对网购假药风险的认知"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Operation SHIELD VI seized EUR 32 million in fake medicines across thirty European countries with EUIPO support.", "Enforcement actions led to nearly five hundred arrests and the closure of sixty-six illicit online sales websites.", "EUIPO and EMA launched a joint consumer awareness campaign to combat risks from unverified online medicine purchases."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["假冒药品在线销售趋势上升，需加强数字平台监控与侵权数据分析", "跨机构合作（EUIPO/Europol/EMA）成为打击IP犯罪与保护公共健康的主流模式", "消费者教育活动常态化，品牌方应积极参与官方认证的渠道推广与风险提示"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pharmaceutical rights holders should leverage multi-agency collaborations to protect brand integrity and address public health risks posed by counterfeits.", "Practice note: EUIPO’s involvement demonstrates the value of providing infringement trend analysis to support coordinated cross-border enforcement actions.", "What to monitor: Rising online sales of counterfeit weight-loss and opioid products, and evolving guidance on authorized digital distribution channels."]
core_points_en: Operation SHIELD VI seized EUR 32 million in fake medicines across thirty European countries with EUIPO support.
Enforcement actions led to nearly five hundred arrests and the closure of sixty-six illicit online sales websites.
EUIPO and EMA launched a joint consumer awareness campaign to combat risks from unverified online medicine purchases.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Pharmaceutical rights holders should leverage multi-agency collaborations to protect brand integrity and address public health risks posed by counterfeits.
Practice note: EUIPO’s involvement demonstrates the value of providing infringement trend analysis to support coordinated cross-border enforcement actions.
What to monitor: Rising online sales of counterfeit weight-loss and opioid products, and evolving guidance on authorized digital distribution channels.

items.title: Long arm not available for amended patent | EIP
items.title_zh: 修正专利无法适用长臂管辖原则
items.title_en: UPC Denies Swiss Injunction for Amended Unitary Patent Due to Validity Risks
items.summary: IMC Créations is a French company specialising in anti-theft systems for vehicles, particularly commercial vehicles. Among other things, it sells locks for the side and rear doors of commercial vehicles. Mul-T-Lock belongs to the Assa Abloy group and specialises in high security locking and access control systems, in particular pick-resistant keys and locks. IMC alleged that Mul-T-Lock’s MPV 1000 padlock infringes its unitary patent EP4153830 and the corresponding Swiss national validation.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎地方分院裁定，对于在诉讼中无条件修正的统一专利，法院无权就瑞士国家部分颁发禁令。因修正暗示原授权版本存在缺陷，导致瑞士部分面临无效风险，依据CJEU判例，法院须拒绝评估存在严重撤销风险的域外国家专利部分。
items.ai_summary_en: IMC Créations sued Mul-T-Lock for infringing its unitary patent and corresponding Swiss national validation. During proceedings, IMC unconditionally amended the patent claims to limit protection for unitary effect purposes. The UPC Paris Local Division ruled that it could not grant an injunction covering Switzerland, a non-UPC state. The court reasoned that the unilateral amendment implicitly acknowledged defects in the originally granted version, creating a serious risk of invalidity for the Swiss part, which was not similarly amended. Citing CJEU precedent, the court held it lacks jurisdiction to rule on the validity of national patents outside UPC territory when such validity is seriously challenged. Consequently, the court refused to exercise long-arm jurisdiction over the Swiss national part due to the linked validity concerns arising from the claim amendments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC在处理涉及非成员国专利时的审慎立场：若统一专利的修正暗示原权利要求不稳定，法院将避免对未同步修正的域外国家部分作出裁决。权利人需注意跨国诉讼中专利修正策略对域外执行力的潜在削弱影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders pursuing cross-border enforcement involving both UPC member states and non-UPC jurisdictions like Switzerland or the UK must carefully coordinate claim strategies. Unilaterally narrowing claims to secure validity within the UPC system may inadvertently undermine the stability and enforceability of corresponding national validations. Courts may view such amendments as admissions of prior invalidity, potentially barring injunctive relief in non-UPC countries where parallel validity challenges arise. Strategic planning should assess how procedural choices in one forum impact substantive rights and remedies across all relevant territories.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC裁定无权对非成员国瑞士的专利国家部分行使有效性管辖权", "原告在UPC程序中无条件修正专利，被视为承认原授权版本存在缺陷", "因修正导致瑞士部分存在严重无效风险，法院拒绝颁发涵盖瑞士的禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC lacks jurisdiction to adjudicate the validity of national patent parts granted by non-UPC member states.", "Unilateral amendment of a unitary patent implies defects in the original grant, risking invalidity for non-amended national parts.", "The UPC must refuse jurisdiction over non-member state patent parts when a serious risk of revocation exists."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC对非成员国专利部分的管辖权受限于该国专利的有效性风险", "在UPC进行的专利修正可能被解读为对原专利稳定性的自认", "需观察后续案件中对卢加诺公约成员国专利侵权与有效性分离的处理逻辑"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Amendments in UPC proceedings may weaken the enforceability of corresponding national patents in non-UPC states.", "Practice note: Coordinate claim scope modifications globally to avoid undermining validity arguments in parallel national actions.", "What to monitor: How UPC decisions on validity risks influence jurisdictional limits for non-UPC national patent parts."]
core_points_en: The UPC lacks jurisdiction to adjudicate the validity of national patent parts granted by non-UPC member states.
Unilateral amendment of a unitary patent implies defects in the original grant, risking invalidity for non-amended national parts.
The UPC must refuse jurisdiction over non-member state patent parts when a serious risk of revocation exists.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Amendments in UPC proceedings may weaken the enforceability of corresponding national patents in non-UPC states.
Practice note: Coordinate claim scope modifications globally to avoid undermining validity arguments in parallel national actions.
What to monitor: How UPC decisions on validity risks influence jurisdictional limits for non-UPC national patent parts.

items.title: Re-establishment of rights following failure to apply for a cost decision in time | EIP
items.title_zh: 逾期未申请费用决定后的权利恢复
items.title_en: UPC Grants Re-establishment of Rights After Missed Cost Decision Deadline Due to Legal Error
items.summary: The dispute arises out of earlier proceedings between Heraeus Electronics GmbH & Co. KG (claimant) and Vibrantz GmbH (defendant), relating to European Patent No. 3215288. The Munich Local Division issued a substantive decision on 10 October 2025 addressing infringement and a counterclaim for revocation. Among other findings, the court partially revoked the patent in three Contracting Member States and dismissed the infringement action. In its cost decision, the court apportioned 40% of the costs
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院在Heraeus与Vibrantz专利纠纷中，裁定原告因代理人错误法律评估而逾期提交费用决定申请后，可恢复权利。法院认定该错误非疏忽所致，且符合程序规则，接受了迟交的申请及权利恢复请求，确立了混合费用比例下仍需主动申请的例外情形。
items.ai_summary_en: In proceedings before the Munich Local Division of the Unified Patent Court, the claimant missed the deadline to apply for a cost decision under Rule 151 of the Rules of Procedure. The claimant subsequently filed a late application alongside a request for re-establishment of rights under Rule 320. The court accepted that the representative’s failure resulted from an erroneous legal assessment regarding mixed cost ratios, rather than negligence. Despite the strict procedural requirements, the court found that the claimant maintained a robust internal monitoring system with four-eyes principles and random checks. Consequently, the court determined that the standard of due care was met and granted the re-establishment of rights, allowing the late application to proceed.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案表明UPC对“应有谨慎”标准的适用存在例外空间，特别是当错误源于合理的法律误解而非系统性疏忽时。实务中需关注法院对代理人主观状态与内部监控机制的审查权重，以及混合费用场景下的程序合规要求。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must ensure precise understanding of Unified Patent Court procedural rules, particularly regarding cost decisions under Rule 151. Even with robust internal deadline monitoring systems, relying on an incorrect legal interpretation of procedural obligations can lead to loss of rights. Legal teams should implement specialized training on UPC procedures and establish dual-check mechanisms for complex procedural deadlines. When facing procedural obstacles, parties should act swiftly to seek professional relief, ensuring that any error is documented as a genuine legal misapprehension rather than administrative negligence to satisfy due care requirements.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["慕尼黑地方法院裁定原告因代理人法律误判逾期，但符合“应有谨慎”标准，准予恢复权利", "法院确认在混合费用分摊情况下，当事人仍需在期限内依据程序规则第151条主动申请费用决定", "被告提出程序异议后，原告补交申请及权利恢复请求，法院认定其具备可受理性及实质理由"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The claimant missed the Rule 151 deadline due to a genuine but incorrect legal belief that no application was required in mixed cost scenarios.", "The court ruled that a specific legal misunderstanding can constitute a valid ground for re-establishment if supported by strong internal controls.", "This decision clarifies that UPC re-establishment standards remain strict yet allow flexibility when due care is demonstrably exercised."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC在特定条件下认可因法律误解导致的逾期可申请权利恢复，即便存在内部监控机制", "混合费用分摊情形下，净支付方仍需主动提交费用决定申请，不可默认豁免程序义务", "需持续观察后续类似案件中法院对“非疏忽”认定的具体边界及证据要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure accurate interpretation of UPC cost decision rules to prevent inadvertent loss of reimbursement rights despite existing monitoring systems.", "Practice note: Implement rigorous dual-check protocols for procedural deadlines and provide targeted training on nuanced UPC procedural requirements.", "What to monitor: How courts assess 'due care' when legal errors occur within otherwise compliant administrative frameworks under the new patent court regime."]
core_points_en: The claimant missed the Rule 151 deadline due to a genuine but incorrect legal belief that no application was required in mixed cost scenarios.
The court ruled that a specific legal misunderstanding can constitute a valid ground for re-establishment if supported by strong internal controls.
This decision clarifies that UPC re-establishment standards remain strict yet allow flexibility when due care is demonstrably exercised.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure accurate interpretation of UPC cost decision rules to prevent inadvertent loss of reimbursement rights despite existing monitoring systems.
Practice note: Implement rigorous dual-check protocols for procedural deadlines and provide targeted training on nuanced UPC procedural requirements.
What to monitor: How courts assess 'due care' when legal errors occur within otherwise compliant administrative frameworks under the new patent court regime.

items.title: UPC confirms that the “Malta Problem” bars a Unitary Patent | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC确认“马耳他问题”阻碍单一专利授权
items.title_en: UPC Confirms Malta Problem Bars Unitary Patent for Pre-2007 Applications
items.summary: It was foreseen that the requirements of the Unitary Patent Regulation (1257/2012) might result that old European patent applications (effective filing date before 1 March 2007) could be ineligible for unitary patent protection. The UPC has now confirmed the view of the EPO that this is indeed the case.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）在2025年12月30日的裁决中确认，根据《单一专利条例》，欧洲专利必须在所有参与成员国拥有相同权利要求。由于马耳他2007年3月1日才加入欧洲专利公约，此前提交的申请无法指定马耳他，因此不符合单一专利条件。UPC维持了欧洲专利局对EP3327608号专利拒绝授予单一效力的决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court has upheld the European Patent Office’s refusal to grant unitary effect to a patent derived from an application filed before Malta joined the European Patent Convention. In decision UPC-CFI-0001771/2025, the Court confirmed that Article 3(1) of Regulation 1257/2012 requires identical claims across all participating Member States. Since Malta was not designatable prior to 1 March 2007, older applications cannot meet this statutory requirement. The ruling validates the EPO’s interpretation that such patents are ineligible for unitary protection, establishing that the lack of Maltese designation creates an insurmountable legal barrier for pre-accession filings and their divisionals.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此裁决明确了单一专利资格的历史遗留边界，即“马耳他问题”构成法定障碍。实务中需关注2007年前申请的衍生专利，若未覆盖所有参与国则仅能进行国家验证。随着此类旧案减少，该问题影响将逐渐消退，但当前仍需严格审查相关专利的指定范围以合规。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies a narrow but definitive eligibility boundary for the Unitary Patent system. Rights holders managing portfolios with legacy European patents should verify filing dates to avoid futile requests for unitary effect. While the practical impact is limited to older cases and multigenerational divisionals, the decision reinforces strict compliance with territorial designation requirements. As these legacy applications naturally phase out, the issue will diminish, but current due diligence must account for this statutory exclusion to ensure accurate portfolio classification and validation strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC确认2007年3月1日前提交的欧专申请因无法指定马耳他，不符合同一权利要求要求", "UPC驳回上诉，维持EPO拒绝授予EP3327608号专利单一效力的决定，确认法律适用…", "该情形仅罕见出现于多代分案申请，随时间推移将自然消失，目前需核查专利指定状态"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC confirms pre-2007 applications cannot obtain unitary effect due to inability to designate Malta.", "Article 3(1) of Regulation 1257/2012 mandates identical claims for all participating Member States.", "EPO refusal of unitary effect for EP3327608 was upheld on appeal by the UPC."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确2007年3月1日前申请的欧专因马耳他非缔约国而天然缺失单一专利资格", "UPC与EPO在此程序性问题上立场一致，确立了严格的同一权利要求审查标准", "需持续监测此类历史遗留分案申请的处理动态，直至该类案件完全退出历史舞台"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patents stemming from applications filed before 1 March 2007 are statutorily ineligible for unitary effect.", "Practice note: Verify effective filing dates and designation histories before requesting unitary effect to avoid procedural refusal.", "What to monitor: Future handling of complex divisional chains derived from pre-2007 parent applications."]
core_points_en: UPC confirms pre-2007 applications cannot obtain unitary effect due to inability to designate Malta.
Article 3(1) of Regulation 1257/2012 mandates identical claims for all participating Member States.
EPO refusal of unitary effect for EP3327608 was upheld on appeal by the UPC.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patents stemming from applications filed before 1 March 2007 are statutorily ineligible for unitary effect.
Practice note: Verify effective filing dates and designation histories before requesting unitary effect to avoid procedural refusal.
What to monitor: Future handling of complex divisional chains derived from pre-2007 parent applications.

items.title: The CJEU SACD case: Admissibility of a copyright action that does not include all the co-authors
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院SACD案：未列全共同作者的版权诉讼可受理性
items.title_en: CJEU Ruling: Copyright Actions Need Not Include All Co-Authors If Unlocatable
items.summary: In 2019 the heirs of the famous French film director, author of many Nouvelle Vague (New Wave) films, Claude Chabrol who died in 2010, brought a case against the assignees of 14 films he made between 1967 and 1974.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在SACD案中裁定，欧盟法律不禁止法国关于版权侵权诉讼需传唤所有共同作者的规定，但要求灵活解释。若权利人经适当努力仍无法 locate 其他共有人，不应因此阻碍其行使诉权。该案源于法国新浪潮导演遗产继承人对电影受让方的索赔诉讼，因无法 locating 所有合作作者而陷入僵局，巴黎法院遂提请欧盟法院初步裁决。
items.ai_summary_en: In a case involving the heirs of French director Claude Chabrol, litigation regarding the exploitation of films was stalled because French law required all co-authors to be joined as parties. Despite diligent efforts, the claimants could not locate all rights holders for these older audiovisual works. The Paris Court of First Instance sought guidance from the CJEU on whether EU directives preclude such strict national admissibility rules. The CJEU ruled that while EU law does not prohibit national requirements for joining all co-authors, these rules must be applied flexibly. Specifically, if a plaintiff has made reasonable efforts to locate other rights holders but fails, the action should not be declared inadmissible. This ensures the effective enforcement of copyright while balancing the protection of co-authors' interests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了欧盟层面对于成员国程序性障碍的界限，平衡了共同作者保护与权利行使效率。实务中需关注法国法院后续如何具体界定“适当努力”的标准，以及该判例对其他拥有复杂共同著作权结构领域的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling provides critical procedural flexibility for rights holders enforcing copyright in Europe, particularly for historical audiovisual works where identifying all contributors is challenging. It prevents rigid national procedural rules from blocking legitimate claims when locating every co-author is practically impossible. Rights holders should document all attempts to identify and contact potential co-authors thoroughly. Demonstrating due diligence in these searches is essential to invoke this exception and avoid dismissal based on non-joinder. This approach supports effective IP enforcement across member states by aligning national procedures with EU principles of effective judicial protection.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院裁定欧盟法不排斥法国要求传唤所有共同作者的诉讼受理规则，但须灵活适用", "若权利人已尽适当努力仍无法找到其他版权共有人，不应因此剥夺其提起诉讼的权利", "案件源于法国导演遗产继承人起诉电影受让方利用不足，因无法定位所有合作作者而停滞"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["French law traditionally requires all co-authors to be joined in copyright infringement actions for admissibility.", "The CJEU mandates flexible application of this rule when co-authors cannot be located despite reasonable efforts.", "The judgment balances protecting co-authors' rights with ensuring effective copyright enforcement mechanisms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确欧盟法允许成员国设定共同作者参与诉讼的程序要求，但不得造成事实上的诉权剥夺", "需观察法国法院后续如何量化“适当努力”以认定无法 locate 其他共有人的情形", "该裁决可能影响其他涉及多方权利人的老旧作品版权维权策略及程序合规审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders can pursue infringement claims without joining all co-authors if they demonstrate reasonable efforts to locate them.", "Practice note: Meticulously document all search activities and communications attempted to identify missing co-authors to prove due diligence.", "What to monitor: How national courts interpret 'reasonable efforts' and apply this flexibility in future copyright admissibility disputes."]
core_points_en: French law traditionally requires all co-authors to be joined in copyright infringement actions for admissibility.
The CJEU mandates flexible application of this rule when co-authors cannot be located despite reasonable efforts.
The judgment balances protecting co-authors' rights with ensuring effective copyright enforcement mechanisms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders can pursue infringement claims without joining all co-authors if they demonstrate reasonable efforts to locate them.
Practice note: Meticulously document all search activities and communications attempted to identify missing co-authors to prove due diligence.
What to monitor: How national courts interpret 'reasonable efforts' and apply this flexibility in future copyright admissibility disputes.

items.title: Trademark News: What you might have missed in February 2026
items.title_zh: 2026年2月商标新闻综述：欧盟网络更新与国际注册费用调整
items.title_en: February 2026 Trademark Roundup: EU SME Support and Global Fee Adjustments
items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.Argentina revises trademark examination procedureOn 11 December 2025, Argentina’s National Institute of Industrial Property approved Resolution 583/2025, changing the way trademark applications are examined to shorten timelines and adjust the examination process.Read the full text here
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文汇总2026年2月商标动态。欧盟知识产权局网络（EUIPN）于1月22日推出新版网站，旨在为欧盟中小企业提供集成知识产权支持。世界知识产权组织（WIPO）于1月26日通知，调整指定芬兰、英国、以色列及格林达的马德里国际注册个别费用。此外提及阿根廷审查程序修订及哈萨克斯坦新知识产权法生效。
items.ai_summary_en: This update compiles key global trademark developments from February 2026. In Europe, the European Union Intellectual Property Network launched a redesigned website to provide integrated intellectual property support for small and medium-sized enterprises across the European Union. The World Intellectual Property Organization notified revised individual fees for Finland, the United Kingdom, and Israel under the Madrid System. Additionally, Argentina revised its trademark examination procedures to shorten timelines, while Kazakhstan enacted amendments to major IP laws. New Zealand also updated its list of conflicting goods. These changes impact international registration strategies, budgeting for renewals, and procedural compliance for rights holders operating in or entering these jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟层面通过EUIPN强化对中小企业的服务集成，反映政策导向。马德里体系下芬兰与英国个别费用的调整，直接影响指定这些法域的国际注册成本结构。实务中需关注WIPO官方公报以获取具体费率数值，并评估对既有国际注册商标组合维持成本的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should leverage the new EUIPN platform to access consolidated resources, potentially reducing administrative burdens and costs associated with trademark enforcement and registration in Europe. For international portfolios managed via the Madrid System, applicants must verify the updated fee structures for Finland, the United Kingdom, and Israel to ensure accurate budgeting for upcoming renewals and new designations. Failure to account for these rate adjustments may lead to unexpected cost overruns or procedural delays. Monitoring these procedural shifts in non-EU jurisdictions like Argentina and Kazakhstan is also essential for maintaining compliant and efficient global brand protection strategies.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EUIPN新平台上线，侧重提升欧盟区域内中小企业知识产权服务的可及性与集成度", "芬兰与英国作为马德里指定缔约方，其个别费用调整将直接改变相关国际注册的经济成本", "需持续监测WIPO后续发布的详细费率表，以准确核算涉及上述法域的国际商标注册预算"]

core_points_en: The EUIPN launched a new website offering integrated IP support services specifically designed for small and medium-sized enterprises within the European Union.
WIPO announced revised individual fees for Finland, the United Kingdom, and Israel regarding designations in international trademark applications and renewals.
Argentina streamlined trademark examination procedures to reduce timelines, while Kazakhstan implemented significant amendments to its core intellectual property legislation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize the new EUIPN digital resources to optimize trademark application workflows and enforcement strategies for SMEs operating in Europe.
Practice note: Verify current Madrid System fees for Finland, the UK, and Israel to adjust international registration budgets and prevent payment discrepancies.
What to monitor: Track procedural updates in Argentina and legislative changes in Kazakhstan to ensure ongoing compliance with local trademark examination requirements.

items.title: Description amendments can extend protection (T 439/22)
items.title_zh: 说明书修改可扩大保护范围（T 439/22）
items.title_en: EPO Board T 439/22: Description Amendments May Extend Protection Scope Under G 1/24
items.summary: The comments space for the last IPKat article on claim interpretation/description amendments appears to have maxed out. Sorry everyone, but maybe it's for the best. However, fear not, we now have a new decision to restart the debate! The decision in T 439/22 takes us back to our old friend, the definition of "gathered". Applying G 1/24, the Board of Appeal in the case is the second to find
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局上诉委员会在T 439/22号决定中，适用G 1/24 enlarged decision，认定说明书的修改可以扩大专利的保护范围。这是继前案后第二个就此问题作出裁决的案例，重新引发了关于权利要求解释与说明书修改之间关系的辩论。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision T 439/22, the EPO Board of Appeal revisited the definition of the term 'gathered' by applying the principles established in Enlarged Board of Appeal decision G 1/24. This ruling marks only the second instance where G 1/24 has been applied, signaling a developing line of case law regarding claim interpretation and description amendments. The Board confirmed that, under specific circumstances, amendments to the patent description can be interpreted as extending the scope of protection. This outcome reignites critical debates within the European patent community concerning the precise boundaries between permissible descriptive clarifications and substantive changes that alter the legal reach of a patent claim.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该判例进一步明确了G 1/24实施后说明书修改对保护范围的影响。实务中需密切关注上诉委员会如何界定“从说明书中 gathered”的技术特征，以及其与权利要求解释的互动关系，这将直接影响专利确权与侵权判定策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should exercise caution when amending patent descriptions during opposition or appeal proceedings. As demonstrated in T 439/22, such amendments carry the risk of being interpreted as extending the scope of protection, potentially leading to invalidity findings under Article 123(3) EPC. Applicants must ensure strict logical consistency between claims and the description to avoid unintended broadening. Proactive monitoring of EPO jurisprudence following G 1/24 is essential for refining drafting strategies. Legal counsel should review amendment proposals carefully to mitigate litigation risks associated with scope expansion disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉委员会在T 439/22案中适用G 1/24，认定说明书修改可扩大保护范围", "该案是第二个就“gathered”定义及说明书修改效力作出裁决的上诉委员会决定", "此决定重启了关于权利要求解释与说明书修改之间关系的法律辩论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Board applied G 1/24 principles to redefine the technical meaning of the term 'gathered' in the context of the disputed patent claims.", "The decision confirms that description amendments may effectively extend protection scope if they alter the technical teaching beyond the original disclosure.", "This ruling triggers renewed industry discussion regarding the legal limits of claim interpretation versus permissible description modifications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["G 1/24后说明书修改对保护范围的影响获进一步司法确认", "需观察上诉委员会对“gathered”技术特征的界定标准演变", "关注后续案例中权利要求解释与说明书一致性审查的具体适用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor EPO case law developments to prevent description amendments from inadvertently expanding protection scope and triggering invalidity.", "Practice note: Optimize patent application drafting to ensure rigorous logical consistency between claims and the description, minimizing interpretation risks.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent Board of Appeal decisions applying G 1/24 to understand evolving standards for claim interpretation and description limits."]
core_points_en: The Board applied G 1/24 principles to redefine the technical meaning of the term 'gathered' in the context of the disputed patent claims.
The decision confirms that description amendments may effectively extend protection scope if they alter the technical teaching beyond the original disclosure.
This ruling triggers renewed industry discussion regarding the legal limits of claim interpretation versus permissible description modifications.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor EPO case law developments to prevent description amendments from inadvertently expanding protection scope and triggering invalidity.
Practice note: Optimize patent application drafting to ensure rigorous logical consistency between claims and the description, minimizing interpretation risks.
What to monitor: Track subsequent Board of Appeal decisions applying G 1/24 to understand evolving standards for claim interpretation and description limits.
