items.title: Hurrah! CA overrules High Court in Zaha Hadid case
items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院推翻高等法院在扎哈·哈迪德案中的判决
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Overrules High Court in Zaha Hadid Trademark Case
items.summary: IP Draughts has previously commented on the High Court decision of Zaha Hadid Ltd v the Zaha Hadid Foundation. See https://ipdraughts.wordpress.com/2024/12/30/when-can-you-terminate-on-reasonable-notice/ In that article, he was troubled by a couple of cases on trade mark licensing, including the Zaha Hadid case, which appeared to up-end his understanding of the law on a right to terminate…
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Zaha Hadid Ltd诉Zaha Hadid Foundation案中推翻高等法院判决。Sir Colin Birss主笔判决，认定涉案商标许可协议期限为“不定期”而非“永久”，因此适用基于合理通知终止合同的法律原则（如Martin Baker案）。该判决澄清了许可协议中默示条款与明示条款解释的区别，恢复了法律确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary reviews the Court of Appeal judgment in Zaha Hadid Ltd v The Zaha Hadid Foundation, which overturns the earlier High Court decision. The initial ruling had raised concerns regarding the right to terminate trademark licenses on reasonable notice. Sir Colin Birss C, with agreement from colleagues, reversed the lower court’s finding. He determined that where an agreement does not explicitly specify a perpetual duration, it is construed as being of indefinite duration. Consequently, established case law, specifically Martin Baker v Canadian Flight Equipment, applies, permitting termination upon reasonable notice. This approach resolves prior legal ambiguities by distinguishing between perpetual and indefinite terms without relying on artificial distinctions regarding implied terms. The judgment is viewed as a pragmatic resolution that aligns legal interpretation with commercial common sense.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了未约定固定期限的知识产权许可协议可经合理通知终止。实务中需审查许可协议期限条款的措辞，区分“永久”与“不定期”的法律后果。建议持续关注该判决对既有长期许可协议稳定性的潜在影响，以及后续是否会有进一步上诉或立法回应。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties negotiating trademark or intellectual property licenses in Europe should explicitly define contract durations and termination conditions. If a fixed term is not specified, counterparties may rely on this precedent to argue for termination on reasonable notice. Drafting agreements with clear, unambiguous language regarding duration helps mitigate the risk of unilateral termination. Rights holders should ensure contracts either specify a fixed period or expressly exclude the default application of reasonable notice termination rights to maintain commercial stability and predictability in licensing relationships.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉法院认定协议期限为不定期，适用合理通知终止规则", "判决推翻了高等法院关于必须通过明示条款解释终止权的观点", "Sir Colin Birss的判决结合了常识与法律理解，获同事一致同意"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal overturned the High Court, confirming that trademark licenses of indefinite duration may be terminated on reasonable notice.", "The judgment distinguishes between perpetual and indefinite durations, applying existing precedent rather than forcing interpretations via implied terms.", "This decision eliminates legal uncertainty surrounding IP license termination rights, restoring standard contractual interpretation principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["未定期限的IP许可协议可能被视为不定期，允许单方合理通知终止", "协议起草时需明确期限性质，避免被认定为不定期而面临终止风险", "观察该判例是否成为处理类似许可合同纠纷的标准先例"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Explicitly define license durations to prevent agreements from being classified as terminable at will under indefinite duration precedents.", "Practice note: Draft precise termination clauses to exclude the default application of reasonable notice rights, ensuring greater commercial certainty.", "What to monitor: Monitor how courts interpret 'perpetual' versus 'indefinite' labels in future IP licensing disputes across UK jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal overturned the High Court, confirming that trademark licenses of indefinite duration may be terminated on reasonable notice.
The judgment distinguishes between perpetual and indefinite durations, applying existing precedent rather than forcing interpretations via implied terms.
This decision eliminates legal uncertainty surrounding IP license termination rights, restoring standard contractual interpretation principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Explicitly define license durations to prevent agreements from being classified as terminable at will under indefinite duration precedents.
Practice note: Draft precise termination clauses to exclude the default application of reasonable notice rights, ensuring greater commercial certainty.
What to monitor: Monitor how courts interpret 'perpetual' versus 'indefinite' labels in future IP licensing disputes across UK jurisdictions.

items.title: Using A Designer's Name After Departure May Create A Deceptive Trademark
items.title_zh: 设计师离职后使用其姓名商标或构成欺骗性
items.title_en: CJEU: Post-Departure Use of Designer Name Trademark May Be Deceptive
items.summary: Does European law preclude the cancellation of a trademark bearing a creator's name when used by the assignee in such a way as to effectively create the impression that the creator is still involved...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院应法国最高法院请求作出澄清：若受让人在设计师离职后继续使用其姓名商标，且使用方式使公众误以为设计师仍参与产品设计，则该商标可能因具有欺骗性而被宣告无效。此案源于时尚设计师与收购其业务及商标的公司PMJC之间的纠纷，设计师离职后公司仍使用其姓名商标，且曾因侵犯设计师未转让作品的版权被判侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has clarified that EU law does not preclude the cancellation of a trademark bearing a creator's name when used by an assignee in a manner that creates the false impression the designer remains involved. This ruling addresses a preliminary question from the French Court of Cassation regarding a dispute between a fashion designer and a company that acquired his business. The CJEU confirmed that while a designer's departure alone does not automatically render a trademark deceptive or invalid, the specific manner of use is critical. If the assignee’s marketing suggests ongoing creative participation by the departed designer, the mark may be deemed deceptive and subject to cancellation. This decision refines previous case law, emphasizing that consumer perception regarding the commercial origin and personal involvement of the creator determines the validity of such trademarks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 企业收购含自然人姓名的商标后，需审慎管理品牌使用方式，避免暗示原创作者持续参与。若创作者已完全脱离业务，营销材料应避免模糊关联，以防被认定为欺骗性使用从而导致商标无效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders acquiring businesses with founder or designer names must carefully assess the boundaries of using those names after the individual's departure. Marketing strategies should avoid implying ongoing creative involvement if the relationship has ended, as this may lead to findings of deception and subsequent trademark invalidity. It is advisable to review assignment agreements for specific clauses governing post-termination use and disclosure obligations. Clear communication regarding the current status of design leadership helps mitigate legal risks. Companies should ensure that promotional materials accurately reflect the actual contributors to product development to maintain trademark validity and avoid consumer confusion regarding the brand's origins.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法律不禁止因设计师离职后的使用方式导致姓名商标产生欺骗性而将其撤销", "若商标使用造成设计师仍参与产品创作的印象，而事实并非如此，可认定具有欺骗性", "此前Emanuel案确立仅离职本身不致商标无效，但新判决澄清了具体使用引发误导的情形"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A designer's departure from a company does not automatically invalidate a trademark bearing their name under EU law.", "Trademarks may be cancelled if post-departure use misleads the public into believing the designer still participates in product creation.", "The case originated from a French dispute where the defendant was previously convicted of copyright infringement against the designer."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["收购含人名商标时，需评估创作者离职后的品牌使用合规性及潜在欺骗性风险", "市场营销中应明确区分历史传承与当前创作团队，避免误导消费者关于设计师参与度", "关注欧盟法院对“欺骗性”认定的具体标准，特别是在版权侵权背景下的商标有效性挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Acquirers of brands with personal names must define strict limits on post-departure usage to prevent deceptive impressions.", "Practice note: Draft assignment agreements with explicit clauses restricting marketing implications of continued designer involvement.", "What to monitor: Monitor promotional campaigns for any subtle suggestions that a departed creator remains active in product design."]
core_points_en: A designer's departure from a company does not automatically invalidate a trademark bearing their name under EU law.
Trademarks may be cancelled if post-departure use misleads the public into believing the designer still participates in product creation.
The case originated from a French dispute where the defendant was previously convicted of copyright infringement against the designer.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Acquirers of brands with personal names must define strict limits on post-departure usage to prevent deceptive impressions.
Practice note: Draft assignment agreements with explicit clauses restricting marketing implications of continued designer involvement.
What to monitor: Monitor promotional campaigns for any subtle suggestions that a departed creator remains active in product design.

items.title: EU escalates global FRAND rate-setting WTO complaint against China
items.title_zh: 欧盟升级针对中国的全球 FRAND 费率设定 WTO 申诉
items.title_en: EU Escalates Global FRAND Rate-Setting WTO Complaint Against China
items.summary: The move follows the European Union’s recent arbitration victory in a parallel SEP trade dispute with China
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟近期在与中国平行的标准必要专利贸易争端仲裁中获胜，随即宣布升级其在世贸组织框架下针对中国全球 FRAND 费率设定的投诉。此举标志着欧中在知识产权定价权上的博弈从双边仲裁扩展至多边贸易体制，旨在通过国际规则施压，确立更有利于欧洲权利人的全球许可费率裁判标准。
items.ai_summary_en: Following a recent arbitration victory in a parallel standard essential patent trade dispute with China, the European Union has announced an escalation of its complaint within the World Trade Organization framework. This strategic move targets China’s judicial practices regarding the setting of global FRAND licensing rates. The action signifies a significant expansion of the intellectual property pricing conflict from bilateral arbitration mechanisms to the broader multilateral trade system. By leveraging international trade rules, the EU aims to exert pressure and establish global adjudication standards for licensing rates that are more favorable to European rights holders. This development highlights the increasing intersection of IP litigation and international trade law, as stakeholders navigate complex jurisdictional challenges in cross-border SEP enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严峻的 SEP 诉讼环境，需警惕欧盟利用 WTO 规则限制中国法院的全球禁令与费率裁定。建议企业提前布局海外许可谈判，评估管辖权风险，并关注欧盟后续可能出台的限制性政策指引。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in SEP licensing should anticipate a more rigorous legal environment in Europe. The EU's utilization of WTO rules may restrict the effectiveness of Chinese court rulings on global injunctions and rate determinations. Companies must proactively assess jurisdictional risks and strengthen compliance frameworks to mitigate potential trade sanctions. Strategic preparation for overseas licensing negotiations is critical to navigating these evolving regulatory dynamics. Stakeholders should closely monitor subsequent policy guidance from the EU, which may introduce further limitations on foreign judicial interventions in global IP disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟借仲裁胜诉之势，将 SEP 争端升级至 WTO 层面", "焦点在于挑战中国法院设定全球 FRAND 费率的管辖权与实践", "此举意在通过多边机制重塑全球标准必要专利许可规则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EU leverages its recent arbitration success to elevate the SEP dispute to the WTO level.", "The core focus challenges Chinese courts' jurisdiction and practices in determining global FRAND rates.", "This initiative seeks to reshape global SEP licensing rules through established multilateral trade mechanisms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟借多边机制削弱中国法院全球费率裁定效力", "出口企业应强化 SEP 合规审查，规避潜在贸易制裁风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders face heightened scrutiny as the EU uses multilateral mechanisms to challenge the validity of Chinese global rate rulings.", "Practice note: Export-oriented entities should reinforce SEP compliance reviews to avoid exposure to potential trade-related sanctions.", "What to monitor: Future EU policy directives that may further constrain the enforceability of foreign judgments on global FRAND terms."]
core_points_en: The EU leverages its recent arbitration success to elevate the SEP dispute to the WTO level.
The core focus challenges Chinese courts' jurisdiction and practices in determining global FRAND rates.
This initiative seeks to reshape global SEP licensing rules through established multilateral trade mechanisms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders face heightened scrutiny as the EU uses multilateral mechanisms to challenge the validity of Chinese global rate rulings.
Practice note: Export-oriented entities should reinforce SEP compliance reviews to avoid exposure to potential trade-related sanctions.
What to monitor: Future EU policy directives that may further constrain the enforceability of foreign judgments on global FRAND terms.

items.title: Nokia agrees to UK-set interim and final licences with Paramount, Warner Bros
items.title_zh: 诺基亚与派拉蒙、华纳兄弟达成英国设定的 interim 及最终许可
items.title_en: Nokia Accepts UK-Set SEP Licences for Paramount and Warner Bros While Appealing Acer and Asus Ruling
items.summary: Meanwhile, the Finnish company continues to appeal against the interim licence declaration granted on behalf of Acer and Asus
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示，芬兰公司诺基亚已与派拉蒙和华纳兄弟达成由英国法院设定的临时及最终标准必要专利许可协议。摘要同时指出，诺基亚继续就代表宏碁和华硕授予的临时许可声明提起上诉。
items.ai_summary_en: Nokia has agreed to accept the interim and final standard essential patent licence terms set by the UK High Court on behalf of media giants Paramount Global and Warner Bros Discovery. This development marks a significant step in resolving long-standing licensing disputes between the Finnish telecommunications equipment manufacturer and major Hollywood studios regarding the use of its cellular technology patents. Meanwhile, Nokia continues to pursue an appeal against the earlier interim licence declaration granted in cases involving consumer electronics manufacturers Acer and Asus. This parallel legal action highlights the ongoing complexity and high stakes involved in global FRAND rate determinations. The situation underscores the influential role of the UK judiciary in setting global licensing benchmarks, creating a dynamic environment where rights holders and implementers must navigate concurrent settlements and appeals across different defendant categories.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国法院在SEP费率设定中的持续影响力，以及诺基亚在不同被许可人案件中采取的差异化合规与上诉策略对后续FRAND判例的潜在参照意义。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor UK judicial trends in SEP litigation, as these rulings often carry substantial global weight. Given the potential for worldwide effect, companies must proactively assess patent infringement risks and develop flexible licensing negotiation strategies. It is critical to track appellate developments in related cases to effectively manage exposure to injunctions and ensure compliance with evolving FRAND obligations. A coordinated approach to multi-jurisdictional disputes is essential for mitigating legal and financial risks associated with standard essential patent enforcement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示诺基亚与派拉蒙、华纳兄弟达成英国设定的临时及最终SEP许可", "原文摘要显示诺基亚正就针对宏碁和华硕的临时许可声明提起上诉"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Nokia accepts UK court-determined interim and final SEP licence terms for Paramount and Warner Bros.", "The company continues appealing the interim licence declaration previously granted for Acer and Asus.", "UK courts maintain significant influence in determining global FRAND rates for standard essential patents."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国法院设定的SEP许可条款对全球权利人的合规参考性", "诺基亚针对不同被许可人（如宏碁/华硕）的上诉策略动向", "后续类似SEP纠纷中临时许可声明的司法认定趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UK SEP rulings have global reach; rights holders and implementers must anticipate cross-border impacts on licensing portfolios.", "Practice note: Develop integrated negotiation strategies that account for concurrent settlements and active appeals in parallel proceedings.", "What to monitor: Track appellate progress in hardware manufacturer cases to gauge shifts in judicial interpretation of FRAND commitments."]
core_points_en: Nokia accepts UK court-determined interim and final SEP licence terms for Paramount and Warner Bros.
The company continues appealing the interim licence declaration previously granted for Acer and Asus.
UK courts maintain significant influence in determining global FRAND rates for standard essential patents.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: UK SEP rulings have global reach; rights holders and implementers must anticipate cross-border impacts on licensing portfolios.
Practice note: Develop integrated negotiation strategies that account for concurrent settlements and active appeals in parallel proceedings.
What to monitor: Track appellate progress in hardware manufacturer cases to gauge shifts in judicial interpretation of FRAND commitments.

items.title: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.title_zh: EUIPO呼吁外观设计专家加入中小企业公益法律援助项目
items.title_en: EUIPO Seeks Design Rights Experts for Pro Bono SME Support Programme
items.summary: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO公益项目2025年收到1375份中小企业支持请求，创历史新高，超九成来自微型企业。尽管商标咨询仍占主导，但2026年将重点加强外观设计保护领域的专家参与。目前网络已有289名活跃专家，主要来自德、意、西等国。EUIPO正招募具有外观设计权利经验的专业人士，以支持欧洲创意企业发展。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO Pro Bono Programme recorded 1,375 SME support requests in 2025, the highest volume since its inception. Over 90% of applicants were microenterprises, with significant participation from Germany, Spain, Italy, France, and the Netherlands. While trademark assistance remains the primary demand, the programme is expanding its focus for 2026 to address growing needs in design protection. The network currently comprises 289 active experts, having added 165 new members in 2025. The EUIPO explicitly calls for IP practitioners with design rights expertise to join the network, aiming to strengthen support for design-driven businesses and improve user experience for both SMEs and professionals.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This expansion signals a strategic shift toward supporting design-intensive industries within the EU pro bono framework. For rights holders, it offers a viable channel for early-stage design strategy validation. Practitioners should note the opportunity to engage with emerging design-driven SMEs, potentially fostering long-term professional relationships through initial pro bono consultations.

The programme will prioritize engaging design rights experts in 2026 to meet rising demand in this sector.
The expert network grew to 289 active members in 2025, with strong representation in major EU markets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Microenterprises and design-driven startups can leverage this network for cost-effective initial guidance on design registration and protection strategies.
Practice note: IP practitioners with design expertise are encouraged to join the network, where consultations typically last one to four hours, ensuring manageable time commitment…
What to monitor: Track the uptake of design-specific consultations in 2026 to assess whether increased expert availability improves access to justice for smaller design firms.

items.title: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.title_zh: EUIPO 提醒申请人关注欧盟手工艺品和工业产品地理标志新制度过渡安排
items.title_en: EUIPO Reminder on New EU Geographical Indications System for Craft and Industrial Products
items.summary: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO 提醒申请人，自 2025 年 12 月 1 日起，该局已负责新的手工艺品和工业产品地理标志（CIGI）注册；到 2026 年 12 月 2 日，现有各成员国的相关国家保护制度将被新的欧盟制度取代。二十个成员国的申请人通常需先经本国主管机关完成国家阶段审查，其余七个成员国的申请人则直接向 EUIPO 申请。
items.ai_summary_en: Since 1 December 2025, the EUIPO has managed registrations for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products (CIGIs). National protection systems will be fully replaced by this EU framework by 2 December 2026. Applicants in twenty Member States must file through national authorities for initial examination and opposition, while applicants in seven specific states file directly with the EUIPO. The Office also accepts applications for existing national CIGIs protected or established by use before the competence transfer date. Early uptake is significant, with sixty-four applications published by late February 2026, primarily covering stones, textiles, and ceramics. The EUIPO provides the GIportal e-filing tool and educational resources via its GI Hub to support stakeholders during this transition.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这是一条实务价值很高的制度落地提醒。对涉及陶瓷、石材、纺织等传统产业的权利人和顾问而言，关键不是原则性介绍，而是尽快确认适用路径、时间窗口和是否需要把既有国家保护迁移到新欧盟体系内。
items.ai_insight_en: This transition creates immediate procedural obligations for rights holders in craft and industrial sectors. Companies must verify whether their jurisdiction requires national pre-screening or permits direct EUIPO filing to avoid procedural delays. Existing national rightsholders should prioritize converting protections to the EU register before the 2026 deadline to maintain continuous coverage. The early volume of applications in traditional sectors suggests active portfolio migration, making timely engagement with the GIportal system essential for effective rights management.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 已接管新的手工艺品和工业产品地理标志注册职能", "现有国家保护制度将在 2026 年 12 月 2 日前完成向欧盟制度的过渡", "不同成员国申请路径不同，部分申请人先走国家阶段，部分可直接向 EUIPO 递交"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["National CIGI protection systems will be fully phased out and replaced by the new EU regime by 2 December 2026.", "Filing routes differ: twenty Member States require national phase examination, while seven states allow direct application to the EUIPO.", "Rights holders can seek EU protection for existing CIGIs established under national law or by use prior to 1 December 2025."]

core_points_en: National CIGI protection systems will be fully phased out and replaced by the new EU regime by 2 December 2026.
Filing routes differ: twenty Member States require national phase examination, while seven states allow direct application to the EUIPO.
Rights holders can seek EU protection for existing CIGIs established under national law or by use prior to 1 December 2025.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders in ceramics, textiles, and minerals must determine if their existing national protections require conversion to the new EU CIGI system by December…
Practice note: Applicants must confirm the correct filing route, as twenty Member States mandate national examination while seven allow direct submission to the EUIPO.
What to monitor: Track the publication of new applications via GIportal and observe how national authorities handle the initial examination and opposition phases.

items.title: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.title_zh: 知识产权惠及各方：权利对中小企业的关键重要性
items.title_en: EUIPO Commentary: Critical Role of IP Rights for SME Revenue and Investment
items.summary: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局文章强调知识产权对欧盟中小企业至关重要。引用EUIPO与EPO联合研究显示，拥有知识产权的企业人均收入更高，中小企业注册知识产权后人均收入通常高出44%。知识产权密集型行业吸引了欧盟绝大多数风险投资，且初创企业若有注册商标或专利，获得早期风投的可能性高出十倍，成功退出的可能性高出200%。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO highlights the strategic value of intellectual property for small and medium-sized enterprises, citing joint studies with the EPO. Data indicates that SMEs registering IP rights achieve 44% higher revenue per employee compared to non-IP holders. Furthermore, IP-intensive industries attracted 88% of private equity and venture capital funding in the EU between 2021 and 2023. These sectors also demonstrate a significant wage premium. The commentary underscores IP as a central asset for commercial success, competitiveness, and securing investment, aligning with broader European Commission reports on single market competitiveness and innovation signals for startups.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This commentary quantifies the commercial impact of IP registration for SMEs, linking protected assets to superior revenue metrics and access to venture capital. For in-house counsel and advisors, these statistics provide empirical support for prioritizing IP strategy in early-stage business planning. The data suggests that IP rights function not merely as legal protections but as critical financial signals to investors. Companies should view IP portfolio development as integral to valuation and growth strategies, particularly when seeking external funding in competitive EU markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["拥有知识产权的中小企业人均收入比无保护 counterparts 高出44%", "至2023年间，欧盟88%的风投资金流向知识产权密集型初创企业", "拥有注册商标或专利的初创企业获早期风投可能性高十倍，退出成功率高200%"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["SMEs with registered IP rights generate 44% higher revenue per employee than counterparts without protection.", "IP-intensive industries attracted 88% of EU private equity and venture capital funding from 2021 to 2023.", "IP-intensive sectors pay significantly higher wages, showing a 40.9% premium over other industries."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["知识产权注册与中小企业人均营收增长存在显著正相关", "知识产权密集型行业是欧盟风险投资的主要流向领域", "早期知识产权布局显著提升初创企业融资成功率及退出概率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: IP registration correlates with 44% higher revenue per employee and dominates venture capital allocation.", "Practice note: Position IP assets as key value drivers and risk mitigation tools during investor due diligence processes.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO and EPO joint studies tracking IP intensity against startup exit rates and funding rounds."]
core_points_en: SMEs with registered IP rights generate 44% higher revenue per employee than counterparts without protection.
IP-intensive industries attracted 88% of EU private equity and venture capital funding from 2021 to 2023.
IP-intensive sectors pay significantly higher wages, showing a 40.9% premium over other industries.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: IP registration correlates with 44% higher revenue per employee and dominates venture capital allocation.
Practice note: Position IP assets as key value drivers and risk mitigation tools during investor due diligence processes.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO and EPO joint studies tracking IP intensity against startup exit rates and funding rounds.

items.title: Opinion of the Advocate General in Cases C-496/23 P Meta Platforms Ireland v Commission (Facebook Marketplace) and C-497/23 P Meta Platforms Ireland v Commission (Facebook Data)
items.title_zh: 总检察长建议驳回Meta就Facebook数据及市场服务上诉
items.title_en: Advocate General Proposes Dismissing Meta Appeals on Commission Data Requests
items.summary: Advocate General Rantos proposes the dismissal of the appeals by Meta Platforms Ireland relating to an investigation into abuse of a dominant position concerning the use of Facebook Data and the Facebook Marketplace service
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院总检察长Rantos发布意见，建议驳回Meta Platforms Ireland针对欧盟委员会的两项上诉。该案源于欧委会对Meta滥用市场支配地位（涉及Facebook数据及Marketplace）的调查，要求提供内部文件。普通法院此前认定欧委会的信息请求合法、必要且符合隐私权保护。总检察长认为普通法院在法律适用上无误，欧委会拥有广泛调查权，无需在初期证明每份文件的具体相关性。
items.ai_summary_en: Advocate General Rantos proposes dismissing Meta Platforms Ireland’s appeals against General Court judgments upholding European Commission decisions. The Commission had requested internal documents via electronic search terms during an investigation into alleged abuse of dominant position regarding Facebook Data and Marketplace services. The General Court previously ruled these requests were reasoned, necessary, proportionate, and compliant with privacy rights. The Advocate General finds no error in law regarding the necessity assessment or safeguards, including virtual data room procedures for sensitive personal data. This opinion supports the Commission’s broad investigative powers under competition procedural regulations, suggesting the Court of Justice will likely uphold the lower court’s validation of the information requests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This opinion reinforces the European Commission’s extensive fact-finding powers in competition investigations, even when involving large volumes of internal digital records. Rights holders should note that privacy objections alone may not suffice to block document production if procedural safeguards like virtual data rooms are implemented. The anticipated judgment will likely confirm that regulators need not demonstrate specific relevance for every document at the initial request stage, maintaining a low threshold for evidence gathering in antitrust probes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["总检察长建议驳回Meta上诉，维持普通法院支持欧委会调查决定的判决", "确认欧委会有权要求提供内部文件以调查滥用市场支配地位，包括涉及敏感个人数据的内容", "认定信息请求理由充分、必要且成比例，符合隐私权及良好行政原则，无需详尽法律评估"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Advocate General recommends dismissing Meta’s appeals challenging Commission document requests in dominance investigation.", "General Court rulings confirming necessity and proportionality of information requests are upheld as legally sound.", "Virtual data room safeguards for sensitive personal data deemed sufficient to protect privacy rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟竞争法调查中的数据调取权优先于一般性隐私抗辩，除非程序严重违规", "总检察长意见倾向于支持监管机构在调查初期的广泛取证权力，降低举证门槛", "需关注欧盟法院最终判决是否采纳该意见，这将确立数字市场反垄断调查的证据规则基准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies facing EU competition investigations must prepare for broad document requests, as privacy defenses have limited scope when adequate technical safeguard…", "Practice note: Legal teams should ensure robust virtual data room protocols are ready to handle sensitive personal data during regulatory inquiries to mitigate proportionality cha…", "What to monitor: Watch for the final Court of Justice judgment to confirm if it adopts this strict stance on evidentiary thresholds in digital market antitrust cases."]
core_points_en: Advocate General recommends dismissing Meta’s appeals challenging Commission document requests in dominance investigation.
General Court rulings confirming necessity and proportionality of information requests are upheld as legally sound.
Virtual data room safeguards for sensitive personal data deemed sufficient to protect privacy rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies facing EU competition investigations must prepare for broad document requests, as privacy defenses have limited scope when adequate technical safeguard…
Practice note: Legal teams should ensure robust virtual data room protocols are ready to handle sensitive personal data during regulatory inquiries to mitigate proportionality cha…
What to monitor: Watch for the final Court of Justice judgment to confirm if it adopts this strict stance on evidentiary thresholds in digital market antitrust cases.

items.title: Judgment of the Court of Justice in Case C-337/22 P EUIPO v Nowhere
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：过渡期后仅依英国法享有的在先权利不得用于支持欧盟商标异议
items.title_en: CJEU Rules UK Rights Cannot Support EU Trademark Oppositions Post-Transition
items.summary: Brexit and EU trade marks: Earlier rights based solely on UK law can no longer be relied upon in support of an opposition where EUIPO rules after the end of the transition period
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在EUIPO诉Nowhere案中撤销普通法院判决，维持EUIPO决定。法院裁定，若EUIPO在脱欧过渡期结束后作出裁决，仅依据英国法律享有的在先未注册商标权利，不再能作为反对欧盟商标注册的依据。因为在先权利必须在EUIPO作出决定时仍然有效，而英国权利在过渡期后在欧盟境内已失效。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union set aside the General Court’s judgment in EUIPO v Nowhere, ruling that earlier rights based solely on UK law cannot support an opposition to an EU trademark application if the EUIPO decision is rendered after the Brexit transition period. Although the EU application was filed before the transition ended, the Court held that unregistered signs must confer a right to prohibit use at the time of the EUIPO decision. Since UK rights no longer have effect in the EU post-transition, they fail this second requirement. The Court thereby upheld the EUIPO’s rejection of the opposition, clarifying that the temporal validity of earlier rights is assessed at the date of the administrative decision, not merely the filing date.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了脱欧后欧盟商标异议程序中“在先权利持续有效性”的时间判断标准。实务中需严格区分权利产生时间与权利存续时间，仅凭申请日前的英国使用事实不足以支撑过渡期后的欧盟异议，权利人需确保在EUIPO裁决时刻拥有欧盟境内有效的在先权利基础。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment establishes that the substantive validity of earlier rights in EU trademark oppositions is determined at the time of the EUIPO decision. Rights holders cannot rely on national rights that have ceased to have effect in the EU territory due to Brexit, even if those rights existed when the contested application was filed. This reinforces the territorial nature of IP rights and the impact of geopolitical changes on pending proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院规定基于未注册标志的异议需满足两项要求：申请日前取得权利及EUIPO裁决时仍享…", "仅依英国法享有的在先权利在脱欧过渡期结束后，无法在欧盟境内继续产生禁止使用的法律效力", "即使欧盟商标申请提交于过渡期结束前，若EUIPO裁决时在先英国权利已失效，异议不能成立"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Earlier rights must confer prohibition rights at the time of the EUIPO decision, not just at filing.", "UK-only rights lost effect in the EU after the Brexit transition period expired.", "Oppositions relying solely on pre-transition UK unregistered rights fail if decided post-transition."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确EUIPO裁决时点在判断在先权利有效性中的决定性作用，而非仅看申请日", "纯英国法下的未注册商标权利在过渡期后彻底失去阻碍欧盟商标注册的程序资格", "后续需关注此类判决对涉及跨境未注册商标权益冲突案件的既判力影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Owners of UK-only unregistered rights cannot block later EU trademark registrations through oppositions decided after the transition period, regardless of prior…", "Practice note: In opposition proceedings, verify that cited earlier rights remain legally effective in the EU at the anticipated date of the EUIPO decision.", "What to monitor: Watch for similar applications of this temporal validity rule in other contexts where national rights diverge from EU protection scopes."]
core_points_en: Earlier rights must confer prohibition rights at the time of the EUIPO decision, not just at filing.
UK-only rights lost effect in the EU after the Brexit transition period expired.
Oppositions relying solely on pre-transition UK unregistered rights fail if decided post-transition.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Owners of UK-only unregistered rights cannot block later EU trademark registrations through oppositions decided after the transition period, regardless of prior…
Practice note: In opposition proceedings, verify that cited earlier rights remain legally effective in the EU at the anticipated date of the EUIPO decision.
What to monitor: Watch for similar applications of this temporal validity rule in other contexts where national rights diverge from EU protection scopes.

items.title: What happens to global SEP strategy if Wilus wins US injunction?
items.title_zh: 若 Wilus 获美禁令，全球 SEP 战略将如何演变？
items.title_en: Global SEP Strategy Shifts If Wilus Secures US Injunction and Restores Enforcement Venue
items.summary: It could dramatically reshape global SEP litigation by restoring the US as a top enforcement venue
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨若 Wilus 在美国成功获得标准必要专利（SEP）禁令，将如何重塑全球诉讼格局。分析指出，此举可能恢复美国作为顶级执行地的地位，迫使全球权利人与实施者重新评估其许可谈判策略及管辖地选择，进而影响包括欧洲在内的全球 SEP 生态体系。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the potential transformation of global standard-essential patent litigation should Wilus successfully obtain an injunction in the United States. Such a ruling could significantly restore the status of US courts as premier venues for enforcing SEP rights, altering the current landscape dominated by other jurisdictions. The outcome would likely compel both rights holders and implementers worldwide to reassess their licensing negotiation strategies and jurisdictional choices. Consequently, this shift would impact the broader global SEP ecosystem, including European markets, by changing leverage dynamics in cross-border disputes. The article highlights how a favorable US decision could rebalance enforcement power, influencing where parties choose to litigate and settle complex SEP conflicts globally.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营需警惕美国判决的域外溢出效应。建议提前评估在美专利风险，优化全球许可布局，避免因地缘司法变动导致在欧洲市场的谈判筹码受损或面临双重诉讼压力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating across jurisdictions should anticipate the extraterritorial ripple effects of significant US SEP rulings. A decisive US injunction could alter negotiation leverage in Europe, potentially exposing companies to dual litigation pressures or diminished bargaining power. It is prudent to evaluate patent exposure in the US early within the licensing cycle. Stakeholders should develop defensive strategies that account for divergent judicial approaches in key markets like the US and Europe. Monitoring these developments helps mitigate risks associated with geopolitical judicial shifts and ensures robust positioning in global FRAND negotiations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Wilus 若获美禁令将显著提升美国法院执行力", "全球 SEP 诉讼重心可能向美国市场发生转移", "权利人需据此调整跨国许可与诉讼组合策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A successful US injunction for Wilus would substantially enhance the enforceability and strategic importance of US courts in SEP disputes.", "The center of gravity for global SEP litigation may shift toward the US market, altering traditional venue preferences for major stakeholders.", "Rights holders must adapt their multinational licensing frameworks and litigation portfolios to align with evolving US judicial enforcement trends."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕美国禁令对全球许可谈判的连锁反应", "需构建兼顾欧美司法特点的防御性专利策略"]

items.title: The never-ending story of Brexit – The Final Chapter
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定异议程序中在先权利须持续存在至决定作出时
items.title_en: CJEU Rules: Earlier Rights Must Exist Until EUIPO Decision in Opposition Proceedings
items.summary: The Court of Justice of the EU (“CJEU”) has rendered its long-awaited decision in EUIPO v Nowhere (C-337/22), disagreeing with the Advocate General’s (“AG”) opinion. In short, the CJEU held that, in opposition proceedings, the earlier right must continue to exist until the time the European Intellectual Property Office (“EUIPO”) or its Board of Appeal (“BoA”) renders the decision.   Background In
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在EUIPO诉Nowhere案（C-337/22）中作出裁决，驳回了总检察官的意见。法院认定，在商标异议程序中，作为异议基础的在先权利必须持续存在，直至欧盟知识产权局或其上诉委员会作出最终决定之时。该判决明确了异议审查期间权利有效性的时间截止点。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU has issued its final judgment in EUIPO v Nowhere (C-337/22), departing from the Advocate General’s opinion. The Court clarified that, within opposition proceedings, the earlier right serving as the basis for opposition must remain valid and existing until the moment the European Union Intellectual Property Office or its Board of Appeal renders its decision. This ruling resolves significant uncertainty regarding the temporal validity of rights, particularly concerning United Kingdom trademarks following Brexit. By establishing that the critical assessment point is the date of the administrative decision rather than an earlier procedural stage, the CJEU provides a definitive standard for evaluating the continuity of earlier rights. This interpretation ensures legal certainty for all parties involved in cross-border intellectual property disputes within the EU framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了异议程序中在先权利有效性的关键时间节点，即需维持至行政决定作出时刻。实务中需关注此标准对异议证据提交及权利状态监控的影响，特别是针对可能在审查期间失效的权利基础。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders relying on third-country marks, such as those from the UK, to oppose EU applications must ensure these rights remain in force throughout the entire administrative process. Since the validity is assessed at the time of the final decision, any lapse or cancellation before that date will invalidate the opposition grounds. Practitioners should implement robust monitoring systems to track the status of cited earlier rights dynamically. It is advisable to prepare alternative legal strategies or supplementary evidence in case the primary earlier right expires during lengthy proceedings, thereby mitigating the risk of opposition failure due to loss of standing.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院在C-337/22案中作出判决，不同意总检察官此前发表的意见", "裁定在异议程序中，在先权利须持续存在至EUIPO或上诉委员会作出决定时"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Earlier rights relied upon in opposition must remain valid until the EUIPO or Board of Appeal issues its final decision.", "The judgment overturns the Advocate General's opinion, establishing a new procedural standard for assessing rights continuity.", "The ruling clarifies the temporal limits of post-Brexit UK trademark validity within EU opposition procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确异议程序中在先权利有效性的截止时间为行政决定作出时", "需监测该裁决对EUIPO后续异议案件审查标准的具体执行影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure third-country earlier rights remain valid until the EUIPO decision date to sustain opposition grounds.", "Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring of cited rights' status and prepare fallback strategies against mid-process lapses.", "What to monitor: Changes in the legal status of UK and other non-EU earlier rights during pending opposition proceedings."]
core_points_en: Earlier rights relied upon in opposition must remain valid until the EUIPO or Board of Appeal issues its final decision.
The judgment overturns the Advocate General's opinion, establishing a new procedural standard for assessing rights continuity.
The ruling clarifies the temporal limits of post-Brexit UK trademark validity within EU opposition procedures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure third-country earlier rights remain valid until the EUIPO decision date to sustain opposition grounds.
Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring of cited rights' status and prepare fallback strategies against mid-process lapses.
What to monitor: Changes in the legal status of UK and other non-EU earlier rights during pending opposition proceedings.

items.title: Presidium announces establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division
items.title_zh: UPC主席团宣布杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭
items.title_en: UPC Presidium Establishes Second Panel at Düsseldorf Local Division Effective March 2026
items.summary: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has decided the establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division (DE) as of 1 March 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）主席团于2026年1月20日作出决定，自2026年3月1日起在杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭。贝雷尼斯·托姆法官被任命为第二合议庭审判长，英戈·林肯法官加入该庭。第一合议庭由罗尼·托马斯法官任审判长，朱勒·舒马赫法官加入。该决定依据UPCA及程序规则作出，旨在优化案件审理能力。
items.ai_summary_en: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has decided to establish a second panel at the Düsseldorf Local Division, effective 1 March 2026. This decision, adopted on 20 January 2026 under Article 8 of the UPCA and Rule 341.4 of the Rules of Procedure, aims to enhance judicial capacity. Ms Bérénice Thom is appointed as Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joined by legally qualified judge Mr Ingo Rinken. Panel 1 remains presided over by Mr Ronny Thomas, with Ms Jule Schumacher serving as the second legally qualified judge. The expansion reflects ongoing adjustments to manage caseloads in one of the court’s busiest divisions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This structural expansion addresses capacity needs in Düsseldorf, a key venue for European patent litigation. Rights holders should note that case allocation mechanisms may evolve to distribute work between the two panels. Monitoring the composition of these panels is essential for understanding potential judicial approaches in upcoming proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC主席团决定自2026年3月1日起在杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭", "贝雷尼斯·托姆法官任第二合议庭审判长，英戈·林肯法官为成员", "罗尼·托马斯法官继续担任第一合议庭审判长，朱勒·舒马赫法官为成员"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A second panel at the UPC Düsseldorf Local Division becomes operational on 1 March 2026.", "Ms Bérénice Thom serves as Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joined by Mr Ingo Rinken.", "Mr Ronny Thomas continues as Presiding Judge of Panel 1, alongside Ms Jule Schumacher."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["杜塞尔多夫地方法院案件量增长推动司法资源扩容，反映其作为专利诉讼首选地的地位", "新合议庭的成立可能改变案件分配机制，影响具体案件的审理法官组合", "需观察后续是否还有其他地方法院跟进增设合议庭，以应对UPC诉讼量的整体变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Increased judicial capacity in Düsseldorf may influence forum selection decisions for parties seeking efficient resolution of patent disputes in Germany.", "Practice note: Litigators should track case assignment protocols to anticipate which panel will hear specific matters, as judicial composition can impact procedural management.", "What to monitor: Observe whether other high-volume Local Divisions implement similar panel expansions to handle growing UPC caseloads across the system."]
core_points_en: A second panel at the UPC Düsseldorf Local Division becomes operational on 1 March 2026.
Ms Bérénice Thom serves as Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joined by Mr Ingo Rinken.
Mr Ronny Thomas continues as Presiding Judge of Panel 1, alongside Ms Jule Schumacher.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Increased judicial capacity in Düsseldorf may influence forum selection decisions for parties seeking efficient resolution of patent disputes in Germany.
Practice note: Litigators should track case assignment protocols to anticipate which panel will hear specific matters, as judicial composition can impact procedural management.
What to monitor: Observe whether other high-volume Local Divisions implement similar panel expansions to handle growing UPC caseloads across the system.

items.title: Article 33.1(b) UPCA: “same infringement” does not require identical accused products
items.title_zh: UPCA第33.1(b)条：“同一侵权”不要求被告产品完全相同
items.title_en: UPC Ruling: Same Infringement Claim Does Not Require Identical Accused Products
items.summary: Valeo v Bosch – Local Division Paris, 17 February 2026 (UPC_CFI_1963/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎地方法院在Valeo诉Bosch案中裁定，UPCA第33.1(b)条规定的“同一涉嫌侵权”指侵犯同一专利，而非要求被控产品、分销渠道或具体行为完全一致。法院驳回博世关于管辖权的初步异议，强调该条款应灵活解释以避免多重诉讼，只要被告间存在商业关系且涉嫌侵犯同一专利即可合并审理。
items.ai_summary_en: In Valeo v Bosch, the Paris Local Division of the Unified Patent Court clarified Article 33.1(b) of the UPC Agreement regarding multi-defendant actions. The court rejected Bosch’s preliminary objection challenging internal competence, which argued that different entities were accused regarding distinct product variants and distribution channels. The judges held that 'same alleged infringement' refers to the violation of the same patent, not identical products or commercial conduct. This flexible interpretation aims to prevent multiple proceedings and irreconcilable decisions. The ruling confirms that a commercial relationship within a corporate group, combined with allegations of infringing the same patent, is sufficient to consolidate cases against multiple defendants, regardless of differences in specific product embodiments or roles in manufacturing and distribution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决降低了在UPC针对多个被告合并起诉的门槛，原告无需证明所有被告涉及完全相同的产品。企业需注意，即便供应链角色不同，只要涉及同一专利侵权，均可能在同一分院被诉。这有助于减少碎片化诉讼，但增加了被告应对联合诉讼的风险，需关注法院对“商业关系”认定的灵活性。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent litigation in Europe should recognize that the threshold for consolidating multi-defendant actions has been lowered. Even if different corporate entities handle distinct product variants or operate in separate regions, they may still be sued together for infringing the same patent. Companies must reassess the effectiveness of their current entity structures and risk isolation measures within Europe. It is crucial to evaluate potential liability across the entire corporate group rather than assuming geographic or product-based separation provides jurisdictional safety. Proactive coordination of defense strategies and compliance assessments across all relevant entities is essential to manage exposure effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定“同一涉嫌侵权”指侵犯同一专利，不要求被控产品或分销链完全相同", "不同被告涉及不同产品变体或制造分销角色，仍可满足合并审理的管辖条件", "隶属同一集团并参与相关商业活动足以建立所需的商业关系，支持合并管辖"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Same infringement means violating the same patent, not requiring identical product models or distribution chains.", "Multiple defendants can be joined if they share a commercial relationship and are accused of infringing the same patent.", "Flexible jurisdictional interpretation prevents fragmented litigation and reduces risks of conflicting judicial outcomes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确UPC合并被告管辖标准，降低原告举证难度，利于集中维权", "被告不能仅以产品型号或销售渠道差异为由挑战法院管辖权", "需持续观察后续案件中对“商业关系”及“同一专利”认定的具体边界"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Lowered consolidation thresholds mean diverse product lines within a corporate group face unified litigation risks.", "Practice note: Re-evaluate European entity layouts and patent risk isolation strategies to ensure they remain effective under new precedents.", "What to monitor: Develop unified European patent defense and compliance frameworks to address cross-entity infringement allegations proactively."]
core_points_en: Same infringement means violating the same patent, not requiring identical product models or distribution chains.
Multiple defendants can be joined if they share a commercial relationship and are accused of infringing the same patent.
Flexible jurisdictional interpretation prevents fragmented litigation and reduces risks of conflicting judicial outcomes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Lowered consolidation thresholds mean diverse product lines within a corporate group face unified litigation risks.
Practice note: Re-evaluate European entity layouts and patent risk isolation strategies to ensure they remain effective under new precedents.
What to monitor: Develop unified European patent defense and compliance frameworks to address cross-entity infringement allegations proactively.

items.title: UPC Court of Appeal: ATE insurance can eliminate the need for security for costs
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院：事后保险可免除诉讼费用担保
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: ATE Insurance Can Eliminate Need for Security for Costs
items.summary: On 18 February 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal set aside an order for security for costs of €2 million, holding that the Munich Local Division (Munich LD)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月18日，UPC上诉法院撤销慕尼黑地方法院要求原告提供200万欧元诉讼费用担保的命令。法院认定，下级法院未考量原告持有的事后保险（ATE），属行使裁量权错误。该保险由马耳他 insurer 承保，含不可撤销条款及欧盟偿付能力II监管保障，足以保护被告利益，故无需额外担保。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal set aside a €2 million security for costs order, ruling the Munich Local Division erred by ignoring the claimant’s After The Event (ATE) insurance. In Syntorr LP v Arthrex Inc., the court held that ATE coverage forms part of the claimant’s financial position under Rule 158.1 RoP. The policy, issued by a Maltese insurer with an anti-avoidance endorsement, provided adequate protection for recoverable costs. The decision clarifies that valid ATE insurance can remove the need for traditional bank guarantees or cash deposits in UPC proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确ATE保险在UPC程序中可作为评估诉讼费用回收风险的实质因素。当事人若持有符合欧盟监管标准的保险，可有效抗辩高额现金或银行担保要求。需关注法院对保险人偿付能力及保单条款（如不可撤销性）的审查标准，这将影响后续类似案件的担保策略与成本结构。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment establishes that robust ATE insurance can replace traditional security for costs in UPC litigation. Rights holders should ensure policies include anti-avoidance clauses and direct enforcement rights to mitigate defendant objections regarding insurer solvency or policy validity.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院认定慕尼黑地方法院未考量原告ATE保险，属裁量权行使错误", "法院确认符合欧盟偿付能力II监管的ATE保险构成原告财务状况一部分，可替代担保", "保险中的反规避背书及60天终止宽限期，足以消除被告对保单有效性的顾虑"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["ATE insurance constitutes part of the claimant’s financial position when assessing security for costs needs.", "Anti-avoidance endorsements ensuring non-cancellability and direct benefit to defendants satisfy recovery concerns.", "Lower courts must actively consider existing insurance coverage rather than assuming security is automatically required."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["ATE保险被纳入原告“财务状况”评估，成为免除传统费用担保的关键依据", "欧盟偿付能力II指令下的保险人资质，是法院认可保险效力的重要信任基础", "需持续观察UPC对不同类型保险条款（如终止权、直接执行权）的具体审查尺度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Valid ATE insurance with appropriate endorsements can avoid tying up capital in bank guarantees or cash deposits for UPC cases.", "Practice note: Drafting ATE policies with anti-avoidance endorsements and clear direct benefit clauses is critical to satisfying the Court’s recovery risk test.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions on how strictly courts scrutinize insurer solvency and specific policy termination rights under EU regulatory frameworks."]
core_points_en: ATE insurance constitutes part of the claimant’s financial position when assessing security for costs needs.
Anti-avoidance endorsements ensuring non-cancellability and direct benefit to defendants satisfy recovery concerns.
Lower courts must actively consider existing insurance coverage rather than assuming security is automatically required.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Valid ATE insurance with appropriate endorsements can avoid tying up capital in bank guarantees or cash deposits for UPC cases.
Practice note: Drafting ATE policies with anti-avoidance endorsements and clear direct benefit clauses is critical to satisfying the Court’s recovery risk test.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions on how strictly courts scrutinize insurer solvency and specific policy termination rights under EU regulatory frameworks.

items.title: Exemptions and compensation schemes for commercial users: Private copying levies after the CJEU’s ruling in bluechip v ZPÜ (C-822/24)
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院bluechip案：商业用户私人复制 levy 豁免与补偿
items.title_en: CJEU Clarifies Private Copying Levy Exemptions and Compensation Conditions for Commercial Users
items.summary: In bluechip Computer Aktiengesellschaft v Zentralstelle für Überspielungsrechte (ZPÜ), the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) further determines the conditions under which EU Member States can implement the exception or limitation for reproduction of protected works for private uses under art.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在bluechip诉ZPÜ案中裁定，成员国仅当存储设备被自然人用于非直接或间接商业目的的私人复制时，方可依据《信息社会指令》征收公平补偿金。法院强调需平衡权利人利益与用户权益，明确了向商业终端用户销售设备时的举证责任及补偿金收取条件。
items.ai_summary_en: In Bluechip Computer Aktiengesellschaft v Zentralstelle für Überspielungsrechte (ZPÜ), the Court of Justice of the European Union clarified that Member States may only levy fair compensation under Article 5(2)(b) of the InfoSoc Directive when devices are used by natural persons for non-commercial private copying. The Court addressed whether a rebuttable presumption applies to devices sold to commercial end-users, assuming such devices facilitate private reproduction. It confirmed that while this presumption exists, manufacturers, importers, or traders can avoid liability if they provide evidence proving that the devices were not used for private copying on more than a small scale. This ruling defines the boundaries for collecting levies and establishes the specific burden of proof required from rights holders and device suppliers within the EU copyright framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案澄清了商业环境下私人复制补偿金的适用边界，强调了“非商业目的”的核心地位。企业需关注设备销售对象及使用场景的举证责任分配，以及各国据此调整国内补偿金征收机制的可能性。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants selling storage media in the EU should review their historical levy payment records, particularly in jurisdictions like Germany where presumptions favor collection from commercial sales channels. Establishing robust mechanisms to document final user purposes is critical for challenging these presumptions effectively. Companies should assess past transactions to identify potential overpayments and evaluate eligibility for reimbursement claims. Proactive evidence retention regarding customer usage profiles will help mitigate financial exposure and prevent double costs arising from conflicting national interpretations of private copying exceptions and compensation schemes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院明确私人复制例外仅适用于自然人非商业目的的复制行为", "成员国可要求制造商支付补偿金，除非其证明设备仅用于小规模私人复制", "立法目的在于补偿未经授权使用造成的损害，需平衡权利人与用户利益"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Fair compensation is strictly limited to reproductions made by natural persons for non-commercial private purposes.", "A rebuttable presumption exists that devices sold to commercial users facilitate private copying subject to levies.", "Manufacturers bear the burden of proof to demonstrate that devices were not used for private copying to avoid fees."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确私人复制补偿金征收前提是设备用于自然人非商业目的", "制造商需承担反驳推定的举证责任，证明设备未用于私人复制", "观察成员国如何根据此裁决调整针对商业用户的补偿金征收政策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers must implement systems to capture final user data to rebut statutory presumptions linking commercial sales to private copying.", "Practice note: Review historical sales and levy payments to identify opportunities for refund claims based on the clarified burden of proof standards.", "What to monitor: National implementations of the CJEU ruling regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving non-private use by commercial entities."]
core_points_en: Fair compensation is strictly limited to reproductions made by natural persons for non-commercial private purposes.
A rebuttable presumption exists that devices sold to commercial users facilitate private copying subject to levies.
Manufacturers bear the burden of proof to demonstrate that devices were not used for private copying to avoid fees.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers must implement systems to capture final user data to rebut statutory presumptions linking commercial sales to private copying.
Practice note: Review historical sales and levy payments to identify opportunities for refund claims based on the clarified burden of proof standards.
What to monitor: National implementations of the CJEU ruling regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving non-private use by commercial entities.

items.title: Via adds two major Chinese auto suppliers to Qi patent pool
items.title_zh: Via 无线充电专利池新增两家中国主要汽车供应商
items.title_en: Via Adds Major Chinese Auto Suppliers to Qi SEP Pool as Powermat Expands Independent Licensing
items.summary: The programme gets fresh licensee endorsement while non-pool licensor Powermat steps up own licensing efforts
items.ai_summary_zh: Via Licensing 管理的 Qi 标准必要专利池获得两家中国主要汽车供应商的许可背书，显示该专利池在汽车行业渗透力增强。与此同时，非专利池成员 Powermat 正加强其独立许可计划。这一动态反映了无线充电领域 SEP 许可格局的演变，可能影响进入欧洲市场的中国汽车供应链企业的合规策略与授权成本结构。
items.ai_summary_en: Via Licensing’s Qi standard-essential patent pool has secured license endorsements from two major Chinese automotive suppliers, signaling strengthened penetration within the global automotive supply chain. This development highlights the growing acceptance of pooled licensing models for wireless charging technologies among key industry participants. Concurrently, non-pool licensor Powermat is intensifying its independent licensing efforts, creating a dual-track enforcement environment. These parallel dynamics illustrate the evolving landscape of SEP licensing in the wireless charging sector. For market participants, particularly those supplying European automakers, this shift necessitates careful evaluation of compliance strategies and potential authorization cost structures. The coexistence of pool-based and bilateral licensing approaches suggests a complex negotiation environment where rights holders are actively pursuing diverse monetization paths across different jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及供应商应评估 Via 与 Powermat 双重许可压力，避免重复付费。建议提前开展 FTE 分析，利用 FRAND 原则谈判，并关注欧洲潜在反垄断监管动向以维护自身权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should assess exposure to dual licensing pressures from both Via and Powermat to mitigate risks of double-dipping or redundant royalty payments. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses early in product development cycles allows companies to leverage FRAND principles effectively during negotiations. Additionally, monitoring potential antitrust scrutiny by European regulators regarding cumulative royalty rates and licensing practices is crucial. Rights holders and implementers alike must navigate these overlapping claims carefully, utilizing EU competition law tools where appropriate to optimize licensing terms and ensure compliant market access without excessive financial burden.

items.title: BREAKING: Ericsson sues Verifone at UPC in first IoT SEP enforcement actions
items.title_zh: 爱立信在 UPC 起诉 Verifone，启动首例物联网 SEP 执法
items.title_en: Ericsson Sues Verifone at UPC in First IoT SEP Enforcement Action
items.summary: SEP owner has asked Mannheim Local Division to determine whether its offer is FRAND
items.ai_summary_zh: 标准必要专利持有人爱立信已向统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院提起诉讼，指控 Verifone 侵权。此案标志着 UPC 受理的首批物联网领域 SEP 执法行动。核心诉求在于请求法院裁定爱立信提出的许可要约是否符合公平、合理及无歧视（FRAND）原则，这将测试新法院在处理复杂许可纠纷上的效能。
items.ai_summary_en: Standard essential patent holder Ericsson has initiated infringement proceedings against Verifone before the Unified Patent Court. Filed at the Mannheim Local Division, this case represents the first enforcement action concerning Internet of Things standard essential patents within the new court system. The core legal request asks the tribunal to determine whether Ericsson’s licensing offer complies with fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory principles. This litigation serves as a critical test for the UPC’s capacity to handle complex global licensing disputes. It highlights the court's emerging role as a central venue for resolving high-stakes intellectual property conflicts across Europe, setting important precedents for future technology sector litigation involving connectivity standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 IoT 企业需警惕 UPC 成为 SEP 诉讼新高地，其裁决具泛欧效力。建议提前评估专利风险，制定符合 FRAND 原则的谈判策略，避免在德国等地遭遇禁令威胁，并密切关注曼海姆分院的裁判风向。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the connected device sector should recognize the UPC as an increasingly influential forum for SEP disputes, given its pan-European injunctive relief capabilities. Rights holders and implementers must ensure their licensing negotiations strictly adhere to FRAND obligations to mitigate litigation risks. Companies should proactively assess their patent exposure and refine negotiation protocols to withstand judicial scrutiny, particularly in jurisdictions like Germany known for rigorous enforcement. Monitoring early procedural developments and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division is essential for adapting corporate IP strategies to this new legal landscape effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["爱立信发起 UPC 首例物联网 SEP 诉讼，被告为 Verifone", "案件由曼海姆地方分院审理，聚焦许可要约的 FRAND 合规性", "此举确立 UPC 作为欧洲 SEP 争议解决新核心平台的地位"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ericsson filed the first IoT SEP lawsuit at the UPC against Verifone, marking a significant milestone in European patent enforcement history.", "The Mannheim Local Division will adjudicate the FRAND compliance of Ericsson's licensing offer, focusing on equitable negotiation standards.", "This action establishes the UPC as a primary platform for resolving SEP disputes, influencing future European intellectual property litigation strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC 首案确立新范式，中国出口企业面临泛欧禁令风险", "需优化 FRAND 谈判合规性，以应对德国分院的严格审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: The UPC offers pan-European injunctive relief, significantly increasing leverage for SEP owners and risk for implementers.", "Practice note: Parties must rigorously document FRAND-compliant negotiation steps to defend against potential injunctions in strict jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: Early case management decisions and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division regarding SEP licensing norms."]
core_points_en: Ericsson filed the first IoT SEP lawsuit at the UPC against Verifone, marking a significant milestone in European patent enforcement history.
The Mannheim Local Division will adjudicate the FRAND compliance of Ericsson's licensing offer, focusing on equitable negotiation standards.
This action establishes the UPC as a primary platform for resolving SEP disputes, influencing future European intellectual property litigation strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: The UPC offers pan-European injunctive relief, significantly increasing leverage for SEP owners and risk for implementers.
Practice note: Parties must rigorously document FRAND-compliant negotiation steps to defend against potential injunctions in strict jurisdictions.
What to monitor: Early case management decisions and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division regarding SEP licensing norms.

items.title: Unitary effect requires designation of all participating Member States
items.title_zh: 单一效力要求指定所有参与成员国：Papst Licensing案判决
items.title_en: UPC Confirms Unitary Effect Requires Designation of All Participating Member States
items.summary: Papst Licensing v EPO – Court of Appeal, 9 February 2026, UPC_CoA_8/2026
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院驳回Papst Licensing针对EP 3 327 608单一效力注册被拒的上诉。因该专利源于2005年申请，当时马耳他未加入EPC，导致授权时未指定马耳他。法院依据第1257/2012号条例，确认若授权专利未覆盖所有参与成员国，则不得授予单一效力，不存在领土 carve-out 空间。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal dismissed Papst Licensing’s appeal, upholding the EPO’s refusal to register unitary effect for EP 3 327 608. The patent originated from a 2005 application filed before Malta joined the EPC, resulting in a granted text that excluded Malta. By the time of grant in 2025, Malta was a participating Member State. The Court ruled that Article 3(1) of Regulation 1257/2012 strictly requires the same claims for all participating states, prohibiting territorial carve-outs. Arguments based on proportionality or fundamental rights were rejected as contra legem interpretations. This decision clarifies that historical designation gaps prevent unitary effect, even if unintentional. While currently rare due to Malta’s early EPC accession, similar compliance risks may arise if Montenegro joins the EU and UPCA, given its later EPC entry in 2022.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案揭示了单一专利体系的结构性限制：仅影响马耳他加入EPC前提交的极老旧申请。实务中需关注黑山等后续加入EPC但暂未参与单一专利体系的国家，若其未来加入欧盟及UPCA，可能引发类似资格问题。目前风险局限于特定历史申请。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders managing legacy patent portfolios must verify whether early applications predate the EPC membership of current unitary participating states. If a participating state was not designated due to historical non-membership, the patent cannot obtain unitary effect. Stakeholders should assess such assets for conversion into traditional European patent bundles to ensure enforceability. Proactive audits of long-standing patent families are essential to identify territorial coverage gaps. For affected patents, alternative litigation strategies or licensing structures should be developed to mitigate validity risks associated with the lack of unitary status.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院确认，授予单一效力的前提是欧洲专利必须以相同权利要求覆盖所有参与成员国", "因马耳他在申请日尚未加入EPC，涉案专利未指定该国，故EPO拒绝注册单一效力获法院支持", "法院强调，比例原则或基本权利不能作为违背法律明文规定的理由，且拒绝向欧盟法院提请裁决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unitary effect mandates that the European patent be granted with identical claims covering all participating Member States without exception.", "Historical inability to designate a state at filing does not justify partial unitary effect or territorial carve-outs under current regulations.", "Future EU accession by EPC members like Montenegro could recreate this eligibility barrier for older patent families lacking prior designations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["单一专利不允许领土排除，必须覆盖所有参与国，否则无法注册", "当前风险仅存在于马耳他加入EPC（2007年）前提交的专利申请家族", "需监测黑山等非欧盟EPC成员国未来加入欧盟后的制度衔接潜在影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify if legacy patent applications predate the EPC accession of current unitary participating states to avoid registration refusals.", "Practice note: Convert affected patents to traditional European patent bundles and adjust enforcement strategies to address territorial coverage gaps.", "What to monitor: Potential future EU and UPCA accession by EPC members like Montenegro, which may trigger similar designation issues for newer filings."]
core_points_en: Unitary effect mandates that the European patent be granted with identical claims covering all participating Member States without exception.
Historical inability to designate a state at filing does not justify partial unitary effect or territorial carve-outs under current regulations.
Future EU accession by EPC members like Montenegro could recreate this eligibility barrier for older patent families lacking prior designations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify if legacy patent applications predate the EPC accession of current unitary participating states to avoid registration refusals.
Practice note: Convert affected patents to traditional European patent bundles and adjust enforcement strategies to address territorial coverage gaps.
What to monitor: Potential future EU and UPCA accession by EPC members like Montenegro, which may trigger similar designation issues for newer filings.

items.title: A new sense of self: Denmark's copyright amendment against deepfakes
items.title_zh: 丹麦修订版权法应对深度伪造风险
items.title_en: Denmark Amends Copyright Act to Regulate Deepfakes and Digital Imitations
items.summary: The explosion in the use of generative artificial intelligence has heightened concern about deepfakes. How can Intellectual Property (IP) legislation address the risks?
items.ai_summary_zh: 丹麦政府于2025年6月提出版权法修正案，旨在应对生成式人工智能带来的深度伪造风险。该法案获跨党派支持，预计年内生效。修正案新增两项保护：未经同意不得公开表演者的逼真数字模仿作品；未经同意不得公开自然人的逼真数字形象（如外貌、声音）。两项权利保护期均为死后50年。
items.ai_summary_en: Denmark is amending its Copyright Act to address risks from generative AI and deepfakes, with reforms expected to enter force in late 2025. Proposed in June 2025 with cross-party support, the legislation introduces safeguards against unauthorized digital imitations of individuals and artists’ performances. The government cites concerns that realistic AI-generated media causes severe emotional distress and undermines democratic integrity by blurring the line between reality and fabrication. These amendments aim to protect both natural persons and performers from non-consensual use of their likeness, voice, or performance in synthesized media, marking a significant legislative step in Europe to mitigate the societal and legal impacts of advanced AI tools.
items.ai_insight_zh: 丹麦通过版权法扩展保护范围至数字身份模仿，反映了欧盟成员国在AI治理上的立法趋势。实务中需关注“逼真”标准的司法界定及与现有肖像权、数据保护法规的竞合关系。建议持续跟踪法案最终文本及后续配套指南，评估对内容创作及AI训练数据合规的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This amendment expands copyright-adjacent protections to cover personality rights within IP frameworks, offering rights holders new tools against non-consensual AI synthesis. For European practitioners, it highlights a trend where national laws address AI-specific harms faster than EU-wide harmonization. Companies should assess how these personal and performance rights intersect with existing data protection and portrait rights regimes to ensure compliance in content creation and licensing workflows.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["丹麦拟在版权法中新增第65a条，禁止未经许可公开表演者的逼真数字模仿作品", "新增第73a条，禁止未经许可公开自然人外貌及声音等特征的逼真数字生成模仿", "上述针对数字模仿的保护期限均规定为相关个人去世后五十年"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New provisions prohibit public dissemination of realistic digital imitations of natural persons without consent.", "Amendments extend protection to performers against unauthorized realistic digital reproductions of their performances.", "Legislation targets deepfake risks including false endorsements, defamatory speech, and political misinformation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["丹麦将数字身份模仿纳入版权法保护，强化了表演者及自然人对自身形象的控制权", "需观察司法实践中如何界定“逼真数字模仿”及其与言论自由、艺术创作的边界", "后续应关注该立法是否引发其他北欧或欧盟成员国的类似修法跟进"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and individuals gain specific statutory grounds to challenge unauthorized deepfakes, complementing existing privacy and defamation claims.", "Practice note: Legal teams must distinguish between copyright infringement and these new personal/performance imitation rights when drafting takedown notices or licensing agreemen…", "What to monitor: Watch for judicial interpretation of 'realistic' imitations and potential ripple effects in other Nordic or EU jurisdictions adopting similar AI-specific IP refor…"]
core_points_en: New provisions prohibit public dissemination of realistic digital imitations of natural persons without consent.
Amendments extend protection to performers against unauthorized realistic digital reproductions of their performances.
Legislation targets deepfake risks including false endorsements, defamatory speech, and political misinformation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and individuals gain specific statutory grounds to challenge unauthorized deepfakes, complementing existing privacy and defamation claims.
Practice note: Legal teams must distinguish between copyright infringement and these new personal/performance imitation rights when drafting takedown notices or licensing agreemen…
What to monitor: Watch for judicial interpretation of 'realistic' imitations and potential ripple effects in other Nordic or EU jurisdictions adopting similar AI-specific IP refor…

items.title: Ericsson reveals market-by-market IoT approach, new healthcare/agriculture deals
items.title_zh: 爱立信披露分市场 IoT 策略及新医疗农业协议
items.title_en: Ericsson Unveils Market-Specific IoT Licensing Strategy and New Healthcare, Agriculture Deals
items.summary: New insights from Swedish company helps build picture of how leading licensors are tackling tricky and fragmented IoT markets
items.ai_summary_zh: 瑞典爱立信公司披露其针对碎片化物联网市场的逐国许可策略，并宣布在医疗与农业领域达成新交易。该动态揭示了主要 SEP 权利人如何应对复杂市场，为观察欧洲物联网领域的标准必要专利许可实践提供了关键视角。
items.ai_summary_en: Swedish technology giant Ericsson has disclosed its market-by-market approach to licensing Internet of Things (IoT) patents, alongside announcements of new agreements in the healthcare and agricultural sectors. This strategic shift provides critical insights into how leading standard-essential patent (SEP) holders are navigating the complex and highly fragmented IoT landscape. By tailoring licensing efforts to specific industry verticals and geographic markets, Ericsson demonstrates a methodical response to the challenges posed by diverse IoT implementations. These developments offer a significant perspective on current SEP licensing practices within Europe, highlighting how major licensors are adapting their commercial strategies to address the unique regulatory and technical requirements of distinct industrial sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 IoT 企业应关注爱立信分市场策略，评估出口欧洲的许可成本。建议提前布局医疗农业领域专利交叉许可，防范 SEP 诉讼风险，优化合规架构。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely analyze Ericsson’s segmented licensing model to anticipate potential fluctuations in European royalty rates and compliance costs. Companies operating in connected health or smart agriculture sectors must strengthen their patent reserves to better position themselves during future negotiations with SEP holders. Proactively assessing cross-licensing opportunities in these high-growth verticals can help mitigate litigation risks. Furthermore, firms should review their internal IP compliance architectures to ensure alignment with evolving industry-specific licensing norms, thereby optimizing cost structures and reducing exposure to enforcement actions in key export markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["爱立信采取按市场细分的物联网专利许可策略", "新增医疗健康与农业科技领域的专利授权合作", "揭示头部许可方应对碎片化市场的实际操作路径"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ericsson employs a segmented, market-specific strategy for licensing its IoT-related patent portfolio across different regions.", "The company has secured new patent licensing collaborations specifically targeting the healthcare and agricultural technology sectors.", "These moves illustrate practical pathways for top-tier licensors to manage and monetize rights in fragmented IoT markets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注分市场策略以预判欧洲许可成本波动", "加强医疗农业领域专利储备以应对许可谈判"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor segmented licensing strategies to forecast European royalty cost volatility and budget accordingly.", "Practice note: Enhance patent portfolios in healthcare and agriculture to strengthen leverage in upcoming SEP negotiations.", "What to monitor: Track new sector-specific licensing deals to identify emerging compliance benchmarks and litigation trends."]
core_points_en: Ericsson employs a segmented, market-specific strategy for licensing its IoT-related patent portfolio across different regions.
The company has secured new patent licensing collaborations specifically targeting the healthcare and agricultural technology sectors.
These moves illustrate practical pathways for top-tier licensors to manage and monetize rights in fragmented IoT markets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor segmented licensing strategies to forecast European royalty cost volatility and budget accordingly.
Practice note: Enhance patent portfolios in healthcare and agriculture to strengthen leverage in upcoming SEP negotiations.
What to monitor: Track new sector-specific licensing deals to identify emerging compliance benchmarks and litigation trends.

items.title: BREAKING: Wilus enforces Wi-Fi 6 injunction, asks Asus to remove products from Germany
items.title_zh: 突发：Wilus 执行 Wi-Fi 6 禁令，要求华硕撤出德国市场
items.title_en: Wilus Enforces Wi-Fi 6 Injunction Against Asus in Germany Following Munich Court Ruling
items.summary: Sisvel licensor moves to impose sales ban awarded by Munich Regional Court
items.ai_summary_zh: Sisvel 被许可方 Wilus 依据慕尼黑地区法院此前作出的判决，正式执行针对华硕（Asus）产品的销售禁令。该行动聚焦于 Wi-Fi 6 标准必要专利侵权纠纷，强制要求华硕立即从德国市场移除相关涉案产品。此举标志着德国法院在 SEP 执法层面的强硬立场，展示了专利权人利用当地司法程序迅速实现市场排除的商业策略，对涉及无线通信标准的终端制造商构成直接合规挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: Wilus, a Sisvel licensor, has moved to enforce a sales ban against Asus products in Germany. The action follows an injunction awarded by the Munich Regional Court regarding alleged infringement of Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents. This development highlights ongoing enforcement activities in the German jurisdiction concerning wireless communication technologies and the immediate commercial implications for device manufacturers facing such judicial orders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类执法动作给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case underscores the continued significance of German courts in SEP disputes, particularly regarding Wi-Fi standards. Rights holders are actively utilizing local injunctive relief to secure market exclusivity or leverage licensing negotiations. For stakeholders, this reinforces the need to monitor enforcement trends in key European jurisdictions where patent litigation outcomes can rapidly impact product availability and supply chain stability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Wilus 依据慕尼黑法院判决，强制执行针对华硕的销售禁令", "涉案技术为 Wi-Fi 6 标准必要专利，要求产品立即撤出德国", "凸显德国作为 SEP 诉讼高地，禁令救济执行力度显著增强"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Wilus is enforcing a sales ban on Asus products in Germany based on a Munich Regional Court injunction.", "The enforcement action relates to alleged infringement of Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents held by Sisvel."]

Asus proceedings and any related appeals or settlement announcements affecting the German market."]

The enforcement action relates to alleged infringement of Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents held by Sisvel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Device manufacturers should assess exposure to SEP enforcement actions in Germany, especially for Wi-Fi 6 compliant products.
Practice note: Monitor the execution phase of Munich Regional Court decisions involving Sisvel licensors to gauge enforcement speed and scope.
What to monitor: Further developments in Wilus v. Asus proceedings and any related appeals or settlement announcements affecting the German market.
